and wonders
on the earth below.
There will be blood and fire
and clouds of smoke.
20The sun will turn dark,
and the moon
will be as red as blood
before the great
and wonderful day
of the Lord appears.
21Then the Lord
will save everyone
who asks for his help.”
22Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this. 23 God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross. 24 But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power. 25 What David said are really the words of Jesus,
“I always see the Lord
near me,
and I will not be afraid
with him at my right side.
26Because of this,
my heart will be glad,
my words will be joyful,
and I will live in hope.
27The Lord won't leave me
in the grave.
I am his holy one,
and he won't let
my body decay.
28He has shown me
the path to life,
and he makes me glad
by being near me.”
29My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here. 30 But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
31David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay. 32All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
33Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
34 David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side, 35until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.” 36Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
37When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
38Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit. 39This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
40Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.” 41On that day about 3,000 believed his message and were baptized. 42They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together.
Life among the Lord's Followers
43Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked. 44 All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had. 45They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need. 46Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely, 47while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_text:
Yennu Seyeeŋ baaru labaar
1Tɔn, jaamm nba ki bi yir Pentekost daare baar; daar maŋe ki Yisaweira kura lakin taan leeb boyennkɔɔ ni. 2Li taakpaak ni ki fuut nyii sanpaapo ki fu naan nan wonpaaruko da na, ki baar sik ki gbee diiuk nba ki bi kar na ni. 3Ki bi la ki siar naan nan mupeuŋ na, ki yat ki tɔɔ tɔɔ sɔɔ kur paak, 4ki Yennu Seyeeŋ gbee yent sɔɔ kur ni, ki bi piin piak barganii kaman nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nba wannib biaŋinba.
5Ki Juu teeb nba tee yentina mun din be Jerusalem li yoo, binba nyii tinii kur po tingbouŋ na ki baar leŋ. 6Ba gbat fuut maŋ yoonba, ki nibur tikir leŋ, ki li teemm yaarlituk, kimaan Yisaweira maŋ piak barganii sɔɔ kur maan ni ki u gbia. 7Ki li teemm bakitnauŋ nan yaarlituk, ki bi boi bi leeb a “Niib na kur nba piak na ki tee Galilee teeb kaa-aa? 8ki li teen nlee ki ti kura tan jia gbia ki bi piak ti mɔŋ mɔŋ maan nna? 9Ti siab nyii Patia nan Media nan Elam nan Mesopotamia nan Judea nan Kapadosia nan Pontus nan Asia, 10nan Frijia nan Pamfilia nan Ijipt, nan Libia yaka po siaminba kpia nan Sairene na; ki ti ni yemm mun bia nyii Rom; 11ki ti siab tee Juu teeb, ti siab mun bia tee boorganu nba din lebit bi jiantu ki waa Juu teeb Yennu; ki ti yemm mun bia nyii Kreet nan Arabia, ŋaan ki ti kura lek gbia ki bi piak ti mɔŋ mɔŋ maan ni, ki piak dont Yennu sann nan u bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki u tun.” 12Ki li bakit ŋaan yaar litib ki ŋmat bi yan, ki bi boi bi leeb a “Linba na paak tee bee?” 13Ki siab mun bia laa ki sarikit Yisaweira maŋ a “Bi nyuu daame ki yib.”
Piita mɔɔntii po
14Ŋanne ki Piita nan u leeb piik nan yenɔkɔɔ na fiir ki set nibur na tɔɔnn ni; ki Piita pak sanpaapowa a
“Yimm Juu teeb, nan yimm nba kur be Jerusalem na, gbiintir man ma yaa n paki linba na, kimaan n mɔk siare ki loon ki i bann. 15Ya dukii nan ti yibe na, ŋaan ti ki yib kaa, kimaan li daa tee sanyayendaanne. 16Li tee linba ki Yennu sɔkinii Joel din bunt buntir ki yaa linba tan sa teen nee, a
17‘Yennu yet a: Dunia joontu yoo
n tan sa tun nnee:
n tan sii sikint n Seek sɔɔ kur paak,
ki i bonjabis nan i sapaanbis tan sii sɔkint n maan,
ki i naasimm mun tan sii laat yerintu,
ki i jakpera tan sii damii damiit.
18Ki li yoo maŋ n tan sii sikint n Seek maŋ
n daaba nba tee jab nan binba tee poob paak,
ki bi mun bia tan sii sɔkint n maan.
19N tan sii tuun toonjaana sanpaapo,
nan bakitnauŋ nyina tingbouŋ na ni;
ki sɔn nan muu nan munyuuk tan sii be,
20ki yennu tan sa kpint ki bɔnn,
ki ŋmaarik mun tan sa kpint
ki mɔnn kaman nan sɔn na.
Li tan sa teen nneewa,
ki ti Yomdaanɔ-n tan jen sik tingbouŋ na ni
ki bu niib buut;
ki u jenu daar maŋ tan sii tee dajaanne;
21ki sɔɔ kur nba tan miarɔ nan u sann ni sa la tinnu.’ ”
22Ŋanne ki Piita bia ŋamm yet a “Yimm Israel teeb, gbiintir man linba ki n yaa n beti ki jikit saa Yisa nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na po; i mɔŋ mɔŋ mi nan Yennu te ki u tuu tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana nan nyina i ni, ki li set want nan mɔnii nan Yennue tumɔ. 23Ŋaan Yennu mi linba yaa lin tan teen. Li paak, ki u dɔŋ lor lor ki kubinɔ i nuu ni, ki i te ki biitdamm kpaa tabinɔ dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu, ki li want nan yimme set kpiiu.
24“Wa daan kpo ki daa be kuun nii ni na, ki Yennu fatɔ ki fiinɔ kuun ni, kimaan kuun ki fit diau. 25Kimaan David din bunt buntir ki jikit saa Yisa po ki yaa a
‘N mi nan n Yomdaanɔ Yennu yɔɔ be nanin,
ki u bia see n poorpo ki guun ki n ki laat daŋi.
26Li paak n mɔk parpeenn ki piak parpeenn maan.
Mi-i lekii taŋŋi kpo gbaa, ŋaan n sii mɔk dindann,
27kimaan u tan kan nyik n seek ki lin kpakin kpeentiŋi.
N tee u popeendaanɔe,
ki u kan sak ki n gbanant-n tan bati;
28ki u bia te ki n bann manfoor sɔnii,
ki tan sa baar kii be u tɔɔnn ni
kii mɔk parpeenn bonchiann.’ ”
29Ki Piita bia ŋamm yet yaa a “N naa waas, n yaa n pak yent i nummpoe ki jikit saa ti yejasaakɔɔ David po. U din kpo, ki bi piiu, ki u kaauk daa be ti boor nna na. 30U din tee Yennu sɔkiniie, ki din mi nan Yennu senn mɔsonn ki turɔ, ki yet yaa a li-i tee ki u kpo, u tan sa jii u yaaboon-yenɔkɔɔ ki dinnɔ u mɔŋ naan. 31David din mi linba ki Yennu yaa wun tan teen. Li paak ki u din pak Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia kuunfiiru po, ki yet a Yennu Niganntɔɔ tan kan kpakin kpeentiŋ, ki u gbanant bia kan bati.”
32Ki Piita bia ŋamm yet a “Yisa nba nee ki Yennu daan fiinɔ kuun ni, ki ti kura na mun tee u kuunfiiru maŋ siara damm. 33Ki Yennu bia donnɔ a wun kar u niidiitu po. Wa kar yoonba ki u gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki yaa a u tan sa turit na, ki sikin ti ni bonchiann. Tɔn, mɔtana, li Seyeeŋe ki i laat ki bia gbia ti ni na. 34David nba ŋarin din kpo na, u din ki doo Yendɔuŋ ni nan u gbananti. Tɔn, u buntir maŋ din ki want u mɔŋ donnu po kaa, ŋaan u din want Yisa poe, ki din yet a
‘Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu yet n Yomdaanɔ Masia a
Karin n niidiitu po
35mɔkmɔk nan ma tan sa jii a datai
ki teemm a taakpakta.’ ”
36Ki Piita bia yet a “Tɔn, li ŋan ki yimm Israel teeb kur-n bann nan li tee Yisa nee, wunba ki i daan te ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu na, ŋɔɔe ki Yennu wann nan u tee u Niganntɔɔ Masia, ki bia tee ti Yomdaanɔ biak.”
Biaŋinba ki niib tusa ŋanntaa teen Yisa yada
37Niib na nba gbat nna maŋ, ki bi para ni biir bonchiann. Ki bi boi Piita nan u leeb na a “Ei! ti naa waas, ki ti sa teen nlee?” 38Ki Piita betib a “See ki i kura-n nyik i biit, ki tin wuri Yennu nyunwuru nan Yisa Masia sann ni, ki Yennu-n nyik i biit ki chabi, ki sa turi u piinii nba tee u Seyeeŋ na; 39kimaan u poŋ din senn mɔsonn a u sa jii u Seyeeŋ na ki tur yimm nan i waas, nan binba kur be banfɔka, yimm nba kur ki u tan sa yiini ki yin sak u mɔb.”
40Li poorpoe ki Piita pak mɔmaan bonchiann ki nyinn siara, ŋaan bia kpaamm a “Hei! Nyint i mɔŋ man nan Yennu tubdatu nba baat mɔtana nibiit na paak na.” 41Ki bi siab bonchiann din sak u maan maŋ, kaman nan niib tusa ŋanntaa na, ki bi wurib Yennu nyunwuru, ki bi pukin bi paak li daar maŋ. 42Ki bi ŋammit ki lek tumii ki bant toomiinba maŋ wannu, ki bi jii bi yoo ki taan dia leeb nan mɔtaauk, ki lakint ki di jeet, ki bia tikii mei Yennu.
Yisaweira benu po
43Ki jijeet ji din mɔk sɔɔ kur; ki toomiinba na tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana bonchiann nan Yennu paŋ ni. 44Ki Yisaweira maŋ kura bia lek dia leeb nan mɔtaauk, ki chentir bi bona ki teen bi leeb, 45ki tuu kɔi bi faa ki jikit li likirii ki toor bi leeb, ki jaŋŋit kaman nan sɔɔ kur nba loon biaŋinba.
46Daar kur bi bia lek tuu taan leebe Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni ki jiantir Yennu, ŋaan ji nyi ki lakint baa jedikpaguur bi mɔŋ ŋei ni, ki saa di jeet nan leeb nan parpeenn, ki ninfɔksai kaa nan bi leebi. 47Ki yoo kur ki bi piak dont Yennu sann, ki sɔɔ kur par mei bi paak. Ki daar kur ki ti Yomdaanɔ bia ŋammit ki te ki niib laat tinnu ki pukii bi paak.

english_text:
Peter and John Heal a Lame Man
1The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple. 2A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
3The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money. 4But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
5The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something. 6But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.” 7Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up.
At once the man's feet and ankles became strong, 8and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God. 9Everyone saw him walking around and praising God. 10They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man.
Peter Speaks in the Temple
11While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch. 12Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said:
Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious? 13 The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
14 You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free, 15and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done. 16You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
17My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing. 18But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise. 19So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven. 20Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah. 21But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
22 Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you. 23 No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
24Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening. 25 You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.” 26God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_text:
Biaŋinba ki bi fiin wɔbik ki u somm
1Dasiar yenlekir dinn ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn din tan saa Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni Yenmeiyoo. 2Ŋanne ki bi saa yaa bin kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak tammɔb nba ki bi din yi Tammɔŋaŋ na, ki la jasɔɔ nna ki u kar leŋ, ki poŋ tee wɔbik u maaru niwa; ki daar yejaŋaakur ki bi din tuu jiiu ki baar nanɔ tammɔb maŋ boor ni, a wuu mei likirii niib nba baat Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak maŋ ni na.
3Yoo nba ki u tan la Piita nan Jɔɔnn ki bi yaa bin kɔɔ na, ki u miarib likirii. 4Ki bi jiant ki goriiɔ sirrr; ki Piita tan yetɔ a “I goriit.” 5Ŋanne ki u ji nur u nunii ki goriib, ki daan mi a u sa gaar siare bi peŋ. 6Ŋaan ki Piita betɔ a “Ti lek ki mɔk likirii ki sa turani, ŋaan ta mɔk linbae ki ti yaa tin tura. Mii tura mɔb Yisa Masia nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na sann ni, fiit kii somm,” 7ŋaan soor u niidiitu po nuu ki fiinɔ.
Li taakpaak ni ki jɔɔ na taa kakit yiama, 8ki u tab ki fiir set, ki ji piin somm ki lin, ki tan weib ki kɔɔ Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak maŋ ni, ki yuuk ki mɔbit nan parpeenn, ki piak dont Yennu sann. 9Ki niib kur lau ki u somm ki piak dont Yennu sann. 10Ba ji din bann nan ŋɔɔe tee miatɔɔ nba tuu kaar ki mei niib likirii Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak tammɔŋaŋ nna, ki li ji yaar ŋaan litib nan wa paak na paak.
Biaŋinba ki Piita mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii
11Ki wɔbik maŋ puur wɔb Piita nan Jɔɔnn; ki li teen niib na kura bakitnauŋ bonchiann, ki bi chiar saan lakin bi boor ni. Ba lakin sian na yi Solomon barintir ni.
12Piita nba la niib na yoonba ki u yet a “Yimm Israel teeb, bee ki li yaar ŋaan liti nan nirɔ na? ki bee ki i barii timm nna? Ta te ki jawɔbik na fiir somm na, i dukii mi a li tee ti mɔŋ paŋ amii ti mɔŋ yentinu paakee? 13Yennu nba din tee Abraham nan Aisak nan Jakɔb nan ti yejaleeb Yennu na, ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba donn u Toontunnɔ Yisa ki turɔ u mɔŋ yabint. Yisa maŋe ki i jiiu ki teenɔ ti yudamm nuu ni na, ki Pailat din poŋ bikin sinsinn a wun ŋaau, ki i bia yêtɔ Pailat boor ni. 14Ŋɔɔe lek tee Yennu nirɔ, ki bia jenna tee Popeendaanɔ, ŋaan ki i lek yêtɔ, ŋaan bia miar Pailat a wun nyinn nikpiiruk ki turi. 15Li paak, yimme te ki bi kpii Manfoturtɔɔ maŋ; ŋaan ki Yennu fiinɔ kuun ni, ki timme tee linba na siara damm.
16“Yisa maŋ sann paŋ nba ki ti yi nee te ki jɔɔ na paak. Wɔbik na tee wunba ki i poŋ tuu miu ki bia laatɔe, ŋaan ki u tan paak na, kimaan wa teen Yisa sann yada na paak. Wa teen Yisa yada na paake te ki u paak ki see i numm nna.
17“Tɔn, n naa waas, n mi nan yimm nan i yudamm nba daan te ki bi kpii Yisa na, i daan ki bann ya tun linba maŋ paaki. 18Ŋaan Yennu din pak u maan ki tur ti sɔkiniinba, ki bi mun pak tur niib, ki yet a see ki u Niganntɔɔ Masia-n lek dii fara. Tɔn, ki Yennu set tan te ki li gbenn teen mɔnii, nan ya set tan tun biaŋinba na.
19“Li paak, i nyik i biit man, kii waa Yennu sɔnu, ki wun nyik chab i toonbiit, 20ki u sa turi foon, ki bia tan sa tun Yisa Masia i boor ni, wunba ki u poŋ gannɔ ki turi na; 21ŋaan Yisa maŋ lek sii be Yendɔuŋ ni, ki Yennu-n tan ŋamm bona kura, kaman nan wa din te ki u popeenn sɔkiniinba nba din be yaayoo ni na yet biaŋinba na, ki bi mun pak turit na.
22“Ŋann maŋe ki Moses mun din yet a ‘Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu sa turi u sɔkiniisɔɔ, a ki u sii tee i mɔŋ nirɔ, kaman nan wa nyinn min biaŋinba ki teenin u sɔkinii i boor nna na. Gbiintirɔ man ki tun linba kur ki u sa pak turi; 23ki daanɔ nba ki gbat sɔkinii maŋ maami, Yennu sa nyinn li yɔɔ i sinsuuk ni ki biirɔ.’
24“Yennu sɔkinii Samuel nan sɔkiniinba nba wei u paak ki din piak Yennu maan, bi kura din want linba tan sa teen Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia ti yoo nee. 25Yennu mɔsona nba ki u din senn ki tur u sɔkiniinba maŋ, li mɔsona tee i yare; ki lor nba ki u din lor nan ti yejamm na, li lor mun bia tee i yare, kimaan u din yet Abraham a ‘A yaaboonyenɔ nie ki tingbouŋ na teeb kura tan sa la n mɔsonŋann.’ 26Li paak ki Yennu gann u Toontunnɔ Masia maŋ ki tumɔ i boor ni sinsinn, a wun te ki i kur-n nyik i biit ki la mɔsonŋann maŋ.”

english_text:
Peter and John Are Brought in Front of the Council
1The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived. 2These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death. 3It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night. 4But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about 5,000 followers of the Lord.
5The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem. 6The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family. 7They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
8Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
9You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed. 10But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life. 11 He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all. 12Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
13The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus. 14But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
15The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other, 16“What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen. 17But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.” 18So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
19Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him? 20We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
21-22The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than 40 years old, and everyone was praising God for what had happened.
Peter and Others Pray for Courage
23As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them. 24 When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said:
Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them. 25 And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say:
“Why are all the Gentiles
so furious?
Why do people
make foolish plans?
26The kings of earth
prepare for war,
and the rulers
join together
against the Lord
and his Messiah.”
27 Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah. 28They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
29Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message. 30Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
31After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message.
Sharing Possessions
32 The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other. 33In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers, 34and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money 35to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
36-37Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
translation_text:
Ba tikir Piita nan Jɔɔnn bartituk kaanu ni biaŋinba
1Yoo nba ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn daa din see ki piak teen niib maŋ na, ki Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak lanjimanba yudaanɔ nan Sajusiinba nan Yennu jiantu yudamm kpir baar Piita-nba boor ni, 2kimaan bi wutoa doo nan Piita nan Jɔɔnn, a bi want niib a Yisa daan fiir kuun ni paak, ki li want nan kpeemm tan sa fiir kuun ni ki jen bi manfoor ni biak.
3Li paak ki bi soorib ki kɔɔmm dansarik a lin yenta, kimaan yoo poŋ gara. 4Ŋaan ki niib bonchiann nba din gbat Piita maan maŋ teen Yisa yada ki teen Yisaweira. Ki jab nba teen u weira na kann din tee kaman nan tusa ŋaŋŋmu na.
5La tan yent sanyiɔk ni, ki Juu teeb yudamm nan saakab nan sennu wannteeb lakin bi tituk kaanu ni Jersualem; 6ki bi lakin nan Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ Annas, nan Kayafas, nan Jɔɔnn nan Aleksander nan jiantu yudaanɔ maŋ ŋaatesiab, 7ki te ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn fiir ki set bi sinsuuk ni, ŋaan ki bi boib a “Panboor bee ki i mɔki? Ki ŋmee sann nie ki i fiin wɔbik nba na?”
8Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ fiir Piita ni, ki u jiin ki yetib a “Yimm chanbaanba nan saakab, gbiintir man, 9li-i tee ki i boit dinna na ki jikit saa toonŋann nba ki ti tun ki tur wɔbik na, nan wa la laafia na paakie, 10ŋann li ŋan ki i kura-n bann, ki Israel teeb kur-n mun bann nan Nasaret nirɔ Yisa Masia sann paake ki jɔɔ maŋ la laafia ki see i numm ni na. Li Yisae ki i daan te ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu, ŋaan ki Yennu fiinɔ kuun ni na. 11Ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba ki Yennu gbouŋ piak u po na, a ‘Tann nba ki yimm tanmaara yêtɔ na, a ŋannii set tee lɔtik ni tanŋann.’ 12Ki sɔɔ sann ji kaa tingbouŋ na ni ki Yennu tur niib a bin la tinnu, see Yisa kuukɔɔ.”
13Tituk teeb maŋ nba din la Piita nan Jɔɔnn parcheenn nba ki bi mɔk na, ki bia bann nan Piita nan Jɔɔnn tee damm nba ki mi gbouŋ bonŋann na, li din yaar ŋaan litibe, ki bi ji bann nan bi tuu tee Yisa weinanleebe. 14Ba la jɔɔ nba ki bi won te ki u paak na, ki u see Piita nan Jɔɔnn boor ni na, bi ji din ki mɔk mɔb nba sa pak ki biiribi.
15Ŋanne ki bi din ŋaan ki bi daa nyii bi lakinu diiuk maŋ ni daann, ŋaan ki bi biar faa bi leeb labaar, 16ki boi bi leeb, a “Ti sa teen jab na nlee na? kimaan sɔɔ kur nba be Jerusalem poŋ mi yaarlituk toonn nba ki bi tun maŋ na. Li paak nna ti ji kan fit nɔi; 17ŋaan ŋaant man ki tin kpaamm kpaanii, ki bi ji daa mi ki yi Yisa sann ki teen niibi, ki Yisa maan maŋ ji daa bia yat ki saa niib ni.”
18Ŋanne ki bi yiimm, ki bi jen kɔɔ diiuk na ni; ki bi betib a “I mi man ki i ji mi ki jii Yisa sann ni ki pak ki wann sɔɔ jik.” 19Ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn jiin ki betib a “Ki i yaa mɔŋ mɔŋ po ŋarin, i dukin nan lanne tokin Yennu boor ni? ti ji sak i mɔb ŋaan nyik Yennu mɔb amii nlee? 20kimaan timm kan fit ki nyik pinpauk linba po ki ti la ki bia gbati.” 21Ki tituk teeb maŋ lek kpaamm kpaanii nan nunmɔnjar, ŋaan ŋaab yann a bii saa, kimaan bi ki la sɔnsau nba ki bi sa dat bi tuba, kimaan niib na din piak ki dont Yennu sann nan ba din fiin wɔbik na paak. 22Jɔɔ nba ki bi te ki u paak na bina din gar kaman nan binpiinna nawa.
Biaŋinba ki bi miar Yennu parcheenn po
23Ba ŋaa Piita nan Jɔɔnn yoonba, ki bi ŋmat kun bi leeb peŋ, ki saa yetib linba ki Yennu jiantu yudamm nan saakab maŋ pakib.
24Ba mun gbat nna maŋ ki bi kur taan leeb nan dudukyent kuukɔɔ, ki mei Yennu a “Ti Yabint Yomdaanɔ, fine din nan sanpaak nan tiŋ na, nan mɔkgbeŋŋa nan linba kur be leŋ, 25ki a Seyeeŋ maŋ nie ki a din wann ti yeja David wunba din tee a toontunnɔ, ki u din bunt buntir ki yet a
‘Binba ki tee Israel teeb na pir nan wutoa,
ki Israel teeb lorin lor yanna,
26ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara nan yudamm taan leeb
ki took a bin kɔn nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu
nan u Niganntɔɔ Masia na.’
27“Li tee barmɔniie nan Herod nan Ponshus Pailat nan binba ki tee Israel teeb na nan Israel teeb daan set lakin doo na ni, ki lor lora a bin kpi a popeenn toontunnɔ Yisa, wunba ki a din gann ki sennɔ na. 28Ŋanne ki bi lakin, a bin tun linba kur ki a poŋ din dukin a yanbɔɔt ni nan a paŋ, ki yaa li tan sa teen na. 29Tɔn, mɔtana, ti Yomdaanɔ, gbiintir niib na nba fɔ kɔnfɔɔt nan nunmɔnn ti paak na, ki ŋaan ki timm nba tee a daaba na-ii piak a maan nan parcheenn bonchiann, 30ŋaan tant a nuu ki te ki baatib-n la paaku, ki ŋaan ki tii tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana, nan a popeenn toontunnɔ Yisa sann paŋ ni.”
31Ba gbenn Yenmiaru na yoonba, ki siaminba po ki bi tikir na ji jek, ki bi kura gbee yent nan Yennu Seyeeŋ, ki ji piin piak Yennu maan nan parcheenn.
Ba chentir bi bona ki teen leeb biaŋinba
32Li yoo na ki Yisaweira maŋ kura taan leeb nan dudukyent kuukɔɔ, ki chentir bi bona ki teen bi leeb. Bi sɔɔ din ki yaa a u yen siar nan u mɔŋi. 33Li yoo na jik ki toomiinba maŋ din nyint ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuunfiiru siara, ki siara maŋ din mɔk paŋ bonchiann, ki Yennu sommir bonchiann din be bi kur ni. 34Ki linba na sɔɔ din kaa bi ni ki pɔt siari, kimaan binba din mɔk bi mɔŋ mɔŋ kpaant nan bi ŋei din tuu kɔie, ki gaar li likirii ki baar nann, 35ki tan teen toomiinba na, ki bi chentir ki teen bi leeb nan ba loon biaŋinba. 36Ki jasɔɔ din be leŋ ki u sann yi Josef, ki u din tee Liifai booru ni nirɔ, wunba ki bi din marɔ Saiprus, ki toomiinba na din yiu Banabas (ki li paak tee Sommtɔɔ na), 37ki u din tan kɔi u kpaab, ki gaar li likirii ki baar nann, ki tan tur toomiinba na.

english_text:
Peter Condemns Ananias and Sapphira
1Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property. 2But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves.
So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles, 3Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit? 4The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
5 As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened. 6Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
7Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband. 8Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?”
“Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
9Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!” 10At once she fell at Peter's feet and died.
When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband. 11The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened.
Peter's Unusual Power
12The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch. 13No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
14Many men and women started having faith in the Lord. 15Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them. 16A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed.
Trouble for the Apostles
17The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous. 18They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail. 19But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said, 20“Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.” 21So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching.
The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail. 22The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said, 23“We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.” 24The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
25Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!” 26The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
27When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them, 28 “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
29Peter and the apostles replied:
We don't obey people. We obey God. 30You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life 31and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven. 32We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
33When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles. 34But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while. 35Then he said to the council:
Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men. 36Not long ago Theudas claimed to be someone important, and about 400 men joined him. But he was killed, and all his followers were scattered. That was the end of that.
37Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
38So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail. 39 But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God.
The council members agreed with what he said, 40and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
41The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus. 42Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_text:
Ananias nan u ŋaapoo Safaira po
1Jasɔɔ nna mun din be ki u sann yi Ananias, ki u ŋaapoo sann mun din yi Safaira, ki u din tan kɔi u kpaab ki gaar li likirii, 2ŋaan paat likirii maŋ paak ki bɔr, ki u ŋaapoo mi li po, ŋaan jii linba tenn na, ki baar nann ki tan tur toomiinba maŋ. 3Ki Piita boiɔ a “Ananias, bee ki a ŋaan ki Sintaanii kɔɔ ki yent a par ni, ki a faar faak ki loon ki a kpann Yennu Seyeeŋ, ki paat likirii nba ki a daan kɔi a kpaab na paaki? 4Fa daan daa ki kɔi a kpaab maŋ na, li daan ki tee a yar kaa-aa? Ki fa bia daan kɔir na, li likirii maŋ bia daan ki be a yent ni kaa-aa? Ki bee teen ki a dukin a par ni ki tun linba na? A ki faar faak maŋ ki paan nirɔ kaa, ŋaan a faar ki paan Yennue.”
5Ananias nba gbat nna maŋ yoonba, ki u kpo ki baa li yoo na. Ki jaŋmaanchiauŋ kɔɔ binba kur gbat u kuun maŋ po. 6Ki naasimm fiir ki jiiu ki pɔbinɔ chinchenn ni, ki jiiu nyii nanɔ ki saa piiu.
7Ŋanne ki li yann waaminna, ki u ŋaapoo baar ki kɔɔ, ŋaan daa ki bann linba teeni. 8Ki Piita bia boiɔ a “Betirin, li set tee barmɔniie nan fin nan a sɔrɔ kpaab likirii nba ki i gaar na kurii na-aa?” Ki u jiin a “Nn, li kurii na.” 9Ki Piita boiɔ a “Bee ki fin nan a sɔrɔ taan mɔb, ki lor lor a i bikin ti Yomdaanɔ Seyeeŋi? Tɔn, naasimm nba jii a sɔrɔ ki saan ki saa piiu na, ŋammii kɔɔ tammɔb nna, ki bi mun bia sa jiia nnee ki saan piia.” 10Li taakpaak ni, ki u kpo ki baa, ki naasimm na kɔɔ ki la ki u kpoa, ki bi bia jiiu ki nyii nanɔ ki saa piiu ki kpianɔ nan u sɔrɔ. 11Li yoo na ki jaŋmaanchiauŋ soor Yisaweira kur nan binba kur gbat maan maŋ po.
12Li yoo na ki toomiinba na tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana niib na ni bonchiann. Ki Yisaweira kura tuu taan leeb Yennu Yennu ŋasaakak, Solomon barintir boor. 13Binba ki tee Yisaweira na din ki mɔk par nba ki bi sa lakin nammi, ŋaan niib din piak dont Yisaweira na bonchiann, 14ki jab nan poob bonchiann din teen yada ki pukin Yisaweira na po.
15Ŋanne ki niib din nyi nan baatib, ki dɔɔntib gadɔi nan dundɔɔnii paak sanjokpinii ni, a Piita-i taŋŋi baar ki gaar yoonba, ki u yunyuŋ-n lek baa bi paak ki bin la laafia. 16Ki niib nba din be Jerusalem digbanbis ni mun jikit baatib, nan binba ki narinbiit daamiib na, ki baat namm, ki bi kura din laat laafia.
Toomiinba na fara dinu po
17Li paak ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ nan u weinanleeb kur, binba tee Sajusiinba na, din fiin punponn bonchiann nan toomiinba na, ki din te ki bi saan 18ki saa soor toomiinba maŋ ki kɔɔmm dansarik. 19Ki li nyiɔk maŋe ki Yennu malaka sik ki loot dansarik tammɔi, ki nyinn toomiinba maŋ, ŋaan betib a 20“I saa man Yennu ŋasaakak ni, ki saa set, ŋaan mɔɔnt ki tur niib manfoor na maan kura.”
21Ba gbat u maan maŋ, ki bi saan ki saa kɔɔ Yennu ŋasaakak maŋ ni, sanyapob ni, ki ji piin ki want niib maan maŋ.
Ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ nan u weinanleeb na din yiin Israel teeb yudamm kura, ki lakimm tituk kaanu, ki bi tan tun lanjimanba a bin nyinn toomiinba maŋ ki baar namm na. 22Ŋanne ki bi saan dansarik maŋ ni ki saa got, ki ki labi, ki ŋmat tituk maŋ ni ki saa betib a 23“Ta baar dansarik ni yoonba, ti la ki tammɔi kpare kenkenkema, ŋaan ki dansarguura na be tammɔi ni ki guu, ki ti loot tammɔi na, ŋaan ki ki la sɔɔ dansarik diiuk maŋ ni.”
24Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak lanjimanba yudaanɔ nan jiantu yudamm nba gbat nna maŋ, ki li teemm yaarlituk nan toomiinba na botu maŋ paak, ki bi ji biris linba sa teen. 25Ki taan sɔɔ ki jasɔɔ nna kɔɔ, ki yetib a “Gotir man, jab nba ki i won kɔɔmm dansarik wonn na, bi see Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni ki want niib Yennu maan.” 26Li yoo na ki lanjimanba yudaanɔ na jii u jab, ki bi saan ki saa nyinn toomiinba maŋ ki jen namm, ŋaan bi ki nyimm nan paŋ kaa, kimaan bi tiin jaŋmaanii a niib na tan sa mann jaatib tana.
27Ba baar namm yoonba, ki bi te ki bi saan set tituk teeb na sinsuuk ni. Ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ na yetib a 28“Ti daan turi mɔb nan nunmɔnn, ki yaa a i daa want niib Yisa sann ni, ŋaan gotir ya tan tun linba na, ya jii i wannu maŋ ki yat Jerusalem kur po, ŋaan ji loon a i teent Yisa kpiira.” 29Ki Piita nan toomiinba leeb na jiin ki yetib a “See ki ti lek-ii saak Yennu mɔb ki gar nirɔ mɔb. 30Yisa maŋe ki i daan poŋ kpaa tabinɔ dapunpunn paak ki kpiiu na, ŋaan Yennu nba tee ti yejamm Yennu na, ŋɔɔe fiin Yisa kuun ni, 31ki jiiu donnɔ ki kaanɔ u niidiitu po, ki teenɔ Yudaanɔ nan Tinntɔɔ, a Israel teeb-ii nyik bi biit, ki wun nyik bi toonbiit ki chabib. 32Timm nan Yennu Seyeeŋe tee Yisa kuunfiiru maŋ siara damm; ki Yennu jii u Seek maŋ ki tur wunba saak u mɔb.”
33Yudamm maŋ nba gbat nna maŋ, ki bi wutoa fiir, ki bi ji yaa bin kpi toomiinba maŋ. 34Ŋaan Farisiisɔɔ nna din be tituk na ni, ki u sann yi Gamaliel, ki u din tee sennu wanntɔɔe, ki niib kur din teeu nibaakir; ki u din fiir ki set, ŋaan yet a bin daa nyinn toomiinba maŋ nanyer po waan; 35ŋaan bet tituk kaanu damm maŋ a “Hei, Israel teeb, i mi man nan ya yaa i teen niib na linba na fan. 36Sianyoo nba din gar, ki Tiudas din want u mɔŋ nan u tee nijaansɔɔe, ki niib kaman nan kobii ŋanna na din tee u poorpojɔɔmu, ŋaan niib nba din tan kpiiu, u jɔɔmu na kura din yate, ki u jagberin na gbenn. 37Ki ŋann tan gar nna, ki Judas, wunba din tee Galilee nirɔ mun din nyii ki set nikant yoo na, ki want u mɔŋ, ki din tunn niib bonchiann yan ki bi waau; ki niib bia mun din tan kpiiu, ki u jɔɔmu na kur yat. 38Mɔtanara, kpan ŋaant Yisaweira maŋ man, ki daa teeb siari; ki li-i tee ki bi dudukit nan bi toona na-i tee nisaarik yarie, li tan sa gbenna. 39Ŋaan li-i tee ki li tee Yennu yarie, i kan fit ki nyannibi. I sa la ki i mann kpet ki kɔn nan Yennue gbaa.”
Ŋanne ki tituk teeb maŋ ji sak Gamaliel mɔb na, 40ki yiin toomiinba maŋ ki kɔɔmm, ki te ki bi boob kpaasir, ŋaan ŋaab, ŋaan turib mɔb a “I mi man ki i ji kii yi Yisa sann maŋ.”
41Ŋann maŋe ki toomiinba maŋ nyii tituk kaanu na ni nan parpeenn, Yennu nba la ki bi jaŋ nan feidinu, Yisa sann paak na. 42Ki daar kur ki bi saa Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni, ŋaan bia lin niib ŋei ni, ki want niib a Yisa tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masiae, ki bia mɔɔntir u barŋanii na, ki ki nyikiti.

english_text:
Seven Leaders for the Church
1A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
2The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables. 3My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things. 4We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
5This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch. 6These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work. 7God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord.
Stephen Is Arrested
8God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people. 9But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him. 10But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him. 11So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
12They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
13Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses. 14We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.” 15 Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_text:
Biaŋinba ki bi gann sommteeb bannlore
1Li poorpoe ki Yisaweira ŋammit yabitir ki pukii, ki bunburimii tan baar Juu teeb nba piak Griik maan, nan Juu teeb nba piak bi mɔŋ maan na sinsuuk ni; kimaan binba piak Griik maan na din yet a bi-i toor jeet daar kur, bi pakɔi ki laat bi tora nan leeb na. 2Ŋanne ki toomiinba piik nan bannlee na yiin Yisaweira maŋ kura, ki tan yetib a “N naa waas, li ki ŋan ki tin nyik Yennu maan mɔɔntii ŋaan biar jeet toru ni. 3Li paak, gotir ki gann jab bannlore i sinsuuk ni, binba ki i mi nan bi binbeŋ ŋan, ki bi bia mantik gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nan yanfantir, ki tin teemm toona na yudamm; 4ŋaan ki timm-n jii ti mɔŋ yoo ki teen Yenmiaru ni, nan Yennu maan wannu ni, ki daa naki.” 5Ki mɔtaauk niib na kur ji din mɔk parpeenn nan toomiinba maan maŋ paak. Ŋanne ki bi gann Stiifen, wunba mantik gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ nan yada na, nan Filip, nan Prokorus, nan Nikanor, nan Timon, nan Pamenas, nan Nikolaus, wunba din nyii Antiok, ki ki tee Juu nirɔ, ŋaan din kpint u jiantu ki waa Juu teeb jiantu na. 6Mɔtaauk niib na nba gannib yoonba, ki bi te ki bi saan set toomiinba na tɔɔnn po, ki toomiinba maŋ miar Yennu ki turib ŋaan jii bi nii ki yii bi paak.
7Li poorpoe ki Yennu maan ŋammit ki yat kɔɔ niib ni, ki Yisaweira mun bia yabitir ki pukii bonchiann Jerusalem, ki Yennu manteeb niburchiauŋ sak Yisa barŋanii ki teenɔ yada.
Stiifen sooru labaar
8Ki Stiifen din la Yennu sommir nan yikoo bonchiann, ki din tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana niib na sinsuuk ni. 9Ŋaan nisiab din ki sakɔ. Binba din nɔi nan Stiifen maŋ din tee Juu teeb nba din tee daaba, ki bi ŋaab bi daabsin ni. Ki bi nyii Sairene nan Aleksandria nan Silisia nan Asia nie. Bi mun din mɔk bi mɔŋ Yenjiantu diiuko. 10Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din tur Stiifen yanfantir bonchiann, ki bi ki fit nyannɔ nan u maan maŋi. 11Ba din ki fit nyannɔ na, ki bi din kɔɔ nisiab a bin yet a nna, “Ti gbat ki u piak bonbilankant ki biir Moses nan Yennu.” 12Ba yet nna maŋ yoonba, ki niib na nan saakab nan sennu wannteeb wutoa doo, ki bi fiir ki soorɔ, ki saan nanɔ bi yudamm tituk boor ni; 13ki bi tan baar nan nisiab nna biak a bin faar faak, ki yet a “Yoo kur jɔɔ na yɔɔ piak ki biir Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak maŋ, nan Moses sennu nee, 14kimaan ti gbat ki u yet a Yisa nba tee Nasaret nirɔ na sa bet Yennu ŋasaakak na, a ŋaan lebit Moses kaar nba ki u din senn na.” 15Binba kur din lakin kar tituk maŋ ni din kpaa bi ninbina ki gorii Stiifen, ki tan la ki u nummpo lebit ki ji naan nan malaka nummpo na.

english_text:
Stephen's Speech
1The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
2 Stephen answered:
Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran. 3God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.” 4 Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran.
After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live. 5 God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children. 6 God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated 400 years. 7 But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
8 God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons. 9 These men were our ancestors.
Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him 10 and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
11 Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat. 12But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time. 13 It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
14 Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were 75 of them. 15 His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did. 16 Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
17 Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased. 18Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph. 19 He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
20 During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home. 21 Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son. 22Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
23 When Moses was 40 years old, he wanted to help the Israelites because they were his own people. 24One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian. 25Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
26The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
27But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge? 28Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?” 29 When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
30 Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai. 31Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said, 32“I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
33The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy. 34With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
35 This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
36 In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for 40 years. 37 Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.” 38 Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us. 39But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
40 The people said to Aaron, “Make some gods to lead us! Moses led us out of Egypt, but we don't know what's happened to him now.” 41 Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
42 God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those 40 years in the desert. 43Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
44 The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen. 45 Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David. 46 He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel. 47 And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
48But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
49 “Heaven is my throne,
and the earth
is my footstool.
What kind of house
will you build for me?
In what place will I rest?
50I have made everything.”
51 You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did. 52Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him. 53Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it.
Stephen Is Stoned to Death
54When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious. 55 But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side. 56Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
57The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen 58and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
59As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!” 60He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_text:
Stiifen pinpauk
1Ŋanne ki Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ boi Stiifen a “Li set tee mɔniiee?” 2Ki Stiifen yaa a
“Chanbaanba nan n naa waas, gbiintir man, yoo nba ki ti yeja Abraham din be Mesopotamia yakir po, Kaldea tiŋ ni na, li yoo ki u din daa ki saan Haran na, ki paŋ daanɔ Yennu na dɔkit u paak, 3ki yetɔ a ‘Fiit ki nyik a tiŋ nan a niib, ŋaan saan siaminba ki n sa wanna na.’ 4Li yoo na ki u fiir ki nyik Kaldea tiŋ maŋ, ŋaan saan ki saa kar Haran doo ni. U baa kuun poorpoe, ki Yennu bia te ki u fiir, ki baar tiŋ nba ki ti be na ni. 5Ŋaan Yennu daa din ki turɔ tiŋ na nan bonmintika a wun yenti, ŋaan senn mɔsonn a u tan sa turɔ tiŋ nna, ki ŋɔɔ nan u yaaboona-ii yen. Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn nna yoonba na, ki sɔɔ ki Abraham daa ki mɔk biki. 6Ki Yennu din yetɔ a ‘A yaaboona tan sa saan ki be tingaŋ niewa, ki tan sii tee daaba leŋ, ki bi tan sa la muksuk leŋ bonchiann, ki tan jaŋ bina kobiinna. 7Ŋaan tiŋ maŋ niib nba ki bi sii tuun ki teemm na, n tan sa dat bi tuba, ki li poorpo, a yaaboona maŋ tan sa nyi tiŋ maŋ ni, ki baar tiŋ na ni, ki jiantin.’
8“Li poorpo ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki tur Abraham, ki bia din wannɔ mɔsonn maŋ nyinn, a ŋɔɔ nan u yaaboona-ii porii puna. Ki Abraham din tan mar Aisak, ki li daa ŋanniin daar ki u potɔ; ki Aisak mun din tan pot u bik Jakɔb; ki Jakɔb mun din tan pot u waas piik nan bannlee binba tee ti yabint yejateeb. 9Ki Jakɔb waas maŋ din tan mɔk punponn nan bi waarɔ Josef, ki din tan soorɔ ki kɔiɔ, ki nisiab daau ki saan nanɔ Ijipt tiŋ ni, ŋaan Yennu din be nanɔ, 10ki din nyinnɔ fara kura ni, ki bia turɔ yan ki te ki Ijipt kpanbar Faro nianɔ ki jiiu teenɔ yudaanɔ Ijipt tiŋ ni, ki bia te ki u tee yudaanɔ u ŋaak ni.
11“Li poorpo ki kon tan baa Ijipt tiŋ nan Israel tiŋ Kenann ni kura, ki kon soor niib bonbiir, ki ti yejamm kɔŋ jeet. 12Jakɔb nba din gbat a dii be Ijipt yoonba, ki u tun u waas leŋ. Ŋannii din tee bi sinsinn saanu.
13“Bi saanu taar munnlee nie, ki Josef ji din te ki u naa waas na bannɔ. Ŋanne ki Faro ji din bann Josef booru. 14Ki Josef ji din tumm a bin ŋmat kun u chanbaa Jakɔb boor, ki saa jii ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb kur ki jen namm Ijipt na; ki bi din tee niib piinnlore nan baŋŋmu.
15“Ŋanne ki Jakɔb nan u ŋaateeb maŋ fiir saan Ijipt ki saa kar; ki li poorpo, ŋɔɔ nan u waas din tan kpo gbenn leŋe. 16Jakɔb nba din kpo na, ki bi jii u gbanant ki kun nann Shekem nba be Kenann tiŋ ni na, ki saa piiu kakur nba ki Abraham din daa nan likirii Hamor nibooru boor ni na; ki li poorpo Jakɔb waas nba din tan kpo na, ki bi mun din tan jii ŋamm yaa kpaba ki kun nann, ki saa pii leŋ biak.
17“Yoo nba din tan per nan Yennu-n gbeen u mɔsonn nba ki u din senn Abraham na, li yoo maŋ, ti niib Israel teeb nba be Ijipt na ji yabit bonchiann. 18Li poorpoe ki sɔɔ din dii Ijipt naan, ŋaan ŋɔɔ din ki mi Josefi. 19Ki kpanbar maŋ din dia ti niib diabiiuk, ki din tuu ton namm bonchiann, ki bia din tuu miab a bin nyinn bi sanpantii bi ŋei ni ki lub ki bin kpo.
20“Ŋanne ki bi din tan mar Moses, ki u fan bonchiann, ki be u baa ŋaak ŋmaarii ŋanntaa. 21Ba din tan nyinnɔ ŋaak ni yoonba, ki kpanbar na sapaamɔ din lau, ki gaarɔ ki jiiu ki wubiiɔ; ki u ji tee u mɔŋ bik. 22Ki bi din wann ŋɔɔ Moses yan kur nba ki Ijipt teeb mɔk, ki u din tan teen wunba mi pinpauk bonchiann, ki bia tuun paŋ toona.
23“Moses nba din tan teen binpiinna Ijipt niib ni yoonba, ki dasiar ki u tan dukin a wun gɔn u mɔŋ mɔŋ booru Israel teeb. 24Wa lin gɔntib na, ki u tan la ki Ijipt nirɔ boo Israel teeb ni yenɔ; ki u baar a wun fatɔ, ki tan kpii Ijipt nirɔ maŋ. 25(U din mi a u naa waas na sa bann a Yennu tan sa te ki ŋɔɔ-n tan nyinnib daabsin ni, ŋaan bi din ki bann nna.) 26Ki li yent sanyiɔk, ki u bia la Israel niib bannlee ki bi kɔn; ki u loon ki wun taamm ki bin maŋ nan bi leeb, ki yetib a ‘Gotir man, i tee naa waase, ki bee teen ki i boo i leeb ki teen i leeb danii?’ 27Ki wunba tur u lɔɔ daŋ na pii Moses, ŋaan betɔ yaa a ‘Sɔɔ ki ganna a a teen ti yudaanɔ amii ti barbuurɔ. 28A yaan loon ki a kpin kaman nan fa won kpii Ijipt nirɔ na amii?’ 29Moses nba gbat nna yoonba, ki u chiar nyii Ijipt tiŋ ni, ki baar Midian tiŋ ni ki be leŋ, ki din tan kɔɔn poo ki mar bonjabis bannlee.
30“Bina piinna nba din tan gar, li poorpo, ki u din tan saan muuk ni dasiar, ki saa baar Sainai jɔɔr boor ni, ki la mupeuŋ, ki li di ki lenii kunkonjagotuk paak, ki malaka dɔkit muu maŋ ni.
31“Moses nba din la nna maŋ ki li teenɔ yaarlituk. Ki u chat nakin a wun ŋamm got, ki ji gbat ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu kunkɔr ki u beerɔ ki yaa a 32‘Mine tee Abraham Yennu na, ki bia tee Aisak Yennu nan Jakɔb Yennu. Mine tee a yejamm maŋ Yennu.’ Moses nba gbat nna yoonba, ki jaŋmaanii ji kɔɔu, ki u ji see ki jekir ki ji ki mɔk par nan wun goti. 33Ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu betɔ a ‘Pitir a taasaat nba ki a pir na, kimaan siaminba ki a see na tee boyeenne. 34Tɔn, n got ki la Ijipt teeb nba dint n mɔŋ niib Israel teeb farchiauŋ Ijipt tiŋ ni biaŋinba, ki n gbia bi fabinii. Ŋanne ki n sik ma n fat nyinnib. Li paak n sa tuma Ijipt.’ ”
35Ŋanne ki Stiifen bia lek piak ki saa a “Moses nba nee ki Israel teeb din yêtɔ na, ki bi din betɔ yaa a ‘Sɔɔ ki ganna a a teen ti yudaanɔ amii ti barbuurɔ,’ ŋaan ŋɔɔ nee ki Yennu mun din gannɔ a wuu tee bi yudaanɔ nan bi fatitɔɔ biak; ki ŋɔɔ Yennu din te ki malaka pak nanɔ ŋaan tumɔ. Malaka maŋe ki Moses din lau kunkonjagotuk paak muu na ni na. 36Ŋɔɔ Moses din tee daanɔ nba din nyinnib Ijipt tiŋ nna, ki din bia tuun bakitnauŋ toonjaana Ijipt tiŋ ni, nan ba bia din saa poot Mɔkgbeŋmuŋ na, nan ba bia din be muuk ni binpiinna na. 37Li Mosese din yet Israel teeb a ‘Yennu tan sa tun sɔkiniisɔɔ i boor ni kaman nan wa tumin biaŋinba na, ki u sii tee i booru ni yenɔ.’
38“Li bia din tee Moses maŋe din be nan ti niib Israel teeb lakinu ni, muuk ni na. Leŋe ki u din tan doo Sainai jɔɔr paak, ki saa be nan Yennu malaka, ki malaka maŋ din piak ki teen Moses, a Moses-n sɔkin tur ti yejamm Israel teeb. Li yooe ki Moses din gaar Yennu manfoor maan ki tur ti yejamm nan timm. 39Ŋaan ti yejamm din ki loon ki bin sakɔ. Li paak ki bi din yêtɔ, kimaan bi yan tunn Ijipta; 40ŋaan din bet Aron a wun maa pata, ki bii tee bi ŋmakit damm ki bii waab, ki bia yetɔ a ‘Ŋaan Moses ŋarin, wunba nyinnit Ijipt na, ti ki mi linba teenɔ.’ 41Ŋanne ki bi maa patir ki nannɔ naajabik, ŋaan mann marouŋ li paak, ki teen jaamm a bin mantik donnɔ, linba ki bi maa nan bi mɔŋ nii na.
42“Li paak ki Yennu ji ki goriibi, ŋaan chabib, ki bi ji jiantir ŋmaabira, kaman nan la sɔb biaŋinba Yennu sɔkiniinba gbouŋ ni na, a Yennu yet a
‘Israel teeb, ya din kpi bonkobit
ki mann marouŋ muuk ni bina piinna na,
i din ki mann marouŋ maŋ ki tee min kaa.
43I din tuu jikit patir Molok lanbouŋo,
ki bia din tuu jikit ŋmaabir Refan naauŋo,
ki lii tee patir, ki saa namm.
Ŋamm tee yenn-nante ki i maa ki jiantir na.
Li paak, n sa te ki i datai-n nyinni i tiŋ ni
ki saan nani siaminba fɔk ki gar Babilɔnn.’ ”
44Ŋanne ki Stiifen bia piak a “Ti yejamm din faa Yennu lanbouŋ muuk ni, ki li want a Yennu be namm. Yennue din wann Moses ba sii faa lanbouŋ maŋ biaŋinba, ki bi faa. Li din tee kaman nan Moses nba din lar biaŋinba yerintu ni nee.
45“Li poorpo, ki ti yejamm ji din tan jii lanbouŋ maŋ ki tur bi waas, ki bi gaar ki mun jikit ki saa nann, ki ŋamm nan Joshua din tan baar nna, ki fat tiŋ nba ki niboorganii daa din yen li yoo na. Yennue din ber nyinn niib maŋ ti yejamm tɔɔnn po, ki lanbouŋ na ji din be nna dik dik dik nan David yoo mun. 46David maŋ dudukit din gboot Yennu numm, ki u din tan miar Yennu a wun chabɔ ki wun maa ŋaak ki tur ŋɔɔ Yennu nba din tee u yeja Jakɔb Yennu na. 47Ŋaan ŋɔɔ David bija Solomone din tan maa ŋaak maŋ.
48“Ti lek mi nan Yabint Yennu ki kaar ŋei nba ki niib maa ni kaa. Li tee kaman nan Yennu sɔkinii nba din yet a
49‘Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu yet a
Sanpaak tee n naangbouŋo,
a ki tingbouŋ na mun tee n taakpaktir.
I kan fit maa ŋaak nba jaŋ ki turin,
ki n foi leŋi.
50Min kaa din nan bona na kurii?’ ”
51Ŋanne ki Stiifen lek bia piak ki saa a “I mantik tee nigbaba hei, ki i dudukit bia fɔk nan Yennu bonchiann, ki i ki sak ki yin gbat Yennu maan. I kpan mun yêen nan ki i sak Yennu Seyeeŋ kaman nan i yejamm nba mun din yêt biaŋinba nae. 52Yennu sɔkinii sɔɔ din kaa ki i yejamm din ki mukisɔ. Bi bia din kpii binba din poŋ sɔkint a Yennu Popeendaanɔ Masia tan sa baar nawa. Tɔn, ki mɔtana yimme tee damm nba nyii u poor ki bia tee u kpiira. 53Yimme bia tee damm nba din gaar Yennu sennu nba ki i yejamm din gaar malakanba nuu ni, ŋaan bia lek ki sakiri na.”
54Tituk kaanu damm na nba gbiint Stiifen maan maŋ ki bi donn wutoor bonbiir ŋaan gbab mɔgbaba.
55Ŋanne ki Yennu Seyeeŋ fiir Stiifen ni, ki u yaat ki got Yendɔuŋ ni, ki la Yennu yentsaakar, ki Yisa see ŋɔɔ Yennu niidiitu po; 56ki u yet a “Gotir, n la ki sanpaapo loot ki nisaarik Bik see Yendɔuŋ ni, Yennu niidiitu po.” 57Ŋanne ki tituk teeb na yikin sanpaapo, ŋaan pii bi tuba; ki bi kura tan lakin wuur baar ki kpirɔ, 58ki berɔ ki nyinnɔ doo maŋ ni, ŋaan liat bi liata ki bir naasimɔ nba ki bi yiu Sɔɔl na boor ni, a wuu guu, ŋaan jaat Stiifen tana. 59Bi din jaatɔe dik dik dik; ki u din tan yiin ti Yomdaanɔ a “N Yomdaanɔ Yisa, gaat n seek na;” 60ŋaan gbaan ki yikin sanpaapowa a “N Yomdaanɔ, daa jikit bi biit na ki biiribi.” U yet nnee ŋaan ji kpo.

english_text:
1-2Saul approved the stoning of Stephen. Some faithful followers of the Lord buried Stephen and mourned very much for him.
Saul Makes Trouble for the Church
At that time the church in Jerusalem suffered terribly. All of the Lord's followers, except the apostles, were scattered everywhere in Judea and Samaria. 3 Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail.
The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
4The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news. 5Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ. 6They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles. 7Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed. 8Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
9For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great. 10Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
11For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him. 12But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized. 13Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
14The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John. 15When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit. 16Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. 17Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
18Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money 19and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
20Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift! 21You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right. 22Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you. 23I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
24Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
25After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria.
Philip and an Ethiopian Official
26The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.” 27So Philip left.
An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship 28and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
29The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot. 30Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
31The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
32 The man was reading the passage that said,
“He was led like a sheep
on its way to be killed.
He was silent as a lamb
whose wool
is being cut off,
and he did not say
a word.
33He was treated like a nobody
and did not receive
a fair trial.
How can he have children,
if his life
is snatched away?”
34The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?” 35So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
36-37As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?” 38He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
39After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
40Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_text:
Biaŋinba ki Sɔɔl din mukisir Yisaweira
1Ki Sɔɔl din sak ba din yaa bin kpiu na po.
Li daar maŋe ki muksubiir din baar Yisaweira nba din be Jerusalem na paak; ki bi kura din nyii yat ki saan Judea nan Samaria yaka po, ŋaan nyik toomiinba na kuukɔɔ leŋ. 2Ki yentinsiab din jii Stiifen, ki saan saa piiu, ŋaan mɔ yikin ki fabin bonchiann u paak.
3Ki Sɔɔl ŋarin din loon wun biir Yisaweirae, ki din lin kɔɔ ŋaak ŋaak, ki so Yisaweira, jab nan poob, ki nyintib ki saa kɔɔntib dansarii.
Biaŋinba ki Filip mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii Samaria
4Yisaweira nba din nyii ki yat na ji din saa lokir kur poe ki mɔɔntir Yisa maan maŋ; 5ki Filip nyii ki sik Samaria doo ni, ki saa mɔɔnt Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia po ki tur leŋ niib. 6-7Ki nigbouŋ na mun din tikir ki gbiint Filip mɔɔntii maŋ, ki bia la nan u bakitnauŋ toona nba ki u tuun; u din nyinn narinbiit niib bonchiann ni, ki bi yikin sanpaapo ki nyii; ki u bia te ki gbanankpeena damm nan wɔbii bonchiann paak. Li paak din teen ki bi mantik gbiintir u maan fanu nan dudukyent. 8Ki parpeenn din be doo maŋ ni bonchiann.
9Jasɔɔ nna mun din be doo maŋ ni, ki tee nyɔkluurɔ, ki u sann din yi Simon, ki yosau ki u tuu tan tee u bakitnauŋ toona, ki li din bakitir niib kur nba be doo maŋ yakir po, ki u din yi u mɔŋ a u tee nijaanne. 10Niib kur nba din tee nijaana nan tarii din baakitɔ, ki bi din tuu yaa a “Jɔɔ na mɔk paŋ nba nyii Yennu boore.” Li paak ki bi yiu Panchiauŋ Daanɔ. 11U din tuu tee u bakitnauŋ toona maŋ, ki li bakitirib ki yukira. Li paak ki bi din teeu nibaakir ki bia gbiintir u maan.
12Ŋaan ki Filip nba din tan baar ki mɔɔntir Yennu naan barŋanii nan Yisa sann po na, jab nan poob din sak u mɔɔntii maŋ, ki u wurib Yennu nyunwuru. 13Simon mɔŋ mun din teen Filip maan yada, ki u wurɔ. Ki u teen Filip weinanlɔɔ, ki laat bakitnauŋ toonjaana nba ki u tuun na, ki li tuu yaar ŋaan litɔ.
14Ki Yisa toomiinba nba din be Jerusalem gbat a Samaria teeb gaar Yennu maan maŋ; ki bi din tun Piita nan Jɔɔnn, 15ki bi sik Samaria ki saa miar Yennu bi paak, a bin gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ, 16kimaan Yennu Seyeeŋ maŋ daa din ki sik bi sɔɔ ni. Bi kpan din wurib Yennu nyunwuru nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni kuukɔɔe. 17Ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn yii bi nii bi paak, ki bi gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ.
18Simon nba din la ki toomiinba na nba yiin bi nii niib paak, ki Yennu turib Seyeeŋ na, ki u yaa a wun tur toomiinba na likirii, 19ŋaan yetib a “Chabimin mun yikoo na man, ki n mun tuu yii n nii sɔɔ paak, ki wun gaar Seyeeŋ maŋ.” 20Ŋaan ki Piita betɔ yaa a “A sa kpo yiake nan a likirii maŋ, kimaan a dukii mi a a sa gaar Yennu piinii nan likiriie. 21Li paak na, a lek kan tun siar ti toonn na ni, kimaan a par ki be fanu nan Yennu. 22Li paak, i mɔk parbiir nan a dudukbiit na, ki lebit a sɔnii ŋaan kii mei Yennu; li pasiari u sa nyik chaba nan a dudukbiit maŋ, 23kimaan n la nan a gbee nan nɔɔntonte, ki bia tee biit yommike.” 24Ki Simon jiin betib a “Miat i Yomdaanɔ ki turin man, ki linba ki i yet na, ki li ji daa baat n paaki.”
25Tɔn, li poorpoe ki Piita nan Jɔɔnn din pak siara ti Yomdaanɔ po ki tur niib, ki bia mɔɔnt u maan, ŋaan ŋmat ki saan Jerusalem. Ba din saa nee, ki bi din mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii Samaria digbanbis ni bonchiann.
Filip nan Etiopia jatɔɔkir po
26Ŋanne ki ti Yomdaanɔ malaka tan baar bet Filip a “Fiit saan tɔkin sɔnu nba nyi Jerusalem ki siik Gasa na.” Leŋ din tee tinpere. 27-28Ki Filip fiir ki saan. Tɔn, ki Etiopia jatɔɔkir nna mun din nyii u sɔnu ki kun. Jɔɔ maŋ din tee nijaann nba tee Etiopia poobat nba ki bi yiu Kandes na likirii karachinba yudaanɔe. U din saan Jerusaleme, a wun saa jiant Yennu, ki din tan ŋmat ki kun, ki kar u toruk ni, ki taanii daarɔ.
Wa din kar ki saa yoonba, ki u karin Yennu sɔkinii Aisaya gbouŋ ni. 29Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ yet Filip a “I saa nakin toruk na.” 30Ki u chiar saan nakin leŋ, ki gbat ki u karin Yennu sɔkinii Aisaya gbouŋ ni. Ki u boiɔ a “A bann linba ki a karin na paakii?” 31Ki u jiin a “Nlee ki n sa bann li paaki, ki li ki tee ki sɔɔ wannin kaa?” ŋaan barin Filip a wun kɔɔ wannɔ. Ki u kɔɔ toruk maŋ ni ki bi lakin kar.
32Yennu gbouŋ maan nba ki jatɔɔkir maŋ din karin na, ŋannii na a
“Ki u sak saan kaman nan peeuk na ki bi daarɔ a bin saa kpiu.
a ki u ŋmin kaman nan pebik nba ki bi tuu pot pot u kobit ki u ŋmin na,
a ki u ki yaat u mɔbi,
33a ki bi sikin u baakir
ki yêt bin bu u buut bonŋann,
a u din ki mɔk yaaboona,
a kimaan u manfoor ji jit tingbouŋ na niwa.”
34Ki jatɔɔkir na boi Filip a “Betirin, ŋmee ki sɔkinii na yeen na? U yeen u mɔŋ amii sɔɔe?” 35Ki Filip piin siaminba ki jatɔɔkir maŋ karin na ki saa, ki piak Yisa barŋanii.
36Ba tɔk sɔnu maŋ ki siik yoonba, ki bi la nyun nna dɔɔ. Ki jatɔɔkir na yetɔ a “Gotir, nyume na. A bee yaa lin teen ki a kan wurin Yennu nyunwuru?” 37Ki Filip yetɔ a “Li-i tee ki a teen Yisa yada nan a par kura, n sa wurawa.” Ki u jiin a “N teen yada nan Yisa Masia tee Yennu Bijae.” 38Ki jatɔɔkir maŋ te ki taanii na set, ki ŋɔɔ nan Filip sik nyun na ni, ki Filip wurɔ Yennu nyunwuru. 39Ki bi tan nyii nyun na ni ki doo; ki Yennu Seyeeŋ jii Filip ki yaat nanɔ, ki jatɔɔkir na ji ki lau; ŋaan ŋamm soor u sɔnu ki kun nan parpeenn.
40Ki Filip ji din saa be Asotusewa, ki din lin digbana kur ki mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii mɔkmɔk nan wa din tan baar Siisarea.

english_text:
Saul Becomes a Follower of the Lord
(Acts 22.6-16; 26.12-18)
1Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest 2and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way. 3When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him. 4He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
5“Who are you?” Saul asked.
“I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to. 6Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
7 The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone. 8Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus, 9and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
10A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
11The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying, 12and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
13Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem. 14Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
15The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me. 16I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
17Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
18 Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized. 19Then he ate and felt much better.
Saul Preaches in Damascus
For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus. 20Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God. 21Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
22Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
23 Later some of them made plans to kill Saul, 24but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him. 25Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket.
Saul in Jerusalem
26When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower. 27Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
28Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord. 29He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him. 30But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
31The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow.
Peter Heals Aeneas
32While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda. 33There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move. 34Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
35Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord.
Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
36In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor. 37But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room. 38Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!” 39At once, Peter went with them.
The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
40After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up. 41He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet.
Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death. 42Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord. 43Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_text:
Biaŋinba ki Sɔɔl teen Yisaweirɔ
1Tɔn, Sɔɔl lek bia din fɔɔ kɔnfɔɔt, ki yeen a u sii dint ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa poorpoweiteeb biak, ŋaan kpib; ki tan saan Yennu jiantu yudaanɔ boor ni, 2a wun gaar kadaat, a ki saan Damaskus ki saa wann Yenjiantu diit yudamm, a wu sii la wunba waa Yisa sɔnu maŋ, jɔɔ amii poo, see wun soorib ki lorib ki jen namm Jerusalem.
3Tɔn, wa ji din saa yoonbae ki saa per Damaskus, ki yentu nyii sanpaapo, ki sik yent lintɔ. 4Ki u baa tiŋ ni, ki gbat ki kunkɔr yiu a “Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bee ki a mukisirimi?” 5Ki u boi a “N Yomdaanɔ, ŋmee?” Ki kunkɔr na jiin a “Min Yisae, ki a mukisirin. 6Ŋaan fiit ki saan doo na ni, ki bi sa saa beta fa ji sa teen biaŋinba.”
7Li yoo na, jab nba din chiant Sɔɔl na ji biar set surimm. Bi din gbia kunkɔr na ŋarin, ŋaan ki laat sɔɔ. 8Ki Sɔɔl tan fiir tiŋ ni, ki munt u nunii, ŋaan ki laati. Ŋanne ki bi dat u nuu ki saan nanɔ Damaskus. 9Damunjaa ŋanntaa ki u ki fit laati. Li daa maŋ ni u din ki dii amii ki nyuu siari.
10Ki Yisaweisɔɔ din be Damaskus maŋ, ki u sann yi Ananias, ki ti Yomdaanɔ yiinɔ yerintu ni a “Ananias!” Ki u jiin a “Mine na, n Yomdaanɔ.” 11Ki ti Yomdaanɔ yetɔ a “Fiit ki saan tɔkin sɔnu nba yi Sɔnsun-sonn na ki saa kɔɔ Judas ŋaak ni, ki boi jɔɔ nba nyii Tasus ki u sann yi Sɔɔl na po. U mei Yennue mɔtana, 12ki u la jasɔɔ yerintu ni, ki u sann yi Ananias, ki u kɔɔ ki jii u nii ki yii u paak, a wuu jikii laat.”
13Ki Ananias jiin a “N Yomdaanɔ, niib bonchianne piak jɔɔ na po, ki jikit saa biaŋinba kur ki u tuun a mɔŋ niib Jerusalem. 14U daan gaar yikoo jiantu yudamm boor ni, ki baar nna, a wun soor wunba kur yi a sann.” 15Ki ti Yomdaanɔ betɔ a “Fini saawa! a ŋɔɔe tee wunba ki n gannɔ a wuu tuun n toonn, ki te ki boorganu nan bi kpanbara nan Israel teeb-n bann n sann, 16ki n mɔŋ sa wannɔ muksuk nba kur ki u sa di n paak.”
17Ŋanne ki Ananias saan kɔɔ ŋaak na ni, ki yii u nii Sɔɔl paak, ŋaan yetɔ a “N naa bik Sɔɔl, n Yomdaanɔ Yisae tumin. Ŋɔɔe tee daanɔ nba ki a daan baat nna ki tan lau sɔnu paak na, ki u tumin a n tan te ki a-ii jikii laat, ki bia gbee yent nan Yennu Seyeeŋ.” 18Li taakpaak ni ki siar tabit u ninbina ni ki baa, ki naan nan janwɔka na, ki u ji laat jik, ki fiir set; ki bi wurɔ Yennu nyunwuru. 19Ŋanne ki u dii jeet ki paŋ jen.
Biaŋinba ki Sɔɔl mɔɔntir Yisa barŋanii
Li yoo na ki Sɔɔl din lakin be nan Yisaweira kaman nan daa ŋannlee na Damaskus, 20ki din saan yiama Yenjiantu diit ni, ki mɔɔntir Yisa po, a Yisa kpan set tee Yennu Bijae. 21Binba kur din gbat u mɔɔntii maŋ, li din teemm yaarlituko, ki bi boi a “Jɔɔ na kaa daan kpi binba yi Yisa sann Jerusalem na-aa? U yaan ki baar a wun soor ki lor Yisaweira, ki ŋmat namm ki saa turib Yennu jiantu yudamm kaa na-aa?”
22Ŋaan ki Sɔɔl mɔɔntii maŋ bia lek ŋammit ki pukii nan Yennu paŋ, ki u maan maŋ want nan Yisae set tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia ki birsuk kaa; ki Juu teeb nba be Damaskus na ki fit jiinɔ siari. 23Ki daa bonchiann tan gar, ki Juu teeb na lakin leeb ki lor lor a bin kpi Sɔɔl, 24ki yennu nan nyiɔk ki bi guu doo maŋ tammɔi bonŋann a bin kpiu. Ki sɔɔ tan kumiiɔ ba lorin u paak biaŋinba; 25ŋaan nyayouŋ ki u weinanleeb tan jiiu ki teenɔ chibuk ni, ki nyinnɔ doo maŋ joonfɔnn ni, ki sikinɔ tiŋ ni.
Sɔɔl benu Jerusalem
26Ŋanne ki Sɔɔl din saan Jerusalem ki saa yabir u mɔŋ paak a wun lakin nan Yisaweira na, ŋaan ki bi ki teenɔ yada nan u ji tee Yisaweirɔe, ki tiinɔ jaŋmaanii. 27Ki Banabas din tan baar u sommir, ki jiiu ki baar nanɔ toomiinba na boor ni, ki kat wannib biaŋinba ki Sɔɔl daan la ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sɔnu ni, nan wa pak nanɔ biaŋinba. U bia din ŋamm ki wannib Sɔɔl nba din mɔɔntir Yisa po nan parcheenn Damaskus biaŋinba. 28Banabas nba wannib linba na, ki Sɔɔl din lakin be namm, ki bi din kak ki lin Jerusalem kur po, ki mɔɔntir ti Yomdaanɔ sann nan parcheenn. 29U bia din piak nan Juu teeb nba piak Griik maan na ki jikit saa Yisa po, ŋaan bi din nɔi nanɔ, ki koor a bin kpiu. 30Ŋaan yoo nba ki Yisaweira bann nna maŋ, ki bi jii Sɔɔl ki sik nanɔ Siisarea, ki te ki u kun Tasus.
31Ŋanne ki Yisaweira nba be Judea nan Galilee nan Samaria na ji ki laat muksuku, ki ŋammit ki tumii ti Yomdaanɔ barii, ki tee yentina, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ be namm ki kɔɔntib para, ki bi pukii.
Biaŋinba ki Piita tuun bakitnauŋ toona
32Li poorpoe ki Piita din yek lin siar kur po, ki bia din tan sik a wun gɔn Yennu niib nba be Lida na. 33Leŋe ki u din saa la jasɔɔ nna, ki u sann yi Eneas, ki u din poŋ tee gbanankpeenn daanɔe, ki din ki fit fi u dɔɔnu ni teee nan bina ŋanniin. 34Ki Piita tan yetɔ a “Eneas, a Yisa Masia te ki a paaka. Fiit ki ŋamm a dɔɔnu.” Li taakpaak ni ki u fiir. 35Lida nan Sharon doi niib kur nba din lau ki u paak na, ki bi lebit bi binbeŋ, ki waa ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu.
36Poosɔɔ mun din be Jɔpa, ki u sann yi Tabita, ŋaan Griik maan ni, bi din yiu Dɔkas, ki li paak tee piarɔ.
