7Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me. 8 Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
9 I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth. 10I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed. 11I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
12King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests. 13About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me. 14We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
15“Who are you?” I asked.
Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to. 16Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
17The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to. 18I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
19King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven. 20 First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
21That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me. 22But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen. 23 I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
24Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
25But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense. 26None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
27Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
28Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
29Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
30Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up. 31But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
32Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_text:
1Li yoo na ki Bat Agripa yet Pɔɔl a “N chaba mɔb. Pakin nyinn a mɔŋ.” Ki Pɔɔl donn u nuu sanpaapo a wun pak ki nyinn u mɔŋ, ki yet a
2“Bat Agripa, li maŋin bonchiann nan ma see a tɔɔnn ni dinna, ki yaa n pak ki nyinn n mɔŋ, ki jikit saa linba kur ki Juu teeb paanin na po. 3Ki bia jenna, n mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nan ma see a tɔɔnn ni na, nan fa tee banntɔɔ, ki mi Juu teeb sennu nan bi mɔniɔk nba tee biaŋinba kur na. Li paak, chanbaa, n barimae, din sukuru ki gbiint ma yaa n pak linba na.
4“Ma fiir n bisin niwa nan mɔtana, Juu teeb kura mi ma din tuu be biaŋinba n tiŋ ni nan Jerusalem biak. 5Bi poŋ min ki li fɔk. Li-i tee ki bi loon, bi sa fit wann nan n tuu waa Farisiinba Yenjiantu sɔnu nba mantik paar nee. 6Ki mɔtana ma ji see ki bi bu n buut na, kimaan n mɔk dindann nan mɔsonn nba ki Yennu din senn ki tur ti yejamm na. 7Li mɔsonn maŋ paake ki timm Juu teeb booru piik nan munnlee tuu mɔk dindann ki dukii nan ti tan sa la. Li paak te ki bi jiantir Yennu nan nunmɔnn yennu nan nyiɔk na. Chanbaa Agripa, li dindann maŋ paakera, ki Juu teeb paanin maan na. 8Bee ki li paar i paak nan ki i teen yada a Yennu fiint kpeemmi?
9“Sinsinn, n mɔŋ mɔŋ din dukin nan li kpaa talase nan ki n tun bonsiar kur ki biir Nasaret nirɔ Yisa sann. 10Nnee ki n din tuun Jerusalem. Yennu jiantu yudamm din tuu chabin yikoo ki n so Yennu niib ki kɔɔntib dansarii. Ba din tuu bu bi maan ki tan gbenn ki chab a bin kpib na, n mɔŋ mun din tuu be binba din tuu chab a bin kpib na kann ni.
11“Yoo kur n din tuu yɔɔ ki mukisirib Yenjiantu diit kur ni, ki din tuu loon ki bii nɔi a bi ki waa Yisa. N din mantik donn wutoor bonchiann bi paak, ki li saa yakira. Li paak, n bia lek din tuu saa doi nba be tinganii ni, ki saa mukisirib biak.
Pɔɔl nba betib u lebitu po biaŋinba
12“Muksuk maŋ paake ki n yaan tan fiir ma n saan ki saa mukisib Damaskus, Yennu jiantu yudamm nba chabin yikoo, ki bia turin mɔb a n tun nna.
13“Tɔn, Chanbaa Agripa, li din tee yensuuk nie ki n be sɔnu paak ki saa, ki tan la ki yentu nyii sanpaapo ki yeen gar yennu, ki yent nyɔkit ki lint min nan n tɔknanleeb na. 14Ki ti kura baa tiŋ ni. Ki n gbat ki kunkɔr pak timm Juu teeb maan ni, ki yin a ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bee ki a mukisirimi? Fa tuu yêenin na, li paake te ki li paar a paak kaman nan bonkobuk nba tuu yêen u daanɔ mɔb ki u boou lanbann na.’
15“Ki n jiin ki boi ma ‘N Yomdaanɔ, ŋmapoe?’ Ki ti Yomdaanɔ jiin a ‘Min, Yisae, ki a mukisirin maŋ. 16Ŋaan fiit ki set. N nyinn fine a a teen n toontunnɔ. Linba nee te ki n dɔkit a paak. Teent niib siara ki wannib fa lan biaŋinba dinna, nan linba ki n sii fiitir wanta yoo nba tan sa baar. 17Ma tan sa tuma siaminba na, n bia tan sa nyinna daar ni Juu teeb nan nileeb nii ni. 18Fine tee wunba sa yent bi numm po, ki bin nyi bunbɔnn ni ki kɔɔ yentu ni, ki bia nyinnib Sintaanii nuu ni ki bin kɔɔ Yennu nuu ni, a wun nyik bi biit ki chabib, ki bin la yiar, binba ki Yennu teemm u niib na sinsuuk ni, kimaan ba teenin yada paake.’
Pɔɔl nba want u toona po biaŋinba
19“Li paak, Chanbaa Agripa, n din ki yêt Yendɔuŋ ni yerintu maŋi. 20Ŋaan sinsinn n din dɔŋ ki mɔɔnt Damaskus teebe, ki pukin Jerusalem nan Judea tiŋ ni doi nba be lokir kur po. Li poorpoewa, ki n tan mɔɔnt ki tur binba ki tee Juu teebi. N wannu ki tur niib na kur tee a bin nyik bi biit kii waa Yennu sɔnu, kii tuun bonŋann, ki lin wann nan bi set nyik bi biit nan mɔnii.
21“Li din tee linba na paake, ki Juu teeb din soorin, ma daa din be Yennu jiantu ŋasaakak ni na, ki bi din loon ki bin kpin, 22ŋaan la jii ki tan tuu dinna, ki Yennu guun, ki n ji be ki see ki mɔɔntir u maan ki teen tatimm nan paarib kura na. Ma piak linba na, ŋaan ŋann nan Moses nan Yennu sɔkiniinba nba din tuu piak linba kur na, li ki mɔk bɔkitu. Ŋamm din piak linba tan sii tee, 23a Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia tan sa fabir di fara ki kpo, a ŋɔɔe bia tan sii tee sinsinn daanɔ nba tan sa fiir kuun ni, a ki li paak ki u bia tan sa mɔɔnt ki tur Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb mun biak, ki beerib barii nba tan sii yentir bi numm po.”
24Pɔɔl nba din piak a wun nyinn u mɔŋ na, ŋanne ki Festus tian u paak ŋaan yet a “Pɔɔl, a waata! A bannchiauŋ paake te ki a waat.” 25Ki Pɔɔl jiin a “Chanbaa Festus, n ki waat kaa. Maan nba ki n piak na, li tee barmɔniie, ki bia mɔk paak. 26Chanbaa Agripa, n mɔk parcheenn ki sa pak ki tura, kimaan a mɔŋ mi linba na po, ki n bia teen yada nan barii maŋ ki jikit saa Yisa po ki bɔr a boori, kimaan linba din teen maŋ, li din ki woon kaa. 27Chanbaa Agripa, a teen Yennu sɔkiniinba maan yadawa-aa? Ŋaan n lek mi nan a teen yadawa.”
28Ki Bat Agripa jiin Pɔɔl a “Fin dukii mi a a pinpauk mintik na sa fit dakin ki n teen Yisaweirɔwa.”
29Ki Pɔɔl jiin a “N pinpauk-i fɔk amii la ki fɔki, min yaa Yenmiaru tee ki fin nan niib kura nba gbiintir n maan na dinna na, see ki fin nan ŋamm-n teen Yisaweira kaman nan ma tee biaŋinba na, ŋaan jarit nba na kuukɔɔe ki i kii mɔki.”
30Ŋanne ki Bat na nan yudaanɔ na nan Beniis, nan binba kur kar leŋ na kur fiir ki nyii. 31Ba yaa bin jii yat yoonba, ki bi ji beer bi leeb a “Jɔɔ na taan ki tun biit ki li ŋan nan bin kpiu, amii ki kɔɔnɔ dansariki.” 32Ki Bat Agripa yet Festus a “Nirɔ na-i daan bo-i kii miar a u sa gaan u maan na kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa peŋi, ti bo sa fit ŋaauwa.”

english_text:
Paul Is Taken to Rome
1When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners. 2We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
3The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed. 4When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind. 5Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia. 6There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
7We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind. 8We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
9By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past. 10Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.” 11But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
12The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there.
The Storm at Sea
13When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete. 14But soon a strong wind called “The Northeaster” blew against us from the island. 15The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
16We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place, 17but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
18The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard. 19Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear. 20For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
21Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men:
You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss. 22But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
23I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel 24to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.” 25Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised. 26But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
27For 14 days and nights we had been blown around over the Mediterranean Sea. But about midnight the sailors realized we were getting near land. 28They measured and found that the water was about 40 meters deep. A little later they measured again and found it was only about 30 meters. 29The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
30The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship. 31But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.” 32The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
33Just before daylight Paul begged the people to eat something. He told them, “For 14 days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing. 34I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
35After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some. 36They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something. 37There were 276 people on the ship, 38and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter.
The Shipwreck
39Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach. 40They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach. 41But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
42The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping. 43But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore. 44Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_text:
Pɔɔl nba piin Rom doo saanu biaŋinba
1Ba ji din tan dukin a bin ji Pɔɔl ki saan nanɔ Rom, Itali tiŋ ni, ki bi jii ŋɔɔ nan dansarkɔɔleeb, ki turib Rom lanjimanba saakɔɔ nna, ki u sann yi Julius, ki u be lanjimanba paabu nba ki bi yi Kpanbara Yudaanɔ Paabu na, ki u jii lanjimanba na, 2ki bi ji kɔɔ ŋaruŋ nba nyii Adramitium, kimaan ŋaruŋ maŋ poŋ teen siir nan wun nyi Siisarea ki saan Asia tiŋ ni doi nba kpia mɔkgbeŋŋir na kpiŋ; ki Masedonia nirɔ Aristakus, wunba din nyii Tesalonika na, nan min Luk mun bia din chian Pɔɔla. 3Ki li tan yent sanyiɔk ni, ki ti baar Saidon. Ki lanjimanba saakɔɔ Julius din dia Pɔɔl fanu, ki din chabɔ a wun saan Yisaweira nba be leŋ na peŋ, ki bii teeu saantuk. 4Ki ti tan nyii leŋ ki yaat saa, ki wouŋ fukit ki da tookit, ki ti pek kpian Saiprus tiŋ po ki saa, ki tiŋ maŋ tia wouŋ na ki turit. 5Ta poot mɔkgbeŋŋir na yoonba na, ki ti tan per Silisia nan Pamfilia tinii po, ki ji baar Maira, Lisia tiŋ ni na.
6Leŋe ki lanjimanba saakɔɔ na la ŋaruŋ nba nyii Aleksandria ki saa Itali tiŋ ni. Ki u te ki ti lɔɔr ki kɔɔ ŋaruŋ maŋ; 7ki saa waan waaminna, ki li tan gbatit. Ti din jii daa saamewa, ki tan baar Kinidus, Kreet tiŋ ni. Ki wouŋ na bia lek ki nyik ki tin saan ta saa siaminba po na. Li paak ti din bɔkii, ki gaar Kreet tiŋ maŋ poe. Li po maŋe ki wouŋ din ki paari, ki ti tan kpian nan Salmone, Kreet tiŋ ni, ki gar. 8Ta din saa na, li din paar bonchiann nan ta tan baar Kreet doo nba yi Setŋanu na. Leŋ din ki fɔk nan Lasea doo, Kreet tiŋ ni.
9Ta baar yukir bonchiann na, Juu teeb mɔloru dagbeŋŋir ji din poŋ gara, ki yoo nba ki ninbɔŋ tuu tan baar mɔkgbeŋŋir paak na tan baar; li paak ki Pɔɔl kpaanib ki yetib a 10“N yɔɔs, n mi nan ti sɔnu na ni, ninbɔŋ sa daamit bonchiann, ki li ki tee tiat nba be ŋaruŋ na ni, amii ŋaruŋ na kuukɔɔ paak kaa, ŋaan nan ti mɔŋ mɔŋ manfoa gbaa sii be ninbɔŋ maŋ ni.” 11Ŋaan lanjimanba saakɔɔ na baka din taan kaa nan linba ki Pɔɔl pak na po, ŋaan fiin ŋaruŋ na saakɔɔ nan li daanɔ na poe. 12Ŋaan ŋaruŋ maŋ nba din tuu see doo nba na din ki fan nan tin set leŋ fufuk yoo, li paak, bi bonchiann ji din yaa a bin ŋaan ki bin fiir leŋ ki saan, a li pasiar bi tan sa baar Fiiniks. Leŋ din tee Kreet tiŋ dooe, ki ŋaruŋ sinsee boor be leŋ ki took yenbaa po, ki bi loon bii be leŋ fufuk yoo.
Ninbɔŋ nba din be mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni labaar
13Li yoo na ki bi la niigaŋ po na wouŋ ji da waan waaminna, ki bi ji dukin a li pasiar li sii tee wounŋaŋo, linba sa te ki bin fit nyann ba dukii biaŋinba, ki li yoo na, ki bi ji fiir ki dat kutkpiatir nba so ŋaruŋ ki sent na, ki kɔɔn ŋaruŋ maŋ ni, ki yaan jak bi sɔnu ki saa, ki bɔɔk Kreet mɔk-kpiŋ.
14Li ki yukiri ki wonpaaruk nba nyi yonput po na fikit tiŋ na ni, ki da siik mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni, 15ki tan baar wurrr ŋaruŋ na paak ki tutir, ki li ji paar bonchiann nan nirɔ-n ji fit kan ŋaruŋ maŋ ki kɔɔ wouŋ na ni; ki ti tat nyik wouŋ na ni kɔɔnu, ŋaan ki wouŋ na da ŋaruŋ maŋ ki saa nann jik, 16ki li jiit, ki ti saa baar ki gar tinbik nba ki bi yi Kɔda na. Ta gaar kpiant yoonba ki tinbik maŋ gɔɔr wouŋ na ki u barik, ki ti ji gbaa gbaa saa gɔɔr burgbetir nba tuuk ŋaruŋ na paak na, 17ki bi ji dat burgbetir na ki kɔɔnn ŋaruŋ na ni, ŋaan bia lor ŋaruŋ na nan ŋmiit kenkema ki lint. Bi din tiin jaŋmaanii, ki mi a bi tan sa mann kpet ki kpian Libia tiŋ ni, siaminba ki nyun ki sumii na. Leŋ din tee ninbɔŋ boore. Ŋanne ki bi sikin ŋaruŋ na chinchenn ki ji ŋaan ki wouŋ na yaan jiib ki saa namm.
18Wonpaaruk nba mantik din da na, ki li yent sanyiɔk ni, ki bi piin ki nyinn bonkɔɔtkara ŋaruŋ maŋ ni, 19ki li sanyafannuk jik, ki bi jii bi mɔŋ nii ki nyinn nyinn ŋaruŋ tiat nba be ŋaruŋ maŋ ni na, ki lu lu mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni. 20Li din jii daa bonchiann ki sanpagbana dɔkin sanpaapo, ki ti ki fit la yennu amii ŋmaabira gbaa, ki wouŋ na lek bia da nan paŋ. Ta din mɔk dindann ki dukii mi a ti sa tinn na, ki li kur nyii peb.
21Li din yukir bonchiann ki bi ki dii, ki Pɔɔl tan fiir set ki yet a “N yɔɔs, i daaŋŋi bo-i sak n mɔb, li daan bo sii ŋan. I bo tan kii laat ninbɔŋ ki di fara na booru. 22Ŋaan mɔtana, yii saak n mɔb kii mɔk parcheenn man. I sɔɔ nan yenɔ kuukɔɔwa kan kpo gbaa, nɔkira ŋaruŋ maŋe sa biir. 23Kimaan Yennu nba ki n tee u yɔɔ ki jiantirɔ na, u malaka won sik n paak nyiɔk na, 24ki tan betin a ‘Pɔɔl, daa tiin jaŋmaanii. See ki bi tan jiia ki saan nana kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa na peŋ. A paak, ki Yennu yaa wun tinn niib kur nba be nana ŋaruŋ na ni.’
25“Li paak, n yɔɔs, i mɔk parcheenn man, kimaan n teen Yennu yada nan li kpan sii tee kaman nan wa betin nae. 26Ŋaan wouŋ lek sa tutit nan paŋ, ki daamit, ki te ki tin baar tinbisiak paak.”
27La din tan teen nyatummuna piik nan ŋanna yoonba, ki wouŋ na bia lek da ki saa nant mɔkgbeŋŋir nba ki bi yir Adria na paak, ki tansuunii ki ŋaruŋ kanna na ji dukii mi a ti per tiŋe. 28Ŋanne ki bi jii bonbikinkar, a bin bikin ki laan li sumii amii li ki sumii, ki bi ji bikin ki sɔɔ ki li sumii baar taapauŋ kobik nan piinnleewa. Ki li yann waaminna, ki bi yaan bikin, ki ji sɔɔ ki li ji sumii tee taapauŋ piinnyiae. 29Ki jaŋmaanii kɔɔb, kimaan bi dukii mi a bi ŋaruŋ na tan sa mann kpet ki do tana paak. Li paak, ki bi ji jii kutkpiata ŋanna nba so ŋaruŋ ki sent na, ki lu nyun ni, ki gaan gaan ŋaruŋ maŋ poorpo, ŋaan lek ji miar Yennu a lin yent sanyiɔk. 30Ŋanne ki ŋaruŋ kanna na ji loon bin bot ŋaan nyik ŋaruŋ na. Ki bi sikint burgbetir na nyun paak, ŋaan ji tee ser ki li tee kaman nan bi yaa bin sikin kutkpiata ŋaruŋ maŋ tɔɔnn poe na.
31Ŋaan ki Pɔɔl yet lanjimanba saakɔɔ na nan lanjimanba maŋ a “Ŋaruŋ kanna na-i kaa ŋaruŋ na ni, i sɔɔ kan tinni.” 32Ŋanne ki lanjimanba na pot pot ŋmiit nba ki bi lor lor burgbetir maŋ, ŋaan te ki li baa nyun ni.
33Tɔn, ki li yaan tan per yentu, ki Pɔɔl bet bi kura a bin di jeet, ki yet a “Dinna li pukii daa piik nan ŋannae na ki i mɔk jaŋmaanii ki daa ki dii siari. 34Li paak, n barimiie ki yi lek di jeet, kimaan li tee linba sa te ki i ŋamm la paŋe. I sɔɔ kan kpo fabaa.” 35Pɔɔl nba pak nna maŋ, ŋaan jii boroboro, ki tur Yennu niipoouk bi nunii ni, ŋaan ji ŋmit dii. 36Ŋanne ki bi para ji chee, ki bi kura jak jeet na ki di. 37Ti kura kann din tee niib kobiinnlee nan piinnlore nan bannloobo. 38Sɔɔ kur nba din dii jeet na ki jaŋ yoonba, ki bi jii boroboro na dii nba din tenn ŋaruŋ maŋ ni na ki lu mɔkgbeŋŋir na ni.
Ŋaruŋ na kɔnnu labaar
39La tan yent sanyiɔk, ki ŋaruŋ kanna na la tiŋ na, ki ji ki banni, ŋaan la lɔtuk nna mɔkgbeŋŋir na kpiŋ, ki li mɔk gbingbanŋann, ki bi bikin a li-i sa fit, bin te ki ŋaruŋ na-n saan kɔɔ leŋ ki do gbingbann na. 40Ŋanne ki bi ji din pot ŋmiit na, ki kutkpiata maŋ baa nyun ni, ŋaan bia lot ŋmiit nba lor daat nba ki bi dia ki nuun ŋaruŋ na, ki ji fit donn chinchenn ŋaruŋ numm po, a wouŋ-ii da kii saa namm. Ŋanne ki bi tan per gbingbann, 41ki ŋaruŋ na tɔɔnn po fer tiŋ ni. Li tɔɔnn po na nba fer tiŋ ni biaŋinba na, ki ŋaruŋ na gbar saanu, ki nyunpana ji lia li poorpo nan paŋ ki jintir.
42Tɔn, ki lanjimanba na ji loon bin kpi dansarkɔɔra na kur, a nnee kaa, li pasiar bi tan sa mann ki duk poot ki chiar. 43Ŋaan lanjimanba maŋ saakɔɔ na ŋarin loon ki wun tinn Pɔɔl. Li paak, ki u tiab a bi daa kpibi, ŋaan tur mɔb a sɔɔ kur nba mi nyunduku, bin yukir baa nyun na ni sinsinn ki duk poot. 44Ŋaan binba biar ki ki mi duku na-n mun soor soor daat amii ŋarunyaka ki wɔb ki duk poot. Ti kura ji din teen nnee ki tinn ti mɔŋ.

english_text:
On the Island of Malta
1When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta. 2The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
3After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand. 4When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
5Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed. 6The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
7The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days. 8His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
9After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed. 10The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed.
From Malta to Rome
11Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.” 12We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days. 13From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli. 14There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
15Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged.
Paul in Rome
16We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
17Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said:
My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans. 18They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death. 19 The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor.
But I don't have anything to say against my own nation. 20I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
21The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you. 22But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
23They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
24Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not. 25Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet 26 to tell our ancestors,
‘Go to these people
and tell them:
You will listen and listen,
but never understand.
You will look and look,
but never see.
27All of you
have stubborn hearts.
Your ears are stopped up,
and your eyes are covered.
You cannot see or hear
or understand.
If you could,
you would turn to me,
and I would heal you.’ ”
28-29Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
30For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him. 31He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_text:
Malta teeb nba gaarit biaŋinba
1Ta din la tinnu na poorpoe, ki ti bann nan tiŋ maŋ sann yi Malta. 2Leŋ niib din diat bonŋann, kimaan saak din baa, ki waat be bonchiann, ki bi joo mugann ki turit a ti nanii, ŋaan teen ti kur sommir sommir. 3Ki Pɔɔl tan tikir daginganii bonchiann ki kpab nan waauk, ki teen muu na ni, ki muu na tonu tan gbat waauk na, ki u nyii ki jokir Pɔɔl nuu. 4Ki leŋ niib nba la ki waauk na jɔɔn Pɔɔl nuu paak, ki bi ji beer bi leeb a “Jɔɔ na kpan tee nikpiiruko. Wa lek tinn u mɔŋ mɔkgbeŋŋir nna, ŋaan kpeemm ŋarin kan nyikɔ ki wuu be u manfoor ni.” 5Ki Pɔɔl ŋak u nuu, ki waauk na baa muu na ni, ŋaan ki Pɔɔl ki la daŋ nan waama. 6Ki bi ji daan a bin laan u siar po sa mɔr, amii u tan sa kpir baa ki kpo. Ba ji daan daan ki tan goo ki ki la siar teenɔ na, ki bi nyik bi dindann maŋ, ŋaan yaa a u tee yensɔɔe.
7Leŋ bia din ki fɔk nan Publius kpaant nba be na. Ŋɔɔe bia din tee tiŋ maŋ ni bat. Ki u din gaarit saantuk ki saa jaŋ damunjaa ŋanntaa. 8Ki Publius baa mun din dɔɔ yiar gbanantouŋ nan nɔniikak ni. Ki Pɔɔl tan kɔɔ u diiuk ni, ki jii u nii ki yii u paak, ŋaan miar Yennu ki te ki u paak.
9Wa din te ki u paak na, ki baatleeb nba be leŋ na kura din baar, ki u tan te ki bi mun paak. 10Ki bi din pit piinii bonchiann. Ta bia din tan fi a tin kɔɔ ŋaruŋ yoonba, ki bi baar nan linba ki ti tan sii loon sɔnu ni, ki kɔɔn ŋaruŋ ni ki turit.
Ta din saa Rom biaŋinba
11Ti benu leŋ ŋmaarii ŋanntaa poorpoewa, ki ti din kɔɔ ŋaruŋ nba din nyii Aleksandria ki din be Malta fufuk yoo na, ki bi yi ŋaruŋ maŋ Yenjaasinba. Ki li fiir saa, 12ki yaan tan baar doo nba ki bi yi Sairakus na, ki biar leŋ daa ŋanntaa, 13ki tan tɔɔt gar baar Regium doo ni. Ki li tan yent ki wouŋ yaan jak danu, ki nyii niigaŋ po ki tutit. Ki ti jii daa ŋannlee ki yaan baar Puteoli doo ni. 14Leŋe ki ti la Yisaweisiab. Ki bi gɔɔrit a tii be namm ki jaŋ daa ŋannlore. Ki ti be namm, ki tan nyii leŋ ki saa Rom doo ni.
15Yisaweira nba be Rom doo ni na nba gbat ti po na, ki bi fiir baar chetit Apius daak ni, ki siab mun chetit Saamŋei Ŋanntaa nba kpia Rom na. Pɔɔl nba lab yoonba, ki li turɔ parcheenn, ki u tur Yennu niipoouk.
16Ta din baar Rom na, ki bi sak chab Pɔɔl ki u be u yur po ŋaak, ki bi te ki lanjima guu-u.
17Tɔn, li daa ŋanntaa daare ki Pɔɔl yiin Juu teeb yudamm lakinu. Ba tikir leeb yoonba, ki u yetib a “N naa waas, n lek ki tun bonbisiar ki biir ti niib amii ti yejamm sennu, ŋaan bi din soorin ki kɔɔnin dansarik Jerusaleme, ŋaan jiin ki kubinin Rom teeb nuu ni. 18Ki bi boin buboit, ki tan loon ki bin nyikin, kimaan bi ki la ma tun bonbisiar nba jaŋ nan bin kpimi. 19Ŋaan Juu teeb nba din ki sak a bin nyikin na, li paak ji din te ki n yaa n sa gaan n maan kpanbara yudaanɔ Siisa peŋ, ŋaan li din ki tee a n loon ki n pak biir ti niib siari.
20“Li paake te ki n yiini ma n tan lai ŋaan beti labaar na po. Ma mɔɔntir Yennu Niganntɔɔ po na, wunba ki Juu teeb daan dindann na poe, ŋanne te ki bi baanin jarit na.”
21Ki bi betɔ yaa “Ti ki la gbant ki li nyii Judea ki baar ki li want a po, amii ti sɔɔ bia ki nyii leŋ ki baar nan labasiari, amii sɔɔ mun bia ki pak bonbisiar ki saan gaan a po biaki. 22Ŋaan ti sii loon ki tin bann a dudukit ni. Ti poŋ mi nan siar po kura, niib bonchiann piak ki biir jiantu nba ki a jiantir na.”
23Li paak ki bi senn daar. Ki niib bonchiann tan baar Pɔɔl peŋ daar maŋ ni. Ki u kat wannib nan nunmɔnn Yennu naan po, sanyiɔk ni mɔkmɔk nan dajouk, ki wantib Yisa barii nba be Moses sennu ni nan Yennu sɔkiniinba sɔbu ni biaŋinba, ki yabii a wun tunn niib na yan ki jiin Yisa po. 24Ki bi siab yan din tunn ki kɔɔ Yisa po, u maan maŋ paaka, ki siab mun ki teen yada. Ki bi jak leeb nan mɔniɔk, ŋaan yaa bin yat saan.
25Ŋanne ki Pɔɔl din yet mɔbonyennkɔɔ a “Yoo nba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ nba din te ki u sɔkinii Aisaya nba din pak tur ti yejamm na, u din pak mɔniie, 26ki din yet yaa nna:
‘I saa ki saa bet niib na a
i tan sa gbiint ki gbiint,
ŋaan lek kan bann li paaki.
a ki bia tan sa got ki got,
ŋaan bia lek kan la,
27kimaan niib na tee yankpeena damme,
ki bi bia kpaa bi tuba nan bin gbat,
a ki bi bia mun bi nunii nan bin la.
Bi-i bo-i kii mun bi nunii,
bi bo sa lawa,
ki biakii bo-i kii kpaa bi tuba,
bi bo sa gbata,
ki bi-i biakii bo-i kii tee yankpeena dammi,
bi bo sa bann li paaka.
Li-i kii teen nna bonni,
bi bo sa nyik bi biit,
ki n te ki bin paaka.’ ”
28Ki Pɔɔl bia ŋamm yet a “Ki bia jenna, li paak, li ŋan ki yin bann nan Yennu tinnu maan baar niib nba ki tee Juu teeb paake, ki bi sa gbat.”
29Pɔɔl nba din pak nna yoonba, ki Juu teeb ji din yat saa, ŋaan jak leeb nan mɔniɔk bonchiann.
30Pɔɔl nba din be ŋaak nba na ni, u din pa likirii ŋaak maŋ paak, ŋaan din be leŋ bina ŋannlee. Nirɔ nba kur tan baar u boor, u tuu gaarɔ bonŋann. 31Ki u bia din tuu mɔɔntir Yennu naan po, ki bia want niib ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia po, ki din tuu piak u yama ni nan parcheenn.

english_text:
1From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus.
God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news 2that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures. 3-4This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
5Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith. 6You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
7This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people.
I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
A Prayer of Thanks
8First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith. 9God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
10In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you. 11I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith. 12What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
13 My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places. 14-15It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome.
The Power of the Good News
16 I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles. 17 The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
Everyone Is Guilty
18From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth. 19They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them. 20 God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse. 21 They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark. 22They claim to be wise, but they are fools. 23 They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
24So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies. 25They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
26God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural. 27Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
28 Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things. 29They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip, 30say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil.
These people don't respect their parents. 31They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others. 32They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_text:
1Min Pɔɔl, wunba tee Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, ki u yiinin a n teen u toomii, ki Yennu gannin a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii.
2Yennu poŋ din dɔŋ senn u mɔsonn barŋanii maŋ poewa, ki u sɔkiniinba din mɔɔntir u mɔsonn maŋ, ki li sɔb u gbounyeeŋ ni. 3Barŋanii maŋ want u Bija poe; ŋɔɔe tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia; wa teen nisaarik na, bi marɔ ki u tee David yaaboonne, 4ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din wann nan paŋ nan u set tee Yennu Bija u kuunfiiru paake. 5Ŋɔɔ paake ki Yennu ninbatinu be n paak ki te ki n tee u toomii, ki n jii u sann ki saan nibooru kur boor ni, ki te ki bi teenɔ yada ki saak u mɔb; 6ki i mun be daansiab nba ki Yisa Masia gannib na kann ni.
7Li paak, ki n sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen yimm nba be Rom doo ni, ki Yennu looni ki ganni a i teen u mɔŋ niib na. Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir.
Pɔɔl nba teen Yennu niipoouk biaŋinba
8Sinsinn, n teen Yennu niipoouk Yisa Masia sann ni, i kura paak, kimaan tingbouŋ na teeb kur gbia i yada po. 9Yennu tee n siara daanɔ nan n ki mi nyik Yenmiaru i kura paaki. Ŋɔɔ maŋe ki n waau nan n par kura ki mɔɔntir u Bija barŋanii na, ŋɔɔe tee n siara daanɔ. 10N mei Yennu a li-i tee u loommie li kan yukir ki n sa baar i boor ki tan gɔni, 11kimaan n mantik loon ki n sommi ki yin gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ piinsiar, ki ŋanne sa te ki yin set fanu kii mɔk paŋ u sɔnu ni. 12Ma want linba tee a min nan yimm nba teen yada biaŋinba na, ti sii kɔɔnt ti leeb para, ti yada teenu na paake.
13N naa waas, n loon yin bann nan n yabir nan taar muŋŋa nawa a n baar ki tan gɔni, ŋaan ki siar gɔɔrin mɔkmɔk nan mɔtana ki n ki fit gɔniini. N loon ki n te ki niib-n wei ti Yomdaanɔ i boor ni kaman nan ma daan teen biaŋinba na nileeb sinsuuk ni na. 14Li kpaa talas ki n bet niib kura barŋanii na po, binba mɔk yan, nan binba ki mɔk yan, nan binba mɔk ninyentir nan binba ki mɔk ninyentir. 15Li paak, ki n ninbinn mɔn bonchiann a n mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ ki tur yimm nba be Rom doo ni na.
Yisa barŋanii nba mɔk paŋ biaŋinba
16Fei ki mɔkin nan ki n mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ, kimaan li tee Yennu paŋ nba tint wunba kur teen Yisa yada nee; sinsinn li tee Juu teeb yare, ki mun bia tee nibooru kur yar. 17Barŋanii maŋ want nan Yennu yet a nirɔ tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn, yada teenu paake; nee nyii paanu ki tur binba teen Yisa yada, kaman nan la sɔb u gbouŋ ni biaŋinba na a “Wunba teen niŋanɔ Yennu boor u yada na paak, li daanɔ sii mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu.”
Nifosaarii kur nba mɔk biit biaŋinba
18Yennu wann nifosaarii a u donn wutoor bi paak, kimaan bi tuun toonbiit ki ki waa u sɔnu, ki bi biit sɔnii maŋ te ki niib ki bant u barmɔnii. 19Yennu donn wutoor, kimaan nifosaarik nba sii fit mi u po biaŋinba, ŋanne fiit ki be paanu ni, kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋe fiitir ki wannib. 20Yoo nba ki Yennu din nan dunia mɔkmɔk nan mɔtana, u naamii maŋ ni ki bi bann u benu nba ki nifosaarik ninbinn ki fit laat na, ki bia mi nan u mɔk paŋ nba kaa gbennu, ki set tee Yennu. Li paak, ki bi ji ki mɔk leku Yennu tɔɔnn ki sa yet a bi ki mi bi biiti. 21Bi mi Yennu, ŋaan bi ki teenɔ nibaakir nba ki u yen na. Bi bia ki teenɔ niipoouk; ki bi mɔk jatdudukit, ki bi yan ki yenti, ki ki mɔk nyɔɔti. 22Ki bi yaa a bi mɔk yame, ŋaan tee jatit; 23ba bo sii jiantir Yennu nba kaa gbennu na, bi ki saki, ŋaan jiantir pata nba ki niib nammir ki li naan nisaarii, amii nɔɔnii, amii bonkobit, amii bonfurinkaa nba furin tiŋ ni.
24Li paak, ki Yennu nyikib, ki bi tuun toonbiit nba ki bi para loon, ki bi dint bi mɔŋ fei bi gbanant toona ni nan bi leeb. 25Bi jii linba tee Yennu barmɔnii ki lu, ŋaan sak faak, ki lebit ki jiantir linba ki Yennu nann, ŋaan nyik Yennu mɔŋ mɔŋ, wunba yen baakir kur mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.
26Li paak, ki Yennu nyikib ki bi tuun bi mɔŋ loomm nba mɔk fei, ki bi poob nyik bii dɔɔ nan jab ŋaan ŋmat ki dɔɔ nan bi leeb; 27ki jab mun nyik bii dɔɔ nan poob ŋaan ŋmat ki dɔɔ nan bi leeb, ki bi niauŋ be bi leeb ni bonchiann, ki jab tee bonsia linba mɔk fei nan bi leeb, li paak, ki bi la tubdatu nba jaŋ nan bin gaar bi gbanant ni, bi biit maŋ paak.
28Nifosaarii nba din ki loon ki bin bann Yennu, li paake ki Yennu nyikib nan bi dudukbiit, ki bi tuun toona nba bi bonchiann. 29Ki biit kur din be bi ni, ki bi gbee nan biit nan nianchiauŋ nan yanbɔmm nan funfunbiit nan nikpinu nan kunkɔna nan fai nan dudukbiit, ki bia tee sanjanjanna; 30ki piak biir bi leeb, ki ki loon Yennu, ki sukii bi leeb ki mɔk garuk ki dont bi mɔŋ, ki bia ŋammii dukii dudukbiit sɔnii, ki ki tee bi naanba nan bi baanba nibaakir. 31Bi bia ki mɔk yansian bi ni; bi ki waa bi mɔsona biaki; bi ki mɔk loomm nan ninbatinu bi leeb po. 32Bi mi nan Yennu sennu wann nan binba tuun linba na booru, bi nyɔɔt tee kuume, ŋaan bi lek kpan tuun ŋanne, ki bia mɔk parpeenn nan binba tuun li toonbooru mun.

english_text:
God's Judgment Is Fair
1 Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things. 2We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way. 3Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse? 4 You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
5But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness. 6 God will reward each of us for what we have done. 7He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever. 8But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil. 9All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles. 10But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles. 11 God doesn't have any favorites!
12Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says. 13God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
14Some people naturally obey the Law's commands, even though they don't have the Law. 15This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned, 16when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says.
The Jews and the Law
17Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God. 18By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right. 19You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark. 20And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
21But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal? 22You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples. 23You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God. 24 It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
25Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised. 26In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised. 27So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
28Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew. 29 To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_text:
Yennu buut labaar
1Li paak, nifosaarik nba want u lɔɔ biit, u ki mɔk sɔnu, kimaan fa want a lɔɔ biit na, li want nan a mɔŋ mɔŋ mɔk biit, kimaan fa want a lɔɔ biit na, a mɔŋ mɔŋ mun tuun nnee. 2Ti mi nan Yennu tan sa bu binba tuun nna na buuta; ki u buut maŋ tan sii tee barmɔniie. 3Fa want leeb biit ba tuun biaŋinba, ŋaan mun tuun nna na, a dukii nan Yennu kan dat a tubirii? 4amii a yisin loonchiauŋ nan sukuru bonchiann nba ki Yennu mɔk nant na amii nlee? a ki mi nan Yennu mɔk burchint nana a wun te ki a nyik a biit na ŋaan kii be fanuee? 5Ŋaan a par wakii ki a lek ki sak nan fan nyik toonbiit tumu na, li paak, a pukii a mɔŋ fara nba ki a tan sa la daar nba ki Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan barmɔnii, ki u wutoor na tan sa nyi paanu; 6kimaan sɔɔ kur sa la u toona nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba. 7Nisiab mɔk mintir ki tuun toonŋana ki loon ki Yennu-n pakib ki turib nyɔɔt nan manfoor, ki ŋamme set sa la manfoor nba ki mɔk gbennu. 8Ŋaan binba mɔk kunkɔna, ki ki sak linba tee barmɔnii na, ŋaan tuun toonbiit, Yennu wutoor sa do bi paak. 9U sa jii fara nan muksuk ki tur nisaarii nba kur tuun toonbiit na; Juu teeb nee sa la li tubdatu sinsinn, ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na-n ji joont; 10ŋaan Yennu sa jii paku nan nyɔɔt nan parmaasir ki tur binba kur tuun toonŋana na; Juu teebe sa gaar sinsinn, ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na-n joont; 11kimaan Yennu ki mɔk ningannii.
12Binba kur ki mi Yennu sennu ki tuun toonbiit, bi lek sa kpo bi toonbiit tumu na paaka, li ki tee ba ki dia sennu maŋ paak na kaa. Binba kur mɔk Yennu sennu na, ŋaan tuun toonbiit, ki Yennu sa jii sennu maŋe ki bu namm. 13Li ki tee a binba gbia sennu nee tee niŋamm Yennu boori, ŋaan li tee binba tuun biaŋinba ki sennu na wann na, ŋamme tee niŋamm u boor. 14Binba ki tee Juu teeb na, bi ki mɔk sennu na, ŋaan bi-i tuun sennu na nba wann biaŋinba nan bi tiauŋ yamie, bi mɔk bi tiauŋ sennu bi para ni, nan ba lek ki mɔk sennu maŋ na. 15Ba tuun biaŋinba want nan sennu nba senn biaŋinba na be bi para ni, ki bi yangana mun wantib linba ŋan nan linba ki ŋani, kimaan sianyoo bi dudukit tuu tan sak bi toon-yena nan li ŋan, ŋaan yosau bi dudukit-n bia yêt bi toonlia paak nan li ki ŋani. 16Tɔn, li tan sii tee nnee daar maŋ daar nba ki Yennu tan sa bu niib kur para ni buut Yisa Masia paak, kaman nan ma mɔɔntir barŋanii maŋ biaŋinba na.
Juu teeb nan sennu labaar
17Gotir, a yi a mɔŋ Juu nirɔe, ki mɔk parbifaanu a sennu na tee a yare, a li paak a a tee Yennu yɔɔe, 18ki mi linba ki Yennu loon fii tuun, ki a bia tumii sennu na ni ki mi linba ŋan nan tumu, 19ki teen yada nan a fit want jɔɔnii sɔnu, ki bia tee yentu ki tur binba be bunbɔnn ni na; 20ki fit kpaan jatit na kpaanii, ki bia want binba ki mi, kimaan a mi barmɔnii na nba sɔb biaŋinba sennu gbouŋ na ni. 21A want leeb barmɔnii na, ŋaan bee ki a ki want a mɔŋi? A want leeb a nanyuk ki ŋani, ŋaan fin jaamii? 22Fin nba want niib a bi daa tuun bonchonchonn toona na, ŋaan fin chommii? Fin nba ki loon pata maan na, ŋaan a jaan jiantu diit ni bona-aa? 23Fin nba nyu bara sennu maŋ paak na, a yêen sennu maŋ ki dint Yennu fei, 24kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yimm Juu teeb paake ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na piak ki sukii Yennu.”
25Fi-i sak Yennu sennu, a punpotu mɔk nyɔɔt, ŋaan fi-i kii sak sennu na, li ŋman a ki poti. 26Ŋaan binba ki tee Juu teeb ki ki pot, ŋaan sak sennu maŋ mɔbona ki waar, Yennu sa gaarib kaman nan wunba pot nee. 27Yimm Juu teeb mɔk sennu maŋ ki li sɔb gbouŋ ni; ki i bia porii puna, ŋaan yimme bia biir sennu na; ki binba ki pot bi puna ŋaan waa sennu na, ŋamme sa bu i buut ki biiri. 28Nirɔ nba tee Juu nirɔ paanu ni kuukɔɔ, u ki tee Juu nirɔ nan mɔnii. Potu nan mɔnii mun ki tee gbanant potu, linba be paanu ni. 29Juu nirɔ nan barmɔnii tee wunba tee Juu nirɔ u par nie. Potu nan mɔnii mun tee par nie, ki li potu tee Yennu Seyeeŋ tumu; li ki tee gbanant paanu ni kaa. Li daanɔ laat paku Yennu boore, li ki tee nisaarik boori.

english_text:
1What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised? 2It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews. 3It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith? 4 No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God,
“Your words
will be proven true,
and in court
you will win your case.”
5If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask. 6But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world? 7Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner. 8You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well.
No One Is Good
9What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said. 10 The Scriptures tell us,
“No one is acceptable to God!
11Not one of them understands
or even searches for God.
12They have all turned away
and are worthless.
There isn't one person
who does right.
13 Their words are like
an open pit,
and their tongues are good
only for telling lies.
Each word is as deadly
as the fangs of a snake,
14 and they say nothing
but bitter curses.
15 These people quickly
become violent.
16Wherever they go,
they leave ruin
and destruction.
17They don't know how
to live in peace.
18 They don't even fear God.”
19We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty. 20 God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin.
God's Way of Accepting People
21Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses. 22 God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ. 23All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory. 24But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins. 25-26 God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
27What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith. 28We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law. 29Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is! 30 There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith. 31Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_text:
1Tɔn, nyɔlante ki Juu teeb la ki gar binba ki tee Juu teebi? Bi punpotu mɔk nyɔɔtii? 2Li mɔk nyɔɔt bonchiann. Sinsinn tee Yennu din jii u sennu mɔbona na ki guun Juu teebe; 3ki bi siab-i diŋŋi kii tee yadateenteeb, li want nan Yennu ki tee barmɔnii daanɔ amii? 4Aaii, li ki want nna kaa. Niib kur-i bo-i tee fai damm, ŋaan Yennu ŋanin tee barmɔnpakirɔɔe. Li waa nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba nee a
“Niib-i bu fin Yennu buut,
bi sa la ki a mɔk mɔnii,
ki bia di mɔniie.”
5Ŋaan ti toonbiit-i fiit wann nan Yennu mɔk mɔnii, bee ki ti sa yeti? Wa dat ti tuba na paak, ki ti ji yet a u mɔk biitii? (Nisaarik dudukite ki n yet nna.) Aaii, n kan yet nna kaa. 6Yennu-i kii be fanu, u bo kan fit kii bu tingbouŋ na niib buuti.
7Tɔn, sɔɔ-i boi a “N faak na-i want nan Yennu set tee barmɔnii daanɔ, ki bia tee ki niib-n turɔ baakir, bee teen ki u sa dat n tubir a n tee biitdaanɔ?” 8Siab bia dukii a “Ŋaant ki tin tun toonbiit a bonŋann-n tan baar.” Nisiab sukiin ki yeen a mine yet nna. Yennu sa dat bi tuba kaman nan la jaŋ namm biaŋinba.
Nirɔ kaa ki tee niŋanɔ
9Ki li tee nlee? Timm nba tee Juu teeb na, ti binbeŋ chee binba ki tee Juu teeb binbeŋ amii? Aaii, ti ki chee bi. N poŋ dɔŋ yet a biit yikoo be Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb ni 10kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a
“Sɔɔ kaa ki tee niŋanɔ,
aaii, nan yenɔkɔɔwa gbaa.
11Sɔɔ kaa ki mɔk minu,
sɔɔ kaa ki loon Yennu.
12Sɔɔ kur kpet sɔnuwa,
sɔɔ kur tuun biite;
sɔɔ kaa ki tuun toonŋana,
sɔɔ kaa nan yenɔkɔɔwa gbaa;
13a bi mɔi yaa nan kaayaant nee,
ki fai nyi bi jerima ni,
ki linba nyi bi mɔi ni tee kaman nan waauk lɔbii nee;
14mɔpora nan mɔtont kuukɔɔe nyi bi mɔi ni.
15Bi sa kpi nirɔ yiama;
16a ki siaminba kur ki bi gar,
parbiir tuu tan baar leŋe.
17Bi ki mi sɔnu nba ki bi sa tɔkin
ki lin baar nan parmaasiri.
18Bi binbeŋ ni, bi ki tiin Yennu.”
19Tɔn, ti mi nan Yennu sennu-i yet linba, li yeen ki teen binba be li yikoo ni, li paake ki niib kur sii muu bi mɔi, ki tingbouŋ na teeb kur be Yennu buut ni, ki u tɔɔnn sɔɔ kaa ki mɔk mɔnii. 20Sɔɔ kaa ki sa fit wei sennu na nba senn biaŋinba ki teen niŋanɔ Yennu tɔɔnn; sennu na paake te ki nifosaarik bann nan u tuun toonbiit.
Biaŋinba ki Yennu te ki nifosaarik tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn
21Ŋaan mɔtana, Yennu mɔŋ nba lon sɔnsau nba sa te ki nifosaarik-n teen niŋanɔ na ji be yeenin nie, ki u sɔkiniinba maan nan sennu nee wantit li po, ŋaan li ki nyi sennu na saku ni kaa. 22Yennu te ki nisaarii kur nba teen Yisa Masia yada nee tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, li nyii ba teen Yisa Masia yada nie, ki lukitin kaa, 23kimaan nifosaarii kur tun toonbiit ki ki be fanu kaman nan Yennu nba loon biaŋinba na. 24Yennu ninbatinu paake ki u yet a bi tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn; li tee piiniie, kimaan Yisa Masia pa bi paak ki nyinnib daabsin ni. 25Yennu te ki Yisa Masia nyii yeenin ni, ki bia te ki u sɔn nba nyii u ni na tee marouŋ ti biit paak, ki ti-i teen linba na yada, Yennu sa nyik chab ti biit; ŋanne wann nan Yennu be fanu. Yoo nba gar, Yennu din ki dat nisaarii tuba bi toonbiit paaki, kimaan u sukuru paak; 26ŋaan mɔtana u nyik chabit ki loon wun wann nan u set be fanu, ki bia yet a wunba teen Yisa yada na mun tee niŋanɔ ŋɔɔ Yennu tɔɔnn.
27Li paak, siar be ki ti mɔk parbifaant nannii? Aaii, li ki tee a nifosaarik nba waa sennu na paak kaa ki ti sa la tinnu, ŋaan li tee ta teen Yisa Masia yada na paake. 28Ti dukii ki yeen a nisaarik yada teenu paake ki Yennu yet a u tee niŋanɔ; ki li ki tee a wa waa sennu na paak kaa. 29Yennu tee Juu teeb kuukɔɔ Yennuee? Aaii, u tee Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb Yennue. 30Yennu tee Yen-yenɔkɔɔe ki sa yet a Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ki bi kur-n teen niŋamm u tɔɔnn bi yada teenu paak. 31Tɔn, ta yet a nifosaarik laat tinnu u yada teenu nie na, ŋanne te ki sennu na tee yannii? Aaii, li ki tee nna kaa. Ti lek see sennu na poe.

english_text:
The Example of Abraham
1Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham? 2If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God. 3 The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
4Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working. 5But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him. 6In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
7-8 “What a blessing
when God forgives our sins
and our evil deeds.
What a blessing
when the Lord erases our sins
from his book.”
9Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him. 10But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
11 Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised. 12This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised.
The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
13 God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable. 14 If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
15God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken. 16 Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all. 17 The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
18 God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations. 19 Abraham's faith never became weak, not even when he was nearly 100 years old. He knew he was almost dead and that his wife Sarah could not have children. 20But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
21Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised. 22So God accepted him, 23just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham. 24They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life. 25 God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_text:
Abraham labaar
1Tɔn, ti sa yet a bee ki jikit saa ti yeja Abraham po, nan u binbeŋ po? 2Wa din waa sennu na-i diŋŋi te ki Yennu yet a u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn, u bo sii mɔk linba bo sa te ki u mɔk parbifaant, ŋaan u kii mɔk parbifaant Yennu tɔɔnni. 3Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Abraham din teen Yennu yadae, ki u yada maŋ paake ki Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn.” 4Li-i tee ki nirɔ tuun toona, u nyɔɔt kii tee piinii kaa, ŋaan li sii tee paauko. 5Ŋaan nirɔ nba ki teen sennu weiu na yada, ŋaan teen Yennu wunba nyikit biitdamm bi biit ki chabitib na yada, Yennu sa jii li daanɔ ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn; li daanɔ yada paake ki Yennu sa jiiu. 6Nnee ki David din yet a daanɔ nba ki Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn ki li ki tee a u toonŋana paak kaa, li daanɔ mɔk parpeenn bonchiann. 7David din yet a
“Damm nba ki Yennu nyik bi biit ki chabib
ki kpeen bi biit na, bi mɔk parpeenn;
8Damm nba ki Yennu ki tian bi toonbiit po, bi mɔk parpeenn.”
9Li parpeenn maŋ tee binba pot na kuukɔɔe yar amii? Aaii, nan binba ki pot na mun yaa yare. Ti poŋ bo yet a li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Abraham din teen Yennu yadae, ki u yada maŋ paake ki Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn.” 10Nlee yooe ki Yennu din jiiu nna? Yennu poŋ din yiu niŋanɔewa ki u tan pot amii u din potewa ki Yennu yiu niŋanɔ? Aaii, Yennu poŋ din yiu niŋanɔewa ki u tan pot. 11U din pota, ŋaan u potu maŋ tee siara nba want nan u yada maŋ paake ki Yennu poŋ din jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ, li poorpoewa ki u ji tan pot. Li paak, binba kur tee Yennu yada ŋaan ki pot na, Abraham tee bi baae; ba tee Yennu yada paake, ki Yennu yib niŋamm. 12Abraham tee binba pot na mun yaa baae, li ki tee a ba pot na paak kaa, ŋaan ba bia waa biaŋinba ki ti baa Abraham din teen Yennu yada na, li poorpoewa ki u din tan pot; bi yada paake ki Abraham tee bi baa.
Ta gaar Yennu mɔsonn yada paak biaŋinba na
13Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki tur Abraham nan u yaaboona a tingbouŋ na tan sii tee bi yare, li din ki tee nan Abraham nba waa sennu na paak kaa ki Yennu senn li mɔsonni, ŋaan li din tee u yada paake nan Yennu nba din jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ u tɔɔnn na. 14Li-i bo-i tee ki Yennu mɔsonn na tee binba dia sennu maŋ yarie, ŋann ŋanin, nirɔ yada bo sii tee yanne, ki Yennu mɔsonn bo kii mɔk nyɔɔti. 15Sennu nee baat nan Yennu wutoor li mɔyêtuk paak, ŋaan bi-i kii senn sennu, mɔyêtuk sii kaa.
16Yennu mɔsonn na tee binba teenɔ yada na yare; u ninbatinu paak, ki u senn mɔsonn maŋ ki li set tee Abraham yaaboona kur yar, li ki tee a binba waa sennu na kuukɔɔ yar kaa, ŋaan binba kur teen Yennu yada kaman nan Abraham nba din teen biaŋinba, kimaan ŋɔɔ Abrahame tee ti kura baa 17kaman nan la sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni ki u yet Abraham a “N teena nibooru bonchiann baae.” Li paak, Yennu boor Abraham tee ti baae, Yennu wunba ki Abraham teenɔ yada na, ŋɔɔe te ki kpeemm laat manfoa, ki bia piak linba daa ki be po kaman nan li set be. 18Siar din ki want dindann ŋaan Abraham lek ji din mɔk dindann nan Yennu sa turɔ bika, ki bia teen yada, ki u yada teenu paak ki u din teen nibooru bonchiann baa kaman nan Yennu nba yetɔ a “A yaaboona tan sa pɔt bonchiann.” 19Abraham din la nan bina kobik na, ki din mi nan u gbanant kpeta, ki u ŋaapoo Sara bia din tee pookanmatire, ŋaan nan li kura gbaa, Abraham yada din ki wati. 20U mantik din teen Yennu yada, u din ki mɔk birsuk nan Yennu mɔsonn na, u yadae din te ki u mɔk paŋ bonchiann, ki u din piak Yennu. 21U mantik din teen Yennu yada ki ki birisi, ki mi nan Yennu nba senn biaŋinba, u set sa fit tuma. 22Li paak, ki “Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ,” kimaan u teen Yennu yada. 23Ŋaan Abraham kuukɔɔ paak kaa ki li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Yennu jiiu ki u tee niŋanɔ,” 24ŋaan li sɔb ki teen timm nba ki Yennu sa gaarit mun ki ti tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, kimaan ta teenɔ yada, ŋɔɔ Yennu wunba fiin ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuun ni na. 25Ŋɔɔe din jii Yisa ki chab ki u kpo ti toonbiit paak, ŋaan bia fiinɔ kuun ni, a wun te ki tii tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn.

english_text:
What It Means To Be Acceptable to God
1By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God. 2Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God. 3But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure. 4And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope 5that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
6Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful. 7No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person. 8But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
9But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger. 10Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son. 11And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us.
Adam and Christ
12 Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die. 13Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law. 14Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did.
In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later. 15But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
16There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times. 17Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
18 Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life. 19Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
20The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful. 21Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_text:
Binba ki Yennu yet a bi tee niŋamm
1Tɔn, ta teen Yisa yada na paake, ki Yennu yet a ti tee niŋamm u tɔɔnn, ki parmaasir ji be timm nan Yennu sinsuuk ni ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak. 2U paake ki Yennu tint ninbaauk ti yada maŋ paak; ki ti la u ninbatinu maŋ ki mɔk parpeenn, kimaan ti mɔk dindann nan ti tan sii mɔk binbenŋaŋ nba ki Yennu sa turit na. 3Ti bia mɔk parpeenn ti fardinu ni, kimaan ti mi nan fardinu baat nan sukurue, 4ki sukuru mun baat nan binbenŋaŋ, ki binbenŋaŋ maŋ mun baat nan dindann, 5ki dindann maŋ ni ti tan kan di fei, kimaan Yennu jii loomm nba ki u mɔk na ki teen ti para ni, u Seyeeŋ nba ki u turit na paak.
6Kimaan yoo nba ki ti daa din ki mɔk sommir na, Masia din gaar timm biitdamm paak ki kpo, ki li tee yoo nba ki Yennu din ganne. 7Li paar nan nirɔ-n gaar nipeemɔ paak ki kpo, ŋaan li pasiar a sa la sɔɔ wun gaar niŋanɔ paak ki kpo; 8ŋaan Yennu wann nan u set loonit bonchiann kimaan ta din tee biitdamm na Yisa Masia din gaar ti paak ki kpo. 9U sɔn paak, ki Yennu yet a ti tee niŋamme; li paak mun u kpan sa nyinnit tubdatu nba baat ti paak na ni. 10Ti din tuu tee Yennu dataie, ŋaan mɔtana jik u teent u yɔɔsinbae kimaan u Bija nba din kpo na sɔn paak, ki ta tee u yɔɔs na, birsuk ji kaa nan u sa tinnit u manfoor paaki. 11Ŋaan li kur kaa na; ti bia mɔk parpeenn Yennu nba sommit bonchiann biaŋinba ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak, kimaan u paake ki Yennu teent u yɔɔsinba mɔtana.
Adam nan Masia labaar
12Tɔn, niyenɔkɔɔe din te ki biit baar tingbouŋ na ni, ki u biit na paake te ki kuun be. Li paake te ki kuun kpi niib kur, kimaan nisaarik kur tun toonbiita. 13Biit din be tingbouŋ na niewa ki Yennu din tan tur Moses sennu na, ŋaan sennu-i kaa, sɔɔ kan fikis nirɔ ki yet a u biir sennu maŋi. 14Adam yoo neewa ki tan tuu Moses yoo, ki kuun lek mɔk yikoo sɔɔ kur paak, ki li kpi binba mɔk biit nba ki tee kaman nan Adam biit nba din tee biaŋinba na.
Tɔn, Adam din tee wunba din yaa wun wei poor na ninnauŋo. 15Ŋaan bi bannlee na ki jan, kimaan Yennu piinii nba ki u pit yann na ki tee kaman nan Adam toonbiit na kaa. Li tee mɔniie nan niib bonchiann kpo niyenɔkɔɔ toonbiit paake; Yennu ninkpabauŋ yab, ki u piinii nba ki u teen niib bonchiann ki li nyii yenɔkɔɔ Yisa Masia ninkpabauŋ na mun bia yab. 16Bɔkitu be Yennu piinii nan niyenɔkɔɔ Adam toonbiiuk sinsuuk ni; kimaan niyenɔkɔɔ Adam toonbiiuk maŋ poorpoe ki Yennu din bu u buut ki yet a u toonbiiuk maŋ ŋan nan tubdatu; ŋaan ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia piinii nba ki u pit yann na tee nyikinchab, toonbichiauŋ poorpoe. 17Niyenɔkɔɔ maŋ toonbiiuk paake ki kuun kpi sɔɔ kur, ki li nyii niyenɔ maŋ boore; ŋaan linba wei niyenɔkɔɔ Yisa Masia toonn na gar nnawa; niib kur nba la Yennu ninbatinu nba yab, nan u piinii nba tee nyikinchab piinii na, bi sa di naan kii mɔk manfoa Yisa Masia paak.
18Niyenɔkɔɔ maŋ toonbiiuk paake te ki sɔɔ kur ŋan nan tubdatu, nnee mun, niyenɔkɔɔ toonŋann paake te ki sɔɔ kur laat tinnu nan manfoor. 19Niyenɔkɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Adam na mɔyêtuk paake te ki niib kur teen biitdamm; li mun tee nnee nan niyenɔkɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Masia na mɔsaku paake te ki niib bonchiann teen niŋamm Yennu boor.
20Sennu na baar a lin ŋamm fiit wann toonbiit nba yab biaŋinbae; ki toonbiit nba yab na, Yennu ninbatinu mun yab ki gar nna. 21Li paak, biit nba din kpi nifosaarii ki te ki bi kpenn biaŋinba na, nnee mun ki Yennu ninbatinu paak te ki u yet a bi tee niŋamm u boor, ki turib manfoa nba kaa gbennu, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak.

english_text:
Dead to Sin but Alive because of Christ
1What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better? 2No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning? 3Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death? 4 When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
5If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him. 6We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin. 7We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
8As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him. 9We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die. 10When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God. 11In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
12Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires 13or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God. 14Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law.
Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
15What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not! 16Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him. 17You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received. 18Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
19I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
20When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God. 21But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death. 22Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life. 23Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_text:
Toonbiit nan yentinu labaar
1Li paak, ti sa yet nlee? Ti bia sii be ti toonbiit tumu nie ki Yennu ninbatinu na-n pukin amii-ii? 2Aaii, ŋann kaa. Li tee kaman nan ti kpo nee, ki li paak biit ki mɔk ti yaaki. Ŋannimi ti kan biar toonbiit tumu ni. 3Timm nba ki bi wurit Yennu nyunwuru nan Yisa Masia sann ni na, li tee kaman nan ti lakin kpo nanɔ nee. 4Li paak, ti din kpoe nan Yisa Masia ki bi lakin piit nanɔ Yennu nyunwuru ni; ki u Baa Yennu din jii u paŋ ki fiinɔ kuun ni, nnee ki ti mun fiir a tii be manfopaann ni.
5Li-i tee ki timm nan Masia lakin kpo, nnee biak ki ti bia sa lakin ki fiir kuun ni nan wa din fiir biaŋinba na. 6Ti mi nan ti binbenkper na lakin ki kpo nan Yisa u dapunpunn paak, a ti binbenkper na ji daa mɔk yaak, ki ti ji kii tee daaba biit paŋ ni. 7Nirɔ-i kpo, u nyii biit paŋ niwa. 8Ta kpo nan Masia na, ti mɔk yada nan ti sii be nanɔ. 9Ti mi nan Yennu fiin Masia kuun niwa ki u ji kan mi kpo; kuun ki mɔk u yaaki. 10U din kpo ti biit paake taar yomm, ŋaan wa ji be u manfoor ni na, u be ki teen Yennue. 11Li paak, i mun dukin nan li tee kaman nan i kpoe ki biit ji ki mɔk i yaaki, ŋaan Yisa Masia paak i ji be i manfoor ni Yennu boor.
12Li paak, i daa te ki biit-n yent i gbanant ki te ki i tuun biit loommu. 13Daa teen i gbanant siar po yaak ki li tuun toonbiit, ŋaan jii i mɔŋ ki tur Yennu, ŋaan kii tee kaman nan i nyii kuun nie ki kɔɔ manfoor ni na; jiin i mɔŋ kur ki tur Yennu ki wun jii-i ki tun yentinu toona. 14Biit daa mɔk i yaak, kimaan sennu na ki yeniini, ŋaan Yennu ninbatinu nee yeni.
Yentinu daaba labaar
15Tɔn, sennu na nba ki yenti, ŋaan ki Yennu ninbatinu nee yent na, ti sa biar toonbiit tumu ni amii? Aaii, ti kan teen nna kaa. 16Fi-i jii a mɔŋ ki pet ki tur nirɔ ki tee u daabir ki saak u mɔb, li tee mɔniie nan a set tee u daabire. Fi-i sak ki tuun toonbiit, a tee biit daabire, ki li joontu tee kuume; ŋaan fi-i sak Yennu mɔb, a tee mɔsakdaanɔe, ki ŋanne sa te ki a teen niŋanɔ Yennu boor. 17Sianyoo, biit din mɔk i yaak, ŋaan mɔtana i mantik saak barmɔnii wannu nba ki bi din wanti na; li paak, ki n teen Yennu niipoouk. 18Mɔtana i ji ki tee biit daaba, ŋaan yentinue ji mɔk i yaak. 19(Yimm nisaarii nba ki fit bant Yennu maan yian na paake ki n yaa n pak nisaarik yan ki wanni u labaar.) Ya din jii i mɔŋ ki tuun toonbiit ki li nɔb na, mɔtana jiin man i mɔŋ kii tuun yentinu toona kii tee popeendamm. 20Ya din tuu tee biit daaba na, i din tuu ki tee yentinu yabi. 21Toona nba ki i din tuun ki li mɔki fei mɔtana na, li mɔk nyɔlante ki turiini? Li toonbooru baat nan kuume. 22Ŋaan mɔtana i ji nyii biit daabsin niwa ki ji tee Yennu daabae; li paak, ki i teen popeendamm, ki mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu; ŋanne tee i nyɔɔt. 23Kimaan biit nyɔɔt tee kuume, ŋaan Yennu piinii tee manfoor nba kaa gbennue, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak.

english_text:
An Example from Marriage
1My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive. 2For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free 3to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
4That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God. 5When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die. 6But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law.
The Battle with Sin
7 Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this. 8It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
9Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life, 10and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death. 11 Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died. 12Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
13Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is. 14We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin. 15 In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate. 16Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good. 17So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
18I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot. 19Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong. 20And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
21The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right. 22With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God. 23But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do. 24What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die? 25Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me.
So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_text:
Jakunt nan pookɔɔnt labaar
1N naa waas, i kur mi sennu na po, li paak, i sa fit soor ma yaa n yet linba na paaka. Sennu na so wunba be u manfoor nie. 2Li paak, sennu na nɔɔk ni, li kpaa talas nan poo-n taan nan u sɔrɔ yoo nba ki u sɔrɔ daa be u manfoor ni, ŋaan u sɔrɔ na-i taŋŋi kpo yoonba, sennu na ji ki so poo na. 3Ŋannimi, yoo nba ki u sɔrɔ daa be u manfoor ni na, ŋaan ki u nyii jab, bi sii yiu poochonchonne; ŋaan li-i tee ki u sɔrɔ kpoie, sennu na ji kan soorɔ, li-i tee ki u ji kun jaganɔ u sɔrɔ na kuun poorpo, u ji kii tee poochonchonni. 4N naa waas, li mun tee nnee ki turi; i tee Masia gbanant bɔkire, ki din kpoe ki sennu na ji ki soini; ŋaan mɔtana i gaa nan Masia wunba din fiir kuun ni nee, a tii tuun linba ŋan ki tur Yennu. 5Yoo nba ki ti din tuun ti gbanant loomm nee, ki sennu na te ki ti mɔk nianbiiuk ki li tuun ti gbanant ni, ki li baat nan linba tee kuun ti paak. 6Mɔtana ŋanin, sennu na ki diati, kimaan ti din kpoe ki sennu na ji ki mɔk ti yaaki. Li paak, ti ji fit tuun ki teen Yennu nan manfopaann nba ki u Seyeeŋ turit; ti ji ki tuun nan sennkper nba ki bi din sɔb gbouŋ ni na kaa.
Sennu nan biit labaar
7Li paak, ti sa yet a nlee? Ti sa yet a sennu na ki ŋan amii? Ti kan yet nna kaa. Sennu na-i bo-i kaa, n bo kan bann biit nba tee linba. Sennu na-i bo-i kii wann a n daa mɔk niauŋ n lɔɔ bont paaki, n bo kan bann niauŋ nba tee linba, 8ŋaan ki biit la yaak sennu na ni, ki te ki nianbooru booru bonchiann fiir n par ni. Sennu na-i bo-i kaa, biit bo sii tee bonkpeenne. 9Yoo nba ki n din ki mi sennu maŋ na, n din mɔk manfoore; ŋaan ma tan bann sennu maŋ, ki biit nba be n par ni na fiir, ki n kpo. 10Sennu nba bo yaa li baat nan manfoor na, ki li baar nan kuun ki turin. 11Biit la u sɔnu sennu na nie ki kpannin, ki leŋe ki li kpiin.
12Sennu na tiauŋ ŋanin yeene, ki bia tee barmɔnii nan linba fan. 13Tɔn, li want nan linba ŋane baar nan kuun n paak amii? Aaii, li ki want nna kaa. Biit nee jii linba ŋan na ki baar nan kuun turin, a lin wann biit nba tee linba. Ŋanne ki ti ji bann nan biit tee bonbilankante.
Bona ŋannlee nba ki nisaarik loon
14Ti mi nan sennu na tee Yennu Seyeeŋ yare, ŋaan min ŋanin tee nisaarike, ki tee biit daabir, 15kimaan linba ki n tuun na, n ki mi li paaki, kimaan n ki tuun toonŋana nba ki n looni; ŋaan toonbiit nba ki n nammir na, ŋanne ki n tuun. 16Mi-i tun toonbiit nba ki n bo ki loon ki n tun na, li want nan n sak nan sennu maŋ ŋane. 17Li paak, li ki tee n mɔŋ kaa tuumiri, ŋaan biit nba be n ni nee te ki n tuun. 18N mi nan bonŋansiar kaa n par ni; n loon ki n tun linba ŋan, ŋaan n kan fit ki tuumiri; 19kimaan ma loon ki n tun toonŋana nba na, n ki tuumiri, ŋaan toonbiit nba ki n ki loon ki n tun na, ŋanne ki n tuun. 20Li paak, mi-i tuun linba ki n bo ki loon mii tuumi, li ji ki tee min kaa tuumi, ŋaan li tee biit nba be n ni nee tuun. 21N la ki bonsiar be n ni yoo kura, mi-i yaa n tun linba ŋan, ŋaan biit bia lek tuu be nanin. 22Yennu sennu nee te ki n par peen, 23ŋaan ki n laat sennsiar nba tuun n gbanant ni, ki li ki sak nan sennu nba ki n par loon na, ki lorin nan dansarkɔɔrɔ na, a n sak biit nba be n ni na. 24Aba, n tee parbiir daanɔe. Ŋmee sa fatin ki nyinnin gbanant nba saa nanin kuun ni na? 25N piak Yennu wunba fatin ki lun Yisa Masia ni na paak. Li paak, n mɔŋ waa Yennu sennu na n par ni, ŋaan ki n gbanant waa biit sɔnu na.

english_text:
Living by the Power of God's Spirit
1If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished. 2The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death. 3The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin.
