13I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will bless you and be kind to you! May God bless you with his love, and may the Holy Spirit join all your hearts together.
translation_text:
Kpaanjoontii nan foontjoontii labaar
1N sa baar ki tan foonti ki pukin taar munntaa. Li-i tee ki i jii sɔɔ taari, n kan saki, see ki siaradamm bannlee amii banntaa pak biir li yɔɔ. 2Ma din baar i boor taar munnlee ni na, n din kpaan i kur nba poŋ tun toonbiit na, nan i leeb kura. Ma ji ki be i boor ni na, ŋaan n lek kpaani kaman nan ma din poŋ kpaani biaŋinba sinsinn na; mi-i taŋŋi baar, n sa dat bi tubawa. 3Ŋanne sa wann nan Masiae te ki n piak. U ki tee butimɔɔ kaa i sinsuuk ni, u mɔk paŋ i boor ni. 4U din jii u paŋ maŋe ki bakin bir ŋaan din sak ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak. Mɔtana, Yennu paŋ paak ki u be u manfoor ni. Mɔtana, timm nba lakin be u ni na, ti ki mɔk paŋi, ŋaan ta lakin taan nanɔ na paak, ti mun mɔk manfoor Yennu paŋ paake, ki fit jii li paŋ ki wanni.
5Gotir i mɔŋ mɔŋ binbeŋ ni ki laan i set teen Yisa Masia yada amii i ki teeni; li kpaa talas ki yii gorii i mɔŋ binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba. I ki mi nan Yisa Masia be i niee? U set be i ni, kimaan li-i tee ki u ki be i ni, ŋann i ki tee u niib nan barmɔnii. 6N mɔk dindann nan i sa bann nan timm ŋanin tee u niibe nan barmɔnii. 7Ti mei Yennu teeni a i daa tuun toonbiiti. Li ki tee a ti want ti mɔŋ a ti tee barmɔnii damm kaa; li-i lekii tee ki i dukii a timm ki tee Masiaweira, ŋaan ti loon ki yii tuun toonŋanae, 8kimaan ti ki loon ki tin jiin Yennu mɔmaan poori; ti loon ki mɔmaan maŋe-n dɔŋ tɔɔnn po. 9Li-i tee ki i mɔk paŋ ki tuun toona nba fan, ŋanne sa maŋ ti para ni, kimaan ti ki loon ki tin wanni ti paŋ kaa; li paak te ki ti mei Yennu ki teeni, a i ŋamm i binbeŋ. 10Li paak, ma ki be i boor ni na, ki n sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teeni, a mi-i taŋŋi baar i boor ni yoonba, li kii kpaa talas ki n dat i tuba ki wanni n paŋ; ŋanne te ki n sɔbir. Ti Yomdaanɔe din turin yudantuk na a n sommi ki yin wei u sɔnu fanu. U din ki turin yudantuk maŋ a n biiri kaa.
11N naa waas, linba ji biar tee min chabie. Ŋammit i sɔnu man, kii saak n kpaanii man, kii mɔk mɔtaauk, nan parmaasir; ki Yennu nba loonit ki teent parmaasir na sii be nani.
12I foont i leeb kpammii man. 13Yennu niib nba kur be nna na foonti.
14Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sommir, nan Yennu loomm, nan u Seyeeŋ mɔtaauk na-ii be nan i kura.

english_text:
1-2From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me.
I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work.
To the churches in Galatia:
3I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! 4Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world. 5God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen.
The Only True Message
6I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message, 7when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ. 8I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven. 9I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
10I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ.
How Paul Became an Apostle
11My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach. 12It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
13 You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it. 14 I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us. 15 But even before I was born, God had chosen me by his gift of undeserved grace and had decided 16to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone. 17I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
18 Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for 15 days. 19The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother. 20And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
21Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia. 22But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person. 23They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy. 24And because of me, they praised God.
translation_text:
1-2Min Pɔɔl nba tee Masia toomii, nan ti naa waas nba be nna na kur foont Masiaweira nba be Galasia tiŋ ni. Nisaarik kaa teenin Masia toomii, nisaarii kaa bia tumin, ŋaan ti Baa Yennu nan Yisa Masia nba ki u din fiinɔ kuun ni nee din gannin.
3Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi ki bia turi parmaasir.
4Yisa Masiae din sak ti Baa Yennu loomm ki din kpo ti biit paak a wun fatit tingbouŋ na biit daabsin ni. 5Ŋɔɔ Yennu kuukɔɔe yen dontir nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.
Barŋanii yennkɔɔe be
6Li bakitime nan ya gaan labagann na yiama, ŋaan nyik Yennu wunba din yiini Masia ninbatinu paak na. 7Nisiab be ki yabii bi mɔŋ a bin lebit Masia barŋanii na ki ŋmat i dudukit; ŋaan barmɔniie, barŋansiar ji kaa. 8Li-i lekii tee Yendɔuŋ ni malaka amii timmi mɔɔnt barŋansiar ki li be li kuukɔɔ ki ki tee ta poŋ din mɔɔnt linba turi na, mɔpor-ii be bi paak. 9Ti poŋ din betiewa, ŋaan n bia ŋammit beerie mɔtana: nisɔɔ-i mɔɔnt labaar ki turi ki li ki tee linba ki i din gaar na, mɔpor-ii be u paak.
10N ki yiab ma n gaar nisaarik pinpakit kaa, ŋaan Yennu yare ki n loon, kimaan mi-i loon nisaarik pinpakitie, n bo kan teen Masia daabiri.
Biaŋinba ki Pɔɔl tan teen Masia toomii
11N naa waas, n yaa n tiari nan barŋanii nba ki n mɔɔnt teeni na ki nyii nisaarik nba din pak linba na ni kaa. 12Nisaarik kaa din wannin ki n gaar, ŋaan Yisa Masia mɔŋ mɔŋe din fiit wannin.
13I poŋ gbat ma din tuun biaŋinba yoo nba ki n din waa Juu teeb jiantu na, a n din tuu mukisir Yennu mɔtaauk niib bonchiann, ki yabii n mɔŋ ma n biirib ki gbenn. 14Timm Juu teeb jiantu sɔnu ni, mine din gar tɔɔnn ti waakir ni, kimaan n din jii n par kura ki waa ti yejamm kaar nba want linba.
15Ŋaan Yennu ninbatinu paak, ki u din gannin ki bi daa din ki marimi, ki yiinin a man tun u toona. 16Ki wa din dukin ki nyinn u Bik maŋ paanu ni ki wannin a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb yoonba, n din ki saan nisaarik boor ni ki saa faa sɔɔ baani. 17N bia din ki saan Jerusalem ki saa la binba din dɔŋ teen toomiinba nanin na, ŋaan n din fiire li taakpaak ni ki saan Arabia tiŋ ni; li poorpoe ki n ŋmat saan Damaskus doo ni. 18Ki bina ŋanntaa poorpoe ki n din tan saan Jerusalem ki foont Piita, ki din biar u boor ni daa piik nan ŋaŋŋmu. 19Li-i kii tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa naa bik Jemsi kaa, ŋaan n din ki la toomiinba na sɔɔ.
20Yennu mɔŋ mi nan ma sɔb linba na tee barmɔniie ki faak kaa leŋi.
21Li poorpoe ki n din saan Siria nan Silisia tinii ni, 22ŋaan li yoo na ki Masiaweira nba be Judea tiŋ ni na din ki mimi. 23Ba din mi linba din tee bi leeb nba din yeen a “Jɔɔ nba din tuu mukisirit na mun ji dia barŋanii nba ki u din yaa wun biir nee ki mɔɔntir teen niib.” 24Ki bi din piak Yennu n paak linba na po.

english_text:
1 Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus. 2But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
3Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised. 4We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves. 5But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
6 Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message. 7They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews. 8God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
9James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews. 10They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do.
Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
11When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong. 12He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles. 13He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled. 14But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said:
Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
15We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles. 16 But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
17When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't! 18But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first. 19It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God.
I have been nailed to the cross with Christ. 20I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me. 21I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
translation_text:
Pɔɔl nan toomiinba leeb labaar
1Tɔn, li bina piik nan ŋanna poorpoe, ki min nan Banabas din ŋmat saan Jerusalem, ki n jii Taitus mun; 2Yennue din fiit li sɔnu saanu paanu ni ki wannin, li paake te ki n saan. Leŋe ki n yiin saakab na, ti kuukɔɔ, ki wannib ma mɔɔntir barŋanii mɔɔntii maŋ biaŋinba ki teen binba ki tee Juu teeb na, kimaan n ki loon ma poŋ tun linba, nan ma sa tun linba na-n tan biar yann ki nyɔɔt kaa. 3Tɔn, n tɔknanlɔɔ Taitus tee Griik nirɔe, ŋaan sɔɔ din ki miau a wun kokit u punni. 4Binba be ki fa faak bi mɔŋ paak a bi mun tee Masiaweira na ŋanin din loon ki Taitus-n tun nnawa. Li niib din kɔɔ ti sinsuuk ni woonin ni, a bin bann ta mɔk yentinmɔŋ nba Yisa Masia ni na. Bi loon tii tee Moses sennu daabae, 5ŋaan ta mun loon tin dia barŋanii nan li barmɔnii sɔnu ki turi na paak, ti ki sak turib nan waaminnawa.
6Ŋaan binba ki bi yib saakab na, bi-i tee nijaana amii bi-i kii teeie, li ki kpaa talas min yaa boor, kimaan Yennu ki bikii nan ningannii; ŋamm nba ki bi yib saakab na din ki turin kpaanpaanii. 7Ŋaan ba set la linba n boor ni tee Yennu nba gannin a n mɔɔnt u barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na, kaman nan biaŋinba ki u din gann Piita a wun mɔɔnt li barŋanii ki tur Juu teeb na. 8Kimaan Yennu paŋ paak, Piita nba din tee toomii ki tur Juu teeb biaŋinba na, nnee ki n mun tee toomii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na. 9Jems nan Piita nan Jɔɔnn mɔŋ nba ki bi yib saakab na, ŋaan bi mɔŋ din bann nan Yennue turin toongannkar na; li paak min nan Banabas nan ŋamm din foont leeb ki yokin ti leeb nii ni, ŋanne din want a ti kura mɔk mɔtaauk nan ti leeb. Timm nan ŋamm din sake a min nan Banabas-ii mɔɔntir barŋanii kii teen binba ki tee Juu teeb na, ki bi mun-ii mɔɔntir kii teen Juu teeb. 10Linba yaa kuukɔɔ ki bi din betit li po din tee nnee: a ti daa tammit talasdamm nba be bi sinsuuk ni na po. Li mune ki n set poŋ din loon tumu.
Min Pɔɔl nba din yakii nan Piita biaŋinba
11Ŋanne ki n din tan yakii nan Piita wa din be Antiok yoo nba na, kimaan u din mɔk biit. 12Jems din tun Juu tesiab Antiok doo ni. Ba din daa ki baar yoonba na, Piita nan binba ki tee Juu teeb din lakin di jeet, ŋaan ba tan baar yoonba ki u ji ki sak a u sa di nammi, kimaan u din tiin niib nba teen punkokitu yada nee. 13Li paak, ki Juu leeb na mun piin tuun jaŋmaantoona, ki waa Piita nba din tun linba maŋ, ki Banabas mɔŋ mun ŋmat ki waa bi jaŋmaantoona maŋ. 14Ma din la ki bi ki tɔk barŋanii sɔnu maŋ fanu yoonba, ki n bet Piita bi kur numm ni ma “A tee Juu nirɔe, ŋaan a binbeŋ tee kaman nan wunba ki tee Juu nirɔ nee, ŋannii kaa, bee ki a mukisib a bii be kaman nan Juu teeb na?”
Niib sa tinn yada paak
15Tɔn, ti tee Juu teeb matiie, ti ki tee niboorgamm nba ki bi yib biitdamm na kaa, 16ŋaan ti mi nan see ki nirɔ-n teen Yisa Masia yada, ŋanne sa te ki Yennu-n yet a u tee niŋanɔ u boor; li ki tee ta tuun sennu nba yaa tii tuun linba paak kaa; li paak, ti mɔŋ mun teen Yisa Masia yada a Yennu-n yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor yada maŋ paake, ŋaan li ki tee sennu toona tumu paak kaa. Kimaan Yennu kan yet a sɔɔ tee niŋanɔ u boor sennu maŋ toona tumu paaki. 17Li-i bo-i kii tee nnaie, ŋann ŋanin ti-i yabii a Yennu-n yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor Masia paak, ŋaan ti lek bia tee biitdamme kaman nan binba ki tee Juu teeb na. Linba na want nan Masia ki kɔn toonbiit paak amii? Aaii, li ki want nna kaa. 18Kimaan mi-i jiŋŋi piin ki sent sennu sɔnu nba ki n din biira naie, ŋann want nan n mɔŋ tee sennu maŋ biirtɔɔe na. 19Ŋaan sennu na paak ki n kpo sennu maŋ boor, a mii mɔk manfoor Yennu boor. Li tee kaman nan bi din taan kpaa min nan Masia dapunpunn paak nee. 20Li paak, li ji ki tee min nba fo na kaa, ŋaan li tee Masia nba fo ki be n ni nee. Mɔtana, ma mɔk manfoor ki be n gbanant ni na, n be ki teen Yennu Bija Masia yada; ŋɔɔe loonin ki din gaar n paak ki kpo. 21N ki te ki Yennu ninbatinu teen yanni; li-i tee ki Yennu bo sa yet a nirɔ nba sak sennu nae tee niŋanɔ u boor, li bo want nan Masia kuun maŋ tee yanne.

english_text:
Faith Is the Only Way
1You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you? 2I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him? 3How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you? 4Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing? 5God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
6 The Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith. 7 And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham. 8 Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him. 9This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
10 Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.” 11 No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
12 The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands. 13 But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse. 14And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit.
The Law and the Promise
15My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement. 16That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ. 17 What I am saying is that the Law cannot change or cancel God's promise made 430 years before the Law was given. 18 If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
19What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people. 20There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him.
Slaves and Children
21Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law. 22But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
23The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith. 24In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God. 25But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
26All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus. 27And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes. 28Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman. 29 So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
translation_text:
Yada nba gar sennu biaŋinba
1Yimm Galasia teeb, bonjatana! Yimm nba poŋ mi Yisa Masia dapunpunn paak kuun po nyik nyik na, ŋmee jin tan kpanniini? 2I betin bonyennkɔɔ man, ya din tuun sennu na toona paake din te ki i gaar Yennu Seyeeŋ amii ya din gbat barŋanii na ki teen yada na paake din te ki i gaarɔ? 3Bee te ki i ki mɔk yami? I din piin Masiaweiu nan Yennu Seyeeŋ paŋe; bee ki i ji tan loon yin gbenn nan i mɔŋ mɔŋ paŋi? 4Ya din la Yennu yaarlituk toona nba na kura kaa nyɔɔt nan waama i boorii? N dukin nyɔɔt be li paak. 5Ya din tuun sennu toona na paake te ki Yennu turi Seyeeŋ ki tuun yaarlituk toona i sinsuuk ni amii ya gbat barŋanii ki teen yada na paake?
6Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Abraham din teen Yennu yada, li paak, ki Yennu din yet a u tee niŋanɔ u boor.” 7Ŋanne want nan niib nba teen Yisa yada na, ŋamme tee Abraham yaaboona. 8Ki li bia sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a ŋɔɔ Yennu din poŋ mi nan u tan sa yet a binba ki tee Juu teeb mun tee niŋamm u boor bi yada paak. Ŋann paake ki u poŋ din yet Abraham barŋanii maŋ a “Fin paake ki n tan sa teen mɔsonŋann nisaarik booru kura paak.” 9Li paak, Yennu tee mɔsonŋann binba teenɔ yada na paak, kaman nan wa din teen mɔsonŋann Abraham nba din teenɔ yada na paak.
10Binba waa sennu ki dukii a sennu na sa tinnib na, Yennu mɔtomm be bi paak, kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba ki waa sennu kur nba sɔb gbouŋ maŋ ni na, Yennu mɔtomm be u paak.” 11Ŋanne want fanu nan sennu dianu kan fit te ki nirɔ-ii tee niŋanɔ Yennu boor ni, kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba teen Yennu yada ki Yennu yeen a u tee niŋanɔ u boor, li yɔɔ yada paak, u sii mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu.” 12Ŋaan sennu dianu nan yada teenu bɔkita; kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Daanɔ nba waa sennu kur fanu sii mɔk manfoor.”
13Ŋaan ti din be Yennu mɔtomm nie, kimaan ti kan fit wei sennu fanu, ŋaan Masia tiauŋo din gaar ti paak ki din be mɔtomm maŋ ni ki nyinnit li mɔtomm ni, kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a “Wunba ki bi kpaau dapunpunn paak na, Yennu mɔtomm be u paak.” 14Yisa Masia din tun linba na a ŋɔɔ yaa paak ki binba ki tee Juu teeb-n mun la mɔsonŋann nba ki Yennu din senn Abraham paak; a ti-i teen yada tin gaar Seyeeŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn a wun turit na.
Sennu nan mɔsonn labaar
15N naa waas, ŋaant ki n jii nisaarik nan u lɔɔ binbeŋ ki bikin ki yin bann linba ki n beeri na paak: niib bannlee-i sak lor a bin tun siar ki pot mɔɔnu nan leeb, bi yenɔkɔɔ ji kan fit nyi u lɔɔ na poorpo nan waama. 16Yennu nba din tur Abraham nan u yaaboonn u mɔsona maŋ yoonba, u din ki yet a “A yaaboona” kaa, ŋaan u din yet a “A yaaboonn yenɔkɔɔ,” wunba tee Masia. 17Ma want linba tee nnee: Yennu din senn mɔsonjaann; ki sennu nba din tan baar bina kobii ŋanna nan piinntaa poorpo na, li din kan fit kpeen Yennu mɔsonn maŋi. 18Kimaan li-i tee sennu dianu paake ki Yennu teen niib u piinii, ŋann li wann nan u bo kan sak tur sɔɔ piinii u mɔsonn paak; ŋaan li din tee Yennu mɔsonn paake ki u tur Abraham u piinii maŋ.
19Li-i bo-i tee nnaie, sennu na nyɔɔt tee bee, ŋann amii? toonbiit paake, ki Yennu din tur nifosaarii sennu na ki pukin u mɔsonn na paak, ki din loon a sennu maŋ-ii diab mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Abraham yaaboonn nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki turɔ na tan baar. Ki malakanba din jii sennu maŋ ki teen nisaarik nuu ni a u tee sɔkintɔɔ, 20ŋaan Yennu tiauŋo din tur Abraham u mɔsonn maŋ, li din ki tee sɔkintɔɔ kaa turɔ.
Sennu nyɔɔt labaar
21Tɔn, ŋanne want nan sennu na kɔn nan Yennu mɔsona-aa? Aaii, li ki want nna kaa; ŋaan Yennu-i bo-i senn sennu nba sa fit teen nirɔ manfoor, u bo sa yet a niib tee niŋamm u boor sennu maŋ weiu paaka. 22Ŋaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a sɔɔ kur saaka mɔk biite, a Yennu-n tur binba teen Yisa Masia yada na piinii nba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa turib na; bi yada maŋ paake ki u sa turib.
23Sennu na din tuu diat kaman nan dansarii nee ki tan baar yoo nba ki Yennu fiit yada teenu labaar ki wannit na. 24Li paak, sennu na din ŋman ti yomdaanɔ nba diat mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Masia tan baar a tin teenɔ yada ki Yennu-n yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor nee. 25Ŋaan mɔtanae ti teen Masia yada; li paak, sennu ji ki tee ti yomdaanɔ.
26Ya teen Yisa Masia yada ki be u ni na, ŋann paake ki i tee Yennu waas. 27Yimm nba ki bi din wuri Yennu nyunwuru Masia sann ni na, yimme gaar u mɔŋ mɔŋ binbeŋ. 28Li paak, bɔkitu ji ki be binba tee Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb sinsuuk ni, li bia ki be binba tee daaba nan yentinmɔŋ damm sinsuuk ni, li ki be poob nan jab sinsuuk ni; ya be Yisa Masia ni na paake te ki i kura tee yenɔkɔɔ. 29Ki i-i tee Masia yabie, ŋann i tee Abraham yaaboonae, ki tan sa gaar piinii nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn a u sa turib na.

english_text:
1Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs. 2This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set. 3This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
4But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law, 5 so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children. 6 Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father. 7You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised.
Paul's Concern for the Galatians
8Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real. 9But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers? 10You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years. 11I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
12My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me 13when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick. 14My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself. 15Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me. 16Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
17Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention. 18It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you. 19My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you. 20I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled.
Hagar and Sarah
21Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says? 22 In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free. 23The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
24All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave. 25Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
26 But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave. 27 The Scriptures say about her,
“You have never had children,
but now you can be glad.
You have never given birth,
but now you can shout.
Once you had no children,
but now you will have
more children than a woman
who has been married
for a long time.”
28My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was. 29 But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today. 30 The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.” 31My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
translation_text:
1Tɔn, n bia ŋamm yeen a ŋaak bik nba lek tuu ya wun tan di u baa faar na, ŋaan yoo nba ki u daa tee bonbunn na, bi daa tuu diau kaman nan daabir nee. 2Yoo nba ki u tee bonbunn na, nisiabe tuu diau ki wubiɔ mɔkmɔk nan wa tan sa beer jɔɔ, ki jaŋ yoo nba ki u baa din senn na. 3Ti mun tee nnee; yoo nba ki ti din tee kaman nan bonbunna Yennu sɔnu ni, dunia nna seek paŋe din tuu diat. 4Ŋaan yoo nba din tan jaŋ na, ki Yennu tun u mɔŋ mɔŋ Bija, ki poo din marɔ, ki u din be Juu teeb sennu ni, 5a wun tinn binba din tuu be sennu maŋ ni na, a tin kpant Yennu waas.
6Ta tee u waas na paak, ki u tun u Bija maŋ Seyeeŋ na ki u be ti para ni, ki ti yi ŋɔɔ Yennu a “N Baa, n Baa.” 7Li paak, a ji ki tee daabir kaa, ŋaan a ji tee Yennu bike; ki fa tee u bik na paak, u mun sa tura linba kur ki u mɔk ki teen u waas.
Pɔɔl baka nba be Galasia teeb ni biaŋinba
8Sianyoo i din tuu ki mi Yennu, li yoo i din tuu tee daaba ki tur linba set ki tee Yennue, 9ŋaan mɔtana i bann Yennuwa, ki Yennu mun mi-i, li jin teen nlee ki i loon ki i ŋmat tunn ki wei bonsiar nba ki mɔk yaak nba sa sommi na boor ni? Bee teen ki i jin loon ki i bia kpant bi daaba? 10I mantik ŋaan ki i baka be dagannkaa ni, nan ŋmaagannkaa ni, nan sakgannkaa ni, nan bingannkaa ni bonchiann. 11Ŋanne te ki n mɔk jijeet i paak nan ma tun linba kur turi na biar yanne na.
12N naa waas, n barimiie, yii naan kaman nan min na man, kimaan n mɔŋ mun ji naan kaman nan yimm nee. I ki tun siar biirimi. 13Ma din yiar na paake din te ki n mɔɔnt barŋanii sinsinn ki turi. 14Li yoo maŋ ki n gbanant din ki maŋi, ŋaan i lek din ki yêtimi; i lek din gaarin kaman nan ya din bo sa gaar Yennu malaka biaŋinba nee, ki bia gaarin kaman nan ya din bo sa gaar Yisa Masia biaŋinba na. 15I din mɔk parpeenn; ki bee te ki i ji ki mɔki? N mɔŋ sa yet nan li-i diŋŋi bo-i sa fit, i din bo sa sak ki nyinn i mɔŋ ninbina ki turima; 16ki mɔtana, ma beeri barmɔnii na, n ji kpant i dataakee?
17Nisiab bikii a bi mɔk loomm bonchiann i paak, ŋaan bi dudukit ki ŋani. Bi lorin a bin bɔkit min nan yimme, ki yin tunn kii waa bi kuukɔɔ. 18Nirɔ baka-i be sɔnŋanu ni yoo saaka, li ŋan, mi-i lekii be i boor ni amii mi-i kaa. 19N walonkai, li yiarin bonchiann i paak mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Masia binbeŋ na tan sa gbee i ni. 20N yan ni ŋmat i paake. N bo summan ki n be i boor ni mɔtana ki lek ŋamm jii n binbeŋ nba sɔ ki turi.
Bibiiuk nba jiin Sara nan Hagar po
21Yimm nba loon ki i be sennu ni na, i ki gbat sennu maŋ nba yet biaŋinba-aa? 22a Abraham din mɔk bonjai bannlee; ki bi yenɔ naa din tee daabire, ki lɔɔ naa mun din tee pookuur poo. 23Ki daabir na din mar u bonjabik kaman nan niib nba maar biaŋinba nee, ŋaan ki pookuur poo na mar u bonjabik kaman nan biaŋinba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn turɔ na. 24Bi poob bannlee na see Yennu mɔlora ŋannlee na setu nee. Daabir maŋ sann din tee Hagar; ki u see mɔlor nba ki Yennu senn Sainai jɔɔr paak na setu ni, ki din mar u bik daabsin ni, 25kimaan Hagar see Sainai jɔɔr nba be Arabia tiŋ ni setu nee, ki ŋman nan tingbouŋ na ni Jerusalem na, ki ŋɔɔ nan u waas tee daaba, 26ŋaan sanpaapo Jerusalem na tee yentinmɔŋ doo, ki bia tee ti naa; 27kimaan li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a
“Yimm pookanmata, yii mɔk parpeenn!
Yimm nba ki mi matyiaru tonu na
yii yiin parpeenn yanii man!
Kimaan poo nba be u kuukɔɔ
tan sii mɔk waas bonchiann
ki gar poo nba sɔrɔ daa ki mi gɔrin nyikɔ.”
28Tɔn, n naa waas, timme mun tee Yennu mɔsonn waas kaman nan Aisak nba din tee biaŋinba na. 29Ba din mar bonjabik nba kaman nan niib nba maar biaŋinba na din tuu mukis wunba ki bi din marɔ nan Seyeeŋ yikoo ni, ki li mun bia lek tee nnee nan mɔtanawa. 30Ŋaan li sɔb nlee Yennu gbouŋ ni? Li sɔb a “Berin poo nba tee daabir na nan u bonjabik na, kimaan daabir bonjabik na daa mi lakin di bi baa maŋ faar nan pookuur poo bonjabik na.” 31N naa waas, ma yeen linba tee a ti ki tee poo nba tee daabir na waas kaa, ŋaan ti tee pookuur poo na waase.

english_text:
Christ Gives Freedom
1Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
2I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised. 3If you do, you must obey the whole Law. 4And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace. 5But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ. 6If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
7You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth. 8And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you. 9 A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough, 10but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
11My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised. 12I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
13My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love. 14 All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself. 15But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves.
God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
16If you are guided by the Spirit, you won't obey your selfish desires. 17 The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should. 18But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
19People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds. 20They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are 21envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
22God's Spirit makes us loving, happy, peaceful, patient, kind, good, faithful, 23gentle, and self-controlled. There is no law against behaving in any of these ways. 24And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires. 25God's Spirit has given us life, and so we should follow the Spirit. 26But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
translation_text:
Yentinmɔŋ dianu po
1Yisa Masia poŋ fat timma, ki ti be ti yama nie. Li paak, i daa te ki siar ji dia-i ki i tee li daabir.
2Min Pɔɔl mɔŋ mɔŋ beeri a li-i tee ki i sak ki dia punkokitu sennu na, li want nan Masia ki mɔk nyɔsiat turiini. 3N ŋammit beer nifosɔɔ nba waa punkokitu sɔnu nee, nan li kpaa talase ki wun wei sennu kura. 4Yimm nba yabii a Yennu-n yet a i tee niŋamm u boor sennu weiu paak na, i poŋ bɔkit i mɔŋ nan Masia, ki bia nyinn i mɔŋ Yennu ninbatinu ni. 5Ŋaan timm teen Masia yada, ki u Seyeeŋ mun be ti ni; li paak, ti mɔk dindann ki guu yoo nba ki Yennu sa yet a ti tee niŋamm u boor. 6Kimaan ti-i be Yisa Masia ni, ti-i lekii pot ti puna amii ti-i kii poti, li ki kpaa talasi. Ti-i teen Yisa Masia yada ki loon leeb, ŋanne tee linba kpaa talas.
7Ŋaan i din tuun fanu na, ki ŋmee tan te ki i nyik barmɔnii saku? 8Yennu nba ganni na kaa tun linba na. 9Bi tuu yeen a “Datiŋ waaminnee te ki boroborkunn fikitir piir.” 10Ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ paak, n bia mɔk yada a i sii mɔk dudukyenn nanin; ki li-i tee nirɔ nbae ŋmat i dudukit, Yennu sa dat daanɔ maŋ tubira.
11N naa waas, mi-i bia lekii mɔɔntir a niib-ii porii bi puna gbaa, nlee ki bi bia sii mukisimi? Li-i bo-i tee barmɔniie ki n mɔɔntir punpotu po nan ma mɔɔntir dapunpunn po na, ŋann li bo kii mɔk muksuku. 12Min bo summan ki binba ŋmat i dudukit na-n nyik punpotue, ŋaan tɔɔ bi mɔŋ lana.
13N naa waas, Yennu yiini yentinmɔŋ nie, ŋaan i daa te ki i yentinmɔŋ maŋ tan te ki i niauŋ-n tan mann nyanniini. Ŋaan yimm-n jii loomm ki somm i leeb man. 14Kimaan sennu maŋ yur yet a “I loon a lɔɔ kaman nan fa loon a mɔŋ biaŋinba na.” 15Ŋaan li-i tee ki i tee i leeb danii ki biir i leeb kaman nan muuk ni bonkobit na, i set sa baan i leeb yann na.
Yennu Seyeeŋ nan nisaarik binbeŋ labaar
16Ŋaan n beerie, ŋaant ki Seyeeŋ-ii dia i binbeŋ man, ki daa te ki i niauŋ tuun nisaarik loommu. 17Kimaan nisaarik loomm nan Seyeeŋ maŋ loomm ki waa leebi. Loomm-nba taar munnlee na tee dataie nan leeb i ni; li paak, i kan fit tun ya loon linba fanu. 18Li-i tee ki i saak ki Yennu Seyeeŋ ŋmakitiri sɔnu, i ji ki be sennu maŋ ni.
19Nisaarik binbeŋ nba want li mɔŋ linba ni tee bonchonchonn toonae, nan jakint toona, nan lanmiok toona, 20nan patmannu, nan nyɔkdinu, nan dataar, nan kunkɔna, nan funfunbiiuk, nan wutoor, nan bɔkitu, nan lukitin, 21nan punponn, nan dayibu, nan bandakir, ki bia jii bonlia nba naan nna ki pukin; n ji beeri kaman nan ma poŋ beti biaŋinba na, binba tuun linba na kan mi di Yennu naami.
22Ŋaan Yennu Seyeeŋe te ki nirɔ mɔk loomm, nan parpeenn, nan parmaasir, nan sukuru, nan burchint, nan ŋamm, nan yada, 23nan sikininmɔŋ, nan mɔŋdianu. Sennsau ji kaa ki sa biir wunba diari. 24Ki Yisa Masia nba yen binba na ŋanin poŋ din kpii bi nisaarik binbeŋ nan li loonbiit kura. 25Yennu Seyeeŋ nba turit manfoor na, ŋɔɔe ki li ŋan tii waau. 26Li ki ŋan ki tii mɔk karinbaanii, amii tii fiint ti leeb wutoa, amii tii mɔk funfunbiit nan ti leebi.

english_text:
Help Each Other
1My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself. 2You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
3If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong. 4Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others. 5We each must carry our own load.
6Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
7You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant. 8If you follow your selfish desires, you will harvest destruction, but if you follow the Spirit, you will harvest eternal life. 9Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up. 10We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord.
Final Warnings
11You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
12Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ. 13They are circumcised, but they don't obey the Law of Moses. All they want is to brag about having you circumcised. 14But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
15It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
16If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
17 On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
18My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
translation_text:
Jikitir i leeb jikpiata man
1N naa waas, li-i tee ki i la ki sɔɔ tun toonbiit, yimm nba be nan Yennu Seyeeŋ na, li ŋan ki yin sommɔ nan loomm sɔnii kur ni, ki wun ŋamm set fanu, kii mi ki bikinu sa te ki i mun bot. 2Yi jii i leeb jikpiata man, ki sak Masia sennu. 3Sɔɔ-i kii tee siar, ŋaan bikii a u mun tee sɔɔe, u kpann u mɔŋe. 4Li ŋan ki sɔɔ kur-n bikin u mɔŋ binbeŋa, ki fit kii mɔk yuugontir u mɔŋ mɔŋ toonŋana paak, ki daa bikii wa tun linba nan u lɔɔ toona, 5kimaan li kpaa talase nan sɔɔ kur-n tun u mɔŋ toona.
6Nisɔɔ nba ki bi wantɔ Yisa Masia barŋanii na, li ŋan ki li yɔɔ-n mun jii linba ki u mɔk kur ki chent nan u wanntɔɔ maŋ.
7I daa kpann i mɔŋ mani; sɔɔ kan fit kpann Yennu; nisaarik nba bur linba, ŋanne ki u tan sa jaan kpaŋ. 8Wunba bur u mɔŋ gbanant loomm, u tan sa jaan kuume u gbanant loonbiit maŋ paak; ŋaan wunba bur Seyeeŋ nba loon biaŋinba na, u tan sa jaan manfoor nba kaa gbennu, Seyeeŋ maŋ paak. 9Li paak, ti daa bakit nan toonŋana tumu mani, kimaan ti-i kii nyik toonŋana, yoo nba jaŋ na ki ti tan sa gaar li nyɔɔt. 10Li paak, ti-i la yaak, ŋaant ki tin tun linba ŋan ki tur sɔɔ kura; ŋaan mantik kii tuun kii teen binba teen Masia yada ki tee u weira na.
Kpaanjoontik nan foontii labaar
11Tɔn, i la ma sɔb sɔbin gbeŋŋa na nan n mɔŋ mɔŋ nuu. 12Niib nba loon bin penn Juu teeb para na, ŋamme mukisi a yin pot i puna, a Juu teeb maŋ daa dintib fara Masia dapunpunn na paaki. 13Binba porii bi puna na mɔŋ ki waa Yennu sennu, ŋaan bi loon ki yin pote ki bii faa japaat i punpotu maŋ paak; 14ŋaan min ŋanin kan mi faa japaat see ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia dapunpunn na kuukɔɔ paak; kimaan u dapunpunn maŋ paake te ki n kpii tingbouŋ na niauŋ n gbanant ni, ki tingbouŋ na niauŋ mun nyii n gbanant ni. 15Bɔkitu kaa wunba pot u punn nan wunba ki pot u punn sinsuuk ni. Linba ji kpaa talas tee binbenpaaŋ nba ki Yennu turit nee. 16Parmaasir nan ninbatinu-ii be binba kur waa wannu nba na paak; bi tee Yennu niibe.
17I mi man ki sɔɔ-n ji turin farsiar, kimaan n poŋ mɔk fara dɔkit n gbanant paak, ki li want nan n tee Yisa daabire.
18N naa waas, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, i para ni. Mɔmɔmm.

english_text:
1 From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus.
To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
2I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
3Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven! 4Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people. 5God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children. 6God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
7-8 Christ sacrificed his life's blood to set us free, which means our sins are now forgiven. Christ did this because of God's gift of undeserved grace to us. God has great wisdom and understanding, 9and by what Christ has done, God has shown us his own mysterious ways. 10Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
11God always does what he plans, and this is why he appointed Christ to choose us. 12He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him. 13Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God. 14The Spirit also makes us sure we will be given what God has stored up for his people. Then we will be set free, and God will be honored and praised.
Paul's Prayer
15I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people. 16So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers. 17I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God. 18My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
19I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used 20 when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven. 21There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well. 22 God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything. 23The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
translation_text:
1Min Pɔɔl wunba ki Yennu gannin a n teen Yisa Masia toomii na foont Yisa Masiaweira nba teenɔ yada ki be Efesus doo ni.
2Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir.
Yennu Seyeeŋ nba sommitit Masia paak biaŋinba
3Ŋaant ki ti tur Yennu wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na niipoouk man, kimaan ta be Yisa Masia ni na paak ki ŋɔɔ Yennu turit Seyeeŋ piinii kur nba be sanpaapo. 4Yennu nba poŋ daa din ki nan tingbouŋ yoonba nawa ki u din gannit a tii tee u niib Yisa Masia paak, kii tee popeendamm ki daa mɔk biit u boor ni.
Kimaan Yennu loonit, 5li paak, u loomm nan u lora ni u poŋ din dukinewa a Yisa Masia nie ki u sa teenit u waas. 6Ŋaant ki tin pak Yennu kimaan u ninbatinu nba yab bonchiann biaŋinba na, nan wa tint ninbaauk u Bilonkak na paak. 7Kimaan Yennu nyik chab ti biit ki nyinnit daabsin ni u Bilonkak maŋ sɔn paake. 8Gotir, Yennu ninbatinu nba be ti paak ki ki mɔk bikinu na.
Yennu nba mɔk yanŋann nan bannu bonchiann biaŋinba na ki u din tun wa poŋ dukin biaŋinba 9ki fiit wannit woonin ni lora nba ki u din senn a wun tan tun Masia ni na. 10Yoo maŋ-i taŋŋi jaŋ chib, ki u ji sa tun gbenn u lora maŋ, ki lakin u naamii kur sanpaapo nan tiŋ ni ki Masia-ii tee yudaanɔ.
11Yennu tun siar kur u dudukit nan u lora nee, ki gannit a tii tee u niib, kaman nan u bibikit nba din tee biaŋinba pinpiik niwa na. 12Timm nba dɔŋ mɔk dindann Masia ni na, tii piak Yennu kii dontir u sann man.
13Ŋaan yimme mun din gbat tinnu barŋanii nba tee barmɔnii maŋ ki teen Yisa Masia niib. Ya teen Masia yada na paak ki Yennu jii u Seyeeŋ nba tee u yent dɔk na turi nan wa din lor biaŋinba na. 14Ŋanne tee siara nan ti tan sa la ti yiar Yennu boor, ki bia want nan u sa tur u niib yentinmɔŋ nan barmɔnii. Tii pakɔ kii dontir u sann man.
Pɔɔl Yenmiaru labaar
15Li paak, yoo nba ki n din gbat ya teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa yada na nan ya loon Yennu niib biaŋinba na po mɔkmɔk nan dinna, 16n teen Yennu niipoouk i paak ki ki nyiki. N tuu tian i poe n miaru ni, 17ki lek mei ti Baa Yennu wunba tee paŋ daanɔ ki bia tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Yennu, a wun turi Seyeeŋ nba sa turi yan ki fiit Yennu wanni kii ŋamm bannɔ. 18N bia meiɔ ma wun yent i nummpo ki yin bann dindann nba paak ki u yiini na, ki bia bann nan bona nba ki u tan sa tur u niib nae ŋan bonchiann, 19ki bia bann wa tuun toonjaana nba teen timm nba teenɔ yada na. 20U panchiauŋ toonbooru nba ki u din jii ki fiin Masia kuun ni ki kaanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni u niidiitu po nee, ki u bia tuun ti ni na. 21Masiae yen sanpaapo yent damm, nan yikoo damm, nan paŋ damm, nan yomdamm kur; ŋɔɔe mɔk sann nba gar yikoo kur sana nba be dunia na ni nan dunia nba baat na ni. 22Yennu jii bonsiar kura yaak ki teen Masia nuu nee. U jii ŋɔɔe a wuu tee Yennu nigannkab kura Yomdaanɔ. 23U nigannkab maŋe tee u gbanant. Ŋamme gbee u gbanant maŋ ni, ki u mun jii piinii kur ki gbeen bi ni.

english_text:
From Death to Life
1 In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God. 2You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God. 3Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
4-5But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you. 6God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven. 7God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done. 8You were saved by faith in God, who treats us much better than we deserve. This is God's gift to you, and not anything you have done on your own. 9It isn't something you have earned, so there is nothing you can brag about. 10God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are.
United by Christ
11Don't forget that you are Gentiles. In fact, you used to be called “uncircumcised” by those who take pride in being circumcised. 12At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God, 13and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
14Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body 15 to destroy the Law of Moses with all its rules and commands. He even brought Jews and Gentiles together as though we were only one person, when he united us in peace. 16 On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body. 17 Christ came and preached peace to you Gentiles, who were far from God, and peace to us Jews, who were near God. 18And because of Christ, all of us can come to the Father by the same Spirit.
19You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God. 20You are like a building with the apostles and prophets as the foundation and with Christ as the most important stone. 21Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord. 22And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
translation_text:
Kuun nan manfoor labaar
1Yoo nba din gar na, i mɔyêtuk nan biit paak, li want nan i kpoe. 2I din waa tingbouŋ na narinbiit sɔnue ki sak tingbouŋ na bat Sintaanii nba dia niib nba ki saak Yennu mɔb na. 3Barmɔnii, ti kura mun din naan kaman nan li nibooru nee, ki waa ti gbanant niauŋ, ki tuun ti gbanant nan ti dudukit loomm. Timm nan ŋamm toona din baat nan Yennu wutoore ti kura paak.
4Ŋaan Yennu loomm nan u ninbatinu yab bonchiann ki kaa bikinu, 5li paak, ta din yêt Yennu mɔb ki li want nan ti kpoe na yoonba, li yoo na, ki u din nyinnit kuun ni, ki kɔɔnit manfoor ni Masia paak; Yennu ninbatinu paak ki i tinn na. 6Ta be Yisa Masia ni na paak ki Yennu din lakin fiin timm nan ŋɔɔ, ki ti lakin dii naan nanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni. 7U din tun linba na a wun wann sɔɔ kura u ninbatinu nba yab biaŋinbae, ki tun burchint turit Yisa Masia paak. 8Yennu ninbatinu na paake ki i tinn, kimaan i teen Masia yada. 9Sɔɔ kan fit nyu bara li paaki, kimaan li ki tee u tiauŋ toona nyɔɔt kaa; li tee Yennu piiniie. 10Yisa Masia nie ki Yennu teent ta tee binba, ki turit toonŋana binbeŋ a tii tuun linba ki u din poŋ lor a ti tun na.
Masia niib kur tee yomme u ni
11Yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na, Juu teeb yi yimm ŋanin punjoote, ŋaan yi bi mɔŋ sankpanna; yi tian man ya din tee nibooru nba sianyoo nba gar na. 12I din ki mi Masia li yoo. I din tee saamme ki bi ki kaani ki pukinti Yennu nigannkab ni. I din ki mi Yennu tingbouŋ na ni, ki bia din ki mɔk dindann, kimaan wa din tur u niib mɔsona nba i din ki pukin li ni; 13ŋaan mɔtana i ji be Yisa Masia nie, li paak yimm nba din tuu be banfɔkira maŋ ji nyat nakinɔɔe u kuun paak, 14kimaan ŋɔɔe taan Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb ki teemm niyenɔkɔɔ, ki parmaasir baar; u gbanant paak ki u biir dataar nba din tuu puun bi sinsuuk ni na. 15U din kpeen Juu teeb sennu nan li kɔɔt na kura a wun te parmaasir-n baar nibooru munnlee maŋ sinsuuk ni, ki bii be u ni ki taan kii tee nipaamm yenɔkɔɔ. 16U dapunpunn paak kuun maŋ te ki u biir dataar maŋ bi sinsuuk ni, ki jiib ki taamm booru yenɔkɔɔ ki jen namm Yennu boor ni. 17Li paak, Masia din baar mɔɔnt parmaasir barŋanii mɔɔntii ki tur yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb ki din tuu fɔk nan Yennu na, ki mun bia baar nann ki tur Juu teeb nba din tuu kpia nanɔ na. 18Masia paake ki Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb na kura fit baar ti Baa Yennu maŋ boor ni, u Seyeeŋ yenɔkɔɔ ni.
19Mɔtana, yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb nan Yennu niib tee tiŋ yeŋkɔɔ waase, ki ji tee Yennu ŋaateeb; i ji ki tee saamm kaa. 20Fiakir yennkɔɔ nba ki sɔkiniinba nan toomiinba din fɔkin na paake ki i mun tɔɔnn ki Yisa Masia mɔŋ tee li tanjaann. 21Ŋɔɔe dia Yennu niib maŋ ki bi be fanu ki kpaatir ti Yomdaanɔ boor ni. 22Yimm nan ŋɔɔ nba taan nan leeb na paak, ki yimm nan ŋamm mun bia taan teen diiuk nba tur Yennu ki u Seyeeŋ be i ni.

english_text:
Paul's Mission to the Gentiles
1Christ Jesus made me his prisoner, so I could help you Gentiles. 2You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you. 3In fact, this letter tells you a little about how God has shown me his mysterious ways. 4 As you read the letter, you will also find out how well I really do understand the mystery about Christ. 5No one knew about this mystery until God's Spirit told it to his holy apostles and prophets. 6And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
7God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news. 8I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured. 9 God, who created everything, wanted me to help everyone understand the mysterious plan that had always been hidden in his mind. 10Then God would use the church to show the powers and authorities in the spiritual world that he has many different kinds of wisdom.
11God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done. 12Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith. 13This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you.
Christ's Love for Us
14I kneel in prayer to the Father. 15All beings in heaven and on earth receive their life from him. 16God is wonderful and glorious. I pray that his Spirit will make you become strong followers 17and that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. Stand firm and be deeply rooted in his love. 18 I pray that you and all God's people will understand what is called wide or long or high or deep. 19I want you to know all about Christ's love, although it is too wonderful to be measured. Then your lives will be filled with all that God is.
20-21I pray that Christ Jesus and the church will forever bring praise to God. His power at work in us can do far more than we dare ask or imagine. Amen.
translation_text:
Pɔɔl nba tuun ki teen binba ki tee Juu teeb
1Linba na paake te ki min Pɔɔl wunba kɔɔ dansarik Yisa Masia toona paak na, n lek mei Yennu yimm nba ki tee Juu teeb na paak. 2N ki biris nan i poŋ gbat nan Yennu turin toona na i sommir paaki. 3U din fiit u woonin ni lor na ki wannima. 4(I-i karin ma din sɔb linba waaminna na, i sa bann nan n set mi siar ki jiin Masia po.) 5Mɔtana Yennu ji ŋaan ki u Seyeeŋ fiit u woonin ni lor na ki wann u toomiinbaŋamm nan u sɔkiniinba, ŋaan li din ki tee nna sianyoo ni. 6U woonin ni lor maŋ tee nee: barŋanii maŋ paak ki binba ki tee Juu teeb na ji fit taan la yiar Yendɔuŋ ni nan Juu teeb; ŋaan bia taan namm ki tee gbananyennkɔɔ ki fit mɔk namm wa din senn mɔsonn nba Yisa Masia ni na.
7Yennu ninbatinu paak ki u din turin yaak a n teen u toomii kii mɔɔntir u barŋanii wa turin paŋ biaŋinba na. 8Yennu niib sinsuuk ni, mine tan joont wunba ki be kann ni, ŋaan Yennu tur mine yaak a man jii barŋanii mɔɔntii nba wantit Masia nba sii teent binba ki tee Juu teeb na bonŋana bonchiann biaŋinba, 9ki bia te ki niib kur nunii-n yent ki bin bann biaŋinba ki Yennu woonin ni lor maŋ tuun gbeent Masia ni. Yaayoo niewa ki Yennu, wunba din nan bonsiar kur, din diar woonin ni 10a Masia niib paak malakanba yent damm nan Yendɔuŋ ni yikoo damm-n tan tumii mɔtana u subinbooru booru. 11Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nie ki u din tun linba na kaman nan wa din lor biaŋinba na. 12Ta teen Masia yada ki be u ni na, li paak, ki ti mɔk yuugontir ki sa baar Yennu boor nan parcheenn. 13Li paak, n barimiie a i daa te ki i para-n biir nan ma di fara i paak na, kimaan li kur tee i nyɔɔt paake.
Masia loomm labaar
14Linba na paak, ki n gbaa n duna paak ti Baa Yennu tɔɔnn po, 15wunba yaa boor ni ki Yendɔuŋ ni nan tingbouŋ na ni nibooru kur gaan bi yejamm maaru ni sana. 16Yennu paŋ nba yab bonchiann biaŋinba na, n meiɔ a wun turi paŋ maŋ u Seyeeŋ paak, ki paŋ maŋ-ii be i ni, 17n bia meiɔ a Masia-n jii i para ki teen u binbeboor i yada paak. N meiɔ a wun te ki i fit set chib, ki baa jiina ki fɔkin fiakir, ki loomm na-ii paar, 18ki yimm nan Yennu niib kur-n fit bann Masia loomm yarimu, nan li fɔkint, nan li sumiiu nba jan biaŋinba, 19ki fit bann Masia loomm nba gar siar kur, ki i benu-ii tee kaman nan Yennu benu na.
20Yennu paŋ nba tuun ti ni na paak, ki u sa fit tun bonsiar kur ki turit ki gar ta dukii linba po ki meiɔ; 21ŋɔɔ Yennu tan sa la nibaakir Yisa Masia nan u weira paak, yoo kur nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

english_text:
Unity with Christ
1As a prisoner of the Lord, I beg you to live in a way that is worthy of the people God has chosen to be his own. 2 Always be humble and gentle. Patiently put up with each other and love each other. 3Try your best to let God's Spirit keep your hearts united. Do this by living at peace. 4All of you are part of the same body. There is only one Spirit of God, just as you were given one hope when you were chosen to be God's people. 5We have only one Lord, one faith, and one baptism. 6There is one God who is the Father of all people. Not only is God above all others, but he works by using all of us, and he lives in all of us.
7Christ has generously divided out his gifts to us. 8 As the Scriptures say,
“When he went up
to the highest place,
he led away many prisoners
and gave gifts to people.”
9When it says, “he went up,” it means that Christ had been deep in the earth. 10This also means that the one who went deep into the earth is the same one who went into the highest heaven, so he would fill the whole universe.
11Christ chose some of us to be apostles, prophets, missionaries, pastors, and teachers, 12so his people would learn to serve and his body would grow strong. 13This will continue until we are united by our faith and by our understanding of the Son of God. Then we will be mature, just as Christ is, and we will be completely like him.
14We must stop acting like children. We must not let deceitful people trick us by their false teachings, which are like winds that toss us around from place to place. 15Love should always make us tell the truth. Then we will grow in every way and be more like Christ, the head 16 of the body. Christ holds it together and makes all of its parts work perfectly, as it grows and becomes strong because of love.
The Old Life and the New Life
17As a follower of the Lord, I order you to stop living like stupid, godless people. 18Their minds are in the dark, and they are stubborn and ignorant and have missed out on the life that comes from God. They no longer have any feelings about what is right, 19and they are so greedy they do all kinds of indecent things.
20-21But this isn't what you were taught about Jesus Christ. He is the truth, and you heard about him and learned about him. 22 You were told that your foolish desires will destroy you and that you must give up your old way of life with all its bad habits. 23Let the Spirit change your way of thinking 24 and make you into a new person. You were created to be like God, and so you must please him and be truly holy.
Rules for the New Life
25 We are part of the same body. Stop lying and start telling each other the truth. 26 Don't get so angry that you sin. Don't go to bed angry 27and don't give the devil a chance.
28If you are a thief, quit stealing. Be honest and work hard, so you will have something to give to people in need.
29Stop all your dirty talk. Say the right thing at the right time and help others by what you say.
30Don't make God's Spirit sad. The Spirit makes you sure that someday you will be free from your sins.
31Stop being bitter and angry and mad at others. Don't yell at one another or curse each other or ever be rude. 32 Instead, be kind and merciful, and forgive others, just as God forgave you because of Christ.
translation_text:
Gbananyennkɔɔ labaar
1Min nba kɔɔ dansarik ti Yomdaanɔ toona paak na, n kpaaniie a yii ŋaan ki i binbeŋ-ii tee fanu kaman nan Yennu nba loon ki lii tee biaŋinba na. 2Yii sikint i mɔŋ, kii be soonna nan sukuru, kii mɔk yandiŋir nan i leeb, nan loomm. 3Yennu Seyeeŋ nba din turi mɔtaauk nba na, yi yabir i mɔŋ kii be nan parmaasir. 4-5Masia niib tee gbananyenn, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ yenɔkɔɔe be, ki Yennu tinni ki turi dindann yennkɔɔ. Yomdaan-yenɔkɔɔe be, ki ti taan teenɔ yada, ki Yennu nyunwuru mun tee yennkɔɔ. 6Yennu tee yenɔkɔɔe ki tee nisaarii kura Baa, ki bia tee sɔɔ kura Yomdaanɔ nba tuun siar kura ni, ŋaan bia be siar kura ni.
7Masia nba turit linba na paak, ti kura gaar u piingaŋire, 8kaman nan la poŋ sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni biaŋinba ki yet a
“Wa din doo sanpaapo yoonba
ki u nyinn yommii ki ŋaab yann,
ŋaan turib piinii.”
9Tɔn, “U din doo sanpaapo” paak tee bee? Li want nan sinsinn u din sik tingbouŋ na nie. 10Ŋɔɔ nba din sik tingbouŋ na ni maŋe bia din doo sanpanagbeŋ na. 11Ŋɔɔe tur nisaarik piinii, ki gann nisiab ki teemm toomiinba, ki leeb mun tee sɔkiniinba, ki yemm mun bia teen barŋanimɔɔnt teeb, ki siab mun lek tee Masia niguuteeb nan wannteeb. 12U tun linba na a binba tee Yennu niib kura-n fit jaŋ u toona maŋe, ki chee Masiaweira. 13Li paak, ti sa taan teen Yennu Bija yada ki bannɔ, kii tee niib nba yaa binbeŋ ŋan ki jaŋ nan ŋɔɔ Masia yar na. 14Ti ji kii tee waas kaa; nifobiit nan kpinkpannii damm ji kii diat laŋ lin kaman nan ba poŋ teen siab biaŋinba na. 15Ŋaan ti sii piak barmɔniie nan loomm kii nɔb kii pukii, ki Masiae tee ti yudaanɔ. 16Ŋɔɔe te ki gbanant bɔka kur dia bi leeb, ki gbanant maŋ tukita dia nan leeb; li kura-i bo-i tuun nan leeb chib chib, gbanant maŋ bo sii nɔbee nan leeb.
Ti binbenpaaŋ labaar
17Ti Yomdaanɔ sann paak, n kpaaniie, i ji daa be kaman nan binba ki mi Yennu, ki bi dudukit be bunbɔnn ni, ki li ki mɔk nyɔɔt biaŋinba na. 18Bi tee tubkangbatae ki ki bant siari, li paak, bi ki pukin Yennu nba tur manfopaann nba na ni. 19Bi ji ki tiin fei, ki lin tuun jakint toona, ki ki dia bi mɔŋ nan waaminnawa.
20Ŋaan yimm din ki tumii ŋann kaa Masia boor ni. 21I din gbat u po ki tee u weirae, ki bi bia wanni barmɔnii nba be u niwa. 22Li paak, yabit i mɔŋ man ki nyann niauŋ nba be i binbekperik nba na ni. 23Ŋaant ki i dudukit nan i para-n ŋamm ki pant, 24kii mɔk binbenpaaŋ nba ki Yennu turi na, kii naan Yennu, kii be fanu, kii tee popeendamm nan barmɔnii.
25Li paak, ŋaant ki ti nyik faak, ŋaan sɔɔ kur-ii piak barmɔnii kii teen u lɔɔ, kimaan ti tee niyenɔkɔɔe Masia ni. 26Li-i tee ki a donn wutoor ŋaan fa daa tuun toonbiiti, ki bia daa diar a par ni ki yennu baa, 27ki daa teen Sintaanii yaaki. 28Wunba tuu jaan u leeb bont na wun ji nyik jamu, ŋaan yabir tun toona ki fit tur tatimm linba ki bi loon. 29I daa piak mɔbonbiit ki teen i leebi, ŋaan te ki i mɔbona-ii tee linba kpaa talas ki mɔk nyɔɔt, ki i leeb-i gbat, lin pukimm binbeŋ nba fan. 30I daa te ki Seyeeŋ nba tee Yennu yent dɔk i paak na-n la parbiiri. Ŋɔɔe tee dɔk nba want nan Daar baat na ki Yennu tan sa turi yentinmɔŋ nan barmɔnii. 31Yi nyik tonu binbeŋ kur, nan wutoor. Sɔɔ ji daa mɔk kunkɔnchiauŋ nan u lɔɔ, sɔɔ daa sukii u lɔɔ, ki bia nyik naŋ dudukit kura i ni. 32Yii tee burchimm nan ninkpabauŋ damm man i leeb boor. Yii nyikit leeb biit kii chabitib kaman nan Yennu nba nyikit i yara ki chabiti biaŋinba Masia paak na.

english_text:
1Do as God does. After all, you are his dear children. 2 Let love be your guide. Christ loved us and offered his life for us as a sacrifice that pleases God.
3You are God's people, so don't let it be said that any of you are immoral or indecent or greedy. 4Don't use dirty or foolish or filthy words. Instead, say how thankful you are. 5Being greedy, indecent, or immoral is just another way of worshiping idols. You can be sure people who behave in this way will never be part of the kingdom that belongs to Christ and to God.
Living as People of Light
6Don't let anyone trick you with foolish talk. God punishes everyone who disobeys him and says foolish things. 7So don't have anything to do with anyone like that.
8You used to be like people living in the dark, but now you are people of the light because you belong to the Lord. So act like people of the light 9and make your light shine. Be good and honest and truthful, 10as you try to please the Lord. 11Don't take part in doing those worthless things that are done in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are. 12It is disgusting even to talk about what is done in the dark. 13But the light will show what these things are really like. 14Light shows up everything, just as the Scriptures say,
“Wake up from your sleep
and rise from death.
Then Christ will shine on you.”
15Act like people with good sense and not like fools. 16 These are evil times, so make every minute count. 17Don't be stupid. Instead, find out what the Lord wants you to do. 18Don't destroy yourself by getting drunk, but let the Spirit fill your life. 19 When you meet together, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, as you praise the Lord with all your heart. 20Always use the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to thank God the Father for everything.
Wives and Husbands
21Honor Christ and put others first. 22 A wife should put her husband first, as she does the Lord. 23A husband is the head of his wife, as Christ is the head and the Savior of the church, which is his own body. 24Wives should always put their husbands first, as the church puts Christ first.
25 A husband should love his wife as much as Christ loved the church and gave his life for it. 26He made the church holy by the power of his word, and he made it pure by washing it with water. 27Christ did this, so he would have a glorious and holy church, without faults or spots or wrinkles or any other flaws.
28In the same way, a husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself. A husband who loves his wife shows he loves himself. 29None of us hate our own bodies. We provide for them and take good care of them, just as Christ does for the church, 30because we are each part of his body. 31 As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother to get married, and he becomes like one person with his wife.” 32This is a great mystery, but I understand it to mean Christ and his church. 33So each husband should love his wife as much as he loves himself, and each wife should respect her husband.
translation_text:
Yentu ni benu labaar
1Ya tee Yennu walonkai na, yii yabir i mɔŋ kii naan nan ŋɔɔ na, 2ki ŋaan ki loomm-ii dia-i kaman nan Masia nba din loont bonchiann ki gaar ti paak ki kpo ki teen mannu nba ki Yennu loon na.
3Ya tee Yennu waas na paak, li poŋ ki ŋan niib-ii gbia bonchonchonn toona barii, amii jakint barii, amii nianchiauŋ barii i sinsuuk ni. 4Yii teen Yennu niipoouk yoo kura, ŋaan daa te ki bonchonchonn toona barii nan jakint barii nan baryana nyi i mɔi ni. 5Li ŋan yii mi nan wunba tee bonchonchonn amii jakint daanɔ amii nianchiauŋ daanɔ, ŋamme kan la yiar Masia nan Yennu naan ni; ki nianchiauŋ tee kaman nan patmannu nee.
6Kimaan li toonbiit tumu paake te ki Yennu wutoor baat mɔyêtdamm paak. I daa te ki sɔɔ-n jii u jat wannu ki kpanniini. 7I bɔkit i mɔŋ i kɔɔ, ŋaan nyik li damm. 8I ji tee Yennu waase, ki be yentu nie; i ki be bunbɔnn ni kaa kaman nan ya din tuu be na; li ŋan ki yi tee yentu na waas, 9kimaan linba tee barmɔnii nan yentinu nan toonŋana kura tee yentu yare. 10Li paak, yii yabir i mɔŋ ki tumii linba sa penn ti Yomdaanɔ par ni, 11ki daa tokii binba tuun bunbɔnn ni toona ki nyɔɔt kaa li ni, ŋaan te ki bi bunbɔnn ni toona maŋ-n yent. 12Linba ki bi tuun bunbɔnn ni na mɔk fei bonchiann gbaa nan tii piak li po. 13Ŋaan ba tan sa nyinn bonsiar kur yeenin ni yoonba, bi bunbɔnn ni binbeŋ maŋ kura sa fiite perrr. 14Kimaan linba kur ki bi fiit li gbina, li ji tuu dɔɔ yeenin nie. Li paake ki li din sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a
“Fin nba gɔɔnt, fan fiir gɔɔn ni,
ki li want nan a fiir kuun nie na,
ki Masia sa tura u yentu.”
15Li paak, i daa be jatbenu, ŋaan kii tee subindamm. 16Yii jii i yoo kii tuun fanu, kimaan yoo na ni tee yoo biiuko. 17Li paak, yii bant linba tee ti Yomdaanɔ loomm ki daa tee i mɔŋ jatiti.
18I daa nyu daan ki yibi; ŋann sa biirie, ŋaan yii yabir i mɔŋ paak kii gbee nan Yennu Seyeeŋ. 19Yii yiin yanii kii piak ti Yomdaanɔ i para ni, kii yiin nan i leeb Yennu gbouŋ ni yanii nan Seyeeŋ yanii. 20Yoo kur yii teen ti Baa Yennu niipoouk bonsiar kur paak, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni.
Ŋaapoob nan bi sɔrib labaar
21Masia paak, yi sikin i mɔŋ ki tur i leeb man. 22Poob, ya baakit Masia biaŋinba na, yii saak i sɔrib mɔi, 23kimaan Masia nba mɔk yaak u niib paak biaŋinba na, nnee ki jɔɔ mun mɔk yaak u poo paak. Masia niib tee u gbanante ŋaan ki u tee bi Tinntɔɔ. 24Poob mun-ii saak bi sɔrib mɔi bont kura ni kaman nan Masia niib nba saakɔ biaŋinba na.
25Jab, yii loon i poob kaman nan Masia nba loon u niib ki gaar bi paak ki kpo biaŋinba na. 26U din tun linba na, ki bia jii Yennu barii nan nyun ki wurib, a wun ŋammib fanu 27ki jiin teemm u mɔŋ nuu ni nan fant, a bi fant na daa mɔk dansiari, ki bia tee popeendamm ki daa mɔk biit nan waaminnawa. 28Jɔɔ nba loon u poo, u loon u mɔŋe, li paak, li ŋan ki jab-ii loon bi poob kaman nan ba loon bi mɔŋ gbanant biaŋinba na. 29(Sɔɔ ki mi yêt u mɔŋ gbananti; sɔɔ kura gorii u gbanante fanu ki dint u mɔŋ kaman nan Masia nba tuun biaŋinba teen u niib na. 30Timme tee u gbanant maŋ,) 31kaman nan Yennu gbouŋ nba yet biaŋinba na a “Linba na paak ki li ŋan ki jɔɔ-n nyik u naa nan u baa, ŋaan jii u mɔŋ ki taan nan u ŋaapoo ki bin ji teen yomm.” 32Yennu mɔmaan na sumii bonchiann, ŋaan min mi nan li want Masia nan u niib poe. 33Ŋaan li bia want yimme a li ŋan ki jɔɔ kura-ii loon u poo kaman nan wa loon u mɔŋ biaŋinba na, ki poo kura-ii munii baakit u sɔrɔ.

english_text:
Children and Parents
1 Children, you belong to the Lord, and you do the right thing when you obey your parents. The first commandment with a promise says, 2 “Obey your father and your mother, 3and you will have a long and happy life.”
4 Parents, don't be hard on your children. Raise them properly. Teach them and instruct them about the Lord.
Slaves and Masters
5 Slaves, you must obey your earthly masters. Show them great respect and be as loyal to them as you are to Christ. 6Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. You are slaves of Christ, so with your whole heart you must do what God wants you to do. 7Gladly serve your masters, as though they were the Lord himself, and not simply people. 8You know that you will be rewarded for any good things you do, whether you are slaves or free.
9 Slave owners, you must treat your slaves with this same respect. Don't threaten them. They have the same Master in heaven that you do, and he doesn't have favorites.
The Fight against Evil
10Finally, let the mighty strength of the Lord make you strong. 11 Put on all the armor that God gives, so you can defend yourself against the devil's tricks. 12We are not fighting against humans. We are fighting against forces and authorities and against rulers of darkness and powers in the spiritual world. 13So put on all the armor that God gives. Then when that evil day comes, you will be able to defend yourself. And when the battle is over, you will still be standing firm.
14 Be ready! Let the truth be like a belt around your waist, and let God's justice protect you like armor. 15 Your desire to tell the good news about peace should be like shoes on your feet. 16Let your faith be like a shield, and you will be able to stop all the flaming arrows of the evil one. 17 Let God's saving power be like a helmet, and for a sword use God's message that comes from the Spirit.
18Never stop praying, especially for others. Always pray by the power of the Spirit. Stay alert and keep praying for God's people. 19Pray that I will be given the message to speak and that I may fearlessly explain the mystery about the good news. 20I was sent to do this work, and this is the reason I am in jail. So pray that I will be brave and will speak as I should.
Final Greetings
21-22 I want you to know how I am getting along and what I am doing. This is why I am sending Tychicus to you. He is a dear friend, as well as a faithful servant of the Lord. He will tell you how I am doing, and he will cheer you up.
23I pray that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give peace, love, and faith to every follower! 24May God be kind to everyone who keeps on loving our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_text:
Waas nan wadamm labaar
1Waas, yii saak i damm mɔi ti Yomdaanɔ Masia paak, kimaan ŋanne took nan yin tun. 2Sinsinn sennu nba pukin Yennu mɔsonn tee a “Teent a naa nan a baa nibaakir, 3ki a bont kur-n somm fanu, ki a manfoor-ii biakii fɔkit tingbouŋ na ni.”
4Baanba, i daa dia i waas diabiiuk ki dont bi wutoa, ŋaan kii wantib kii kpaamm ti Yomdaanɔ nba loon biaŋinba.
Daaba nan bi chanbaanba labaar
5Tɔn, binba tee daaba mun-ii saak bi chanbaanba mɔi, kii tiimm jaŋmaanii, kii tuun toonn kii teemm nan i para kura kaman nan ya bo sii tuun ki teen Masia biaŋinba na. 6I daa tiimm yoo nba ki bi tuu gorii-i ki i tuun na kuukɔɔ kaa, a bin pakiini, ŋaan yi jii i mɔŋ teen Masia daaba kii tuun linba tee Yennu loomm nan i para kura. 7Yii tuun i toona nan parpeenn ki lii want nan i tuun ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ Masiae, ŋaan daa dukii nan i tuun teen nisaariki; 8kii tian nan ti Yomdaanɔ tan sa tur wunba yen u mɔŋ nan daabir kura bi toonŋana paauko.
9Chanbaanba, yii mun-ii dia i daaba bonŋann ki daa diab nan ninmɔnni. Yii mun-ii tian nan yimm nan i daaba maŋ kura taan mɔk Yomdaanɔ yenɔkɔɔe Yendɔuŋ ni, ki u ki mɔk lukitimi.
Yennu naagbankɔŋŋir labaar
10Linba joont tee nnee, yii chee i para ti Yomdaanɔ ni, kii mɔk yabint paŋ nba ki u turi na. 11Yi jii Yennu naagbankɔŋŋir na ki lia, ki lin te ki yin fit nyann Sintaanii muksubiiuk. 12Kimaan ti ki kɔn nan wunba mɔk gbanant nan sɔn kaa, ŋaan ti kɔn nan narinbiite nan bi yikodamm, nan mɔtana dunia bunbɔnn ni yudamm, nan sanpagbann ni narinbiit. 13Li paak, yi jii Yennu naagbankɔŋŋir na ki lia, ki fit set dabiir nba tan sa baar maŋ daar, ki daa nyikiti.
14I set man, ki jii barmɔnii ki bobin i sai ni, kii mɔk yentinu nba tee kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir na, 15ki teen siir kii mɔɔntir barŋanii nba teen niib parmaasir na, linba tee kaman nan taasaat na. 16Linba pukin na paak, yi jii yada nba tee kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir nba ki i sa fit kpeen Sintaanii peenii nba mɔk muu na, 17ki dia i tinnu nba tee kaman nan kut fokirik, ki bia dia Yennu barii nba tee kaman nan jukbanjiak nba ki u Seyeeŋ tuun nann na. 18Yii mei Yennu yoo kur nan ninmɔnn, u Seyeeŋ nba sommi biaŋinba na; ki yin chekin i mɔŋ nan gbankur ki pukin Yenmiaru kii teen Masiaweira kura, 19ki bia miar Yennu tur n mun ki man fit la sɔnu ki pak nan parcheenn ki mɔn woonin ni barŋanii na, 20kimaan li paake ki n tee toomii ki kɔɔ dansarik ni. Yii mei Yennu ki turin ki man mɔn barŋanii maŋ nan parcheenn kaman nan la kpaa talas nan man pak biaŋinba na.
Foont joontii labaar
21Mɔtana ti naa bilonkak Tikikus wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ toontunnɔ nan barmɔnii na sa beti ma be biaŋinba nan linba ki n tuun. 22Li paake ki n tuumɔ i boor ni, ma wun turi labaar ki yin bann ta be biaŋinba, ki wuu biakii kɔɔnti para.
23Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n tur yimm Masiaweira kur parmaasir nan loomm nan yada. 24Yennu-n somm binba kur loon ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nan loomm nba kaa gbennu na.

english_text:
1 From Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus.
To all God's people who belong to Christ Jesus at Philippi and to all your bishops and deacons.
2I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
Paul's Prayer for the Church in Philippi
3Every time I think of you, I thank my God. 4And whenever I mention you in my prayers, it makes me happy. 5This is because you have taken part with me in spreading the good news from the first day you heard about it. 6God is the one who began this good work in you, and I am certain that he won't stop before it is complete on the day that Christ Jesus returns.
7You have a special place in my heart. So it is only natural for me to feel the way I do. All of you have helped in the work God has given me, as I defend the good news and tell about it here in jail. 8God himself knows how much I want to see you. He knows I care for you in the same way Christ Jesus does.
9I pray that your love will keep on growing and you will fully know and understand 10how to make the right choices. Then you will still be pure and innocent when Christ returns. And until that day, 11Jesus Christ will keep you busy doing good deeds that bring glory and praise to God.
What Life Means to Paul
12My dear friends, I want you to know that what has happened to me has helped to spread the good news. 13 The Roman guards and all the others know I am here in jail because I serve Christ. 14Now most of the Lord's followers have become brave and are fearlessly telling the message.
15Some are preaching about Christ because they are jealous and envious of us. Others are preaching because they want to help. 16They love Christ and know I am here to defend the good news about him. 17But the ones who are jealous of us are not sincere. They just want to cause trouble for me while I am in jail. 18But that doesn't matter. All that matters is that people are telling about Christ, whether they are sincere or not. This is what makes me glad.
I will keep on being glad, 19because I know that your prayers and the help that comes from the Spirit of Christ Jesus will keep me safe. 20I honestly expect and hope I will never do anything to be ashamed of. Whether I live or die, I always want to be as brave as I am now and bring honor to Christ.
21If I live, it will be for Christ, and if I die, I will gain even more. 22I don't know what to choose. I could keep on living and doing something useful. 23It is a hard choice to make. I want to die and be with Christ, because this would be much better. 24-25But I know that all of you still need me. This is why I am sure I will stay on to help you grow and be happy in your faith. 26Then, when I visit you again, you will have good reason to take great pride in Christ Jesus because of me.
27Above all else, you must live in a way that brings honor to the good news about Christ. Then, whether I visit you or not, I will hear that all of you think alike. I will know that you are working together and are struggling side by side to get others to believe the good news.
28Be brave when you face your enemies. Your courage will show them that they are going to be destroyed, and it will show you that you will be saved. God will make all of this happen, 29and he has blessed you. Not only do you have faith in Christ, but you suffer for him. 30 You saw me suffer, and you still hear about my troubles. Now you must suffer in the same way.
translation_text:
1Min Pɔɔl nan Timoti, timm nba tee Yisa Masia toontunna nee sɔb gbouŋ na ki teen Yennu niŋamm kur nba tee Yisa Masiaweira nba be Filipi na, nan bi yudamm nan bi sommteeb na.
2Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir.
Pɔɔl mei Yennu bi paak
3N teen Yennu niipoouk yoo nba kura ki n tiar i po. 4Yoo kura, mi-i mei Yennu teeni, n tuu mei nan parpeenne, 5kimaan ya sommitin biaŋinba barŋanii toona maŋ pinpiik niwa nan mɔtana na paak. 6Li paak, n ki mɔk birsuk nan Yennu nba din piin toonŋann i ni na, u sii diar nna mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki u tan sa gbenn toonŋann maŋ Yisa Masia daar maŋ. 7N yan be i ni. Ki mi-i dukii i po nna, li set ŋan, kimaan la nyii yoo nba ki n din be n yama ni ki gbaa barŋanii maŋ paak ma n sennir nawa, ki tan baar yoo nba ki n ji kɔɔ dansarik na, i kura mun lek mɔk li yaak booru nba ki Yennu turin na. 8Yennue tee n siara daanɔ nan n yan set be i ni nan Yisa Masia mɔŋ mɔŋ loomm.
9N mei Yennu ma i loomm-ii yɔɔ kii pukii yoo nan yoo, ki i barmɔnii bannu nan i subinii kura bia sii pukii, 10ki yin fit gann linba mantik ŋan, ki li poorpo ki i be ki tan daa mɔk jakint nan biit Masia daar maŋ ni, 11ki i binbeŋ-ii mɔk yentinu ki yii tuun toonŋana bonchiann, Yisa Masia paŋ ni, ki Yennu sann-n la dontir nan paku.
Manfoor tee Masia
