12N naa waas, n loon ki yin bann nan linba kura tumin na somm barŋanii maŋ a lin saan tɔɔnne nan barmɔnii. 13Li paak, kpanbar gbanjot nan nileeb nba be nna na kur bann nan ma tee Masia toontunnɔ na paake ki n kɔɔ dansarik na. 14N sarik ni benu na tur ti naa waas bonchiann yuugontir ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu ni, ki pukii yoo kur, ki bi mɔɔntir Yennu maan nan parcheenn, ki jaŋmaanii ji kaa.
15Ŋaan siab mɔɔntir Masia maan maŋ kimaan bi tee punpondamm nan kunkɔnkɔnnae, ŋaan ki siab mun mɔɔntir nan bi para ni nan barmɔnii loomm. 16Binba tuun nan bi loomm na, bi mi nan Yennue poŋ din turin barŋanii toona na. 17Nisiab ki mɔɔntir Masia po nan bi para kuri, ŋaan bi tuun nan funfunne; bi dukii nan bi sa pukin mine fara yoo nba ki n daa be dansarik nna.
18Ŋaan ŋann ki biiri. N mɔk parpeenn li paak, ŋamme sa fit ki mɔɔnt Masia maan maŋ sɔnbooru nba kur ni; bi-i mɔɔntir bi dudukŋant ni amii bi dudukbiit ni, min lek bia sii mɔk parpeenn li paak, 19kimaan n mi nan i Yenmiaru nan i sommir nba nyi Yisa Masia Seyeeŋ boor ni na paak, n sa nyi dansarik na niwa. 20N par ni loomm nan n dindann tee ki n daa dint n mɔŋ feie, ŋaan n sii mɔk parcheenn mɔtana nan yoo kura, ki mi-i lekii kpo amii ma be, ŋaan li kura sa dont Masia sann n po, 21kimaan mi-i be n manfoor ni, li tee Masia paake; ŋaan li-i tee kuun ki n kpo, ŋanne mantik sa baar nan nyɔɔt ki turin. 22Li-i tee ki n sii be n manfoor ni, n sa fit ŋamm tun toona nba kpaa talasa; li paak, n ki fit bann n par ni linba kpaa talas ki n sa gann. 23Li bona ŋannlee nee daarin; n mantik bo loon man nyik manfosiar nba nee, ki saan kii sai be Masia boor ni, ŋanne bo sii tee linba chee ki gar. 24Ŋaan i paake te ki li mantik kpaa talas bonchiann nan mii be n manfoor ni. 25N mi nan ŋanne sɔ ki birsuk kaa, li paak te ki n mi nan n sii beewa. N sii be i kura boor ni, ki i sii mɔk parpeenn ki i yada-n chee, 26ki mi-i taŋŋi jen i boor ni yoonba i binbeŋ sa turin yuugontir bonchiann nan li sɔnu, ki yin pak Yisa Masia n manfoor paak.
27Mɔtana, linba kpaa talas tee a yi ŋaan ki i binbeŋ sɔnu-ii tee nan Masia barŋanii nba loon lii tee biaŋinba na, ki mi-i lekii fit saan lai amii mi-i kii fit laini, ŋaan n sa gbat a i see fanu nan dudukyennkɔɔ, ki taan mɔtaauk ki yabii i mɔŋ ki barŋanii maŋ-ii saa tɔɔnn. 28I daa tiin i datai mani; yii mɔk parcheenn ki lin wannib nan ŋamme tan sa baa ŋaan ki yin do, kimaan Yennue teeni li nyannu. 29Ya poŋ mɔk yaak nan yin tun ki tur Masia na paak, ki li ki tee ya teenɔ yada na paak kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan ya bia di fara u paak na, 30ki gbaa kaman nan ya poŋ din la ma lek gbaa biaŋinba na, ki mɔtana i bia gbat nan n daa gbaa nna.

english_text:
True Humility
1Christ encourages you, and his love comforts you. God's Spirit unites you, and you are concerned for others. 2Now make me completely happy! Live in harmony by showing love for each other. Be united in what you think, as if you were only one person. 3Don't be jealous or proud, but be humble and consider others more important than yourselves. 4Care about them as much as you care about yourselves 5and think the same way that Christ Jesus thought:
6Christ was truly God.
But he did not try to remain
equal with God.
7Instead he gave up everything
and became a slave,
when he became
like one of us.
8Christ was humble.
He obeyed God and even died
on a cross.
9Then God gave Christ
the highest place
and honored his name
above all others.
10 So at the name of Jesus
everyone will bow down,
those in heaven, on earth,
and under the earth.
11And to the glory
of God the Father
everyone will openly agree,
“Jesus Christ is Lord!”
Lights in the World
12My dear friends, you always obeyed when I was with you. Now that I am away, you should obey even more. So work with fear and trembling to discover what it really means to be saved. 13God is working in you to make you willing and able to obey him.
14Do everything without grumbling or arguing. 15 Then you will be the pure and innocent children of God. You live among people who are crooked and evil, but you must not do anything they can say is wrong. Try to shine as lights among the people of this world, 16as you hold firmly to the message that gives life. Then on the day when Christ returns, I can take pride in you. I can also know that my work and efforts were not useless.
17Your faith in the Lord and your service are like a sacrifice offered to him. And my own blood may have to be poured out with the sacrifice. If this happens, I will be glad and rejoice with you. 18In the same way, you should be glad and rejoice with me.
Timothy and Epaphroditus
19I want to be encouraged by news about you. So I hope the Lord Jesus will soon let me send Timothy to you. 20I don't have anyone else who cares about you as much as he does. 21The others think only about what interests them and not about what concerns Christ Jesus. 22But you know what kind of person Timothy is. He has worked with me like a son in spreading the good news. 23I hope to send him to you, as soon as I find out what is going to happen to me. 24And I feel sure the Lord will also let me come soon.
25I think I ought to send my dear friend Epaphroditus back to you. He is a follower and a worker and a soldier of the Lord, just as I am. You sent him to look after me, 26but now he is eager to see you. He is worried, because you heard he was sick. 27In fact, he was very sick and almost died. But God was kind to him, and also to me, and he kept me from being burdened down with sorrow.
28Now I am more eager than ever to send Epaphroditus back again. You will be glad to see him, and I won't have to worry any longer. 29Be sure to give him a cheerful welcome, just as people who serve the Lord deserve. 30He almost died working for Christ, and he risked his own life to do for me what you could not.
translation_text:
Masia sikininmɔŋ nan u yudantuk labaar
1Ya be Masia ni biaŋinbae teeni paŋ, ki u loomm maŋŋit i para, ki i mɔk burchint nan ninbatinu i leeb paak, ki mɔk Seyeeŋ weinanleeb, 2ŋanne te ki n beeri ma yii mɔk dudukit yennkɔɔ ki pukin n parpeenn na, kii mɔk loomm yennkɔɔ ki i dudukit kura-ii tee yennkɔɔ. 3I daa tuun bonsiar ki li nyi i funfunn nan i karinbaanii loomm ni, ŋaan yii be soonna nan i leeb man, kii dukii i leeb po ki gar ya dukii i mɔŋ po biaŋinba. 4I daa te ki i baka be i kuukɔɔ bona ni, ŋaan te ki i baka-ii be i leeb bona ni biak. 5Yi ŋaan ki binbeŋ booru nba ki Yisa Masia din mɔk na mun-ii be i ni:
6U din tuu yɔɔ be kaman nan Yennu nee,
ŋaan u din ki mi dukin a u sa jii u mɔŋ
ki yekin nan Yennu,
7ŋaan din sak nan u mɔŋ mɔŋ loomm ni
ki nyik wa din mɔk linba na,
ki kpint u mɔŋ ki teen kaman nan daabir na
ki teen nisaarik,
ki tan dɔkit ki teen nisaarik.
8U din sikin u mɔŋ,
ki sak ki kpo dapunpunn paak gbaa.
9Linba na paake ki Yennu din donnɔ gar sɔɔ kura,
ŋaan turɔ sansiar nba tee sanjaann
ki gar sana kura.
10Ŋanne te ki binba be sanpaapo nan tingbouŋ na nan kpeentiŋ,
bi kur tan sa gbaan bi duna paak
ki tur ŋɔɔ Yisa sann maŋ nibaakir,
11ki sɔɔ kura tan sa yet a
ŋɔɔ Yisa Masia tee Yomdaanɔ
kii dont ti Baa Yennu sann.
Yii nyirii kaman nan ŋmaajara na man
12N naa walonkai, ma din be i boor ni ki i sak n mɔb biaŋinba na, ki mi-i lekii kaa i boor ni, li kpaa talas ki yin sak n mɔb. Yii biakii tuun i toona nan yentinu nan gbananjekint man, ki tont i tinnu, 13kimaan Yennu yɔɔ tuun nanie, a yii loon yii tuun u loomm kii biakii tuun u loomm maŋ.
14Yii tuun siar kura ki daa burimi, amii ki nɔi mɔniɔku; 15ŋanne sa te ki i daa mɔk biit, ŋaan kii mɔk popeenn, kii tee Yennu waas nba ki mɔk daŋ, kimaan i be nijakina nan biitdamm sinsuuk nie na. Li kpaa talas ki yii nyirii bi sinsuuk ni kaman nan ŋmaajara nba nyirii sanpaapo biaŋinba na, 16ŋaan yetib manfoor labaar. Li-i tee ki i tuun nna, n tan sii mɔk yaak nan mii mɔk yuugontir i paak Masia daar maŋ, kimaan li tan sa wann nan n fara nan n toona ki tee yanni.
17Li-i tee ki n lu n miar na ki pukin fara nba ki n poŋ dii ma n te ki yin teen yada na, li bo sii manin, ki n bo sii mɔk parpeenn nan i kura. 18Li kpaa talas ki i mun-ii mɔk parpeenn nanin man.
Timoti nan Epafrodaitus labaar
19N mɔk dindann ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa ni nan man tun Timoti i boor ni mɔtana, ki gbat i labaar ki n par ni-n dɔɔr. 20Ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe fiin i po nan barmɔnii kaman nan ma fiin i po biaŋinba na. 21Sɔɔ kur nba ji biar gbaa nan bi mɔŋ mɔŋ yarae jiak jiak, bi ki fiin linba tee Yisa Masia yar na po. 22I mɔŋ mi wa tun biaŋinba ki wann u kotir biaŋinba po, nan wa tuun nanin barŋanii toona ni kaman nan bik nba tuun nan u baa biaŋinba na. 23Mi-i la bont nba yaa lin ŋmakit n po biaŋinba, n mɔk dindann ma n tumɔe yiama. 24Ti Yomdaanɔ paak, n mɔk yada nan n mɔŋ sa fit baar i boor ni mɔtana.
25N dukin ki li kpaa talase nan man tun ti naa bik Epafrodaitus, wunba tuun nan nunmɔnn ki gbaa a wun sommin nan Masia toonn, ŋɔɔe bia tee wunba ki i daan teenɔ i toomii ki u daan sommin na. 26U set loon wun la i kura. U par daan biir bonchiann, kimaan i daan poŋ gbat nan u yiare. 27Li set tee barmɔniie, u daan yiare ki per kuuma, ŋaan Yennu daan tinɔ ninbaauk, ki li ki tee u kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan bia tin n mɔŋ ninbaauk, ki nyinnin parbijaann ni. 28Li paak, n jekiree nan man tumɔ i boor ni, ki i-i lau yoonba ki yin yaan la parpeenn, ki n mɔŋ parbiir-n bot. 29Yii gaarɔ nan parpeenn man kaman nan i naa bik na ti Yomdaanɔ ni. Yi tur nibooru nba tee nan ŋɔɔ na nibaakir man, 30kimaan u daan ki kɔn u manfoor paak ki tan yaa wun kpo Masia toonn na paak, a wun turin sommir nba ki i daan ki fit turin na.

english_text:
Being Acceptable to God
1Finally, my dear friends, be glad that you belong to the Lord. It doesn't bother me to write the same things to you that I have written before. In fact, it is for your own good.
2Watch out for those people who behave like dogs! They are evil and want to do more than just circumcise you. 3But we are the ones who are truly circumcised, because we worship by the power of God's Spirit and take pride in Christ Jesus. We don't brag about what we have done, 4although I could. Others may brag about themselves, but I have more reason to brag than anyone else. 5 I was circumcised when I was eight days old, and I am from the nation of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a true Hebrew. As a Pharisee, I strictly obeyed the Law of Moses. 6 And I was so eager I even made trouble for the church. I did everything the Law demands in order to please God.
7But Christ has shown me that what I once thought was valuable is worthless. 8Nothing is as wonderful as knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. I have given up everything else and count it all as garbage. All I want is Christ 9and to know that I belong to him. I could not make myself acceptable to God by obeying the Law of Moses. God accepted me simply because of my faith in Christ. 10All I want is to know Christ and the power that raised him to life. I want to suffer and die as he did, 11so that somehow I also may be raised to life.
Running toward the Goal
12I have not yet reached my goal, and I am not perfect. But Christ has taken hold of me. So I keep on running and struggling to take hold of the prize. 13My friends, I don't feel I have already arrived. But I forget what is behind, and I struggle for what is ahead. 14I run toward the goal, so I can win the prize of being called to heaven. This is the prize God offers because of what Christ Jesus has done. 15All of us who are mature should think in this same way. And if any of you think differently, God will make it clear to you. 16But we must keep going in the direction that we are now headed.
17 My friends, I want you to follow my example and learn from others who closely follow the example we set for you. 18I often warned you that many people are living as enemies of the cross of Christ. And now with tears in my eyes, I warn you again 19that they are headed for hell! They worship their stomachs and brag about the disgusting things they do. All they can think about are the things of this world.
20But we are citizens of heaven and are eagerly waiting for our Savior to come from there. Our Lord Jesus Christ 21 has power over everything, and he will make these poor bodies of ours like his own glorious body.
translation_text:
1Li gbennu nira, n naa waas, yii mɔk parpeenn yimm nan ti Yomdaanɔ benu ni. Min yaa baka kaa nan ma ŋamm sɔb linba ki n poŋ daan sɔb turi sinsinn na; mi-i tuun nna, li sii tee i yumanne. 2I mi man biitdamm na, nisiab nba naan nan bɔi na, ki pot bi gbanant na. 3Timm jiantir Yennu u Seyeeŋ nie, ki mɔk yuugontir Yisa Masia paak; linba na gar punpotuwa. Timm ki teen nifosaarik yar yada kaa. 4Wunba-i dukii a ŋɔɔ sa fit teen nifosaarik yar yada, min mɔŋ sa fit teen li bonbooru yadawa. N mɔk linba te ki n sa tun nna, 5kimaan n mun din tee daa ŋanniin bike ki bi pot n punn. Bi marin ki n tee Juu nirɔe, Benjamin booru ni, ki tee Juu nirɔ nan barmɔnii. Li-i tee Juu teeb sennu dianu nba tee biaŋinba po naie, n din waa sennu maŋ kaman nan Farisiinba nba waa biaŋinba nee nan nunmɔnn, 6ki din tan mukis Masia niib gbaa. Nirɔ-i sa sak sennue ki fit kii tee yentinɔ, min din be ki kaa bisiat jiin li po. 7Ki n din bo sa fit kan linba na kur kaman nan li mɔk nyɔɔte, ŋaan min yiin linba kaa nyɔɔte Masia paak. 8Li ki tee li kuukɔɔ kaa ki mɔk nyɔɔt turimi; min yiin bonsiar kura linba kaa nyɔɔte; kimaan ma bann n Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia ki li bannu nyɔɔt yab bonchiann na paak, kimaan u paake ki n fok bonsiar kur ki lu; min yi li kur tanpenn paak mɔɔte ma n tan la Masia nan n ninbinn, 9kii set kii be nanɔ nan barmɔnii. N ji ki mɔk n tiauŋ yentinu booru nba tee sennu weiu na kaa. Mɔtana, ma teen Yisa Masia yada nee ki n gaar yentinu booru nba na, li yentinu tee yada paake, ki nyi Yennu boor ni na. 10Linba kur ki n ji loon tee ki n bann Masia, ki bann u kuun fiiru paŋ, ki di fara nanɔ, ki n binbenkperik-n gbenn kaman nan wa din kpo biaŋinba na, 11ki n mɔŋ-ii mɔk dindann nan n tan sa fiir kuun ni nan manfoora.
Poot nantatinu labaar
12N ki yaa a n poŋ nyann ki yent binbeŋ maŋ amii n poŋ ji be fanu kaa; n gbaae ma n nyann ki yent binbeŋ maŋ; kimaan Yisa Masia din fatima, ki n ji tee u yɔɔe. 13N naa waas, n ki dukii a n poŋ nyannir ki li tee n yare, ŋaan bonyennkɔɔe ki n tuun, n lek ji tammit linba be n poorpoe, ŋaan gbaa ma n baar linba be n tɔɔnn po na. 14Li paake te ki n tɔkin poot sɔnu maŋ ki tiin sunsonn saa nyannu bɔɔnu maŋ peŋ ma n sa gaar paauk nba tee sanpaapo manfoor ki Yennu din yiinin li paak Yisa Masia ni na.
15Li kpaa talase ki timm nba kur tee subirteeb Seyeeŋ ni na-ii mɔk dudukit maŋ booru, ŋaan i siab-i mɔk dudukgantie, Yennu sa te lin yent li damm nunii powa. 16Ŋaant man ki tii dia barmɔnii kuukɔɔ kaman nan ta din dia biaŋinba nan mɔtana na.
17N naa waas, yii biakii tokii min man. Yi jii i dudukit teen damm nba waa siarŋana nba ki ti din senn turi na ni. 18N poŋ din beti linba na po yoo nba gar nawa, ŋaan n bia ŋammit beerie mɔtana nan ninnyuut, nisiab be ki bi binbeŋ jiib teemm Masia dapunpunn paak kuun na dataie. 19Bi tan sa joont nan tubdatu nba kaa gbennue, kimaan bi poore tee bi yennu. Bi mɔk yuugontir nan linba dintib fei nee. Bi dukii linba tee tingbouŋ na bona kuukɔɔ poe. 20Ŋaan timm ŋanin tee Yendɔuŋ ni waase, ki jekir ki guu ti Tinntɔɔ, wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia, a wun nyi Yendɔuŋ ni baar. 21U tan sa lebit ti gbananchɔkint nba sa kpo na ki lii naan kaman nan u Yendɔuŋ ni gbananpaann nee. U tan sa jii paŋ nba te ki u yen bonsiar kur nee ki tun nna.

english_text:
1Dear friends, I love you and long to see you. Please keep on being faithful to the Lord. You are my pride and joy.
Paul Encourages the Lord's Followers
2Euodia and Syntyche, you belong to the Lord, so I beg you to stop arguing with each other. 3And, my true partner, I ask you to help them. These women have worked together with me and with Clement and with the others in spreading the good news. Their names are now written in the book of life.
4Always be glad because of the Lord! I will say it again: Be glad. 5Always be gentle with others. The Lord will soon be here. 6Don't worry about anything, but pray about everything. With thankful hearts offer up your prayers and requests to God. 7Then, because you belong to Christ Jesus, God will bless you with peace that no one can completely understand. And this peace will control the way you think and feel.
8Finally, my friends, keep your minds on whatever is true, pure, right, holy, friendly, and proper. Don't ever stop thinking about what is truly worthwhile and worthy of praise. 9You know the teachings I gave you, and you know what you heard me say and saw me do. So follow my example. And God, who gives peace, will be with you.
Paul Gives Thanks for the Gifts He Was Given
10The Lord has made me very grateful that at last you have thought about me once again. Actually, you were thinking about me all along, but you didn't have any chance to show it. 11I am not complaining about having too little. I have learned to be satisfied with whatever I have. 12I know what it is to be poor or to have plenty, and I have lived under all kinds of conditions. I know what it means to be full or to be hungry, to have too much or too little. 13Christ gives me the strength to face anything.
14It was good of you to help me when I was having such a hard time. 15 My friends at Philippi, you remember what it was like when I started preaching the good news in Macedonia. After I left there, you were the only church that became my partner by giving blessings and by receiving them in return. 16 Even when I was in Thessalonica, you helped me more than once. 17I am not trying to get something from you, but I want you to receive the blessings that come from giving.
18 I have been paid back everything, and with interest. I am completely satisfied with the gifts you sent with Epaphroditus. They are like a sweet-smelling offering or like the right kind of sacrifice that pleases God. 19I pray that God will take care of all your needs with the wonderful blessings that come from Christ Jesus! 20May God our Father be praised forever and ever. Amen.
Final Greetings
21Give my greetings to all who are God's people because of Christ Jesus.
The Lord's followers here with me send you their greetings.
22All of God's people send their greetings, especially those in the service of the Emperor.
23I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless your life!
translation_text:
Kpaanii labaar
1Li paak, n naa waas, yimm nba ki n looni, ki loon ki n lai na, yimme te ki n mɔk parpeenn nan yuugontir. N naa walonkai, setir fanu man Yennu sɔnu ni, ti Yomdaanɔ tɔɔnn.
2N niipoob Yuodia nan Sintike, n barimiie, yii yabir kii be nan parmaasir nan i leeb ti Yomdaanɔ paak. 3Ki fin nba mun tee n weinanlɔɔ nan barmɔnii, n loon ki fin somm poob bannlee maŋ, kimaan ŋamm nan Klement nan binba kur tee n toontunleeb ki bi sana be Yennu manfoor gbouŋ ni na din lakin tun nanin bonchiann barŋanii maŋ mɔnu ni.
4Yii mɔk parpeenn yoo kur, yimm nan ti Yomdaanɔ benu ni. N bia ŋammit beeri ma yii mɔk parpeenn man.
5Yi ŋaan ki sɔɔ kur-n bann i niŋantin po. Ti Yomdaanɔ baate mɔtana. 6I daa biir i para bonsiar paaki. Yimm-ii mei Yennu linba kpaa talas turi paak. Yii mei Yennu, kii teenɔ niipoouk i para ni yoo kura ki betɔ i loomm nba tee linba. 7Ki Yennu parmaasir nba gar nirɔ bannu terik na sa dia i para nan i dudukit Yisa Masia ni.
8N naa waas, linba joont tee nnee, yi ŋaan ki i dudukit-n gbee nan linba tee barmɔnii, nan linba ŋan nan baakir, nan linba ŋan, nan linba mɔk burchint, nan linba took, nan linba yeen, nan linba mɔk fant, nan linba ŋan nan paku. 9Jiin linba ki i tumii ki gaar n boor ni nan n mɔbona nan n toona na kii tuun nann toona. Ki ti Yennu nba teent parmaasir na sii be nani.
Piinii niipoouk turu labaar
10N mɔk parpeenn bonchiann ti Yomdaanɔ ni nan la yukira ŋaan ki i fiin n po na paak; setik nan barmɔnii, i daan loonima, ŋaan i daan ki mɔk yaake. 11N ki dukii nan i tamm n po kaa ki n yeen linba na, kimaan min poŋ tumii biaŋinba ki n numm sa gboo linba ki n mɔka. 12N mi kɔŋŋir ni binbeŋ nan jaŋŋir ni binbeŋ. N poŋ tumii li kura; li paak, mi-i be siaminba kur ki li tee kon, amii gboor, li-i lekii yab amii li-i kii yabi, ŋaan n numm gboot linba ki n mɔke yoo kura. 13Masia nba teenin paŋ na paak, n sa fit set binbeŋ kur ni.
14Ŋaan i daan tun fanu bonchiann nan ya daan sommin n fara dinu ni na. 15Yimm Filipi teeb mi nan ma din mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii pinpiik niwa, yoo nba ki n din nyii Masedonia tiŋ ni na, yimm kuukɔɔe din tee Masia niib nba sommitin; ki i gaan piinii nba tee Yennu Seyeeŋ yar na n boor ni, ŋaan mun teenin i piinii nba tee tingbouŋ na yar. 16Ma din tan be fara ni Tesalonika doo ni na, yimme din tun ki teenin sommir. 17Li ki tee a n loon mii gaan i piinii kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan n loon ki yin gaar Seyeeŋ nyɔɔt nba sa pukin i po. 18N gaar i piinii na munna ki li jaŋŋima. Piinii nba ki i daan tun Epafrodaitus nann na te ki n gboowa. Li piinii naan nan mannu nba nubir mane ki teen Yennu, ki penn u par ni, ki u gaarir. 19Li paak, Yennu sa turi linba tee i loomm kur kaman nan u mɔkint nba yab biaŋinba sanpaapo, Yisa Masia paake. 20Niib-ii dontir ti Baa Yennu sann mɔtana nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.
Foont joontii labaar
21Yii foont Yisa Masia popeendamm kura. Yisaweira nba be nanin nna na foonti. 22U popeendamm na kur foonti, ki mantik tee binba tee Kpanbar Siisa ŋaak toontunna na. 23Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi i para ni.

english_text:
1From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is also a follower.
2To God's people who live in Colossae and are faithful followers of Christ.
I pray that God our Father will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
A Prayer of Thanks
3Each time we pray for you, we thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. 4We have heard of your faith in Christ and of your love for all God's people, 5because what you hope for is kept safe for you in heaven. You first heard about this hope when you believed the true message, which is the good news.
6The good news is spreading all over the world with great success. It has spread in this same way among you, ever since the first day you learned the truth about God's wonderful kindness 7 from our good friend Epaphras. He works together with us for Christ and is a faithful worker for you. 8He is also the one who told us about the love that God's Spirit has given you.
The Person and Work of Christ
9We have not stopped praying for you since the first day we heard about you. In fact, we always pray that God will show you everything he wants you to do and that you may have all the wisdom and understanding his Spirit gives. 10Then you will live a life that honors the Lord, and you will always please him by doing good deeds. You will come to know God even better. 11His glorious power will make you patient and strong enough to endure anything, and you will be truly happy.
12I pray that you will be grateful to God for letting you have part in what he has promised his people in the kingdom of light. 13God rescued us from the dark power of Satan and brought us into the kingdom of his dear Son, 14 who forgives our sins and sets us free.
15 Christ is exactly like God,
who cannot be seen.
He is the first-born Son,
superior to all creation.
16Everything was created by him,
everything in heaven
and on earth,
everything seen and unseen,
including all forces
and powers,
and all rulers
and authorities.
All things were created
by God's Son,
and everything was made
for him.
17God's Son was before all else,
and by him everything
is held together.
18 He is the head of his body,
which is the church.
He is the very beginning,
the first to be raised
from death,
so that he would be
above all others.
19God himself was pleased
to live fully in his Son.
20 And God was pleased
for him to make peace
by sacrificing his blood
on the cross,
so that all beings in heaven
and on earth
would be brought back to God.
21You used to be far from God. Your thoughts made you his enemies, and you did evil things. 22But his Son became a human and died. So God made peace with you, and now he lets you stand in his presence as people who are holy and faultless and innocent. 23But you must stay deeply rooted and firm in your faith. You must not give up the hope you received when you heard the good news. It was preached to everyone on earth, and I myself have become a servant of this message.
Paul's Service to the Church
24I am glad I can suffer for you. I am pleased also that in my own body I can continue the suffering of Christ for his body, the church. 25God's plan was to make me a servant of his church and to send me to preach his complete message to you. 26For ages and ages this message was kept secret from everyone, but now it has been explained to God's people. 27God did this because he wanted you Gentiles to understand his wonderful and glorious mystery. And the mystery is that Christ lives in you, and he is your hope of sharing in God's glory.
28We announce the message about Christ, and we use all our wisdom to warn and teach everyone, so all of Christ's followers will grow and become mature. 29This is why I work so hard and use the mighty power he gives me.
translation_text:
1Min Pɔɔl, wunba tee Yisa Masia toomii Yennu loomm paak na, nan ti naa bik Timoti, sɔbin gbouŋ na 2ki teen Yennu niib nba be Kolosa ni, ki tee ti naa waas nba teen Masia yada na. Ti Baa Yennu-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir.
Pɔɔl nba teen Yennu niipoouk Yenmiaru ni biaŋinba
3Ti-i mei Yennu i paak yoonba, ti teen ŋɔɔ Yennu wunba tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia Baa na niipoouk yoo kura, 4kimaan ti gbat ya teen Yisa Masia yada biaŋinba na, ki bia loon Yennu niib kura, 5kimaan pinpiik ni ya din gbat barmɔnii barŋanii yoonba, i din gbat bonŋana nba guui Yendɔuŋ ni na poe. 6Ki barŋanii maŋe baat nani kaman nan la yat ki kpaatir tingbouŋ na kur ni ki ŋammit niib binbeŋ, ki bia tuun nna i mɔŋ boor ni, la nyii daar nba ki i din gbat ki bann Yennu ninbatinu nan barmɔnii maŋ nba tee biaŋinba na. 7I din tumii Yennu ninbatinu maŋ po ti toontunlɔɔ Epafras wunba ki ti loonɔ na boor ni; ŋɔɔe tee Masia toontunnɔ nba tuun nan barmɔnii i boor ni ki turit, 8ki bia betit i loomm nba tee biaŋinba Yennu Seyeeŋ ni.
9Linba na paak, ta daan gbat i po, ti ki nyik Yenmiaru i paaki a ŋɔɔ Yennu-n te ki i bann u loomm nba tee biaŋinba, ŋaan turi Seyeeŋ yan nan li subinii kur, 10ki yin fit kii be kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ nba loon biaŋinba, ki bia fit kii tuun linba sii pent u par, ki yin fit kii tuun toonŋana kur, kii ŋammit bant Yennu. 11Ŋɔɔ Yennu-n turi paŋ bonchiann kaman nan wa mɔk paŋ kur biaŋinba na, linba sa te ki i mɔk gbankur bonsiar kur ni, nan sukuru nan parpeenn. 12Yii teen ti Baa Yennu niipoouk man. Ŋɔɔe te ki ti jaŋ nan tin fit la yiar u boor, yentu ni, u niib sinsuuk ni, 13ki bia nyinnit Sintaanii bunbɔnn naan ni, ŋaan jiit nan laafia ki kɔɔnt u Bilonkak maŋ naan ni. 14U Bija maŋ paak ki u nyik chab ti biit ŋaan nyinnit daabsin ni.
Masia nan u toona labaar
15Sɔɔ kan fit la Yennu, ŋaan u Bija maŋ Masia tee u naauŋo. Ŋɔɔe gar bont kura, ki tee Yennu bonnankaa kura yur, 16kimaan u nie ki Yennu din nan bonsiar kur nba be sanpaapo nan tingbouŋ na ni. Ŋɔɔe din nan ta laat bona nba nan ta ki laat bona nba, yentdamm, nan yomdamm, nan yudamm, nan yikodamm. Yennu din nan bonsiar kura ŋɔɔ Masia nie, ŋaan jii bonsiar kur turɔ. 17Ŋɔɔ Masia din be ki bonsiar kura din kaa, ki u paake ki bont kur daa be. 18Ŋɔɔe tee u niib yur, ŋaan ki bi tee u gbanant. U niib maŋ manfoor nyi u boor nie. Ŋɔɔe tee Yennu Bija wunba din kpo ki fiir kuun ni sinsinn, a u kuukɔɔ-ii tee bonsiar kur yudaanɔ, 19kimaan Yennu tiauŋ din dukin a li ŋan a Masia-ii mɔk ŋɔɔ Yennu ninnauŋ. 20Li paak, u Bija maŋ paake ki Yennu te ki bont kur nba be sanpaapo nan tiŋ ni ŋamm teemm u yɔɔsinba nan parmaasir; ki sɔn nba nyii u Bija maŋ ni dapunpunn paak na paake ki u te ki parmaasir be timm nan ŋɔɔ Yennu sinsuuk ni.
21Sianyoo, i dudukbiit nan i toonbiit din te ki i tee Yennu dataie, ki fɔk nanɔ, 22ŋaan mɔtana, u Bija gbanant kuun maŋ paak, u ji teeni u yɔɔsinbae a wun jii-i ki yii tee popeendamm, ki daa mɔk jakint nan biit, ki baar u boor ni, 23li paak, yii yabii man setik nan mɔnii, ki teen Yisa yada kii see chimm, ki daa fɔrii nyi dindann nba ki i la yoo nba ki i din gbat u barŋanii; ki bi din mɔɔntir barŋanii maŋ ki teen niib kur nba be tingbouŋ na ni; ki min Pɔɔl teen li toontunnɔ na.
Pɔɔl nba teen Masia niib toontunnɔ biaŋinba
24Ki mɔtana n mɔk parpeenn n fara dinu ni i paak, kimaan ma di fara maŋ biaŋinba na, n tontir fara dinu nba ki Masia din senn ma n di u gbanant nba tee u weira na paak na. 25Ki Yennu nba turin toonn maŋ a n tun na, ŋɔɔe teenin Masia niib maŋ daabir i sommir paak, a niib-n bann Yennu barii maŋ mun. 26Barii maŋe tee woonin ni maan nba ki Yennu din woon sianyoo, ki nibooru kur ki bannir, ŋaan ki u tan fiit wann u niib mɔtana. 27Yennu lor a wun fiit woonin ni maan maŋ ki wann u niib maŋe. U woonin ni maan maŋ ŋan bonchiann, ki tee niib kur yare. Li want nan Masia be i ni; li paak, i mɔk dindann nan i tan sii be fanu ki gbenn. 28Li paak, Masia maŋ poe ki ti mɔɔntir, ki kpaan sɔɔ kura, ki want sɔɔ kur nan yan booru kur, a tin te sɔɔ kur-n chee nan Masia weiu, ki jiib wann Yennu. 29Linba na paak ki n lek yabii nan n paŋ kur nba ki Masia turin ki li yab bonchiann.

english_text:
1I want you to know what a struggle I am going through for you, for God's people at Laodicea, and for all of those followers who have never met me. 2I do it to encourage them. Then as their hearts are joined together in love, they will be wonderfully blessed with complete understanding. And they will truly know Christ. Not only is he the key to God's mystery, 3but all wisdom and knowledge are hidden away in him. 4I tell you these things to keep you from being fooled by fancy talk. 5Even though I am not with you, I keep thinking about you. I am glad to know that you are living as you should and your faith in Christ is strong.
Christ Brings Real Life
6You have accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord. Now keep on following him. 7Plant your roots in Christ and let him be the foundation for your life. Be strong in your faith, just as you were taught. And be grateful.
8Don't let anyone fool you by using senseless arguments. These arguments may sound wise, but they are only human teachings. They come from the powers of this world and not from Christ.
9God lives fully in Christ. 10And you are fully grown because you belong to Christ, who is over every power and authority. 11Christ has also taken away your selfish desires, just as circumcision removes flesh from the body. 12 And when you were baptized, it was the same as being buried with Christ. Then you were raised to life because you had faith in the power of God, who raised Christ from death. 13 You were dead, because you were sinful and were not God's people. But God let Christ make you alive, when he forgave all our sins.
14 God wiped out the charges that were against us for disobeying the Law of Moses. He took them away and nailed them to the cross. 15There Christ defeated all powers and forces. He let the whole world see them being led away as prisoners when he celebrated his victory.
16 Don't let anyone tell you what you must eat or drink. Don't let them say you must celebrate the New Moon festival, the Sabbath, or any other festival. 17These things are only a shadow of what was to come. But Christ is real!
18Don't be cheated by people who make a show of acting humble and who worship angels. They brag about seeing visions. But it is all nonsense, because their minds are filled with selfish desires. 19 They are no longer part of Christ, who is the head of the whole body. Christ gives the body its strength, and he uses its joints and muscles to hold it together, as it grows by the power of God.
Christ Brings New Life
20You died with Christ. Now the forces of the universe don't have any power over you. Why do you live as if you had to obey such rules as, 21“Don't handle this. Don't taste that. Don't touch this.”? 22After these things are used, they are no longer good for anything. So why be bothered with the rules that humans have made up? 23Obeying these rules may seem to be the smart thing to do. They appear to make you love God more and to be very humble and to have control over your body. But they don't really have any power over our desires.
translation_text:
1Kimaan n loon ki yin bann n baka nba be i ni biaŋinbae, nan Masia niib nba be Laodisea, nan binba daa ki mi lan nan bi ninbina na. 2N yaa bi para-n chee, ki bin lakin taan leeb nan loomm, ki bann Yisa barii paak, ki teenɔ yada nan barmɔnii, ki daa mɔk birsuku, ki bann Yennu woonin ni labaar maŋ, linba tee Masia po; 3ki ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe mɔk barmɔnii yan nan bannu kura.
4Li paak te ki n beeri, i daa te ki sɔɔ-n jii mɔmant ki kpanniini, 5kimaan ma lek ki be i peŋ nan n gbanant na, ŋaan n seek be nani, ki n par peen i paak ma la ya be fanu biaŋinba na, nan ya teen Masia yada nan barmɔnii biaŋinba na.
Barmɔnii manfoor be Masia nie
6Biaŋinba ki i din poŋ gaar Yisa Masia a wuu tee i Yomdaanɔ na, yii be u ni nna man, 7ki sikin i yada jiina u ni, ki i yada-ii be u ni, ki i yada-ii mɔk paŋ kaman nan ba din wanni biaŋinba na, kii teenɔ niipoouk yoo kura.
8Li paak, i daa te ki sɔɔ dia nisaarik yan kpinkpann-yana ki kpanni; linba ki gaa nan Masia po, ŋaan tee tingbouŋ na yan nan nisaarik wannu, 9kimaan Masia gbanant nie ki Yennu ninnauŋ be, 10ki i gaar manfomunn yimm nan u benu ni. Ŋɔɔe tee yudamm nan paŋ damm kura paarɔ.
11Yimm nan u benu nie ki i gaar punpotu; ŋaan li ki tee nisaarik nuu pot i gbananti; Masia punpotu na nee nyinnit biit ni, 12kimaan ba din wuri Yennu nyunwuru yoonba na i din pukin Masia poe ki bi pii-i, ki i bia fiir nanɔ nyunwuru maŋ ni, i yada nba be Yennu paŋ toona nba din fiin Masia kuun ni na paak. 13I din tee kpeemme sianyoo, kimaan i toonbiit paak nan ya din ki tee Juu teeb, ki bia din ki mɔk sennu na paak. Ŋaan mɔtana Yennu nyik chab i toonbiite, ki turi manfoor Masia ni. 14Ba din kpaau dapunpunn paak yoonba na, u din kpeen ti biit pann nba din sɔb dɔɔ na nan li sennu munn munn na. 15Dapunpunn maŋ paake, ki Masia din biir Sintaanii nan narinbiit paŋ kura, ki din nyinn wa din nyannib biaŋinba na siara paanu ni.
16Li paak, i daa te ki sɔɔ senti sennu ya sii di linba, amii ya sii nyu linba; i daa te ki sɔɔ gani jaamm dagaŋŋa, amii ŋmaapaaŋ daa, amii foon daa ki teeniini. 17Li sennu booru din tee yunyuŋo ki want nan sɔɔe baat; li yɔɔe tee Masia. 18Sɔɔ-i yaa ŋɔɔe gar leeb ki laat yerintii ki loon a yi sikin i mɔŋ kii jiantir malakanba, i daa saak man; nna sa biiriwa. Li daanɔ dont u mɔŋ u dudukbiit ni, ŋaan ki li kaa paaki; 19u ji ki dia Masia; ŋɔɔ Masia tee u niib yur, ŋaan taamm, ki bi gaa nan leeb kaman nan gbanant tukita nan li jiina na, ŋɔɔ Masiae dintib ki bi nɔbii kaman nan Yennu nba loon biaŋinba na.
Barmɔnii manfoor tee Masia yare
20I kpo nan Masia, ki u fati dunia ni bona paŋ yent ni, ki bee ki i jin be kaman nan i bia daa tee tingbouŋ na yabi? Bee te ki i jɔbi i mɔŋ tee sennii nba yaa a 21“A daa so nanna,” amii “A daa lemi linba na,” amii “A daa siit bonsia,” 22linba ki nirɔ sa di ki lin biir ki kan yukiri. Li sennii tee nisaarii wannue. 23Setik nan mɔniie, li sennboorii naan linba mɔk yame, ŋaan li ki mɔki; bi jiantir ba loon biaŋinbae, ki sikint bi mɔŋ, ki mukis bi gbanant; ŋaan nirɔ-i wei linba na kur, li kan fit sommɔ ki wun dia u gbanant loommu.

english_text:
1 You have been raised to life with Christ. Now set your heart on what is in heaven, where Christ rules at God's right side. 2Think about what is up there, not about what is here on earth. 3You died, which means that your life is hidden with Christ, who sits beside God. 4Christ gives meaning to your life, and when he appears, you will also appear with him in glory.
5Don't be controlled by your body. Kill every desire for the wrong kind of sex. Don't be immoral or indecent or have evil thoughts. Don't be greedy, which is the same as worshiping idols. 6God is angry with people who disobey him by doing these things. 7And this is exactly what you did, when you lived among people who behaved in this way. 8But now you must stop doing such things. You must quit being angry, hateful, and evil. You must no longer say insulting or cruel things about others. 9 And stop lying to each other. You have given up your old way of life with its habits.
10 Each of you is now a new person. You are becoming more and more like your Creator, and you will understand him better. 11It doesn't matter if you are a Greek or a Jew, or if you are circumcised or not. You may even be a barbarian or a Scythian, and you may be a slave or a free person. Yet Christ is all that matters, and he lives in all of us.
12 God loves you and has chosen you as his own special people. So be gentle, kind, humble, meek, and patient. 13 Put up with each other, and forgive anyone who does you wrong, just as Christ has forgiven you. 14Love is more important than anything else. It is what ties everything completely together.
15Each one of you is part of the body of Christ, and you were chosen to live together in peace. So let the peace that comes from Christ control your thoughts. And be grateful. 16 Let the message about Christ completely fill your lives, while you use all your wisdom to teach and instruct each other. With thankful hearts, sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs to God. 17Whatever you say or do should be done in the name of the Lord Jesus, as you give thanks to God the Father because of him.
Some Rules for Christian Living
18 A wife must put her husband first. This is her duty as a follower of the Lord.
19 A husband must love his wife and not abuse her.
20 Children must always obey their parents. This pleases the Lord.
21 Parents, don't be hard on your children. If you are, they might give up.
22 Slaves, you must always obey your earthly masters. Try to please them at all times, and not just when you think they are watching. Honor the Lord and serve your masters with your whole heart. 23Do your work willingly, as though you were serving the Lord himself, and not just your earthly master. 24In fact, the Lord Christ is the one you are really serving, and you know he will reward you. 25 But Christ has no favorites! He will punish evil people, just as they deserve.
translation_text:
1I din fiir nan Masia kuun ni, li paak yi jii i para gaan nan sanpaapo bona, siaminba ki Masia kar Yennu niidiitu po na. 2Yii lor i dudukit nan leŋ bona na, ŋaan daa lorinn nan tingbouŋ na bona kaa, 3kimaan i poŋ din kpo nan Masia, ki bɔr i manfoor nanɔ Yennu ni. 4Masia nba teent manfoor na nba tan sa dɔkit yoonba, i mun tan sa dɔkit nanɔe nan yentchiauŋ.
Manfopaann nan manfokper labaar
5Li paak, yi nyik tingbouŋ na ni niauŋ nba tuun toona i ni na; li yenninbae tee bonchonchonsin, nan linba tee jakint toona, nan kunkɔmaasu, nan nianbiiuk, nan nianchiauŋ, linba tee patmannu booru. 6Nanna paake te ki Yennu wutoor tan sa do binba ki sak u mɔb na paak. 7Sianyoo li niauŋo din tuu yeni, ki i din be li ni.
8Ŋaan mɔtana yi fian linba na nba munna ki lu; ki linba tee naŋ, nan wutoor, nan nifobiisin, nan niib sanbiiruk, nan barbiit, i daa te ki li nyi i mɔi ni. 9I daa fa faak ki teen leebi, kimaan i poŋ nyik linba tuu tee i binbenkper na nan li toonawa; 10ŋaan ji mɔk linba tee binbenpaaŋe. Binbenpaaŋ nee ki Yennu nba tee li nantɔɔ na pantir yoo kura, ki li naan u mɔŋ binbeŋ, a wun te ki yin bannɔ setik nan mɔnii. 11Li paak, bɔkitu ji kaa binba tee Juu teeb nan binba ki tee Juu teeb sinsuuk ni; binba pot puna nan binba ki pot kura ji ki mɔk bɔkitu nan leebi; bɔkitu ji kaa muuk ni niib nan doo ni yab sinsuuk ni; bɔkitu bia kaa daaba nan yentinmɔŋ damm sinsuuk ni, ŋaan i kur tee Masia yab, ki u be i kur ni.
12Li paak, ya tee Yennu nigannkab ki tee popeendamm ki bia tee u walonkai na, yii mɔk ninkpabauŋ, nan burchint, nan sikininmɔŋ, nan soonna binbeŋ, nan sukuru, 13kii di leeb sukuru; ki sɔɔ-i mɔk bunburimii u lɔɔ paak, wun nyik chabɔ. Li kpaa talas ki yin mun nyik chab leeb kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ nba poŋ nyik chabi biaŋinba na. 14Yi pukin loomm; ŋanne fan ki gar binbeŋ kur, ki taan dia li kura nan barmɔnii mɔtaauk. 15Yi ŋaan ki Masia parmaasir nba na-ii dia ya dukii linba; Yennu lakin yiini gbananyennkɔɔ ni a yii mɔk parmaasir maŋe. Li paak, yii teen Yennu niipoouk man. 16Li kpaa talas ki Masia labaar na-ii be i para ni chib chib, kii want i leeb, kii bia kpaan i leeb nan i yan kur; kii yiin Yennu gbouŋ ni yanii, nan yenpaku yanii, nan Seyeeŋ yanii; kii yiin yanii kii teen Yennu nan niipoouk i para ni. 17Li paak, ya tuun linba, amii ya yeen linba, yii tuun nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni, kii teen ti Baa Yennu niipoouk u sann ni.
Masia niib binbeŋ labaar
18Poob, yi jii i mɔŋ tur i sɔrib, kaman nan la kpaa talas nan Masia niib-ii tuun biaŋinba.
19Poosɔrib, yii loon i poob man, ki daa dia nibisin benu ki be nammi.
20Waas, yii saak i damm mɔi yoo kura, kimaan ŋanne pent Yennu par ni.
21Baanba, i daa dont i waas wutoa, i-i tee nna bi sii mɔk parbiire.
22Daaba, yii saak i yomdamm mɔi bonsiar kur ni, ki li daa tee yoo nba ki bi gorii-i na kuukɔɔ kaa, a yin penn bi para ki gaar pinpakiti, ŋaan yii tuun nan nunmɔnn yoo kura, kimaan ya baak ti Yomdaanɔ biaŋinba na. 23I-i tuun linba kur, yin tun nan nunmɔnn kaman nan i tuun teen ti Yomdaanɔe, ŋaan ki li ki tee nisaarik kaa. 24Yi tian man nan ti Yomdaanɔ maŋ tan sa turi nyɔɔt nba tee u niib yar sanpaapo, kimaan Masia tee barmɔnii Yomdaanɔ ki i tuun teenɔ. 25Ki biitdaanɔ mun tan sa gaar u toonbiit tubdatu, wa tun linba paak, kimaan Yennu tan sa dat u tuba, ki lukitin kaa.

english_text:
1 Slave owners, be fair and honest with your slaves. Don't forget that you have a Master in heaven.
2Never give up praying. And when you pray, keep alert and be thankful. 3Be sure to pray that God will make a way for us to spread his message and explain the mystery about Christ, even though I am in jail for doing this. 4Please pray that I will make the message as clear as possible.
5 When you are with unbelievers, always make good use of the time. 6 Be pleasant and hold their interest when you speak the message. Choose your words carefully and be ready to give answers to anyone who asks questions.
Final Greetings
7 Tychicus is the dear friend, who faithfully works and serves the Lord with us, and he will give you the news about me. 8I am sending him to cheer you up by telling you how we are getting along. 9 Onesimus, the dear and faithful follower from your own group, is coming with him. The two of them will tell you everything that has happened here.
10 Aristarchus is in jail with me. He sends greetings to you, and so does Mark, the cousin of Barnabas. You have already been told to welcome Mark, if he visits you. 11Jesus, who is known as Justus, sends his greetings. These three men are the only Jewish followers who have worked with me for the kingdom of God. They have given me much comfort.
12 Your own Epaphras, who serves Christ Jesus, sends his greetings. He always prays hard that you may fully know what the Lord wants you to do and that you may do it completely. 13I have seen how much trouble he has gone through for you and for the followers in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
14 Our dear doctor Luke sends you his greetings, and so does Demas.
15Give my greetings to the followers at Laodicea, especially to Nympha and the church that meets in her home.
16After this letter has been read to your people, be sure to have it read in the church at Laodicea. And you should read the letter that I have sent to them.
17 Remind Archippus to do the work that the Lord has given him to do.
18I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
Don't forget that I am in jail.
I pray that God will be kind to you.
translation_text:
1Yomdamm, yii tuun fanu tur i daaba man, kii mi nan i mɔŋ mun mɔk Yomdaanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni.
Kpaanii labaar
2Yii yabii nan Yenmiaru man, kii mei ŋɔɔ Yennu nan nunmɔnn, ki turɔ niipoouk, 3kii biakii meiɔ kii teent ki tin fit la yaak ki mɔɔnt barŋanii ki fiit Masia woonin ni barii, kimaan barii maŋ paake te ki n kɔɔ dansarik na. 4Li paak, yii mei Yennu kii teenin ki n fit pak kaman nan la kpaa talas nan min pakir fanu biaŋinba na.
5I mi man i binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba ki tur binba ki tee Masia niib na, kii jii i yoo kii tuun fanu. 6Li kpaa talas ki i mɔbona-ii man, kii mɔk baakir, ki yii mi biaŋinba ki i sa gat buboit ki lii took ki tur sɔɔ kura.
Foontjoontii labaar
7Ti naa bilonkak Tikikus, wunba tee yada daanɔ, ki bia tee ti toontunanlɔɔ ti Yomdaanɔ ni na sa beti n labaar. 8Li paake te ki n tumɔ i boor ni, ma wun beti ti kur nba be biaŋinba, ki i para-n dɔɔr. 9Ŋɔɔ nan Onesimus, wunba tee ti naa bilonkak nba mɔk yada ki tee i yɔɔ nee sa saan. Bi sa beti bonsiar kura nba tuun nna na.
10Aristakus, min nan wunba kɔɔ dansarik na foonti, ki Banabas niipoo bija Mak mun bia foonti. (I daan poŋ gaar n kpaanii, ma Mak-i taŋŋi baar i boor ni, yin gaarɔ.) 11Joshua, wunba sann yi Justus na mun teeni u foontii. Bi banntaa nba na kuukɔɔe tee Juu teeb nba waa Masia ki tuun nanin Yennu naan toona maŋ ni. Ki bi maak n par.
12Epafras wunba mun bia tee i yenɔ nan Yisa Masia toontunnɔ na foonti. U mei Yennu nan nunmɔnn yoo kura ki teeni a ŋɔɔ Yennu-n te ki yin set kenken, kii tee nisubirteeb nba tuun Yennu loomm na, 13kimaan n dii u siara nan u set tun bonchiann ki sommiti nan Laodisea teeb nan Hierapoli teeb. 14Ti naa bilonkak Luk, wunba tee baat-tebirɔ na, ŋɔɔ nan Demas foonti.
15Yii foont ti naa walonkai nba be Laodisea na, ki bia foont ti niipoo Nimfa nan Masiaweira nba lakin leeb u ŋaak ni a bin jiantir Yennu na. 16I-i karin gbouŋ na gbenn yoonba, yin yabir ki bia karin ki tur Masiaweira nba mun be Laodisea na, ki bia karin Laodisea teeb gbouŋ nba ki n daan sɔb turib na. 17Yi bet Akipus a wun yabir u mɔŋ kii tuun toona nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ din turɔ na fanu.
18N sɔb linba na nan n tiauŋ nuue: Min Pɔɔl foonti. I daa tammit ma baa nan bant na po.
Yennu-n sommi.

english_text:
1 From Paul, Silas, and Timothy.
To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God the Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
I pray that God will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
2We thank God for you and always mention you in our prayers. Each time we pray, 3we tell God our Father about your faith and loving work and about your firm hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
The Thessalonians' Faith and Example
4My dear friends, God loves you, and we know he has chosen you to be his people. 5When we told you the good news, it was with the power and assurance that come from the Holy Spirit, and not simply with words. You knew what kind of people we were and how we helped you. 6 So, when you accepted the message, you followed our example and the example of the Lord. You suffered, but the Holy Spirit made you glad.
7You became an example for all the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia. 8And because of you, the Lord's message has spread everywhere in those regions. Now the news of your faith in God is known all over the world, and we don't have to say a thing about it. 9Everyone is talking about how you welcomed us and how you turned away from idols to serve the true and living God. 10They also tell how you are waiting for his Son Jesus to come from heaven. God raised him from death, and on the day of judgment Jesus will save us from God's anger.
translation_text:
1Min Pɔɔl nan Sailas nan Timotie sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen niib nba be Tesalonika ki tee ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nigannkab na; ŋɔɔ Yennu-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir.
Tesalonika teeb yada nan bi binbeŋ labaar
2Ti teen Yennu niipoouk i kura paak, ŋaan tian i po yoo kur ti Yenmiaru ni, 3kimaan Yennu nba tee ti Baa na tɔɔnn po, ti tian biaŋinba ki i teenɔ yada ki tuun toonŋana, nan i loomm nba te ki i tuun nan nunmɔnn biaŋinba, ki i dindann be ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia ni chib chib biaŋinba. 4Ti naa waas, ti mi nan Yennue looni ki poŋ ganni a yii tee u yab, 5kimaan barŋanii na din ki tee ti mɔb ni mɔmaan kuukɔɔ kaa ki ti din baar nann turiini, ŋaan li bia din mɔk Seyeeŋ yikooe ki ti piakir ki birsuk kaa. Yoo nba ki ti din be i peŋ na, i mi ta din be biaŋinba; i sommir paake ki ti din be nna maŋ. 6I din tokii biaŋinba ki ti din waa ti Yomdaanɔ, ki din gaar barŋanii maŋ i farchiauŋ ni nan parpeenn nba nyi Seyeeŋ boor ni na. 7Ki ya din tuun biaŋinba na din wann ti naa waas nba be Masedonia nan Akaya tinii ni na mun nba sii tuun biaŋinba na. 8Kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ barŋanii maŋ din nyii i boore, ki lin Masedonia nan Akaya tinii ni, ki linba bia pukin, ya teen Yennu yada biaŋinba poŋ lin lokir kur powa. Li paak, li ji ki kpaa talas nan ki ti pak siar bi boori. 9Niib na kur piak jiint ya din gaarit biaŋinba ta din foonti yoonba na, bi piak biaŋinba ki i tunn nyik pata ŋaan baar Yennu boore, a yii jiantir ŋɔɔ Yennu nba tee manfodaanɔ na, 10ki guur u Bija Yisa, wunba tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni ki baar na; wunba ki u din fiinɔ kuun ni, ki u fatit Yennu wutoor nba baat na ni na.

english_text:
Paul's Work in Thessalonica
1My friends, you know our time with you wasn't wasted. 2 As you remember, we had been mistreated and insulted at Philippi. But God gave us the courage to tell you the good news about him, even though many people caused us trouble. 3We didn't have any hidden motives when we won you over, and we didn't try to fool or trick anyone. 4God was pleased to trust us with his message. We didn't speak to please people, but to please God who knows our motives.
5You also know we didn't try to flatter anyone. God himself knows what we did wasn't a cover-up for greed. 6We were not trying to get you or anyone else to praise us. 7But as apostles, we could have demanded help from you. After all, Christ is the one who sent us. We chose to be like children or like a mother nursing her baby. 8We cared so much for you, and you became so dear to us, that we were willing to give our lives for you when we gave you God's message.
9My dear friends, you surely haven't forgotten our hard work and hardships. You remember how night and day we struggled to make a living, so we could tell you God's message without being a burden to anyone. 10Both you and God are witnesses that we were pure and honest and innocent in our dealings with you followers of the Lord. 11You also know we did everything for you that parents would do for their own children. 12We begged, encouraged, and urged each of you to live in a way that would honor God. He is the one who chose you to share in his own kingdom and glory.
13We always thank God that you believed the message we preached. It came from him, and it isn't something made up by humans. You accepted it as God's message, and now he is working in you. 14 My friends, you did just like God's churches in Judea and like the other followers of Christ Jesus there. And so, you were mistreated by your own people, in the same way they were mistreated by their people.
15 Those evil people killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and they even chased us away. God doesn't like what they do and neither does anyone else. 16They keep us from speaking his message to the Gentiles and from leading them to be saved. They have always gone too far with their sins. Now God has finally become angry and will punish them.
Paul Wants To Visit the Church Again
17My friends, we were kept from coming to you for a while, but we never stopped thinking about you. We were eager to see you and tried our best to visit you in person. 18We really wanted to come. I myself tried several times, but Satan always stopped us. 19After all, when the Lord Jesus appears, who else but you will give us hope and joy and be like a glorious crown for us? 20You alone are our glory and joy!
translation_text:
Pɔɔl toona Tesalonika doo ni labaar
1Ti naa waas, i mɔŋ mi nan ta din baar foonti na, li tan ki teen yanni. 2I mi nan ta lek daa din be Filipi doo ni, ki bi dinnit fara nan fei biaŋinba, ki see ti paak bonchiann na, ŋaan ta din baar i boor ni, ti din mɔk parcheenn nba nyi Yennu boor ni nan tin mɔɔnt barŋanii maŋ turi. 3Kimaan ta din beeri linba na din ki tee linba din nyii faak nan dudukbiit ni, amii ti din ki kpanni kaa, 4ŋaan ti mɔɔntir kaman nan Yennu nba loon tii mɔɔntir u maan biaŋinbae, kimaan u poŋ la ki ti jaŋ nan tin dia barŋanii maŋ. Li paak, ti ki mɔɔntir ki pent nisaarik par ni kaa, ŋaan ti pent Yennu nba bikii ti dudukit na par nie. 5Kimaan i mɔŋ mi fanu nan Yennu tee ti siara daanɔ nan ti din ki baar i boor ni nan mɔmant kaa, amii maan nba dɔkin ti nianchiauŋ. 6Ti din ki loon i sɔɔ-n pakiti, amii nisɔɔ. 7Ta tee Masia toomiinba na, ti din mɔk paŋ nba ki ti din bo sa fit boi li powa, ŋaan ti din be i sinsuuk ni soonnae, ki din tee kaman nan biyɔɔrɔɔ nba yɔɔr waas biaŋinba na. 8Ta din looni bonchiann na paak, ti din mɔɔntir Yennu barŋanii ki teeni; li kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan ti bia din jii ti mɔŋ mɔŋ manfoa ki likin saaŋmanii i paak, kimaan i mantik teen ti niloonkab ki li yabita. 9Ti naa waas, setik nan barmɔniie, i mi ta din tuun di fara biaŋinba. Ta din be i boor ni ki mɔɔntir Yennu barŋanii maŋ ki teeni na, ti din tuun yennu ni nan nyiɔko, a ti daa teeni farsiari.
10Yimm nan Yennue tee ti siaradamm ki jiin ta din be nani nan binbenyeeŋ nan yentinu, ki kaa biit biaŋinba na po. 11I mi biaŋinba ki ti din kpaani kaman nan waas baa nba tuu kpaamm biaŋinba na, 12ki maant i para, ki lek tun kpaani a yii be binbensiak nba sa gboo Yennu numm. Ŋɔɔe yiini a wun kɔɔni u tiauŋ naan ni nan yentchiauŋ.
13Linba yaa paak bia te ki ti piak Yennu yoo kura na tee nnee: ta din baar nan Yennu maan i boor ni, i din gbatir ki gaarir; i din ki gaarir kaman nan nisaarik yar na kaa, ŋaan i din gaarir kaman nan li tee Yennu yare. Ki li set tee ŋanne, kimaan yimm nba teenɔ yada na, maan maŋ tuun i para ni. 14Ti naa waas, li muksubooru nba baari na, li muksubooru mun din baar binba tee Yennu niib Yisa Masia paak na Judea tiŋ ni; yimm nan ŋamm dii muksuboor-yeŋkɔɔe. I mɔŋ mɔŋ niib nba mukisi biaŋinba na, li muksuboorue ki bi niib, Juu teeb, mun din mukisib. 15Ŋamm Juu teebe din kpii ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nan Yennu sɔkiniinba, ki bia mukis timm na. Linba na paak, bi ki penn Yennu par ni, ki bia ki penn nirɔ kur par ni. 16Bi yabii bi mɔŋ paak gbaa a bin gɔɔrit ki ti daa mɔɔntir Yennu barŋanii ki tur binba ki tee Juu teeb na a bin la tinnu. Linba nee want nan bi poŋ pukin bi toonbiit nba ki bi tuu tuune mɔkmɔk nan Yennu wutoor nba ji daan tan baar nan tubdatu bi paak.
Pɔɔl nba loon wun bia gɔn Tesalonika teeb biaŋinba
17Ti naa waas, la jiit yoo waaminna ki ti ki be i boor ni na, ti tee nan kpeebii nee, ŋaan ti dudukit ŋanin be nani, ki ti lek bia yabii a ti baar lai nan ti ninbina, 18kimaan yoo kur, min Pɔɔl lek loon ki n baar i boor nee, ŋaan ki Sintaanii gɔin sɔnu. 19Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa-i jen yoonba, yimme sii tee ti dindann nan ti nyɔɔt, ki i paake ki ti tan sa set u boor nan parpeenn nan yuugontir. 20Setik nan mɔnii, i paake ki ti mɔk sann nan parpeenn.

english_text:
1 Finally, we couldn't stand it any longer. We decided to stay in Athens by ourselves 2and send our friend Timothy to you. He works with us as God's servant and preaches the good news about Christ. We wanted him to make you strong in your faith and to encourage you. 3We didn't want any of you to be discouraged by all these troubles. You knew we would have to suffer, 4because when we were with you, we told you this would happen. And we did suffer, as you well know. 5At last, when I could not wait any longer, I sent Timothy to find out about your faith. I hoped Satan had not tempted you and made all our work useless.
6 Timothy has now come back from his visit with you and has told us about your faith and love. He also said that you always have happy memories of us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
7My friends, even though we have a lot of trouble and suffering, your faith makes us feel better about you. 8Your strong faith in the Lord is like a breath of new life. 9How can we possibly thank God enough for all the happiness you have brought us? 10Day and night we sincerely pray that we will see you again and help you to have an even stronger faith.
11We pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus will let us visit you. 12May the Lord make your love for each other and for everyone else grow by leaps and bounds. This is how our love for you has grown. 13And when our Lord comes with all his people, I pray he will make your hearts pure and innocent in the sight of God the Father.
translation_text:
1Li paak, la ji daan tan paak ti nii yoonba, ti daan sak biar Atens doo nie ti kuukɔɔ, 2ŋaan daan tun ti naa bik Timoti, wunba ki timm nan ŋɔɔ taan tuun ki teen Yennu ki mɔɔntir Masia barŋanii, a wun chee i yada ki kɔɔnni para, 3ki lin te ki i sɔɔ daa ŋmat poor muksuk na paaki. I mɔŋ mi nan li muksubooru tee Yennu loomme ki turit, 4kimaan ta din daa be i boor ni yoonba na, ti din beti a bi tan sa mukisit; ki mɔtana i mɔŋ mi fanu nan li set tan baar tee nnee chib chib. 5Li paak, la daan tan paak n nii yoonba, ki n daan tun Timoti, a wun saa bann a i nyik Masia sɔnu amii, ki laan Sintaanii bikini, ki ti toona fara kur teen yannee?
6Mɔtana Timoti poŋ jenna, ki turit labaar nba penn ti para ni ki jikit saa i yada nan i loomm po. U betit a i kpan tun tian ti poe fanu, ki loon yin lat kaman nan ta loon tin lai biaŋinba na. 7Ti naa waas, ti be fara nan parbiir kur ni, ŋaan ta gbat i yada po, li te ki ti para dɔɔre. 8Mɔtana, li set maŋŋit nan ya set chib ti Yomdaanɔ sɔnu ni na paak. 9Ti teen Yennu niipoouk bonchiann i paak, kimaan ta mɔk parpeenn biaŋinba u boor ni i paak na. 10Nyiɔk nan yennu kur ti meiɔ nan ti para kura a wun te ki timm nan yimm-n la leeb ki tin ŋamm i yada.
11Ti Baa Yennu tiauŋ nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa-n ŋamm sɔnu turit ki ti baar i boor ni. 12Ya loon i leeb nan sɔɔ kur biaŋinba na, wun te ki i loomm maŋ-n pukin bonchiann kaman nan ta looni biaŋinba na. 13Nnee ki u sa chee i para, ki i sii tee popeendamm nba ki mɔk biit ti Baa Yennu boor ni, nan yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa tan sa jen nan u yab kura na.

english_text:
A Life That Pleases God
1Finally, my dear friends, since you belong to the Lord Jesus, we beg and urge you to live as we taught you. Then you will please God. You are already living that way, but try even harder. 2Remember the instructions we gave you as followers of the Lord Jesus. 3God wants you to be holy, so don't be immoral in matters of sex. 4Respect and honor your wife. 5Don't be a slave of your desires or live like people who don't know God. 6You must not cheat any of the Lord's followers in matters of sex. Remember, we warned you that he punishes everyone who does such things. 7God didn't choose you to be filthy, but to be pure. 8So if you don't obey these rules, you are not really disobeying us. Instead, you are disobeying God, who gives you his Holy Spirit.
9We don't have to write you about the need to love each other. God has taught you to do this, 10and you already have shown your love for all his people in Macedonia. But, my dear friends, we ask you to do even more. 11Try your best to live quietly, to mind your own business, and to work hard, just as we taught you to do. 12 Then you will be respected by people who are not followers of the Lord, and you won't have to depend on anyone.
The Lord's Coming
13My friends, we want you to understand how it will be for those followers who have already died. Then you won't grieve over them and be like people who don't have any hope. 14We believe Jesus died and was raised to life. We also believe that when God brings Jesus back again, he will bring with him all who had faith in Jesus before they died. 15 Our Lord Jesus told us that when he comes, we won't go up to meet him ahead of his followers who have already died.
16 With a loud command and with the shout of the chief angel and a blast of God's trumpet, the Lord will return from heaven. Then those who had faith in Christ before they died will be raised to life. 17Next, all of us who are still alive will be taken up into the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the sky. From that time on we will all be with the Lord forever. 18Encourage each other with these words.
translation_text:
Binbeŋ nba pent Yennu par labaar
1Ti naa waas, ya din tumii ti peŋ biaŋinba ki i sii tuun ki pent Yennu par ni na, nnee ki i poŋ din tuun. Mɔtana, ti barimiie ki kpaani ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann paak, yii ŋammit kii tuun nna man, 2kimaan i mi kpaanii nba ki ti din turi ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann ni na. 3Kimaan Yennu loon ki yii tee popeendamm ki daa tuun bonchonchonn toona. 4Sɔɔ kur-n bann wa sa kɔɔn poo nan binbenyeeŋ nan baakir sɔnu biaŋinba, 5ki daa tuun nna nan nianbiiuk binbeŋ kaman nan binba ki mi Yennu nba be biaŋinba na. 6Sɔɔ daa tuun biir u lɔɔ nba waa Masia nan li biitbooru ni, amii kii ŋmab u lɔɔ. Ti poŋ din beti li powa, ŋaan bia kpaani nan nunmɔnn a Yennu tan sa dat binba tuun nna tuba. 7Yennu ki yiint a tii tee biitdamm kaa, ŋaan u yaa tii tee popeendamme. 8Li paak, wunba ki sak wannu nba na, li ki tee nisaarik yar kaa ki u yêeni, ŋaan u yêen Yennu nba teeni u Seyeeŋ na mɔŋ mɔŋe.
9Li ki kpaa talas ki tin sɔb gbouŋ ki tiari ya sii loon Masiaweira biaŋinba. Yennu mɔŋe poŋ wanni biaŋinba ki i sii loon i leeb. 10Ki i poŋ set tuun li biaŋe na, ki loon ti naa waas kur nba be Masedonia tiŋ ni na. Ti naa waas, ti barimiie a yii ŋammit kii tuun nna man, 11ki ŋaan ki lii tee i dudukit ki yii be soonna, ki i baka-ii be i mɔŋ toona ni, kii tuun i tiauŋ toona kii di, kaman nan ta poŋ din beti biaŋinba sinsinn na. 12Ŋanne sa te ki binba ki tee Masiaweira na-n turi nibaakir, ki li ji kii kpaa talas ki yin jɔɔb i mɔŋ ki denn sɔɔ i bonlonkara paaki.
Ti Yomdaanɔ jenu labaar
13Ti naa waas, ti loon ki yin bann barmɔniie ki jikit saa Masiaweira nba kpo na po, ki daa mɔk parbiir kaman nan binba ki mɔk dindann na. 14Ti teen yada nan Yisa din kpo ŋaan bia fiir kuun niwa; li paak, ki ti bia teen yada nan Yennu tan sa te ki Yisa-n jen nan u weira nba din kpo na. 15Ta wanti linba na tee ti Yomdaanɔ mɔmaame: ti Yomdaanɔ maŋ jenu daar, damm nba tan sii be bi manfoa ni na kan dɔŋ binba din kpo na ki chet Masia sanpaapo, 16kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni ki sik nan tintian paari, nan malaka saakɔɔ yiiniie, nan Yennu kikaak mɔnii, ki Masiaweira nba din kpo na tan sa fiir sinsinn; 17li yoo, damm nba tan sii be bi manfoa ni na nan ŋamm tan sa chet ti Yomdaanɔ sanpaapo; ki timm nan ti Yomdaanɔ ji tan sa taan leeb ki be nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. 18Li paak, yii dia mɔmaan na kii maant i leeb para man.

english_text:
1I don't need to write you about the time or date when all this will happen. 2 You surely know that the Lord's return will be as a thief coming at night. 3People will think they are safe and secure. But destruction will suddenly strike them like the pains of a woman about to give birth. And they won't escape.
4My dear friends, you don't live in darkness, and so that day won't surprise you like a thief. 5You belong to the light and live in the day. We don't live in the night or belong to the dark. 6Others may sleep, but we should stay awake and be alert. 7People sleep during the night, and some even get drunk. 8 But we belong to the day. So we must stay sober and let our faith and love be like a suit of armor. Our firm hope that we will be saved is our helmet.
9God doesn't intend to punish us, but wants us to be saved by our Lord Jesus Christ. 10Christ died for us, so we could live with him, whether we are alive or dead when he comes. 11This is why you must encourage and help each other, just as you are already doing.
Final Instructions and Greetings
12My friends, we ask you to be thoughtful of your leaders who work hard and tell you how to live for the Lord. 13Show them great respect and love because of their work. Try to get along with each other. 14My friends, we beg you to warn anyone who isn't living right. Encourage anyone who feels left out, help all who are weak, and be patient with everyone. 15Don't be hateful to people, just because they are hateful to you. Rather, be good to each other and to everyone else.
16Always be joyful 17and never stop praying. 18Whatever happens, keep thanking God because of Jesus Christ. This is what God wants you to do.
19Don't turn away God's Spirit 20or ignore prophecies. 21Put everything to the test. Accept what is good 22and don't have anything to do with evil.
23I pray that God, who gives peace, will make you completely holy. And may your spirit, soul, and body be kept healthy and faultless until our Lord Jesus Christ returns. 24The one who chose you can be trusted, and he will do this.
25Friends, please pray for us.
26Give the Lord's followers a warm greeting.
27In the name of the Lord I beg you to read this letter to all his followers.
28I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
translation_text:
Teent siir ti Yomdaanɔ jenu po man
1Ti naa waas, li ki kpaa talas ki tin sɔb wanni yoo nba ki linba na tan sa tume, 2kimaan i mɔŋ poŋ mi fanu nan ti Yomdaanɔ jenu daar maŋ tan sii tee kaman nan nanyukɔɔ nba tuu baat nyiɔk biaŋinba nee. 3Yoo nba ki niib tan sii yeen a “Parmaasir nan laafia be na,” li yoo maŋe ki biiru tan sa baarib yiama kaman nan matyiaru nba baat poo biaŋinba na, ŋaan bi tan kan fit fat bi mɔŋ li barbiiuk ni. 4Ti naa waas, yimm ki be bunbɔnn ni kaa; li paak, li daar tan kii tee kaman nan nanyukɔɔ baaru na kaa i boor ni, 5kimaan i tee yentu waase, ti ki tee nyiɔk yab kaa; ti ki be bunbɔnn ni kaa. 6Li paak, ti daa dɔɔ gɔɔnt kaman nan nisiab nba tuun biaŋinba na, ŋaan tii nyɔi kii sub man, 7kimaan binba gɔɔnt na gɔɔnt nyiɔko, ki binba nyu daan yib na mun yib nyiɔk. 8Ŋaan timm nba ki tee nyiɔk yab na, tii sub man, ki jii yada nan loomm lia kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir na, ki bia jii tinnu dindann ki yir kaman nan naagbankɔŋŋir na. 9Kimaan Yennu din ki gannit a wun tan dat ti tuba kaa, ŋaan u gannit a tin la tinnu ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak; 10ŋɔɔe din gaar ti paak ki kpo, a ti-i lekii kpo amii ti-i be u jenu yoo maŋ, timm nan ŋɔɔ-n tan taan kii be. 11Li paak, yii kɔɔnt i leeb para ki somm i leeb kaman nan ya tuun biaŋinba mɔtana na.
Kpaanjoontii nan foontjoontii labaar
12Ti naa waas, ti barimiie a i baka-ii be binba tuun ti Yomdaanɔ toonn nani, ki tee i yudamm u sɔnu ni, ki teeni kpaanii. 13Yii teemm nibaakir nan loomm nba yab, kimaan bi toona nba ki bi tuun na paak. I mɔŋ-ii be nan i leeb nan parmaasir man.
14Ti naa waas, ti barimiie a yi kpaan gbannyakdamm, ki maŋ jaŋmaandamm para, ki somm tarii, ki di sukuru nan sɔɔ kura. 15I kpan daa tuun biit ki jiint biit paaki, ŋaan yii te ki lii tee i lor yoo kur nan yin tun linba ŋan ki tur i leeb nan sɔɔ kur man.
16Yii mɔk parpeenn yoo kur man, 17kii mei Yennu yoo kur man, 18kii teen Yennu niipoouk bonsiar kur ni, kimaan ŋanne tee Yennu nba loon biaŋinba, ya tee Yisa Masiaweira na paak.
19I daa gɔi Yennu Seyeeŋ nba loon biaŋinba; 20i daa fiakit Yennu sɔkiniinba maami, 21ŋaan yi tuu mintaa ki bikin bonsiar kur, ki gaar linba ŋan, 22ki nyi biitbooru booru kura ni.
23Yennu nba teent parmaasir na tiauŋ-n teeni popeendamm ki gbenn, ki dia i sei nan i manfoa nan i gbanant mun, ki li daa mɔk biit ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia jenu maŋ po. 24Yennu nba yiini na sa sak tun nna, kimaan u tee barmɔnii daanɔe.
25Ti naa waas, yii mei Yennu ki turit man. 26Yii foont Masiaweira na kura ki kpammii.
27Ti Yomdaanɔ sann ni ki n kpaani, yi karin gbouŋ na tur Masiaweira kur.
28Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi.

english_text:
1 From Paul, Silas, and Timothy.
To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
2I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
When Christ Returns
3My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time. 4This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
5All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering. 6It is only right for God to punish everyone who is causing you trouble, 7but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels 8and with a flaming fire.
Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message. 9 Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength. 10This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
11God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do. 12Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
translation_text:
1Min Pɔɔl nan Sailas nan Timoti sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masiaweira nba be Tesalonika ki bia tee Yennu yab na. 2Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n sommi, ki bia turi parmaasir.
Masia baaru labaar
3Ti naa waas, li kpaa talase nan tii teen Yennu niipoouk yoo kur i paak. Ti-i tun nna, li ŋan, kimaan i yada mantik pukii bonchiann, ki ya mɔk loomm nba ki teen i leeb na pukii. 4Li paak te ki ti mɔk yuugontir i paak Yennu niib boor ni, i sukuru nan yada nba ki i mɔk i muksuk nan i fara ni, kii lek be nan gbankur na.
5Linba nee want nan Yennu barbunu tee mɔnii; li paak ki u tan sa yet a i jaŋ nan i be u naan ni; u naan maŋ paake ki i di fara maŋ. 6Yennue tuun linba took; u sa te ki binba dinti fara maŋ na-n mun di fara nnee, 7ki fat yimm nba di fara na, ki mun bia fatit. Yennu tan sa tun linba na yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa nan u yabint malakanba tan sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni ki baar nan mupeenu, 8ki tan dat binba ki sak u barŋanii ki bia ki mi Yennu na tuba; 9ki ti yabint Yomdaanɔ nba be yentchiauŋ ni tan sa nyinnib u boor ni, ki bi sa la biiru nba kaa gbennu; 10u tan sa tun nnee daar nba ki u tan sa baar ki gaar dontir nan paku u weira kur nba teenɔ yada na boor ni. I mun tan sii tee bi yemme, kimaan i poŋ teen barŋanii nba ki ti din beti na yadawa.
11Li paake ki ti mei Yennu yoo kur ki teeni. Ti meiɔ a wun te ki yin jaŋ nan manfoor nba ki u din ganni a i taŋŋi be leŋ na nie. Ti meiɔ a wun jii u paŋ ki te ki yii tuun toonŋana kur nba ki i loon a i tuun na, ki gbenn toona kur nba ki i tuun i yada paak na. 12Nnee sa te ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa sann na-n la dontir i paak, ki yin mun la dontir u paak, ŋɔɔ Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia ninbatinu paak.

english_text:
The Lord's Return
1 When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends, 2not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters. 3But don't be fooled! People will rebel against God. Then before the Lord returns, the wicked one who is doomed to be destroyed will appear. 4 He will brag and oppose everything holy or sacred. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God. 5Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
6You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come. 7His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way. 8 Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
9 When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs. 10Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it. 11So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie. 12All of them will be punished, because they would rather do evil than believe the truth.
Be Faithful
13My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth. 14God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ. 15My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
16God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father 17will encourage you and help you always to do and say the right thing.
translation_text:
Nifobiiuk labaar
1N naa waas, ki jikit saa ti Yomdaanɔ jenu maŋ nan timm nan ŋɔɔ nba tan sa chet leeb ki taan be na po, 2n loon ki n beti ki i daa mɔk jijeet i para ni amii jekiruk nan nisiab nba yaa a daar maŋ poŋ baara na po. Li pasiar bi beeri a timm din yet nna sɔkinii mɔmaan wannu ni, amii barii ni, amii gbouŋ ni. 3I daa te ki sɔɔ kpanni gbaa, kimaan li daar kan baar see ki mɔyêtuk yoo baar sinsinn, yoo nba ki niib bonchiann tan sa yêt Yennu mɔb na; ki nifobiiuk bia tan sa nyi paanuwa; ki Yennu poŋ sennɔ a u sa biirɔ. 4U tan kan sak Yenjiantu nan jiantii kura, ŋaan jɔɔb u mɔŋ teen yudaanɔ nba gar linba ki niib jiantir kura. U tan sa kɔɔ Yenjiantu diiuk ni gbaa ki pur u mɔŋ Yennu.
5Ma din be i boor ni yoonba na n din beti linba na kura; i ki tianii? 6Siare lek daa diau; i mi la tee linba, ŋaan ki u tan sa dɔkit Yennu tiauŋ yoo maŋ nie. 7Ki nifobiit ŋanin poŋ be ki tuun bi toona woonin ni, ki wunba diab nee lek daa sii diab mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Yennu tan sa berɔ. 8Ki yoo maŋ ki nifobiiuk maŋ tan sa nyi paanu ni, ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa tan sa kpiu nan ŋɔɔ Yisa tiauŋ mɔmaan, ki ŋɔɔ Yisa baaru nan u yentchiauŋ tan sa biirɔ. 9Nifobiiuk maŋ tan sa baar nan Sintaanii yaake ki tuun yaarlituk nan bakitnauŋ toonboori kur a wun kpann niib. 10U tan sa jɔɔb faak boori kur ki kpann binba ki Yennu tan sa biirib na. Bi tan sa biira, kimaan ba ki loon barmɔnii nba bo sa tinnib maŋ ki yêtir na paak. 11Li paake te ki Yennu te ki faak paŋ tuun bi ni, ki bi tee linba ki tee barmɔnii na yada. 12Yennu tan sa biir binba ki teen barmɔnii maŋ yada ŋaan loon toonbiit tumu na.
Yennu nba ganni ki tinni biaŋinba
13Ti naa waas, yimm nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ looni na, li kpaa talas ki ti pak Yennu yoo kur i paak, kimaan Yennu din ganni pinpiik neewa a yin la tinnu. I din teen barmɔnii barŋanii yada, ki u Seyeeŋ bia ŋammi ki teeni popeendamm, li paak te ki i la tinnu na. 14Ta din mɔɔnt barŋanii nba ki turi na, ki Yennu yiini ki tinni a yii be fanu kaman nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia nba be biaŋinba na. 15Ti naa waas, li paak, setir man chib, ki dia wannu nba ki ti din wanni ti mɔmaan ni, nan ti gbouŋ ni na.
16Ti Baa Yennu nan ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia binba loonit ki turit pardɔɔnn nba kaa gbennu nan dindann nba yab u ninbatinu paak na, 17bin kɔɔnni para ki turi paŋ ki yin fit tun linba ŋan kii bia piak barii nba ŋan yoo kur.

english_text:
Pray for Us
1Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do. 2Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith. 3But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm. 4He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying. 5I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ.
Warnings against Laziness
6My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you. 7You surely know that you should follow our example. We didn't waste our time loafing, 8and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
9We had the right not to work, but we wanted to set an example for you. 10We also gave you this rule: If you don't work, you don't eat. 11Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody. 12So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living. 13Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
14Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them. 15Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower.
Final Prayer
16I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
17I always sign my letters as I am now doing: PAUL.
18I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
translation_text:
Yii mei Yennu kii teent man
1Ti naa waas, linba joont tee nnee, yii mei Yennu kii teent man ki ti Yomdaanɔ barŋanii maŋ-n lin yian yian kii mɔk yikoo kaman nan la din tee i boor ni biaŋinba na. 2I bia kii mei Yennu ki wun nyinnit nifobiit nan biitdamm nuu ni, kimaan sɔɔ kur kaa teen barŋanii maŋ yada.
3Ŋaan ti Yomdaanɔ tee barmɔnii daanɔe, ki sa turi paŋ kii guui ki biitdaanɔ Sintaanii kan fit teeni siari. 4Ti Yomdaanɔ paak ki ti teen yada nan i tuun ta din beti linba na, kii bia sii tuun nna yoo kura.
5Ti Yomdaanɔ maŋ-n sommi ki yin bann Yennu nba loont biaŋinba, ki bia mɔk gbankur nba ki Masia teent na.
Li kpaa talas ki sɔɔ kur-ii tuun toona
6Ti naa waas, ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni ki ti beeri, yi nyinn i mɔŋ nan ti naa wasiat nba tee gbannyakdamm kura na sinsuuk ni, nan binba ki waa ta din turi kpaanii nba na sinsuuk ni.
7I mɔŋ poŋ mi fanu nan li kpaa talase ki yii tuun kaman nan ta din tuun biaŋinba na. Ta din be i boor ni na, ti din ki tiin gbannyakiri. 8Ti din ki mi gaar sɔɔ sommir yann ki ki pa li paaki. Ŋaan ti kpan din tuune ki nyi naauk, ki lek tuun yennu nan nyiɔk, a ti daa te ki sɔɔ biir i siar ti paaki. 9Ta din tuun nna maŋ na, li ki want nan ti ki mɔk yikoo nba sa boi sommir po kaa; ti din tun nna a ti teen nyinne ki turi ki yin weir. 10Ta din be i boor ni na ti din tuu yɔɔ beeri a “Wunba kur ki saak toona, li yɔɔ ki ŋan nan bin turɔ jeeti.”
11Ti gbia a nisiab be i sinsuuk ni ki ki loon toona, ŋaan lin kɔɔ bi leeb barii nie, li paak te ki ti beeri linba na. 12Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sann ni ki ti beer niib maŋ ki kpaamm a bi baka-ii be bi tiauŋ toona ni, kii be soonna, kii di bi naauk nyɔɔt.
13Ti naa waas, yii mi ki i bak nan toonŋana tumu man. 14Li pasiar ta teeni labaar nba ti gbouŋ ni na, nisɔɔ kan sakiri. Li-i tee nnaie, yin bann i yan ni nan li yɔɔ, ki i baka daa be u po, ki wun di fei. 15Ŋaan i daa teeu kaman nan u tee i dataake na, yi kpaanɔ kaman nan i naa bike na.
Barjoontii
16Ti Yomdaanɔ wunba teent parmaasir na-n turi parmaasir yoo kur, siar kura ni. Ti Yomdaanɔ-ii be nan i kur.
17Min Pɔɔl sɔbin nan n mɔŋ mɔŋ nuue ki foonti na. N gbant kura siarae na; n sɔbin ki joontir n gbant nnee. 18Ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia-n somm i kura.

english_text:
1From Paul.
God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
2 Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace!
Warning against False Teaching
3When I was leaving for Macedonia, I asked you to stay on in Ephesus and warn certain people there to stop spreading their false teachings. 4You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
5You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith. 6There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk. 7They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
