Li ki tee binba din tun toonbiit, ki bi gbanant tan biar muuk ni na kaa-aa? 18Yennu daa din por lamme mɔb a “Bi kan mi baar ma bo yaa n turib foon tiŋ nba na ni.” Li din tee binba din yêt u mɔb nee. 19Li paak, bi din ki fit kɔɔ tiŋ maŋ ni, kimaan bi yadakaarin paak.

english_text:
1The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out. 2We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good. 3 Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say,
“God became angry
and told the people,
‘You will never enter
my place of rest!’ ”
God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation. 4 In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested. 5 We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!” 6This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter. 7 Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said,
“If you hear his voice today,
don't be stubborn!”
8 If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest. 9But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come. 10 On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
11We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did. 12God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts. 13 Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth.
Jesus Is the Great High Priest
14We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him. 15Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin! 16So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
translation_text:
1Mɔtana Yennu ji tur timme mɔlor maŋ a tin la foon nba ki u din piak li po na. Li paak, i bann i yanni man ki daa ŋaant ki sɔɔ-n tan kɔŋ foon maŋi. 2Kimaan ti gbat barŋanii kaman nan ba mun din gbatir biaŋinba, ŋaan li din ki turib nyɔɔti, kimaan ba din gbatir yoonba bi din ki gaarir nan yada. 3Li paak, timm nba teen yada na, ti la foon maŋa. Li din tun kaman nan Yennu nba din yet biaŋinba na a
“N wutoore doo, ki n por mɔb a
ŋamm tan kan kɔɔ foon tiŋ nba
ki n din bo sa turib na ni.”
Ŋaan wa din tan yet linba na na, ki sɔɔ ki u poŋ tun u naamii toona gbenn pinpiik niwa. 4Kimaan Yennu gbouŋ ni siar po li poŋ yet li daaŋannlore daar maŋ po a “Ki Yennu din nyik u toona kura ki foi daaŋannlore daar.” 5Ŋaan ki u bia yet nna na a “Bi tan kan mi kɔɔ n foon tiŋ nba ki n din bo yaa n turib na ni.” 6Binba din gbat barŋanii na sinsinn na din ki la li foon, kimaan bi din yêt Yennu mɔb. Li paak, nisiab be ki tan sa la foon maŋ. 7Li paak, ki Yennu bia senn daleer ki yi daar maŋ “dinna.” Ki bina bonchiann din gar, ki Yennu din te ki David yet li po kaman nan la poŋ piak biaŋinba na a
“Dinna, i-i gbat Yennu kunkɔr yoonba,
ya daa nyar i tuba man.”
8Kimaan Joshua-i diŋŋi turib foon, li poorpo Yennu ji bo kan pak daleer po. 9Li paak, foon dasiar bia daa be ki tee Yennu niib yar, kaman nan wa din foi daaŋannlore daar biaŋinba na. 10Kimaan wunba la Yennu foon maŋ, u nyik u toona nee na kaman nan Yennu nba din nyik u naamii toona ni biaŋinba na. 11Li paak, ŋaant man ki tin yabir ti mɔŋ ki la foon maŋ, ŋaan ki ti sɔɔ tan daa kɔŋŋit kaman nan ba din yêt Yennu mɔb ki tan kɔŋŋir biaŋinba na. 12Kimaan Yennu mɔmaan maŋ tee barii nba foe, ki mɔk paŋ, ki bia di ki gar jukbanjiak nba kur ŋma mɔi ŋannlee. Yennu mɔmaan fit ŋma ki kɔɔ baat nisaarik seek nan u manfoor boor ni, ki kɔɔ baat u tukita nan u kpaba beesin ni, ki kɔɔ bɔkitir u dudukit nan u par ni bibikit. 13Ki bonnankar kur dɔɔ yeenin ni, ki Yennu wunba tan sa bu bi buut na ninbina laatib; ki bonsiar kaa ki bɔri.
Yisae tee ti mantɔjaann
14Li paak, ŋaant man ki tii dia ti yada nba ki ti fiit wann na kenken, kimaan ti mɔk mantɔyudaanjaann, ŋɔɔe tee Yennu Bija Yisa wunba poŋ gar saan Yennu tiauŋ boor ni na. 15Ti mantɔyudaanɔ maŋ ki tee wunba kan fit tint ninbaauk ti butuŋ paak kaa, ŋaan u mɔŋ din la bikinu sɔnii booru kur ni kaman nan ta mun laat biaŋinba na, ŋaan u lek din ki tun biiti. 16Li paak, ŋaant man ki tii mɔk parcheenn ki baar Yennu nba tee burchindaanɔ na naangbouŋ boor ni, a tin la ninbatinu nan sommir ki lin sommit yoo nba ki ti loonn na.

english_text:
1Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins. 2A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people. 3 This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others. 4 But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
5 This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him,
“You are my Son, because today
I have become your Father!”
6 In another place, God says,
“You are a priest forever
just like Melchizedek.”
7 God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers. 8Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God. 9Suffering made Jesus perfect, and now he can save forever all who obey him. 10This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek.
Warning against Turning Away
11Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand. 12 By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food. 13People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right. 14Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
translation_text:
1Tɔn, mantɔyudaanɔ kur nba ki bi gannɔ niib sinsuuk ni na, bi sennɔ a wun gaar u leeb paake ki mann mannu ki tur Yennu bi biit paak. 2Ŋaan u mɔŋ nba tee butundaanɔ na sɔnii bonchiann ni na paak, u sii fit be nan binba pɔɔr yan ki kpet sɔnu na soonna, 3ki u mɔŋ nba tee butundaanɔ na paak, li kpaa talas ki wun mann mannu u mɔŋ toonbiit paak kaman nan wa mann mannu niib na toonbiit paak biaŋinba na. 4Sɔɔ ki fit dont u mɔŋ a wuu tee mantɔyudaanɔ, see ki Yennu gannɔ kaman nan wa din gann Aron ki teenɔ mantɔyudaanɔ biaŋinba na.
5Nnee mun, Masia din ki donn u mɔŋ ki gann u mɔŋ a wuu tee mantɔyudaanɔ, ŋaan Yennue din yetɔ a
“A tee n Bijae;
la nyii dinna ki n wann nan
n tee a Baae.”
6Ki siar po u bia yet a
“A tan sii tee mantɔɔ ki jikit saa
yoo nba kaa gbennu,
kii tee Melkisedek paabu ni mantɔɔ.”
7Tɔn, Yisa nba din be tingbouŋ na ni yoonba na u din mei Yennu ki barimɔ, ki bui nan ninnyuut Yennu nba sa fit fatɔ kuun ni na boor ni. Ki wa din baakit Yennu na paak ki Yennu din gbat u miaruwa. 8U tee Yennu Bijae, ŋaan u mun din dii farae Yennu mɔbsaku ni, 9ki din tan gbenn fara dinu maŋ, ki te ki binba kur saak u mɔb na la tinnu nba kaa gbennu. 10Ŋanne ki Yennu yiinɔ manteeb yudaanɔ, Melkisedek paabu ni.
Yada lunu kpaanii labaar
11Ti bo sa pak Masia nan Melkisedek po bonchiann ŋaan li paar nan ki ti wanni, kimaan i ki bant li paak yiami. 12Nna yoo li ji bo kpaa talase ki yii tee wannteeb, ŋaan i bia loon ki sɔɔ-n wanni Yennu mɔmaan pinpiik ni sɔnii na. I bia daa ŋan nan biime, i ki jaŋ nan jeŋmankati, 13kimaan wunba daa mɔɔr biin tee bibunne, ki daa ki bann yentinu wannu na fanu. 14Ŋaan jeŋmankat tee saakab yare, ŋamm tuu tumii ki fit bann linba tee bonbiir nan linba tee bonŋann.

english_text:
1We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God. 2And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment. 3Let's grow up, if God is willing.
4-6But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
7A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field. 8 But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
9My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved. 10God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people. 11We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is. 12Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them.
God's Promise Is Sure
13No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham, 14 “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!” 15Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised. 16When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves. 17So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow. 18God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed.
We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us. 19 This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place. 20 Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
translation_text:
1Li paak, ŋaant man ki tii dia saakayan kii tumii Masia barii ki saan tɔɔnn po; ŋaan ŋamm ki tumii u barii bonchiann ki pukin pinpiik ni wannu po; li ji ki kpaa talas a bin ŋamm wanni a Nyikin toonbiit nan kuun sɔnu, ki teen Yennu yada, 2ki bia wanni Yennu nyunwuru po, nan niiyiinu niib paak, nan kpeemm fiiru po, nan tubdatu nba kaa gbennu na po. 3Yennu-i sak, ti sa saan tɔɔnne.
4Masiaweira nba yêt u weiu, li ji tan sii paar nan bin jenna ki tan lebit bi dudukit. Sianyoo bi din tuu be Yennu yentu nie, ki gaar bi tora nba tee Yennu Seyeeŋ na, ki lemm Yendɔuŋ ni piiniiwa, 5ki din tuu mi Yennu mɔmaan na manu po, ki din mi dunia nba baat na paŋ po, 6ŋaan ji tan yêt Masia weiu. Li ji paar nan bin ji tan jen lebit bi dudukit, kimaan bi bia kpi Yennu Bija maŋe dapunpunn paak ki dintɔ fei paanu ni.
7Tɔn, tiŋ nba ki saak baa li paak ki li maakit ki tee jeet teen binba ŋammitir na, Yennue senn mɔsonŋann ki teen tiŋ maŋ paak; 8ŋaan tiŋ maŋ-i tee kunkonii nan mɔbiit, li ki mɔk nyɔɔti, li kan yukir ki Yennu tan sa por mɔb ki lin di muu biir.
9Ti naa walonkai, ta lek piak linba na, ŋaan ti ki mɔk birsuk i po. Ti mi nan i yare tee linba chee ki bia tee tinnu yar. 10Kimaan Yennue ŋan, ki kan tamm ya tun linba ki turɔ amii ya loonɔ biaŋinba ki somm Masiaweira, ki bia daa sommitib na. 11Ti loon ki i sɔɔ kur-n yabir u mɔŋ paake mɔkmɔk nan li joontu, ki ya mɔk dindann biaŋinba na, li set sa teen mɔnii ki turi. 12Ti ki loon ki yii tee gbannyakdammi, ŋaan ti loon ki yii tee binba teen Masia yadae, kii mɔk sukuru, ki tan gaar linba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn li po na.
Yennu mɔsonn nba kaa birsuk
13Yennu nba din senn u mɔsonn tur Abraham yoonba na, u din lor mɔlor a u tan sa tun wa din senn mɔsonn nba nawa. Sɔɔ ki be ki chee Yennu tiauŋ; li paak, u din jii u mɔŋ sanne ki lor u mɔlor maŋ. 14U din yetɔ a “Li tee barmɔniie, n senn mɔsonŋann a paak ki bia sa tura yaaboona bonchiann.” 15Ki Abraham din mɔk sukurue, ki tan gaar linba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn a wun tan turɔ na. 16Nirɔ-i senn mɔsonn, u tuu jii wunba garɔ sanne ki senn, ki mɔsonn maŋ-n kpi mɔniɔk kura. 17Tɔn, binba din yaa bin gaar Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn nba na, u din loon wun wannib a u kan mi lebit u dudukiti; li paak ki u din pukin u mɔlor u mɔsonn maŋ paak. 18Li paak, bona ŋannlee nba kaa lebitu na be ki wann nan Yennu set sa gbeen u mɔsonn maŋ. Li paak, timm nba chiar saan u boor ni ki tinn na mɔk parcheenn ki gaar dindann nba be ti tɔɔnn po na chib. 19Dindann nba ki ti mɔk na tee kaman nan manfoor jaantarik nba dia ti manfoor fanu. Li tee dindann nba gar Yendɔuŋ ni diiuk kaaruk nee ki kɔɔ diiuk maŋ linn po. 20Leŋe ki Yisa poŋ din gaar ti paak ki gar ki tee ti sɔnliitɔɔ, ki teen ti mantɔyudaanɔ nan yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki bia tee Melkisedek paabu ni mantɔɔ.

english_text:
The Priestly Family of Melchizedek
1 Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings. 2Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had.
The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.” 3We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
4Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies. 5 The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests. 6Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise. 7Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
8Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive. 9Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth. 10This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
11Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron. 12And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
13The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar. 14Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
15All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest. 16That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end. 17 The Scriptures say about him,
“You are a priest forever,
just like Melchizedek.”
18In this way a weak and useless command was put aside, 19because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
20-21 God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is,
“I, the Lord, promise that you
will be a priest forever!
And I will never
change my mind!”
22This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God. 23There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died. 24But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever! 25He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
26Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven, 27 and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself. 28The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_text:
Yennu mantɔɔ Melkisedek labaar
1Melkisedek maŋ din tee Salem tiŋ bate ki bia din tee yabint Yennu mantɔɔ. Abraham nba din tan nyii tɔb nba ki u din kɔn nyann batnba banna na, Melkisedek din chetɔ ki senn mɔsonŋann u paak; 2ki Abraham din nyinn bona piik ni yennkɔɔ wa din kɔn fat bont nba na kur ni ki turɔ. (Melkisedek, u sann maŋ paak tee a “Yentinu Bat,” ŋaan u din tee Salem bat; ki Salem paak tee parmaasir, li paak, ki u bia tee “Parmaasir Bat.”) 3Labasiar ki be ki want Melkisedek baa po amii u naa po amii u yejamm po. Labasiar kaa ki want u maru po amii u kuun po. U naan nan Yennu Bija nee. U lek bia yɔɔ tee Yennu mantɔɔe nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.
4Li paak, i la wa din tee nijaann biaŋinba, kimaan ti yejasaakɔɔ Abraham lek din tan nyinn wa din kɔn fat bont nba tɔb ni na bona piik ni yennkɔɔ ki turɔ. 5Ki sennu din wann a Liifai teeb yaaboona nba tee manteeb na, a bin tuu gaar bi niib Juu teeb nba mɔk linba li bona piik ni yennkɔɔ, ŋaan bi niib maŋ mun bia lek din tee Abraham yaaboonae. 6Melkisedek din ki tee Liifai teeb yaaboonn kaa, ŋaan u din gaar bona piik ni yennkɔɔ Abraham boor, ki bia senn mɔsonŋann ŋɔɔ Abraham wunba din gaar Yennu mɔsona na paak. 7Mɔniɔk kaa nan nisɔɔ nba senn mɔsonŋanne tee nijaann ki gar wunba gaan mɔsonŋann maŋ. 8Manteeb nba din tuu gaan niib nba mɔk linba li bona piik ni yennkɔɔ na din tee nisaarii ki din tuu tan kpo; ŋaan Melkisedek wunba din gaar Abraham bona piik ni yennkɔɔ na ŋanin be, kaman nan Yennu gbouŋ nba wann biaŋinba na. 9Ti sa fit yet a yoo nba ki Abraham din nyinn u bont ni bona piik ni yennkɔɔ na, u yaaboona Liifai teeb binba din tan gaan bona piik ni yennkɔɔ maŋ, mun din mi bi bona piik ni yennkɔɔ nyinnu, 10kimaan ba lek daa din ki mar Liifai na, ŋaan ti sa fit yet a yoo nba ki Melkisedek din chet Abraham na, ŋɔɔ Liifai din be u yeja Abraham maŋ ni.
11Yennu din tur Juu teeb sennu ki wann Liifai teeb bi mannu toona nba sii tee biaŋinbae. Ki Liifai teeb manteeb toona-i diŋŋi be fanuie, li din bo kan kpaa talas ki mantɔganɔ wunba ki set Aron setu ni ŋaan see Melkisedek setu ni na-n baari. 12Kimaan yoo nba ki manteeb yudantuk lebitu nba din tuu tan baar ki bin lebit na, sennu mun bia din tuu lebite, 13ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa wunba tee mantɔganɔ maŋ nyii boorganu nba ki bi sɔɔ din ki mi tun mannu toonni. 14Sɔɔ kur poŋ mi nan ŋɔɔ Yisa nyii Juda nibooru ni, nibooru nba ki Moses din ki mi pak siar bi po ki jikit saa manteeb po.
Mantɔlɔɔ nba naan nan Melkisedek na labaar
15Yoo nba ki mantɔganɔ nba naan nan Melkisedek na din tan baar nee ki ti mantik ŋamm bann barii maŋ paak. 16Ŋɔɔe tee mantɔɔ, ki nisaarik sennii ni kaa ki u teen li mantɔɔ, ŋaan li tee manfoor nba kaa gbennu na paŋ nie ki u teen mantɔɔ. 17Kimaan bi din sɔb u po Yennu gbouŋ ni a “A tan sii tee mantɔɔe ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu, kii tee Melkisedek paabu ni mantɔɔ.” 18Li paak, sennkperu na ji din bakine, kimaan li din ki mɔk paŋi, ki bia ki mɔk nyɔɔti, 19kimaan Moses sennu maŋ din kan fit te ki siar be fanu. Ŋaan mɔtana ti ji mɔk linba turit dindann nba chee na, ki li nie ki ti chat nakin Yennu boor ni. 20Ki Yennu mɔsonn bia pukin li paak. 21Yennu din tuu ki teen manteleeb na li mɔsonn booru. Ŋaan Yisa din teen mantɔɔ yoo nba ki Yennu din tan tur mɔsonn ki betɔ a
“Min Yennu senn mɔsonn
ki ji kan lebit n dudukit, ki yet a
‘Fine sii tee mantɔɔ ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu.’ ”
22Li paak, nnee te ki Yisa tee wunba wann nan mɔsonn nba chee na kaa lotu.
23Tɔn, yaayoo ni manteeb maŋ din tuu tan kpoe, ki bi leeb na-n mun gaar bi toona; li paak, ki bi din yab. 24Ŋaan Yisa ŋanin sii yɔɔ be ki tee mantɔɔ ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki u toona na kan mi nyi u nuu ni ki sɔɔ-n gaariri. 25Li paak, binba chat nakin Yennu, ŋɔɔ Yisa sann paak na, u sa fit tinnib mɔtana nan yoo nba kaa gbennu, kimaan u lek be u manfoor ni yoo kur, ki mei Yennu ki teemm.
26Li paak, Yisae tee ti mantɔɔ nba jaŋ nan ti sommir. Ŋɔɔe tee popeendaanɔ, ki ki mɔk bisiati; u be u kɔɔe nan biitdamm, ki Yennu donnɔ ki gar Yendɔuŋ ni teeb kura. 27Manteyudaanleeb na din tuu mann marouŋ daar-n tuu daar bi tiauŋ biit paak sinsinn, ki bia mann marouŋ ki tur niib; ŋɔɔ Yisa ki tee nna kaa; u din jii u mɔŋ ki mann marouŋ yomkɔɔe, ŋaan ki li marouŋ gbenn ki tur sɔɔ kura. 28Moses sennu na din tuu gann nisaarii ki teemm manteeb yudamm; ŋaan ki bi din tuu ki be fanu ki gbenni. Ŋaan sennu maŋ poorpoe ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn nan u Bija Yisa wunba sii yɔɔ be fanu ki jikit saa yoo nba kaa gbennu na, ki teenɔ mantɔɔ.

english_text:
A Better Promise
1 What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven. 2He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
3Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer. 4If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices. 5 But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!” 6Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
7If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one. 8 But the Lord found fault with it and said,
“I tell you the time will come,
when I will make
a new agreement
with the people of Israel
and the people of Judah.
9It won't be like the agreement
that I made
with their ancestors,
when I took them by the hand
and led them out of Egypt.
They broke their agreement
with me,
and I stopped caring
about them!
10“But now I tell the people
of Israel
this is my new agreement:
‘The time will come
when I, the Lord,
will write my laws
on their minds and hearts.
I will be their God,
and they will be
my people.
11Not one of them
will have to teach another
to know me, their Lord.’
“All of them will know me,
no matter who they are.
12I will treat them with kindness,
even though they are wicked.
I will forget their sins.”
13When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_text:
Yisae tee ti mantɔyudaanɔ
1Barii na barnɔɔk want nan ti mɔk mantɔyudaanɔ nba tee nna mun, ki kar Yennu nba mɔk paŋ kur na naangbouŋ nuudiitu po Yendɔuŋ ni, 2ki tuun mantɔɔ yudant toona Yennu diiuk ni; li diiuko tee barmɔnii jiantu diiuk ki ti Yomdaanɔ din ŋammir, linba ki tee nisaarik ŋammu.
3Mantɔɔ kur nba ki bi gannɔ na, li tuu kpaa talase a wun jii piinii ki bia mann marouŋ ki tur Yennu, nnee ki li mun kpaa talas ki ti mantɔyudaanɔ-n tur siar. 4Li-i bo-i tee ki u be tingbouŋ na ni, u bo kii tee mantɔɔ fabaa, kimaan manteeb nba teen Yennu piinii, Juu teeb sennu nba senn biaŋinba na, poŋ be. 5Ba tuun linba na tee Yendɔuŋ ni diiuk toona siara ninnauŋo; Moses nba din tun linba na din naan nnee; kimaan Moses nba din yaa wun faa Yenjiantu lanbouŋ yoonba na, Yennu din kpaanɔ ki yet a “A tun bonsiar kur kaman nan ma wanna jɔɔr na paak biaŋinba na.” 6Ŋaan Yennu tur Yisa mannu toona nba chee bi mannu toona na, kaman nan u mɔlor nba chee ki be Yennu nan nisaarii sinsuuk ni biaŋinba na, kimaan u mɔlor maŋ gaa nan mɔsona nba chee nae.
7Kimaan sinsinn mɔlor na-i diŋŋi fit te ki nisaarii be fanu, li ji din bo kii kpaa talas ki Yennu-n tur mɔlor pukin taar munnlee. 8Ŋaan Yennu din la nisaarii biite yoo nba ki u yet a
“Min nba tee i Yomdaanɔ nae beeri a
yoo baat na, ki n tan sa lor mɔlorpaann
ki tur Israel niib nan Juda niib.
9Li tan kii tee kaman nan mɔlor nba
ki n din senn ki tur bi yejamm
daar nba ki n din kub bi nii
ki nyinnib Ijipt tiŋ ni na.
Bi din ki teen mɔlor nba ki n din
lor ki turib na yada,
li paak, ki n baka din kaa bi po.
10Min nba tee i Yomdaanɔ na beeri
ma yaa n senn mɔlor ki tur Israel niib daa nba baat na ni:
n ji sa tabin n sennii maŋ bi dudukit nie,
ki sɔbir tabin bi para ni.
Mine sii tee bi Yennu
ki bi tee n niib.
11Li ji kii kpaa talas ki bi sɔɔ-n wann u lɔɔ
amii ki bet u lɔɔ a
‘Fini, bant ti Yomdaanɔ,’
kimaan bi kura tan sii mi mine,
binba tee nijaana nan binba tee nitarii mun.
12N tan sa nyik bi biit ki chabib,
ki ji kii tian bi biit na po.”
13Yennu nba piak mɔlorpaann maŋ po nee, ki sinsinn mɔlor na teen mɔsonkper; ki bonsiar nba kpet ki kokir, li ji kan yukir nan botu.

english_text:
The Tent in Heaven
1The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth. 2 The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
3 Behind the curtain was the most holy place. 4 The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them. 5 On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy.
Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things. 6 But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties. 7 However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
8All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship. 9This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices. 10These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
11 Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world. 12Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
13 According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf. 14But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
15Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings! 16In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use. 17In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
18Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect. 19 Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water. 20He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.” 21 Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship. 22 The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered.
Christ's Great Sacrifice
23These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better. 24This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
25Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal. 26If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
27We die only once, and then we are judged. 28 So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_text:
Tingbouŋ na ni Yenjiantu nan Yendɔuŋ ni Yenjiantu labaar
1Tɔn, Yennu nba din lor sinsinn mɔlor ki turib na, u bia din turib sennii nba ki bi sii waa-r kii jiantirɔ fanu; ki bi bia din mɔk siaminba ki bi sii jiantirɔ leŋe. 2Bi din faa bonkobgbant lanbouŋo, ki li mɔk paklooru nan linn po, ki yi paklooru na Yennu yar. Bi din senn fitire paklooru na ni, ki teebur see leŋ ki boroboro tɔɔ li paak ki bi teen Yennu. 3Ki li linn po mun ki bi yi leŋ Yennu yar nan barmɔnii. 4Sarima binbintir din be, ki bi jokit wubit li paak ki teen Yennu, ki Yennu sennu lakir din be ki bi tabin sarima li kur po, ki sarima bobir din be lakir na ni nan jeet nba yi mana na leŋ. Ki Aron patu nba din bint fapaana na kpee lakir maŋ ni, ki tana ŋannlee nba ki bi din koor ki sennii sɔb li paak na kpee li ni. 5Bonsia nba ki bi yir cherubim din tɔɔ lakir maŋ paak, ki bi kpinkpant bakin siaminba din tee toonbiit nyikinchab boor ni na, ki li want nan Yennu be leŋ. Ŋaan mɔtana ji ki tee yoo nba ki ti sa pak bont maŋ po nan li barnɔɔk kuri.
6Ba din senn bona kura nna nee, ki manteeb din tuu kɔɔ Yenjiantu lanbouŋ maŋ paklooru ni yoo kur ki tuun Yenjiantu toona. 7Ŋaan manteeb yudaanɔ kuukɔɔe din tuu kɔɔ lanbouŋ na linn po yomkɔɔ binn nɔɔk ni. U din tuu dia sɔme ki mann marouŋ ki tur Yennu, u mɔŋ toonbiit paak, nan niib nba din tuu kpet tun toonbiit paak. 8Yennu Seyeeŋ din want Yenjiantu lanbouŋ na po fanu, a siaminba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na daa ki loot yoo nba ki paklooru maŋ daa din see na. 9Linba na tee barjokit ki tur timm nba be mɔtana, ki want nan binba din jiantir Yennu na nba din tuu teenɔ bi piinii nan bi bonkobit sɔn na kan fit wuur bi para ni biit Yennu boor ni, 10kimaan ŋann din tuu tee jeet nan bonnyunyuut nba kɔɔ nan linba ki kɔɔ na po, nan gbanant ŋammu po. Linba na kur din tee gbanant senniie a bii waa-r mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki Yennu tan sa senn u sennpaanu.
11Ŋaan Masia wunba tee sɔnŋanu nba na mantɔyudaanɔ na baar na. Wa kɔɔ jiantu lanbouŋ nba ni ki tuun na tee lanbonjaann nba be fanu nan barmɔniie; li ki tee nisaarik nba faa linba kaa, li bia ki tee tingbouŋ na ni yar kaa. 12Masia nba din saan kɔɔ jiantu lanbouŋ maŋ ni ki saa gar boor nba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na ni yomkɔɔ yoonba, ŋaan ki li gbenn nna, u din ki dia buunii nan naajai sɔn kaa ki mann marouŋ, ŋaan u din jii u mɔŋ sɔme ki pa ki nyinnit daabsin ni yoo nba kaa gbennu. 13Bi din tuu jii buunii nan naajai sɔme, ki bia jii naasar ki mann ki joo muu, ŋaan jii li tanpent na ki mii mii niib nba din tuu mɔk yanbɔmm Yennu tɔɔnn na paak, ki lin ŋamm bi gbanant a bii yeen. 14Linba na-i ŋamm gbanant, ki Masia sɔn mantik sa nyinn biit ti para ni a tii waa manfodaanɔ Yennu, kimaan Seyeeŋ nba be yoo nba kaa gbennu paŋ ni ki ŋɔɔ Masia din jii u mɔŋ ki mann marouŋ nba be fanu fanu ki tur Yennu.
15Li paak, Masiae tee wunba ŋammii mɔsonpaann na, a Yennu nba din yiin binba na-n la yiar sanpaapo yoo nba kaa gbennu, kaman nan ŋɔɔ Yennu nba lor mɔlor na. Bi sa la yiar maŋ, kimaan Masia poŋ din kpowa; li paak, binba din tuun toonbiit yoo nba ki sinsinn mɔlor daa din senn na, u kuun maŋ nyinnib toonbiit maŋ tubdatu niwa.
16Tɔn, nirɔ-i sɔb u fadinu gbouŋ, see ki bi tan wann nan wunba sɔb fadinu gbouŋ na kpowa. 17Yoo nba ki wunba sɔb fadinu gbouŋ maŋ daa be u manfoor ni na, fadinu gbouŋ maŋ ki mɔk yikoo. Li yikoo be u kuun poorpoewa. 18Li paake te ki sinsinn mɔlor na din tuu mɔk yikoo sɔn marouŋ poorpo. 19Sinsinn, Moses din tuu mɔn Yennu sennu na kure kaman nan la sɔb biaŋinba na. Li poorpo wun jii dufatlai ki lor nan pekobmɔnt ki pakii naajai nan buunii sɔn nba ŋmat nan nyun na, ki miir sennu gbouŋ na paak nan niib maŋ kur paak, 20ki beerib a “Mɔlor nba ki Yennu senn turi a yin sakir na sɔme na.” 21Moses bia din tuu mii sɔn maŋ Yenjiantu lanbouŋ na paake, nan bonsiar kur nba ki bi dia jiantir Yennu na paak. 22Barmɔniie, Yennu sennu wannu ni, sɔme din tuu ŋamm bont kur ki li be fanu ki ki mɔk biiti, ki sɔm-i kii nyii, Yennu kan nyik chab niib biiti.
Masia marouŋ nee kpeen biit
23Li paak, li din tuu kpaa talase ki bin mann marouŋ ki ŋamm bona nba tee Yendɔuŋ ni bona ninnauŋ na; ŋaan marouŋ nba chee sa ŋamm Yendɔuŋ ni bona tiauŋ. 24Nifosaarik nba din ŋammir boor nba tee Yennu yar ki nannir kaman nan Yendɔuŋ ni bont nba tee li barmɔnii na, ki Masia din ki saan kɔɔ leŋ kaa, ŋaan u din saan kɔɔ Yendɔuŋ mɔŋ mɔŋ nie, siaminba ki u be Yennu tɔɔnn ti paak na. 25Juu teeb manteeb yudaanɔ na din tuu kɔɔ nan bonkobit sɔn boor nba tee Yennu yar na ni yomkɔɔe binn kur nɔɔk ni; ŋaan Masia ŋanin ki yaa wun mann marouŋ taar bonchiann kaa. 26Li-i diŋŋi bo-i tee nnaie, ŋann la din nyii dunia namu pinpiik niwa u din bo sa di fara taar bonchiann. Ŋaan mɔtana, yoo nba ji tan per joontu na ki u din dɔkit a wun jii u mɔŋ ki mann marouŋ ki kpeen biit. 27Yennu senn a see ki nirɔ kur-n kpo yomkɔɔ, ki li poorpo Yennu-n bu u buut. 28Li paak, Masia nba din jii u mɔŋ mann marouŋ yomkɔɔ ki nyinn nifosaarii bonchiann biit na tan sa jena, ki ji tan kan boi biit po kaa, ŋaan u tan sa tinn binba guu-u nan bi para kur nee.

english_text:
1The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year. 2If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices. 3-4But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
5 When Christ came into the world, he said to God,
“Sacrifices and offerings
are not what you want,
but you have given me
my body.
6No, you are not pleased
with animal sacrifices
and offerings for sin.”
7Then Christ said,
“And so, my God,
I have come to do
what you want,
as the Scriptures say.”
8The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them? 9Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.” 10So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
11 The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins. 12 But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side, 13and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power. 14By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
15The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
16 “When the time comes,
I will make an agreement
with them.
I will write my laws
on their minds and hearts.
17 Then I will forget
about their sins
and no longer remember
their evil deeds.”
18When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices.
Encouragement and Warning
19My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place 20by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
21We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house. 22 So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water. 23We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us. 24We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things. 25Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
26No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth. 27 They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire. 28 If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death. 29 But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy. 30 We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people. 31It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
32Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light. 33Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others. 34You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
35Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards. 36Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised. 37 As the Scriptures say,
“God is coming soon!
It won't be very long.
38The people God accepts
will live because
of their faith.
But he isn't pleased
with anyone
who turns back.”
39We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_text:
1Juu teeb sennu na din tuu tee Yendɔuŋ ni bonŋana ninnauŋ nba din be sɔnu ki baat nee, ŋaan li din tuu ki tee li mɔŋ mɔŋ kaa. Li paak, binn kur marouŋ nba din ki gbent na kan fit teen binba baat nan marouŋ na fanu. 2Li marouŋ-i bo-i fit ŋamm bi para, bi din bo sa mann yomkɔɔe ŋaan ji nyik, ki biit ji kii waabi. 3Ŋaan binn-n tuu binn marouŋ toona na kpan din tian niib na bi biit poe. 4Kimaan naajai nan buunii sɔn na kan fit nyinn biiti.
5Li paak, Masia nba din yaa wun sik tingbouŋ na ni yoonba na, u din bet Yennu a
“A ki loon marouŋ nan piinii kaa,
ŋaan a ŋamm gbanansiare ki turin.
6A ki mɔk parpeenn nan bonkobit nba ki bi
mann marouŋ ki jokitir muu, biit paak na.
7Li paake te ki n din yet a
‘Yennu, mine na, n sa tun a loomm
kaman nan la sɔb a gbouŋ ni ki yet n po biaŋinba na.’ ”
8Sinsinn, u din yet a “A ki mɔk parpeenn nan marouŋ nan piinii, nan bonkobit nba ki bi mann marouŋ ki jokitir muu na, nan marouŋ nba ki bi tuu mann biit paak na kaa.” Bi din tuu mann marouŋ maŋ kura kaman nan sennu nba wann biaŋinbae. 9Li poorpo ki u bia yet a “Yennu, mine na, n sa tun a lomma.” Li paak, Yennu faa kpeen mannkpera kura, ŋaan jii Masia marouŋ na ki senn li setu ni. 10Yisa Masia din tun linba ki Yennu loon wun tun na, ki jii u gbanant ki mann marouŋ yomkɔɔ ki te ki ti teen Yennu niib.
11Mannbooryeŋ nba ki Juu teeb mantɔɔ kur tuun daar-n tuu daar na kan fit nyinn biiti. 12Ŋaan Masia ŋanin din mann marouŋ yomkɔɔe, linba mɔk yikoo yoo kur biit paak, ŋaan ŋmat doo Yendɔuŋ ni ki kar Yennu nuudiitu po. 13Leŋe ki u be ki guu mɔkmɔk nan Yennu nba tan sa dinn u datai tiŋ ni. 14Li paak, ŋɔɔ din mann marunyemmire ki te ki binba ki u nyinnib biit ni na be fanu yoo nba kaa gbennu.
15Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ mun bia teent u siara a
16“Yennu yet a ‘Ma yaa n turib mɔlor nbae na daar maŋ poorpo;
n tan sa teen n sennii bi para ni,
ki sɔbir bi dudukit ni. ’ ”
17Li poorpo ki u bia ŋamm yet a “N ji kan mi ki tiar bi yanbɔmm nan bi biit po jikii.” 18Yennu nba nyik chab biit na, u ji ki loon marunsiar biit paaki.
Yi ŋaan ki tin chat nakin Yennu boor ni man
19N naa waas, Yisa kuun maŋ paak ti mɔk parcheenn nan ki tin kɔɔ boor nba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na ni. 20Yisa gbanant, linba tee sɔnpaanu nan sɔnu nba mɔk manfoor nae loot tammɔb maŋ. 21Ti mantɔjaann nba yen Yennu ŋaak maŋ na paak, 22yi ŋaan ki tin chat nakin Yennu boor ni man nan para nba be fanu, nan barmɔnii yada, ki daa mɔk birsuku, nan ti para nba ki Yisa sɔn nyinn dudukbiit li ni na, ki jii ti gbanant nba ki ti din wuur nan nyun nba kaa jakint na. 23Yi ŋaan ki tii mɔk dindann nba ki ti fiit wann na fanu man, ki daa birisi, kimaan wunba din senn mɔsonn ki turit na tee barmɔnii daanɔe. 24Yi ŋaan ki tin yabir ki fiin leeb loomm nan bi toonŋana, 25ki daa nyikit lakina nan ti leeb kaman nan nisiab nba nyikit lakinu biaŋinba na, ŋaan i sommit leeb man, ki mantik tee ta ji laat ki ti Yomdaanɔ daar maŋ nyarii naki biaŋinba na paak.
26Ta bann barmɔnii maŋ nba fiit wannit na, ti-i biakii ŋammit tuun toonbiit, marunsiar ji kaa ki sa wuur ti biit maŋi. 27Linba ji biar tee buut bunu nba mɔk jaŋmaaniie, nan muchiauŋ nba baat ki tan sa biir binba yêt Yennu na. 28Wunba din tuu yêt Moses sennu na weiu, ki niib bann ki teen siaradamm bannlee amii ki gar niib bannlee, bi din tuu kpiu, ki ninbatinu kaa. 29Li paak, nirɔ nba fiak Yennu Bija maŋ ki ki baakit sɔn nba ŋamm Yennu mɔlor ki nyinnɔ u biit ni na, ki sukii burchinseyeeŋ na, bikint li yɔɔ nba tan sa jaŋ nan tubdatbiiuk nba. 30Kimaan ti mi wunba din yet a “Mine sa pa pann ki jiin biit.” Li bia sɔb a “Ti Yomdaanɔe tan sa bu u niib buut.” 31Li tee bonbilankante ki sɔɔ-n tan kɔɔ manfodaanɔ Yennu wutoor ni.
32I tian man yoo nba gar na labaar; Yennu yentu nba din yent i paak yoonba, i din di fara bonchiann, ŋaan li farsiar din ki nyanniini. 33Sianyoo ni bi din tuu sukii-i paanu ni, ki daamii bonbiir, ki sianyoo i din tuu tan teen siir nan yin pukin binba ki bi din tun nna maŋ paak ki di namm nan li farawa. 34I din mɔk ninbatinu nan binba be dansarii ni; ki sianyoo bin din tan fat i yent, ŋaan ki i lek mɔk parpeenn, kimaan i mi nan ya bia mɔk yent nba chee ki ki mɔk gbennu yaayoo. 35Li paak, i daa nyikit yabiru mani, kimaan i yabiru na baat nan nyɔchiauŋo. 36Li ŋan ki yii mɔk sukuru, ki fit tun Yennu lomm na, ki la wa din senn mɔsonn a u sa turit linba na. 37Li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a
“Li tenn yoo waaminnae
ki wunba baat na sa baar,
ki kan yukir nan baaru.
38Ŋaan n niŋamm na ŋanin tan sa
teenin yada kii waan;
ŋaan bi yenɔ-i ŋmat poor,
n tan kii mɔk parpeenn nanɔ.”
39Timm ki tee nisiab nba ŋmat poor ki baa yann kaa, ŋaan ti teen Masia yada ki u tinnit.

english_text:
The Great Faith of God's People
1Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see. 2 It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
3 Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
4 Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
5 Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found. 6But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
7 Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
8 Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen. 9 Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise. 10Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
11 Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son. 12 Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
13 Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth. 14When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own. 15If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time. 16But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
17-18 Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac, 19because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
20 Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau. 21 Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons. 22 And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
23 Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders. 24 Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson. 25 He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while. 26Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
27Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger. 28 His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
29 Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
30 God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
31 Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
32 What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets. 33 Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions 34 and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
35 Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life. 36 Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail. 37 Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured. 38The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
39All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised. 40This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_text:
Yada labaar
1Yada tee, ta ki mɔk birsuk nan bont nba ki ti mɔk dindann li paak na set be ŋaan daa ki lar nan ti ninbinn nee. 2Yennu din pak yaayoo ni niib na bi yada paake.
3Ti yada paake ki ti bann nan Yennu mɔmaame din nan dunia na, ki ta laat bona nba na u din ki jii bonsiar kaa ki nanni.
4Abel yada paake ki u din mann mannu tur Yennu ki li mannu ŋan ki gar Keenn yar. U yada paake ki Yennu din pakɔ ki purɔ yentinɔ, kimaan ŋɔɔ Yennu tiauŋ din gaar u piinii nawa. Abel din kpowa, ŋaan u yada paake ki u bia lek daa piak.
5Enok yada paake ki u din tan ki kpo, ŋaan ki Yennu din jiiu ki doo nanɔ Yendɔuŋ ni, ki sɔɔ ji ki lau; kimaan Yennue poŋ din jiiu. Ki li sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni a yoo nba ki Yennu daa din ki jiiu na, ŋɔɔ Yennu numm din gboouwa. 6Nirɔ-i kii teen Yennu yada, li yɔɔ kan fit penn ŋɔɔ Yennu par ni, kimaan wunba kur baat Yennu boor ni, li kpaa talas ki li yɔɔ-n teen yada nan Yennu set be ki teen binba loonɔ na nyɔɔt.
7Nowa yada paake din te ki u gbat Yennu kpaanii, barii nba ki u daa din kan fit lar nan u nunbinn na po. U din sak Yennu mɔb ki kpaa ŋarungbeŋŋir ki ŋɔɔ nan u ŋaateeb kɔɔ leŋ ki tinna. Ŋanne din wann nan tingbouŋ na niib mɔk biit; ŋaan ki u yada maŋ paak ki Yennu din purɔ yentinɔ.
8Yada paake din te ki Abraham sak Yennu yiinii ki saan tiŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki yaa a u sa turɔ na ni. U din nyik u mɔŋ dooe ŋaan ki ki mi wa saa siami. 9U yada paake ki u din daa tee saamɔ tiŋ nba ki Yennu din senn mɔsonn ki yaa a u sa turɔ na ni. U din be bonkobgbant lanbont nie; u waas Aisak nan Jakɔb nba din tan gaar Yennu mɔsonn na mun din be li biaŋe. 10Kimaan Abraham din guu a wun tan kɔɔ Yennu nba din bɔɔn doo nba ki maa na, ki li doo tee linba sii yɔɔ ki be.
11U ŋaapoo Sara mun din kpeta ki ji kan fit mari, ŋaan ŋɔɔ Sara yada paake ki u din tan soor poor ki mar, kimaan u mi nan Yennu nba senn mɔsonn na tee barmɔnii daanɔe. 12Li paak, Abraham nba din tee nirɔ nba ji tan ki fɔk nan kuun na, ŋaan u kuukɔɔ yaaboonae tan yab kaman nan ŋmaabira nba yab biaŋinba na, ki bia yab kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir tanbiinii nba yab ki kaa kann biaŋinba na.
13Niib nba na kur din tuu tan kpo nan bi yada ŋaan daa ki gaar Yennu nba din senn mɔsonn linba a u sa turib na, ŋaan bi yada maŋ paak, bi din dɔŋ lar banfɔkirewa, kimaan bi din bann ki sak nan bi tee saamme ki tee nitintaaka tingbouŋ na ni. 14Niib-i yeen linba na, li set fiit ki want nan bi lek daa gorii bi tiauŋ digbann siar poe. 15Ki niib maŋ baka din kaa ba poŋ din nyii digbansiar nba na po kaa. Bi-i diŋŋi biar dukii li po, bi din bo sa fit ŋmata. 16Ŋaan bi baka din be Yendɔuŋ ni digbann nba chee na poe. Ki li paak Yennu mɔk yuugontir nan bin yiinɔ bi Yennu, kimaan u poŋ din ŋamm dosaue ki guumm.
17Yoo nba ki Yennu din bikin Abraham na, u yada paake biak ki u din jii u bija Aisak, a wun mann mannu ki tur Yennu. Gotir, ŋɔɔ Abraham nba din gaar Yennu mɔsona maŋ na, u tiauŋ bia din jii u bijayemmir a wun mann mannu tur Yennu. 18Ki Yennu din poŋ yetɔ a, “Aisak nie ki a tan sa la yaaboona nba ki n din senn mɔsonn a n sa tura na.” 19Ki Abraham ji din bann nan Yennu fit fiint niib kuun ni; ki li set din tee kaman nan Abraham din gaar Aisak kuun nie.
20Aisak yada paake din te ki u senn mɔsonŋann nba yaa lin tan baar Jakɔb nan Esɔ paak na.
21Yoo nba ki Jakɔb din yaa wun kpo na, u yada paake din te ki u senn mɔsonŋann Josef bonjai na paak, ki din tan diin u patu ki jiant Yennu.
22Josef manfoor nba din tan yaa lin gbenn yoonba na, u yada paake ki u din pak Israel teeb nyinu po Ijipt tiŋ ni, ŋaan din wannib ba tan sa teen u kpaba biaŋinba.
23Moses baa nan u naa nba din marɔ yoonba ki la ki u fan bonchiann na, bi yada paak ki bi din bɔrɔ ki tan jaŋ ŋmaarii ŋanntaa, ki din ki jee bat sennu na.
24Moses nba din tan kpaat yoonba, u yada paake ki u din ki sak bii yiu Bat Faro na bipoo bija. 25U din sak nan wun di fara nan Yennu niibe ŋaan ki ki sak toonbiit parpeenn nba tee waaminna na. 26U din dukin a bi-i sukiiɔ Masia paak, li mɔk nyɔɔt bonchiann ki chee Ijipt tiŋ ni mɔkint kura, kimaan u ninbinn din kpaa nyɔɔt nba baat na paake.
27Ŋɔɔ Moses yada paake din te ki u nyii Ijipt ki ki jee bat na wutoor jaŋmaanii, ŋaan lek saa tɔɔnn kaman nan u la Yennu nba ki sɔɔ ki laatɔ nan ninbinn na. 28U yada paake din te ki u din piin yukitgar jaamm, ki din senn sennu a bin mii sɔn bi ŋei tammɔi paak, ki kuun malaka na tan daa kpi Israel teeb bijakpera.
29Israel teeb yada paake ki mɔkgbenmuŋ na din bɔkit ki teen tinkooŋ ki turib ki bi poot, ŋaan ki Ijipt teeb mun din yabir bi mɔŋ a bin poot, ki mɔkir maŋ nyun na jiin mubin ki diib.
30Israel teeb nba din tan baar Jeriko yoonba, bi yada paake ki bi din somm lint li joona daa ŋannlore, ki li baa. 31Poochonchonn nba ki bi yiu Rahab na yada paake din te ki Israel teeb din ki kpiiu, ŋaan kpii binba din ki gbat Yennu mɔb na, kimaan Israel teeb niib nba din baar a bin diis doo maŋ ni na, u din gaarib saauŋ.
32N bia beti siab labaar pukinii? Yoo-i bo-i yab, n bo sa beti Gideon labaar, nan Barak labaar, nan Samson labaar, nan Jefta labaar, nan David labaar, nan Samuel labaar, nan Yennu sɔkiniinba labaawa. 33Bi yada paak ki bi siab din kɔn ki nyann tinii, ki siab mun tun linba tee fanu, ki siab mun gaar Yennu mɔsona, ki siab soor yambɔra mɔi, 34ki siab mun kpeen mupeuŋ, ki siab tin nyii jukbanjai kuun ni. Ki siab mun din tee tariie ŋaan din tan kpant paarib, ki siab mun din took tɔb nan paŋ ki te ki lanjimanbasaamm tin chiar. 35Ki poosiab din la bi nikpiimm nba din kpo na fiir kuun ni.
Ki siab nba din teen Yennu yada paak, ki niib boob ki kpiib, ki bi ki yêt Yennu weiu a bin nyi kuun maŋ ni, kimaan bi loon bin fiir kuun ni ki la manfoor nba chee. 36Ki niib din daan siab nyatuk, ki bia boob kpaasir; ki bi baan bi siab nan jarit, ki jiib saan kɔɔmm dansarii ni. 37Bi din jaat bi siab tanae, ki ŋma bi siab ki poriib; ki bi jikit jukbanjai ki kpi bi siab; ki leeb din lia pegbant, ki leeb bia lia bugbant ki lin nan namu, ki niib mukisib ki dintib fara biak. 38Tingbouŋ na teeb din ki jaŋ nan bii be nammi. Ki bi din lin yeek kunkoot paak nan jɔɔt ni, ki lin dɔɔ tanfiat nan fɔrii ni.
39Binba na kur yada paake ki Yennu din pakib, ŋaan ki bi din ki gaar linba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa turib na, 40kimaan ti paake ki u din mantik dukii bonsiar nba chee na po, ŋanne tee a u sa te ki timm nan ŋamm-n tan lakin tee binba be fanu nan barmɔnii.

english_text:
A Large Crowd of Witnesses
1 Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us. 2 We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne! 3So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
4None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin. 5 But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children,
“When the Lord punishes you,
don't make light of it,
and when he corrects you,
don't be discouraged.
6The Lord corrects the people
he loves
and disciplines those
he calls his own.”
7Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children? 8God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him. 9Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
10Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is. 11It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
12 Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking 13 and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse.
Warning against Turning from God
14Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord. 15 Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you. 16 Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal. 17 You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
18 You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm 19or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop, 20 because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain. 21The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
22You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate. 23Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect. 24 And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
25 Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven? 26 When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
27The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last. 28We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect. 29 Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_text:
Ti Baa Yennu labaar
1Binba na kur tee siaradamme ki lintit. Li paak, yi ŋaan ki tin yabir ki fok lu linba kur sa gɔɔrit Masia sɔnu ni, nan biit nba sa soorit; yi ŋaan man ki tii di sukuru ki gbaa gbaar nba yaa lin baarit na. 2Ŋaant ki tin kpan kpaa ti ninbina Yisa paak; ŋɔɔe ki ti yada gaa u paak li pinpiik niwa nan li joontu ni na. Dapunpunn na din ki te ki u nyik u yabiru, kimaan parpeenn nba din baat u paak na paak, u din ki dukin ki li mɔkɔ fei nan wun kpo li dapunpunn paaki, ŋanne te ki u ji kar Yennu naangbouŋ paak u nuudiitu po mɔtana.
3Yin lek bikin wa din gaar naŋ biaŋinba biitdamm boor ni ki jii u tiauŋ. Li paak, i daa te ki i gbanu baŋŋit ki i nyikit yabiru. 4Kimaan ya yabii ki kɔn nan biit na ŋaan i sɔɔ daa ki mi yabir teee ki li saa baar kuumi. 5I ki tian Yennu nba kpaani kaman nan baa nba tuu kpaan u waas biaŋinba na-aa? Li sɔb a
“N bik, ti Yomdaanɔ-i kpaana kpaanii yoonba,
fan gbiint,
li-i tee ki u dat a tubir,
fa daa nyikit a yabiru,
6kimaan ti Yomdaanɔ kpaan wunba
ki u loonɔe,
ki wa gaar wunba ki teenɔ u bik na,
li yɔɔe ki u daar u tubir.”
7Yabit i fardinu ni kaman nan bik nba tuu di u baa tubdatu fara biaŋinba na. Yennue teeni tubdatu ya tee u waas na paak, kimaan bik kaa ki ki laat tubdatu u baa boor ni. 8Li-i tee ki u ki dat i tuba kaman nan wa tee u waas kur biaŋinba na, ŋann li want nan i tee kpenkiŋmiae, i ki tee u waas kaa. 9Ti baanba nba be tingbouŋ na ni ki tuu daar ti tuba, ki ti teemm nibaakir. Li paak, li ŋan ki ti mantik sikin ti mɔŋ ki tur Yennu wunba tee sei Baa nae, a tin la manfoor. 10Ti baanba nba be tingbouŋ na ni jikit yoo waaminna ki daar ti tuba ba loon biaŋinbae, ŋaan Yennu ŋanin daar ti tuba ti mɔŋ sommir paake, a ti binbeŋ-n ŋamm kii yeen kaman nan ŋɔɔ Yennu binbeŋ na. 11Tubdatu yoo tone, li ki mɔk parpeenni, ŋaan li poorpoewa ki binba dii tubdatu fara na ji tuu la nyɔɔt nba tee parmaasir nan yentinu binbeŋ.
Kpaanii
12Li paak, i kakit man i dunkpeena ni, ki chekin i nii nba baŋ na man, 13kii tɔɔk sɔnu nba took, ki wunba ki u taa wɔb-n la laafia ŋaan daa kpeti.
14Yabit kii mɔk parmaasir i leeb sinsuuk ni, kii mɔk binbenyeeŋ, kimaan sɔɔ-i kii mɔk ŋann, li yɔɔ tan kan la ti Yomdaanɔ. 15Guut i mɔŋ man ki tan daa kɔŋŋit Yennu ninbatinu na. I sɔɔ daa mɔk tonu u par ni, ki u tonu na-n baar nan parbiir leeb paak, ki biir niburchiauŋi. 16I sɔɔ daa tuun bonchonchonn toona amii ki gorii Yennu barii yann kaman nan Esɔ wunba din jii u saakatuk ki daa jeet ki dii taar yomm na. 17I mi nan li poorpo u din tan loon wun gaar u baa mɔsonŋann ki mɔ nan ninnyuut ŋaan la yêtu, kimaan u ji din ki la sɔnu nba ki u sa teen ki lebit wa poŋ din biir linba na.
18Tɔn, yimm ki baar bonsiar nba ki i sa fit siir kaman nan Israel teeb nba din baar Sainai jɔɔr boor na kaa, siaminba ki mupeuŋ din di ki bunbɔnn bɔnn, ki wounpaaruk daa, 19ki kikaak bui, ki kunkɔr pak labaar. Ba din gbat li kunkɔr yoonba bi din jak baruŋo ki ji ki loon li kunkɔr-n pak pukini. 20Kimaan sennu nba ki bi gbat na, paak bi niiwa. Li din yet a, “Bonkobsiɔk gbaa-i lekii sii jɔɔr maŋ, bin jaatɔ tana ki kpiu.” 21Li boor ni din mɔk jaŋmaansooruk bonchiann, ki Moses din yet a “Jaŋmaanii mɔkin, ki n gbanu jekir.”
22Ŋaan yimm ŋanin baar jɔɔr nba yaa sann yi Sion, linba tee manfodaanɔ Yennu doo nba tee Yendɔuŋ ni Jerusalem, siaminba ki malakanba tusa tusa lakin nan parpeenn. 23Yimm tan baar niib nba ki Yennu teemm u waas na boore, ki bi sana sɔb Yendɔuŋ nie. I baar Yennu nba tee nisaarik kur barbuurɔ na boor ni, nan popeendamm nba ki Yennu te ki bi sei be fanu na boor nie. 24I baar Yisa, wunba tee sindantinnɔ ki baar nan Yennu mɔlorpaann na boore. I baar Yisa boor, ki u sɔn nba din nyii u ni na want barii nba ŋan ki gar Abel sɔn.
25Li paak, i mi man ki i yêt wunba kpaani na mɔb, kimaan Israel teeb nba din yêt wunba din kpaamm tingbouŋ na ni mɔb na, u din tan ki nyik bi tubdatu; ki ti-i yêt u kpaanii nba nyii Yendɔuŋ ni na, ti tan sa teen nlee ki nyi tubdatu ni? 26Li yoo na, u kunkɔr din nik tingbouŋ nae, ŋaan mɔtana u senn mɔsonn ki yet a “N ji kan nik tingbouŋ na kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan n ji tan sa nik tingbouŋ na nan sanpaapoewa mun.” 27Mɔbona nba yet a, “N ji tan” na, li fiit want nan bonnankaa kur tan sa nik ki yaat bot li binbeboore, ŋaan ki linba tan kan fit nik na ŋanin-n tan sa biar li boae.
28Li paak, ta gaar naan nba kan mi ki nik na paak, yi ŋaan ki tii teen Yennu niipoouk man, ki jiantɔ nan nibaakir nan jaŋmaanii ki u numm-n gboo, 29kimaan Yennu maŋ tee kaman nan mudikau nee.

english_text:
Service That Pleases God
1Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
2 Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
3Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
4 Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
5 Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us. 6 This should make you feel like saying,
“The Lord helps me!
Why should I be afraid
of what people
can do to me?”
7Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
8Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever. 9Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them. 10But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
11 After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp. 12Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy. 13This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace. 14On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
15Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus. 16But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
17Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
18Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right. 19I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon.
Final Prayers and Greetings
20God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock. 21 I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
22My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
23By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
24Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people.
His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
25I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
translation_text:
Toona nba pent Yennu par ni labaar
1Ya tee Masia niib na paak, i loon i leeb man yoo kur. 2I daa tammit saamm gaaru po, kimaan nisiab din tan gaar malakanba saauŋ, ŋaan ki mi a bi tee malakanbae. 3Yii tian nisiab nba be dansarii ni na po kaman nan yimm nan ŋamme lakin be li ni; kii tian nisiab nba laat muksuk na po, kimaan i mun daa be tingbouŋ na ni nan i gbanante.
4Yii mɔk nibaakir nan i leeb nan i pookɔɔnt nan i jakunt ni ki daa chommi, kimaan Yennu tan sa dat bonchonchona nan joonbaarit nan sapaanwɔbira nan naasinwɔbira tuba.
5Yi nyinn i niauŋ nan liklommu, ŋaan te ki i numm-n gboo nan ya mɔk linba; kimaan Yennu yet a “N kan mi ki nyikani, n bia kan mi ki ŋaanani.” 6Li paak, ŋaant ki tii mɔk parcheenn ki yet a
“Ti Yomdaanɔ tee n sommtɔɔ,
n kan tin jaŋmaanii,
nisaarik sa teenin bee?”
7I tian man i yudamm nba din tuu piak Yennu maan ki teeni na po; ki yii dukii bi binbeŋ nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba ki yii tokii bi yada. 8Yisa Masia nba won tee biaŋinba wonn na, u bia tee nnee dinna na, u bia sii tun tee nnee nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. 9I daa ŋaant ki wannsaanbooru booru-n te ki i boti, ki i tan sa mann tut sɔnŋanu maŋ. Li ŋan ki Yennu sommir-n tur nirɔ paŋ u par ni; jeet sennii weiu kan turɔ paŋi. Nisiab nba dia jeet sennii poŋ ki laat sommsiar li ni.
10Tɔn, binba mann mannu Juu teeb Yenjiantu boor ni na ki mɔk yaak a bii di linba be ti mannu binbintir na ni. 11Ki Juu teeb manteeb yudaanɔ tuu kɔɔ nan bonkobit sɔn Yennu diiuk ni, boor nba tee Yennu yar nan barmɔnii na ni, ki mann marouŋ biit paak, ŋaan joo bonkobit maŋ nant muu bi doo na kpiŋ po. 12Li paak, ki Yisa mun din kpo doo na kpiŋ po, a wun nyinn niib biit ni, u mɔŋ sɔn paak. 13Li paak, ŋaant ki tin nyi ki saan u boor ni man, ki sak feidinu u paak. 14Kimaan doo nba sa biar kaa tingbouŋ na ni; dosau nba baat nee ki ti guur. 15Li paak yi ŋaan ki tii dontir Yennu sann yoo kur Yisa sann ni; paku nba nyi ti mɔi ni nae tee mannu ki turɔ. 16I daa nyikit toonŋana tumu, kii chentir ya mɔk linba nan i leeb. Li mannboorue pent Yennu par ni.
17Yii saak i yudamm mɔi ki sikin i mɔŋ ki turib, kimaan bi baka be i benu ni, kimaan bi tan sa wann Yennu bi toona nba ki bi din tuun. Ŋaant ki bin tan wannɔ nan parpeenn, ŋaan tan daa wantɔ nan parbiiri. Nna tan kii mɔk nyɔɔt ki turiini.
18I mei Yennu man ti paak. Ti ki mɔk dudukbisiat ti toona ni, kimaan ti loon tin tun linba tooke yoo kur. 19Ŋaan n mantik barimiie bonchiann, i mei Yennu man ki turin ki wun te ki n jen i boor ni mɔtana.
Yenmiarjoontik labaar
20Yennu nba teent parmaasir nae din fiin ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa kuun ni. Ŋɔɔ Yisae tee ti guutɔjaann na, kimaan u din kpo, ki u sɔn ŋamm Yennu mɔlor nba sii see yoo nba kaa gbennu; 21li Yennu-n ŋamm i binbeŋ fanu a yii tuun u loomm, kii tuun toonŋana kur; Yisa Masia paak wun te ki tii tee binba sii pent u par. Ŋɔɔe yen dontir nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.
Barjoontii labaar
22N naa waas, n barimiie, yi di sukuru ki gbiint kpaanii na; kimaan ma sɔb gbouŋ nba turi na ki fɔki. 23N loon ki i bann nan bi nyinn ti naa bik Timoti dansarik niwa. Li-i tee ki u sa baar n boor ni mɔtana, i sa la min nan ŋɔɔ.
24Yi foont i yudamm kura nan Yennu niib na kura ki turit. Ti naa waas nba nyii Itali tiŋ ni na foonti.
25Yennu sommir-ii be i kura ni.

english_text:
1 From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world.
Faith and Wisdom
2 My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble. 3You know you learn to endure by having your faith tested. 4But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
5 If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking. 6But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm. 7-8If you are that kind of person, you can't make up your mind, and you surely can't be trusted. So don't expect the Lord to give you anything at all.
Poor People and Rich People
9Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them. 10 But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers 11scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business.
Trials and Temptations
12 God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
13 Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others. 14We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us. 15Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
16Don't be fooled, my dear friends. 17Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing. 18He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth.
Hearing and Obeying
19 My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry. 20If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done. 21You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
22Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it. 23If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror 24and forget what they look like as soon as they leave. 25But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
26If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless. 27Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
translation_text:
1Min Jems, wunba tee Yennu toontunnɔ ki bia tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, n foont Israel nibooru piik nan bannlee nba yat ki be tingbouŋ na kur ni.
Yada nan yan labaar
2N naa waas, yii mɔk man parpeenn yoo nba ki i di fara booru booru na, 3kimaan i poŋ mi nan fara maŋe baati ki i bant nan i set teen Yisa Masia yada nan barmɔnii, ki li teeni gbankur. 4Ŋaant man kii ŋamm kii mɔk gbankur fanu nan li joontu, ki i tan kan kɔŋ binbenŋaŋi. 5Li-i tee ki i sɔɔ pɔt yan, wun miar Yennu wunba pi sɔɔ kur piinii nan burchint, ki ki dint sɔɔ fei na, ki u sa turɔwa. 6Fi-i mei Yennu, fan teen yada ki daa mɔk birsuku; ŋaan fin nba meiɔ nan birsuk, a tee kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir nyunpana nba ki wouŋ da ki li fi ki baa nee. 7Li nibooru daa dukii nan u sa la siar Yennu boori, 8kimaan u mɔk dudukit munnlee-e.
Tarii nan mɔkita labaar
9Li ŋan ki ti naa waas nba tee tarii-ii mɔk yuugontir Yennu nba donnib na paak, 10ki ti naa waas nba mun tee mɔkita-ii munii mɔk yuugontir Yennu nba sikinib na paak, kimaan li kan yukiri ki bi sa gar kaman nan tipuuk nba tuu koor ki baa na; 11kimaan yennu-i nyii ki yekit tipuuk paak, puuk na tuu koore ki baa, ki li ŋant-n ji biir. Li mun bia tee nnee ki tur mɔkita; yoo nba ki bi dukii bi toona nba sa saan tɔɔnn biaŋinba na, li yooe bin kpo.
Bikinu labaar
12Binba nyann bikinu na, parpeenn tee bi yare, kimaan Yennu sa turib nyɔɔt nba tee manfoor nba ki u din senn mɔsonn a u sa tur binba loonɔ na. 13Sɔɔ-i la bikinu, u daa yaa a Yennue bikinɔ. Siar kaa ki sa fit bikin Yennu, Yennu mun ki bikii sɔɔ. 14Nirɔ-i loon wun tun u mɔŋ nianbiiuk toona, nnee tee bikinu. 15Nianbiiuk-i soor nirɔ, li sa baar nan toonbiite, ki toonbiit joontu tee kuume.
16N naa walonkai, i daa te ki siar kpanniini. 17Bonŋann kur nyii sanpaapoe ti Baa Yennu boor, wunba din nan sanpaapo yentu kura. Ŋɔɔ ŋanin ki lebitir kaman nan sanpaapo yentu nba tuu lebitir biaŋinba na; u bia daa tee ŋɔɔ Yennue. 18U mɔŋ loommo ki u teent u waas, u maan nba tee barmɔnii na paak, a tii chee u bonninaant kura.
19N naa waas, n loon yin bann nee; yii gbiintir yian, ŋaan kii mi biaŋinba ki i piak, ki bia daa dont wutoor yian yiami, 20kimaan nifosaarik wutoor ki tuun Yennu toonŋana. 21Li paak, n naa waas, yi nyik i yanbɔmm nan biit kur nba be i para ni na, ŋaan sikin i mɔŋ Yennu tɔɔnn, ki gaar u maan nba ki u kɔɔnt i para ni na, kimaan maan maŋe mɔk paŋ nba sa nyinni kuun ni.
22Ŋaan yii tuun biaŋinba ki maan maŋ wanni na; li-i tee ki i gbat maan maŋ, ŋaan ki ki tuun biaŋinba ki li wann na, i kpann i mɔŋe. 23Wunba kur gbat maan maŋ, ŋaan ki ki tuun biaŋinba ki li wanni, u tee kaman nan nirɔ nba tuu got ki la u mɔŋ mianu ni nee; 24kimaan u got ki la u mɔŋ mianu na ni, ŋaan seet, u ji tuu tamm wa bo got ki la u mɔŋ ki u tee biaŋinba nee. 25Ŋaan wunba dukii sennŋann nba sa fit nyint niib biit daabsin ni, ŋaan ki tammir, ŋaan mɔk gbankur ki tuun biaŋinba ki li wann na, parpeenn sii tee li daanɔ yare, kimaan u tumu na paak.
26Wunba mi nan u tee Yisaweirɔ ŋaan ki ki so u mɔbi, li daanɔ kpann u mɔŋe. Li want nan u waanu na tee yanne. 27Wunba sommit kpeebii nan pakɔi bi fara ni, ki bia nyinn u mɔŋ tingbouŋ na biit ni, ŋɔɔe set tee Yisaweirɔ nan barmɔnii, ki ki mɔk biit ti Baa Yennu boori.

english_text:
Warning against Having Favorites
1My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others. 2Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes. 3You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor. 4This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
5My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him. 6You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court? 7Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
8 You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves. 9But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
10 If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all. 11 The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
12Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free. 13Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful!
Faith and Works
14My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you? 15If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food, 16you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help? 17Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
18Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.”
I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.” 19You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
20Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless? 21 Well, our ancestor Abraham pleased God by putting his son Isaac on the altar to sacrifice him. 22Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did. 23 This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
24You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe. 25 For example, Rahab had been a prostitute. But she pleased God when she welcomed the spies and sent them home by another way.
26Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
translation_text:
Daa mɔk ningannii
1N naa waas, ya teen ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia wunba tee paŋ daanɔ na yada biaŋinba, i daa mɔk ningannii. 2Li-i tee ki mɔkitɔɔ wunba lia taŋant ki pir sarinbaŋ kɔɔ i Yenjiantu diiuk ni, ki nandaanɔ nba lia tachiat mun kɔɔ, 3ki i tur mɔkitɔɔ na nibaakir, ki betɔ a wun kar tɔɔnn po, ŋaan bet nandaanɔ na a wuu see amii wun kar tiŋ ni, 4n naa waas, li-i tee ki i tuun nna, i tee niib nba mɔk ningannii nan i leeb, ki bia mɔk dudukbiite.
