Bee paake te ki u din kpiiu? U din la u waarɔ tuun toonŋana, ŋaan ki u yar bi. 13Tɔn, n naa waas, li-i tee ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb nami, i daa te ki li teei bakitnauŋu.
14Ta loon ti naa walees na, ŋannii want nan ti nyii kuun paŋ ni, ki be manfoor nba kaa gbennu ni. Ŋaan daanɔ nba ki mɔk li loonbooru, ŋɔɔ ŋarin biar be kuun paŋ nie. 15Ki bia jenna, wunba mun nân u naa bik, u tee nikpiiruko; ki i mi nan manfoor nba kaa gbennu kaa nikpiiruk ni.
16Gotir man, Yisa nba din gaar ti paak ki kpo nee wantit loomm nba tee biaŋinba; ki li mun kpaa talase ki tii nyint ti manfoa ti naa waas paak. 17Daanɔ nba mɔk linba jaŋitɔ, ŋaan la ki u naa bik kɔŋ siar, ki u baka kaa u ni, Yennu loomm kaa daanɔ maŋ ni. 18N waas, ti loomm maŋ daa be ti mɔi ni kuukɔɔ kaa; see ki lii tee barmɔnii loomm nba sommit niibe.
Biaŋinba ki ti sii mɔk parcheenn Yennu boor
19Li-i tee ki ti tuun nna, ŋanne ki ti sa bann nan ti waa barmɔnii wannu, ki bia sii mɔk parcheenn Yennu boor. 20Li-i tee ki ti dudukit wannit nan ti mɔk biit, ŋaant man ki ti tiar nan Yennu chee ti dudukit ki bia mi barii kura. 21Tɔn, n walonkai, ti dudukit-i kii wannit nan ti tun bonkpetiri, ŋann ti mɔk parcheenn Yennu boor. 22Ki bia jenna, ti-i dia u sennii ki tuun linba pent u parie, ŋannii sa te ki u teent linba ki ti meiɔ na. 23Ki nee tee u sennu maŋ, a ti teen u Bija Yisa Masia yada kii loon ti leeb kaman nan wa din sennit sennu biaŋinba na. 24Binba kur dia u sennii maŋ, ŋamme gaa u paak, ki u mun bia be bi ni. Ŋannii teen ki ti bann nan u yɔɔ be ti ni, kimaan u Seyeeŋ be ti ni.

english_text:
God Is Love
1Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world, 2and you can know which ones come from God. His Spirit says that Jesus Christ had a truly human body. 3But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
4Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world. 5These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language. 6We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
7My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him. 8God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him. 9God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life. 10Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven. 11Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
12 No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
13God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us. 14God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him. 15God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God 16and are sure God loves us.
God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us. 17If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment. 18A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
19We love because God loved us first. 20But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see? 21The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
translation_text:
Ta sa bann Yennu Seyeeŋ biaŋinba
1N walonkai, i daa tee nisiab kur nba yaa a bi mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ na maan yada, ŋaan bikintib man, ki got laan bi sei maŋ nyii Yennu boor ni amii lee, kimaan faak sɔkiniinba bonchiann nba kpann niib na poŋ baar na ki yat lin niib ni.
2Wunba kur ki seek piak u ni, ki u yaa a u teen yada a bi din mar Yisa Masia nisaarike, ŋɔɔe mɔk Seek nba nyii Yennu peŋ. Li-i tee ki u yeen nna, ŋanne ki ti sa bann nan li tee Yennu Seyeeŋe. 3Ŋaan wunba ki seek piak u ni, ki u yaa a u ki teen yada nan bi mar Yisa nisaarike, ŋɔɔe ki mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋi. Li daanɔ seeke tee Yisa dataak seek na. Bi din poŋ beti nan Yisa dataak maŋ sa baar na, ki mɔtana u poŋ set baar tingbouŋ na niwa.
4N waas, yimm tee Yennu yabe, ki Yennu Seyeeŋ be i ni, ki u mɔk paŋ ki gar Sintaanii wunba be tingbouŋ na ni. Ŋanne te ki i nyann faak sɔkiniinba maŋ. 5Ŋamme tee tingbouŋ na yab. Li paak te ki bi piak tingbouŋ na niauŋ po, ki tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb fiin bi po. 6Ŋaan timm ŋarin tee Yennu yabe; ki binba mi Yennu, ŋamme fiin ti maan po. Ŋaan binba ki tee Yennu yabi, bi ki fiin ti maan po. Ŋanne sa te ki ti sa bann wunba mɔk Yennu Seyeeŋ nba want barmɔnii wannu, nan wunba mɔk Sintaanii seek nba want faak wannu.
Yennu tee loomm
7N walonkai, tii loon ti leeb man, kimaan loomm tee Yennu yare, ki binba mɔk loomm, ŋamme tee Yennu waas ki bia miu. 8Yennu tee loomme, ki binba ki mɔk loommu, bi ki miu. 9Yennu din sikin u Bijayemmir tingbouŋ na ni a ti la manfoor u paak, ki nee fiit wannit nan Yennu set loont. 10Loomm nba set tee loomm nan barmɔnii ki tee a timm din loon ŋɔɔ Yennu kaa, ŋaan li tee a ŋɔɔ Yennu din loon timm, ki din sikin u Bija tingbouŋ na ni ki u tan gaar ti paak ki kpo, ki teen ti marouŋ ŋɔɔ Yennu boor ni, a tin fit la nyikinchab ti biit paak na.
11N walonkai, Yennu nba loont biaŋinba na, li kpaa talas ki tii munii loon ti leeb nna. 12Sɔɔ kaa ki mi la Yennu nan u ninbina, ŋaan ti-i loon ti leebie, Yennu sii yɔɔ be ti ni, ki ti sii mɔk loomm nba ki u teent na nan barmɔniie.
13Ŋɔɔe bia sikin u Seyeeŋ ki turit, ki ŋanne te ki ti bann nan ti gaa u paak, ki u mun bia be ti ni. 14Ki linba pukin biak, ti din laat u Bija, ki mɔtana ti mɔɔntir nan u Baa Yennu din sikinɔ tingbouŋ na ni, a wuu tee tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb Tinntɔɔ. 15Wunba yaa a u teen yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Bija, ŋɔɔe ki Yennu be u ni, ki u mun bia gaa Yennu paak. 16Ki ti mɔŋ mɔŋ mun bann Yennu nba loonit biaŋinba, ki bia teen yada nan u set loonit.
Yennu tee loomm, ki daanɔ nba yɔɔ mɔk loomm nna, ŋɔɔe gaa Yennu paak, ki Yennu mun bia be u ni. 17Ki bia jenna, li-i tee ki ti waa loomm sɔnu nan barmɔnii, Yennu barbuut daar-i taŋŋi baar, ti kii mɔk jijeeti, kimaan Yisa binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba na, ti binbeŋ mun tee nnee tingbouŋ na ni. 18Ti-i mɔk loomm, jijeet sii kaa. Li-i tee ki loomm mantik be nan mɔnii mɔnii, jijeet kan la yaak kii be ti ni, kimaan tubdatu paake ki ti tuu jee. Li-i tee ki nirɔ jee jijeeti, u ki mɔk loomm nan barmɔnii.
19Yennue sint loonit; ŋanne teen ki ti mun mɔk loomm. 20Daanɔ nba yaa a u loon Yennu, ŋaan bia nân u naa bik, u tee faak daanɔe. Li-i tee ki u ki loon u naa bik nba ki u laatɔ nan u ninbina, nlee ki u sii fit loon Yennu nba ki u ki laatɔ nan u ninbina? 21Ki Yisa din senn sennu ki yet a wunba loon Yennu, see ki wuu biakii loon u naa bik mun.

english_text:
Victory over the World
1If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children. 2If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children. 3 We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
4Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory. 5No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God.
Who Jesus Is
6Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful. 7In fact, there are three who tell about it. 8They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
9We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son. 10If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar. 11 God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son. 12And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life.
Knowing about Eternal Life
13All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life. 14We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him. 15And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
16Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly. 17Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
18We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
19We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
20We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
21Children, you must stay away from idols.
translation_text:
Ta sa nyann jinjammii biaŋinba
1Binba kur tee yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Niganntɔɔ Masia na, li yabe tee Yennu waas. Ki daanɔ nba loon nisɔɔ, u bia sii loon nirɔ maŋ waase. 2Li-i tee ki ti loon Yennu ki tuun linba ki u sennii nba want biaŋinba, ŋanne sa te ki tin bann nan ti loon Yennu waas; 3ki li-i biakii tee ki ti dia u sennu, ŋanne want nan ti loonɔ. Ki bia jenna, u sennu maŋ ki paari, 4kimaan binba kur tee Yennu waas, ŋammii fit nyant tingbouŋ na ni jinjammii. Linba te ki ti nyant tingbouŋ na jinjammii na tee ti yada teenue. 5Ki ŋmee sii nyant jinjammii maŋi? Wunba teen yada nan Yisa tee Yennu Bija.
6Ŋɔɔ Yisa Masiae tee wunba din baar nan nyun nan sɔn; u din ki baar nan nyun kuukɔɔ kaa, ŋaan u din baar nan sɔn mun biak. Ki Yennu Seyeeŋ tee siaradaanɔ, kimaan Seyeeŋ na tee barmɔnii daanɔ. 7Tɔn, binba tee siaradamm maŋ tee banntaae; 8ki damm maŋe tee Yennu Seyeeŋ, nan nyun maŋ, nan sɔn maŋ; ki bi banntaa maŋ lakin taan mɔyomm ki tee siara. 9Nnee ki Yennu teen siara u Bija Yisa po, ki u siara maŋ chee nirɔ yar. Ti-i tee nirɔ siara yadaie, ŋann nna ti sii tee Yennu siarae yada ki gar nirɔ yar. 10Wunba teen Yennu Bija maŋ yada, ŋɔɔe mɔk Yennu siara maŋ u par ni; ki wunba ki teen u siara maŋ yada, u teenɔ faak daanɔe na, kimaan u ki teen yada nan siara nba ki Yennu teen u Bija po na, a li tee mɔnii.
Manfoor nba kaa gbennu po
11Yennu siara maŋ tee nnee, a u turit manfoor nba kaa gbennu, ki manfoor maŋ nyi u Bija Yisa nie. 12Wunba gaa Yennu Bija na paak, ŋɔɔe mun mɔk manfoor maŋ; ŋaan wunba ki gaa Yennu Bija maŋ paaki, u ki mɔk li manfoori.
13N sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen yimm nba teen Yennu Bija yada, kimaan n loon ki i bann nan i mɔk manfoor nba kaa gbennu. 14Tɔn, ti mei Yennu nan parcheenn, kimaan ti mi nan li-i tee ki ti meiɔ linba ki u loon wun turit na po, tii mi nan u gaan ti miaru. 15Ti-i mi nan u set gaan ti miaru, tin bann nan ti poŋ gaar linba ki ti meiɔ li po nawa.
16Sɔɔ-i laat u naa bik ki u tuun biit nba kan baar nan kuumi, li daanɔ-ii mei Yennu u paak, ki Yennu-n chab tur biitdaanɔ maŋ manfoor, kimaan u chabit manfoa ki teen binba tuun biit nba ki tee kuun toonni. Ŋaan toonbisiar be, ki tee kuun toonn. N ki yaa a yii mei Yennu kii teen binba tuun biit maŋ booru kaa. 17Toona kur nba ki ŋan lek tee biite, ŋaan toonbisai be ki kan baar nan kuumi. 18Ki ti mi nan damm nba nyii Yennu ni, bi ki tuun biiti, kimaan Yennu Bija Yisa guub, ki bonbiiuk daanɔ Sintaanii kan teemm siari. 19Ki ti bia mi nan timm Masiaweira tee Yennu yabe, ŋaan tingbouŋ na ŋaateeb kur nba ki tee Masiaweira na be Sintaanii nuu ni.
20Ti mi nan Yennu Bija Yisa din sik tingbouŋ na ni ki tan turit yan a tii bant barmɔnii Yennu. Ta gaa nan u Bija Yisa Masia na paak, ŋanne bia te ki ti gaa nan u Baa Yennu paak; ki ŋɔɔe set tee barmɔnii Yennu, ki bia tee daanɔ nba teent manfoor nba kaa gbennu.
21N waas, nyint i mɔŋ man nan pata ni.

english_text:
1From the church leader.
To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth. 2We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
3I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love.
Truth and Love
4I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do. 5 Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do. 6Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
7Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ. 8So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward. 9Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you. 10 If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them. 11Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds.
Final Greetings
12I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
13Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
translation_text:
1Min Jɔɔnn nba tee Masiaweira saakɔɔ nee, sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen fin nba tee n niipoo nba ki Yennu gannɔ na, nan a waas. N looni nan barmɔnii, ŋaan li bia ki tee min kuukɔɔ kaa looniini. Binba kur mun saak Yennu barmɔnii maŋ wannu, bi mun bia looni nnee. 2Min nan ŋamme looni, kimaan ti kur nba taa barmɔnii wannu maŋ paak, ki barmɔnii wannu maŋ set sii yɔɔ be ti ni mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu.
3Ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa Masia sommir nan bi ninbatinu nan bi parmaasir sii be ti paak, nan ta waa barmɔnii wannu nan loomm sɔnu biaŋinba na.
Loomm sɔnu po
4N daan mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nan ma daan la a wasiat nba ki bi waa barmɔnii wannu nba ki ti Baa Yennu sennit sennu na. 5N niipoo, n meia, ŋaant ki ti kur-ii loon ti naa walees. Linba na ki tee sennpaanu kaa ki n sɔbani, ŋaan li tee sennu nba ki ti din dɔŋ gbia li po pinpiik niwa mɔkmɔk nan dinnae. 6Ti-i yɔɔ saak Yennu sennu, ŋanne want nan ti loonɔ nan mɔnii, ki sennu maŋ nba ki ti din dɔŋ gbia li po pinpiik niwa mɔkmɔk nan dinna na, li sennu yaa a tii waa loomm sɔnue.
Kpinkpannii damm po
7Tɔn, kpinkpannii damm bonchiann poŋ baar ki yat saan siar po kur tingbouŋ na niwa, binba yaa a ŋamm ki teen yada a bi mar Yisa ki u tee nisaarik na. Li nibooru tee kpinkpannii damme, ki bia tee Yisa datai. 8Li paak, i mi man kii tan kɔŋ linba ki ti poŋ yabir i paak na, ki i tan sa la i nyɔɔt kur Yennu boor ni.
9Daansɔɔ nba ki gaan Yisa Masia wannu bonŋann ŋaan pukii u mɔŋ yar, Yennu ki be nanɔ, ŋaan wunba saak wannu maŋ ki ki nyikiti, ti Baa Yennu nan u Bija Yisa Masia kur be u ni. 10Li-i tee ki daansɔɔ nba baar i boor ni, ŋaan ki want Yisa Masia wannu maŋ nba tooki, i daa gaanɔ i ŋei ni, ki bia taan daa foontɔ gbaa, 11kimaan wunba foontɔ, u mɔŋ taant daanɔ maŋ biit poe na.
Chabitii
12N mɔk mɔbona bonchiann ki loon ki n sɔb ki turi, ŋaan n ki loon ki n sɔb gbouŋ ni kaa ki turiini, ŋaan n mɔk dindann nan n sa baar ki gɔni, ki tin pak nan leeb mɔi ni, ki li-i tee ki li fit teen nna, li sa maŋ ti para ni bonchiann.
13A niipoo wunba ki Yennu gannɔ na, u waas foonta.

english_text:
1 From the church leader.
To my dear friend Gaius.
I love you because we follow the truth, 2dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit. 3It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth. 4Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth.
Working Together
5Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before. 6They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve. 7When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower. 8We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
9I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us. 10So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
11Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
12Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true.
Final Greetings
13I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. 14I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
15I pray that God will bless you with peace!
Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
translation_text:
1Min Jɔɔnn nba tee Masiaweira saakɔɔ nee, sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen n yɔɔk fin Gaius, fin nba ki n nian nan mɔnii.
2N yɔɔk, n mei Yennu a paak nan fii mɔk laafia, ki a bont kur-n somm fanu kaman nan a seek nba be fanu Yennu boor biaŋinba na. 3N mɔk parpeenn bonchiann nan ti naa wasiat nba daan baar nna, ki tan betin a po na, nan biaŋinba ki a waa Yennu barmɔnii wannu na, ki a benu nee bia want li sɔnu nan mɔnii. 4Siar ki penn n par ni ki mantik baar ma gbat a n waas waa wannu nba took na.
Yisa mɔɔntdamm sommir po
5N yɔɔk, fa sommit ti naa waas nba tee saamm gbaa biaŋinba na, a tuun linba ŋan. 6Ba tuu tan baar Masiaweira lakinu ni yoonba, bi tuu tiar a burchintuk maŋ po. Li paak, bi-i tuu kii baar a boor ni, ki a bia ŋammit bi sɔnii maŋ ki sommitibie, ŋann a tuun bonŋanne na. I diab bonŋann kimaan bi tee Yennu yabe. 7La tee Yisa sann paak nee ki bi lin ki mɔɔntir u maan, ŋaan ki gaan siar binba ki tee Masiaweira na boor ni. 8Li paak, timm Masiaweira ŋarin, li kpaa talase nan tii gaan li nibooru saantuk. Ŋanne sa te ki timm nan ŋamm-ii taa Yisa barmɔnii wannu mɔɔntii.
Jɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Diotrefes na po
9N poŋ mi sɔb barsai nna ki tur Masiaweira nba be a boor ni na, ŋaan ki Diotrefes yêen n mɔb, kimaan u loon wun pur u mɔŋ nijaanne. 10Li paak, mi-i taŋŋi baar, n sa fiit linba kur ki u tun, nan bonbisiar nan faak nba ki u pak ki biirit na. Ŋaan biit nba ki u tuun maŋ na, li lek daa ki gboo u numm gbaa. Ti naa waas nba tuu tan baar u boor ni, u bia tuu ki gaanibi, ki damm nba tuu loon bin gaar ti naa waas maŋ saantuk, u bia tuu ber nyinnib Masiaweira lakinu maŋ nie.
11N yɔɔk, daa tokii sɔɔ biiti, ŋaan kii tuun linba ŋan. Wunba tuun bonŋann, u tee Yennu yɔɔe; ŋaan wunba tuun biit, u ki bann Yennu.
Jɔɔ nba ki bi yiu Demetrius na po
12Sɔɔ kur piak Demetrius; ki u benu nee bia want nan u waa barmɔnii wannu; ki ti mɔŋ mun piakɔ; ki a mun mi nan ti set tee barmɔnii damme.
Chabitii
13N bia mɔk maan bonchiann ki loon ki n pak ki tura, ŋaan n ki loon ki n sɔb gbouŋ ni kaa ki turani. 14Ŋaan n mɔk dindann nan n tan sa baar ki la-a daa ŋannlelee na ni, ki tin tan pak nan leeb mɔi ni.
15Parmaasir-ii be a paak. Ti yɔɔsinba foonta. Ti yɔɔsinba nba mun bia be a boor ni na, a foont bi sɔɔ kur nan u sann yenɔ yenɔ.

english_text:
1 From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James.
To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
2I pray that God will greatly bless you with mercy, peace, and love!
False Teachers
3My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people. 4Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
5 Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them. 6You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment. 7 We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
8The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels. 9 Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
10But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings. 11 Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
12These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit. 13Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
14 Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said:
Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels 15to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
16These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want.
More Warnings
17My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ. 18 They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God. 19And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
20Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray. 21And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life. 22Be helpful to all who may have doubts. 23Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds.
Final Prayer
24-25Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
translation_text:
1Min Juud wunba tee Yisa Masia toontunnɔ, ki bia tee Jems naa bik nee sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen niib nba ki ti Baa Yennu gannib ki loomm, ki Yisa Masia guub na. 2Yennu-n tini ninbaauk ki turi parmaasir nan loomm bonchiann.
Faidamm labaar
3N walonkai, n ninbinn daan mɔnn nan ki n sɔb gbouŋ ki turi ki saan gaan tinnu nba tee ti kur yar na po; ŋaan mɔtana li ji kpaa talas ki n sɔb ki kpaani ki yin yabir i mɔŋ ki dia yada labaar nba ki Yennu din tur u niib na fanu, ŋaan daa saak ki sɔɔ-n lebitir, 4kimaan nisiab nba ki tiin Yennu na din kɔɔ i sinsuuk ni woonin nie, ki lebit ŋɔɔ Yennu ninbatinu labaar a bii tuun bi gbanant loonbiit; bi ki saak Yisa Masia mɔbi; ŋɔɔ kuukɔɔe tee ti yudaanɔ nan ti Yomdaanɔ. Li yukira ki biitdamm maŋ tubdatu poŋ sɔb Yennu gbouŋ ni na.
5Ma yaa n beti linba na, i poŋ gbat li kura, ki mir, ŋaan n loon ki n tiari li poe; ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu din fat u niib ki nyinnib Ijipt tiŋ ni, ŋaan li poorpo u din kpii bi siab nba din ki teenɔ yada na. 6Nnee biak ki malakanba nba din ki dia yaak nba ki Yennu din turib na nan li sɔnu na, ki bia din ki biar be bi binbeboor sanpaapo, ki Yennu baamm bant ki lub sarik bootsunsuŋ nba bɔn ni, ki guu dagbeŋŋir nba ki u sa bu bi buut na. 7Nnee biak ki doi nba ki bi yi Sodom nan Gomora na nan digbana nba kpiar na niib din tuun bonchonchonn toona ki bia waa niauŋ nba bi bonchiann, ki bi laat tubdatu nba tee muu ki li di yoo nba kaa gbennu; ki li tee kpaanii ki tur sɔɔ kura.
8Nibooru maŋ mun bia daa mɔk dudukit nba ki bi tuun ki biir bi gbanant, ki bia tee tubkangbata, ki bia sukii Yennu malakanba. 9Ŋaan malakanba saakɔɔ nba ki u sann yi Maikel na, ŋɔɔ nan Sintaanii nba din kɔn Moses gbanant paak na, ŋaan Maikel mɔsɔɔ din ki sukir Sintaanii, ŋaan din betɔ a “Ti Yomdaanɔ sa wanna yan.” 10Ŋaan niib na ŋanin sukii bont kur nba ki bi ki bann li paake, ŋaan tuun linba kur ki bi loon kaman nan bonkobit nba ki mɔk dudukit na, ki biir bi mɔŋ. 11Parbiir be bi paak; kimaan bi wei Keenn sɔnbiiuk nawa; bi bia tee kpinkpannii damm likirii paak kaman nan Balaam nba din tee biaŋinba na; ki bia tuun kaman nan Kora nba din tun ki ki sak Yennu mɔb ki tan kpo na; bi mun tan sa kpo nnee. 12Bi tee baritee i mɔtaauk jaamm dinu ni, ki lakin di nan leeb, ki ki tiin Yennu, ki mɔk gɔrit ki dint bi mɔŋ kuukɔɔ. Bi tee kaman nan sanpagbant nba ki baat nan saak ŋaan ki wouŋ da nee; ki bia tee kaman nan tiinii nba ki loon li lomm yoo na, bi kpoe ki sa tɔr, kimaan bi jiina kur fikita ki bi koor. 13Bi tee kaman nan mɔkgbeŋŋir nyunpana nba fi ki pukir nan jakint nee. Bi bia tee kaman nan ŋmaabira nba tuu ŋabir ki lin nee; ki bunbɔnchiauŋ nba kaa gbennu be ki guub.
14Enok wunba paa Adam yoo paak munnlore ni na din sɔkin ki yet bi po a “Ti Yomdaanɔ nan u malakanbaŋamm tusa tusa baat 15a wun bu nifosaarik kur buut, ki biir binba kur ki tiinɔ, ba tuun toonbiit, ki ki saak u mɔb biaŋinba na; nan biitdamm kura, ba piak mabiit kur ki biirɔ biaŋinba na, ki bi baka kaa nanɔ.”
16Bi tee bunburimii damme; bi numm ki mi gboo; ki bi tuun bi mɔŋ loonbiit, ki mɔk parbifaant bonchiann, ki korin niib a bin la siar.
17N naa walonkai, i tian man maan nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia toomiinba din yet na. 18Bi din yet a “Dajoontii daa maŋ ni, niib nba waa bi mɔŋ gbanant loonbiit na tan sa fiir ki sarikiti.” 19Li niboorue baat nan bɔkitu i sinsuuk ni; bi gbanant loomm kuukɔɔe diab, Yennu Seyeeŋ kaa bi ni. 20N naa walonkai, yii chee i para man Masia labayeen nba ki i teenir yada na paak, kii mei Yennu u Seyeeŋ paŋ ni, 21kii tee niib nba ki Yennu loomm, kii guu yoo nba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia sa turi manfoor nba kaa gbennu, u ninbatinu paak.
22Binba dudukit laŋ na, yi tinib ninbaauk, ki soorib 23ki nyinnib Sintaanii muu ni; ki binba mɔk bi loonbiit dɔk na, yi tinib ninbaauk, ki bia ŋmat nan jaŋmaanii bi boor, i daa nakii bi biit boori.
Yenpaku
24Yennu sa fit guui ki i daa baa, ki sa baar nani u tɔɔnn yentsaakar ni, ki i ki mɔk biiti, ŋaan sii mɔk parpeenn bonchiann; 25ŋɔɔ nba tee Yen-yenɔkɔɔ ki tinnit ti Yomdaanɔ Yisa Masia paak na, u yen paku nan yent nan paŋ nan bont kur yudant, yoo nba din gar na, nan mɔtana, nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.

english_text:
1This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John. 2And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
3God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
4 From John to the seven churches in Asia.
I pray that you
will be blessed
with kindness and peace
from God, who is and was
and is coming.
May you receive
kindness and peace
from the seven spirits
before the throne of God.
5 May kindness and peace
be yours
from Jesus Christ,
the faithful witness.
Jesus was the first
to conquer death,
and he is the ruler
of all earthly kings.
Christ loves us,
and by his blood
he set us free
from our sins.
6 He lets us rule as kings
and serve God his Father
as priests.
To him be glory and power
forever and ever! Amen.
7 Look! He is coming
with the clouds.
Everyone will see him,
even the ones who stuck
a sword through him.
All people on earth
will weep because of him.
Yes, it will happen! Amen.
8 The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!”
A Vision of the Risen Lord
9I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus. 10On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet. 11The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
12When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands. 13 There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest. 14 His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire. 15 His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall. 16He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
17 When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said:
Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last, 18and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead. 19Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things. 20I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
translation_text:
1Maan nba ki Yisa Masia din fiit na sɔb gbouŋ na nie. Yennu din turɔ maan maŋ a wun wann u toontunna linba sa teen mɔtana. Masia din tun u malaka u toontunnɔ Jɔɔnn boor ni, a wun betɔ maan maŋ kur. 2Ŋanne ki Jɔɔnn din fiit ki wann linba ki u din la na kur ki li tee barii nba nyii Yennu boor ni, nan linba ki Yisa Masia din fiit ki wannɔ na. 3Wunba karin sɔkinii mɔmaan nba sɔb gbouŋ na ni, parpeenn tee u yare, ki binba gbia mɔmaan maŋ ki saakir, parpeenn tee bi yare mun, kimaan yoo pera ki linba kur sa tun na lin tun.
Masiaweira nba bɔkit ki be digbana ŋannlore na labaar
4-5Min Jɔɔnn sɔbin gbouŋ na ki teen Masiaweira nba bɔkit ki be digbana ŋannlore, ki be Asia yent ni na. Yennu nba be, ki din be, ki bia tan sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki sei ŋannlore be u naangbouŋ tɔɔnn na, nan Yisa Masia wunba mɔɔntir Yennu maan nan barmɔnii, ki tee sinsinn wunba din kpo ki fiir kuun ni ki bia tee tingbouŋ na kpanbara kur Kpanbar na, bin turi sommir nan parmaasir. U loont, ki din gaar ti paak ki kpo, ki nyinnit ti biit daabsin ni; 6ŋaan te ki ti tee u naan ni niib nba tee manteeb, a tin tun u Baa Yennu toonn; ŋɔɔe yen paku nan paŋ mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.
7Gotir man, ŋɔɔe be sanpagbant ni ki baat na. Sɔɔ kur ninbina tan sa lau, nan binba din ŋmu-u na gbaa. Yoo nba ki u tan sii baat na, tingbouŋ na niib kur tan sa bui u paak. Mɔmɔmm, li sa teen nnawa.
8Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu wunba mɔk paŋ kur, ki be, ki din be, ki bia tan sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu, ŋɔɔe yeen a “Mine tee bont kura pinpiik nan li gbennu.”
9Tɔn, min Jɔɔnn wunba tee i naa bik ki taan nani ki di fara nan sukuru Yisa paak, ma din lin ki mɔɔntir Yennu maan, nan Yisa barŋanii, ki bi din jiin ki saan nanin Patmos tiŋ nba ki nyun lintir na ni. 10Ki lasir dasiar ki Yennu Seyeeŋ din fiir n ni, ki n gbat kunkɔsaakar ki li naan nan kikaak na ki piak n poorpo, 11ki betin a n sɔb linba ki n laat na ki teen gbouŋ ni ki nakin ki tur Masiaweira nba be digbana ŋannlore: binba be Efesus nan Simirna nan Pegamum nan Tiataira nan Saadis nan Filadelfia nan Laodisea na.
12Ki n ŋmant ki yaa n got wunba piak ki teenin na; ma ŋmant yoo nba na ki n la sarima fita ŋannlore ki li see. 13Ki sɔɔ be li sinsuuk ni ki naan nan nisaarik Bik na, ki lia liatfouk ki li tan siit u taabira, ki sarima gbann boob u nyaauk ni, 14ki u yut peen kaman nan gungunt na, ki u ninbina naan nan mupeenu na, 15ki u taa nyirii kaman nan kut nba ki bi tuu naantir muu ni ki li nyirii biaŋinba na, ki u kunkɔr fu kaman nan siɔk kpenu ni nyun nba tuu fu biaŋinba na. 16Ki u kub ŋmaabira ŋannlore u nuudiitu ni, ki jukbanjiak nba di mɔleemii tant nyii u mɔb ni, ki u numm po yeen kaman nan yennu nba tuu yeen yensuuk ni na. 17Ma lau yoonba ki n baa dɔɔr u tɔɔnn kaman nan kpeemɔ na. Ki u jii u nuudiitu ki yii n paak, ŋaan yet a “Daa tiin jaŋmaanii; mine tee bont kura pinpiik nan li gbennu; 18ki bia tee footɔɔ na, ki din kpo, ŋaan mɔtana n fiira ki sii fo yoo nba kaa gbennu, ki bia mɔk kuun nan kpeentiŋ paŋ; 19a li paak a sɔb linba ki a la mɔtana, nan linba be mɔtana, nan linba tan sa tun yoonbawa; 20a ŋmaabira ŋannlore nba ki a la n nuudiitu ni na, nan sarima fita ŋannlore na woonin ni paak tee nnee: ŋmaabira ŋannlore nee tee n weira nba be digbana ŋannlore na malakanba, ki fita ŋannlore na mun tee n weira maŋ bujir munnlore maŋ.”

english_text:
The Letter to Ephesus
1This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus:
I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
2I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars. 3You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
4But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to. 5Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand. 6But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
7 If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden.
The Letter to Smyrna
8 This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna:
I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
9I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
10Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
11 If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death.
The Letter to Pergamum
12This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum:
I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
13I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
14 I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral. 15Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans. 16Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
17 If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone.
The Letter to Thyatira
18This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira:
I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
19I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before. 20 But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols. 21I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
22I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop. 23 I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
24Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands. 25But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
26 I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end. 27-28I will give each of them the same power my Father has given me. They will rule the nations with an iron rod and smash those nations to pieces like clay pots. I will also give them the morning star.
29If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_text:
Masia labaar nba tee Efesus teeb yare na
1Ki wunba ki n lau na betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Efesus doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba dia ŋmaabira ŋannlore u nuudiitu ni na ki bia somm sarima fita ŋannlore sinsuuk ni na boore: 2ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba na, nan ya yabir ki tuun toonŋana nan sukuru biaŋinba, ki bia mi nan i ki loon nibiit i sinsuuk ni, li paake ki i din bikin binba tuu yaa a bi tee n toomiinba ŋaan ki ki tee na, ki i bann nan bi tee faidamme. 3N mi nan i mɔk sukuru ki la fara n paak, ŋaan i ki nyikimi. 4Ŋaan i biit tee nnee: i ji ki loonin kaman nan ya din dɔŋ loonin sinsinn biaŋinba na. 5Tiat man ya din tuu be biaŋinba na ŋaan ji tan lebit ki nyikir na. Nyikin man i biit maŋ ŋaan kii tuun ya poŋ tuu tuun biaŋinba sinsinn ki li ŋan na; li-i tee ki i kan nyik i biiti, n sa baar i boor ki jii i fitir na li boor, ŋaan beri. 6Ŋaan ya tuun linba ki li ŋane na; i ki loon toona nba ki Nikola niib tuun na, kaman nan n mɔŋ nba ki loon toona nba ki bi tuun na.
7“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.
“Wunba nyann nyannu maŋ, ŋɔɔe ki n sa turɔ sɔnu ki wun di manfoor tilɔɔna nba be Yennu boor, siaminba ki bi yir Paradais na.’ ”
Masia labaar nba tee Simirna teeb yare na
8Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Simirna doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba tee bont kura pinpiik nan li gbennu na boor ni; ŋɔɔe din kpo ki tan bia fiir kuun ni na; 9ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i fara po, ki bia mi nan i tee nandamme, ŋaan mɔmɔmm, Yennu boor ni, i tee mɔkitae; ki n mi nan nisiab piak ki biir i sana, ki bia yaa a bi tee Juu teebe, ŋaan ki ki tee nna; bi tee Sintaanii niibe. 10I daa tiin jaŋmaanii nan fara nba baat i paak na. Gotir man, Sintaanii sa te bin soor i siab ki kɔɔmm dansarik ki bikimm, i bia tan sa la fara ki saa dian daa piik; ŋaan yii teenin yada mɔkmɔk nan i kuun, ki n tan sa pi-i piinii nba tee manfoor.
11“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.
“Wunba nyann nyannu maŋ, u kan la kuun nba pukin munnlee na.’ ”
Masia labaar nba tee Pegamum teeb yare na
12Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Pegamum doo ni na malaka, a ki yet a “Labaar na nyii wunba mɔk jukbanjiak nba di mɔleemii na boor nie: 13ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi nan siaminba ki i be na tee siaminba ki Sintaanii naangbouŋ na be, ki n mi nan i set dia n sann; yoo nba ki bi din tan kpii Antipas nba din tee n siara daanɔ nan barmɔnii, i mɔŋ doo nba ki Sintaanii be na, li yoo maŋ gbaa ki i ki yêt n sɔnu. 14Ŋaan mɔmɔmm, i mɔk biit ŋannlee nnae ki n sa wanni: siab be i sinsuuk ni ki dia Balaam sennu; ŋɔɔe din wann Balak wa sa teen biaŋinba ki te ki Israel teeb-n tun biit na. U din te ki bi dii pata nant, ki bia te ki bi chomm nan leeb. 15Li mun bia tee nnee i sinsuuk ni, ki i siab mun waa Nikola niib maŋ wannu. 16Li paak, yi nyik i biit maŋ; li-i kii tee nnai kaa, n sa baar mɔtana nan jukbanjiak nba tantir ki nyi n mɔb ni na, ki kɔn biir li nibooru.
17“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.
“Wunba nyann nyannu na, ŋɔɔe ki n sa turɔ woonin ni jeet nba ki bi yir mana na ki wun di, ki n bia sa turɔ tanpeenn nba ki sanpaann sɔb li paak ki sɔɔ ki mir na, see fin nba gaar na kuukɔɔ.’ ”
Masia labaar nba tee Tiataira teeb yare na
18Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin ki be Tiataira doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii Yennu Bija nba ki u ninbina naan nan mupeenu na, ki u taa bia nyirii kaman nan nabint nba tuu nyirii biaŋinba na boor nie; 19ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i binbeŋ nba tee biaŋinba, ki bia mi i loomm, nan i yada teenu, nan i toona, nan ya mɔk mintir biaŋinba, ki bia mi nan i toonŋana tumu pukii ki gar i sinsinn toona na. 20Ŋaan i biit tee nnee: ya sak poo nba ki bi yiu Jesebel ki u yaa a u tee Yennu sɔkinii, ki ji taan ki be i boor ni ki kpann n toontunna ki wantib a bii chomm nan leeb kii di pata nant na. 21N turɔ yoo a wun lebit u binbeŋ na, ŋaan ki u ki saak a wun nyik u chonchonsin na. 22Li paak, n sa jiiu ki lu-u fara gado paak nan binba tuu lakin chomm nanɔ na kur; li-i kii tee ki bi nyik biit nba ki bi lakin tuun nanɔ na, n sa kɔɔmm fargbeŋŋir ni, 23ki kpi u weira kura; ki n weira kur sa bann nan mine tee wunba mi niib dudukit nan bi loomm nba tee biaŋinba, ŋaan sa tur sɔɔ kur u toona nyɔɔt nba tee biaŋinba.
24“Ŋaan i siab nba be Tiataira ki ki waa poo maŋ sɔnii na, ki bia ki waa linba ki siab yir Sintaanii woonin ni labaar na, n yeen yimme a n kan jinni jikpiata. 25Yii dia man linba ki i mɔk na fanu mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba ki n tan sa baar na. 26-27Wunba nyann nyannu na, ki tuun n loomm ki kan nyik, n sa turɔ paŋ nba ki n gaar n Baa boor ni na booru, ki bia turɔ tingbouŋ na nibooru kur yikoo ki u sii diab nan paŋ ki sa fit biirib kaman nan fa tuu dia kut patu ki faa boba ki yee biaŋinba na. 28N bia sa chab li yɔɔ sanyafaar ŋmaabir.
29“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ”

english_text:
The Letter to Sardis
1This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis:
I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say.
I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead. 2Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God. 3 Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
4A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy. 5 Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
6If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
The Letter to Philadelphia
7 This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia:
I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
8I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers. 9 Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
10You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through. 11I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
12 Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
13If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
The Letter to Laodicea
14 This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea:
I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
15I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other. 16But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth. 17You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
18Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
19 I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins. 20Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together. 21Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
22If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_text:
Masia labaar nba tee Saadis teeb yare na
1Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Saadis doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba mɔk Yennu Sei ŋannlore nan ŋmaabira ŋannlore na boore: ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba, ki bia mi nan niib dukii a i fo, ŋaan i ki fo. 2Li paak, fiit man ki yabir i binbeŋ nba biar na a li daa kpenn ki gbenti, kimaan toona nba ki i tuun na, li ki gboot Yennu ninbina. 3Li paak, yii tian man wannu nba ki i din gbat ki waa-r na. Yii diar man ŋaan nyik i toonbiit tumu na; ŋaan li-i tee ki i ki fiir, n sa baar i boor ni kaman nan nanyukɔɔ nba tuu baar biaŋinba nee, ki i kan bann n baaru yoo. 4Ŋaan i siab be Saadis ki bi para ni ki mɔk jakinti. Ŋamme sa lia tapeenii ki min nan ŋamm sii tɔk, kimaan ba ŋan nann na paak. 5Wunba nyann nyannu maŋ na, u tan sa lia tapeenii na, ki n tan kan kpeen u sann manfopaana damm gbouŋ na ni, ki n tan sa yet n Baa nan u malakanba na paanu ni a li daanɔ tee n yɔɔe.
6“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ”
Masia labaar nba tee Filadelfia teeb yare na
7Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Filadelfia doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba tee Popeendaanɔ, ki bia tee Barmɔnii daanɔ na boore; ki u dia David peenu na, ki li-i tee ki u loot gann, sɔɔ kan fit loonni; ki li-i biakii tee ki u loonn, sɔɔ kan fit looti: 8ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba, ŋaan gotir, n loot gann ki turi, ki sɔɔ kan fit loonni. N mi nan paŋ nba ki i mɔk na, li waar, ŋaan nanna kura, i bia lek dia n wannu na, ki ki nɔi nan i ki mimi. 9Nnara, binba tee Sintaanii niib, faidamm nba yaa a bi tee Juu teeb ŋaan ki ki tee na, n sa te ki bin baar ki tan gbaan i tɔɔnn po ki bann nan n set looni. 10Kimaan ya sak n mɔmaan ki mɔk mintir na paak, n sa dia-i bonŋann ki i tan kan kɔɔ bonbiir nba baat tingbouŋ na kur paak a lin tan bikin tingbouŋ na niib na. 11N baate mɔtana. Yii dia man linba ki i mɔk na fanu ki sɔɔ tan kan fat i nyɔɔt maŋi. 12Wunba nyann nyannu na, n sa teenɔ Yennu diiuk ni jaantarik, ki u ji kan nyi leŋi. Ki n bia sa sɔb Yennu sann u paak, ki sɔb Yennu doo sann u paak, ki bia sɔb n mɔŋ sanpaann na u paak. Yennu doo maŋe tee Jerusalem paann nba sa nyi Yendɔuŋ ni Yennu boor ni na, ki tan sik tiŋ na ni.
13“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ”
Masia labaar nba tee Laodisea teeb yare na
14Ki wunba ki n lau na bia betin a n sɔb labaar na ki tur u weira nba lakin be Laodisea doo ni na malaka a “Labaar na nyii wunba sann yi Mɔnii na boore; ki u tee siara daanɔ nba tee barmɔnii daanɔ ki tee siara fanu; ki bia tee linba kur ki Yennu nann na yudaanɔ: 15ŋɔɔe yet a ‘N mi i toona nba tee biaŋinba; i ki tôni, i bia ki mei, ŋaan n bo loon ki yii tee li yenne; 16ŋaan ya ki tôn ki bia ki mei na, n sa siiri pia teriku.
17Yimme yeen a i tee mɔkitae ki mɔk linba kur ki i loon, a ki siar kan pɔtiini, ŋaan ki ki mi ya mɔk parbiir nan ninbaauk biaŋinba. I tee nandamme, ki somm yana, ki tee jɔɔnii. 18Li paak, n kpaaniie a yi daa sarima nba tee sarinmɔnna n boor ni ki set la mɔkint, ki bia daa tapeenii ki dɔkin i fei na, ki bia daa kpan ki baŋ i ninbina na kii laat. 19Binba kur ki n loomm na, n kpaamm kpaanii ki daar bi tuba. Li paak, nyikin man i biit, ki daa jerimi. 20Nnara, n see tammɔb na ni ki kpaan gann; wunba gbat n kunkɔr ki loot gann maŋ, n sa kɔɔ u boor ni, ki min nan ŋɔɔ sa lakin ki di. 21Wunba nyann nyannu na, n sa turɔ yaak ki wun tan lakin kar nanin n naangbouŋ na paak, kaman nan ma nyann nyannu kur ki lakin kar nan n Baa Yennu u naangbouŋ na paak biaŋinba na.
22“Wunba mɔk tuba, wun gbiint linba ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na yeen ki teen Masiaweira na.’ ”

english_text:
Worship in Heaven
1After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.” 2 Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it. 3The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
4Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown. 5 Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne. 6 Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal.
Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes. 7The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle. 8 Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing,
“Holy, holy, holy is the Lord,
the all-powerful God,
who was and is
and is coming!”
9The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever. 10At the same time the 24 elders knelt down before the one sitting on the throne. And as they worshiped the one who lives forever, they placed their crowns in front of the throne and said,
11 “Our Lord and God,
you are worthy
to receive glory,
honor, and power.
You created all things,
and by your decision they are
and were created.”
translation_text:
Ba jiantir Yennu biaŋinba Yendɔuŋ ni
1Li poorpo ki n bia la Yendɔuŋ ni gann ki li loot, ki n gbat kunkɔr nba ki n poŋ gbat na ki li bia piak ki teenin ki naan nan kikaak nba tuu mɔ biaŋinba na; ki kunkɔr maŋ betin a n do nna ki wun wannin linba sa tun nanna poorpo. 2Li taakpaak ni ki Yennu Seyeeŋ na fiir n ni, ki n la ki sɔɔ kar naangbouŋ paak Yendɔuŋ ni, 3ki u numm po nyirii kaman nan tana nba ki bi yi jaspa nan kanelian na, ki naatuuruk bɔɔn ki lint naangbouŋ na, ki naatuuruk maŋ tee kaman nan bonbɔnwuwur na. 4Ki naangbant piinnlee nan ŋanna dɔɔ ki lint naangbouŋ na. Ki saakab piinnlee nan banna lia tapeenii ki yir sarima fokirii ki kar naangbant piinnlee nan ŋanna maŋ paak. 5Ki sanyikintii nyi naangbouŋ maŋ ni nan fuut nan satianii. Ki fita ŋannlore lakin di naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn, ŋanne tee Yennu Sei ŋannlore na. 6Ki siar naan nan mɔkgbeŋŋir ki dɔɔ naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ki tee kaman nan mianu nba nyirii biaŋinba na.
Ki bonfoa banna be leŋ ki lint naangbouŋ na, ki mɔk ninbina bonchiann bi numm po nan bi poorpo kur. 7Sinsinn bonfoor na naan nan yambɔr nee, ki wunba waa bannlee na mun naan nan naajak na, ki wunba waa banntaa na numm po naan nan nifosaarik numm po na, ki wunba waa banna na mun naan nan kanbayuktuk nba yuuk na. 8Ki bonfoa banna na kur mɔk kpinkpant ŋannloob-loob, ki bia mɔk ninbina bonchiann bi gbanant kur po. Ki yennu ni nan nyiɔk kur bi ki nyikit yanii yinu. Bi yiin a,
“Popeendaanɔ, popeendaanɔ, popeendaanɔ tee ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu
nba mɔk paŋ kura,
ki din be, ki bia be nan mɔtana,
ki bia tan sii be yoo nba kaa gbennu na.”
9Bonfoa banna na-i taŋŋi teen wunba kar naangbouŋ na paak ki be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk na niipoouk nan dontir nan paku, 10li yooe ki saakab piinnlee nan banna na tuu baa ki gbaan wunba kar naangbouŋ maŋ paak tɔɔnn, ki pukiiɔ, wunba be mɔkmɔk nan mɔkmɔk na. Bi bia tuu yit bi fokirii nee ki bir naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ŋaan yeen a,
11“Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu, li jaŋ nan tin jianta,
ki tura nibaakir nan pakuwa,
kimaan fine din nan bont kura.
A loomm paake ki a naauŋ maŋ kur be,
ki mɔk manfoor.”

english_text:
The Scroll and the Lamb
1 In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places. 2I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?” 3No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
4I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it. 5 Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
6 Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
7The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne. 8 After he had taken it, the four living creatures and the 24 elders knelt down before him. Each of them had a harp and a gold bowl full of incense, which are the prayers of God's people. 9 Then they sang a new song,
“You are worthy
to receive the scroll
and open its seals,
because you were killed.
And with your own blood
you bought for God
people from every tribe,
language, nation, and race.
10 You let them become kings
and serve God as priests,
and they will rule on earth.”
11 As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them, 12and they were saying in a loud voice,
“The Lamb who was killed
is worthy to receive power,
riches, wisdom, strength,
honor, glory, and praise.”
13Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying,
“Praise, honor, glory,
and strength
forever and ever
to the one who sits
on the throne
and to the Lamb!”
14The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
translation_text:
Gbanpɔɔbir nan Pebik labaar
1Ŋanne ki n bia la ki sɔɔ kar naangbouŋ na paak ki dia gbanpɔɔbir u nuudiitu ni, ki gbouŋ maŋ mɔk sɔbnii li numm po nan li poor po, ki bi din jii tiik yiit ki tabin li mɔi boa ŋannlore ni. 2Ki n la malaka nba mɔk paŋ ki u mɔɔntir nan kunkɔsaakar a “Ŋmee jaŋ nan wun pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir na ki tabit li yiiti?” 3Ŋaan ki sɔɔ kaa Yendɔuŋ ni amii tingbouŋ na ni amii kpeentiŋ ki sa fit pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir na ki got li nɔɔk ni. 4Li yoo ki n bui nan ninnyuut kimaan sɔɔ nba kaa ki jaŋ nan wun pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir na ki got li nɔɔk ni na. 5Ŋanne ki saakab na yenɔ yetin a n daa bui, a n got wunba nyii Juda maaru ni ki u sann yi Juda Yambɔr na, wunba tee David yaaboongbeŋŋir ki nyann bonsiar kur na, ŋɔɔe sa fit ki tabit yiit ŋannlore na ki pɔbit gbanpɔɔbir maŋ.
6Ki n la Pebik ki u see naangbouŋ na boor ki bonfoa banna na nan saakab na see ki lintɔ; ki Pebik na tee kaman nan pebik nba ki bi kpiiu ŋaan ki u fiir kuun ni na, ki u mɔk yina ŋannlore, nan ninbina ŋannlore linba tee Yennu Sei ŋannlore nba ki u tumm tingbouŋ na kur ni na. 7Ki Pebik na saan ki saa gaar gbanpɔɔbir na daanɔ nba kar naangbouŋ na paak u nuudiitu ni. 8Wa gaar gbanpɔɔbir na yoonba ki bonfoa banna na nan saakab piinnlee nan banna na gbaan u tɔɔnn, ki saakab na kur dia biat nan sarima senii, ki bonnunubit nba see Yennu niib miaru seenu niŋ gbee senii na ni ki muu di leŋ. 9Ki bi yiin yanpaanii ki teen Pebik na ki yeen a,
“A set jaŋ nan fan gaar gbanpɔɔbir na
ki tabit li yiit ŋannlore na,
kimaan a din kpoe ki jii a sɔn
ki daa niib ki tur Yennu;
niib maŋ nyii nibooru booru kur ni,
nan mabooru kur ni, nan maaru kur ni,
nan tingbouŋ na yent kur ni.
10A bia te ki bi tee a naan ni niib binba tee manteeb,
a bin tun Yennu toona;
ki bi sii yen tingbouŋ na teeb.”
11Li poorpo biak ki n got ki la malakanba tusa tusa tusa, ki bia gbat bi kunkɔra, ki bi ŋaa lint bonfoa banna na nan saakab na nan naangbouŋ na. 12Ki n gbat ki bi yeen nan kunkɔsaakar a
“Pebik nba ki bi din kpiiu ki u fiir na,
ŋɔɔe jaŋ nan wun gaar paŋ nan mɔkint nan yan
nan nyannu nan nibaakir
nan dontir nan paku.”
13Ki linba kur ki Yennu din nan na, linba be sanpaapo, nan linba be tingbouŋ na ni, nan linba be kpeentiŋ, nan linba be mɔkgbeŋŋa ni, n gbat bi kur yeen a,
“Paku nan nibaakir nan dontir
nan paŋ kur-ii be wunba kar naangbouŋ na paak na
nan Pebik na paak ki jii saa yoo nba kaa gbennu.”
14Ki bonfoa banna na sak ki yet a “Mɔmɔmm.” Ki saakab na kur gbaan Yennu nan Pebik na tɔɔnn ki jiantirib.

english_text:
Opening the Seven Seals
1At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!” 2 Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
3When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!” 4 Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
5 When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand. 6I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
7When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!” 8 Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
9When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith. 10They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
11 Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
12 When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood. 13 The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm. 14 Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
15 The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people. 16 Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb. 17 That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
translation_text:
Yiit na labaar
1Li poorpo ki n la ki Pebik na tabit yiit ŋannlore na yenn, ki n gbat bonfoa banna na yenɔ kunkɔr ki li tee kaman nan satianii na ki yiin a “Baat.” 2Ki n got ki la tanpeeŋ, ki wunba jak u paak na dia tadaauk, ki bi turɔ fokirik, ki u nyii, ki tee paŋ daanɔ, a wun kɔn ki nyann bonsiar kur.
3Ki Pebik na bia tabit yiit nba pukin ŋannlee na, ki n gbat bonfoor nba pukin bannlee na kunkɔr ki u yiin a “Baat.” 4Ŋanne ki n la tanmɔŋ nyii ki set, ki bi tur wunba jakɔ na yaak a wun nyinn parmaasir tingbouŋ na ni ki te ki niib-ii kpi bi leeb; ki bi bia kubinɔ jukbanjagbeŋŋir.
5Ki Pebik maŋ bia tabit yiit nba pukin ŋanntaa na, ki n bia gbat ki bonfoor nba pukin banntaa na yiin a “Baat.” Ki n bia la ki tanbɔŋ see leŋ, ki wunba jakɔ na dia linba ki bi bikii bona na u nuu ni. 6Ki n gbat fuut ki li tee kaman nan kunkɔr na ki nyii bonfoa banna na ni ki yeen a “Diŋmanbaŋ tee daar paauk, a ki karwɔntaa ŋmanbaŋ munntaa mun tee daar paauk; a ŋaan daa biir kpan nan daan na.”
7Ki Pebik na tabit yiit nba pukin ŋanna na; ki n gbat kunkɔr ki li nyii bonfoor nba pukin banna na, ki u yiin a “Baat.” 8Ŋanne ki n la tanpeenwuwur, ki wunba jakɔ na sann tee Kuun; ki kpeentiŋ mun waa u poor; ki Yennu turib yikoo a bin kpi tingbouŋ na niib bɔkitu kann taar munna yenn, a bin kpib nan jukbanjiak nan kon nan yiaru, ki bia kpib nan tingbouŋ na bonkobibiit.
9Ki Pebik na bia tabit yiit nba pukin ŋaŋŋmu na, ki n la ki binba ki niib din kpiib kimaan ba din dia Yennu maan, nan barŋanii nba ki bi din mɔk na paak, ki bi be binbintir na nyakir ni, 10ki yikin sanpaapo ki yeen a, “Ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu, fin nba tee popeendaanɔ ki bia tee barmɔnii daanɔ, a li sa yukir nleewa ki a tan bu tingbouŋ na niib na buut ki dat bi tuba, kimaan ba kpiit na paaki?” 11Ŋanne ki bi tur bi kur liatfopeenii, ŋaan yetib a “I toontunleeb nan i naa waas nba ki niib tan sa kpib kaman nan ba din kpii yimm biaŋinba na, yin foi waan kii guu yoo nba ki bi tan sa kpib ki gbenn.”
12Ki n la Pebik na ki u tabit yiit nba pukin ŋannloob na; ki tiŋ jek bonchiann, ki yennu bɔnn chimm, ki ŋmaamunn mɔnn kaman nan nyumɔnt na. 13Ki ŋmaabira nyii sanpaapo ki baa tiŋ ni kaman nan wouŋ nba tuu fikit ki niŋ kinkaŋ ki u lɔɔna tuu baa biaŋinba na. 14Ki sanpaak pɔbin pɔbin kaman nan pɔɔbit na ki bot, ki jɔɔt kur nan gbingbana kur chaat chaat siaminba ki li tuu be na. 15Ŋanne ki tingbouŋ na kpanbara, nan nijaana, nan lanjimanba yudamm, nan nitarinyikaa, nan panii damm, nan sɔɔ kur, binba tee daaba, nan binba yen bi mɔŋ, ki bi kur chiar chiar ki kɔɔ tankɔrit nan jɔlɔnt ni, 16ki yeen a jɔɔt nan tana na-n baa bi paak, a ki wunba kar naangbouŋ paak na kan lab, a ki Pebik na mun kan lab, nan u wutoor nba doo bi paak na, 17kimaan wunba kar naangbouŋ na paak na nan Pebik na wutoor dagbeŋŋir daar maŋ baar na, ki sɔɔ kaa ki sa set bi wutoor maŋ tɔɔnn po.

english_text:
The 144,000 Are Marked for God
1-2 After this I saw four angels. Each one was standing on one of the earth's four corners. The angels held back the four winds, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree. These angels had also been given the power to harm the earth and the sea. Then I saw another angel come up from where the sun rises in the east, and he was ready to put the mark of the living God on people. He shouted to the four angels, 3 “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
4Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were 144,000, and they came from every tribe of Israel:
512,000 from Judah,
12,000 from Reuben,
12,000 from Gad,
612,000 from Asher,
12,000 from Naphtali,
12,000 from Manasseh,
712,000 from Simeon,
12,000 from Levi,
12,000 from Issachar,
812,000 from Zebulun,
12,000 from Joseph, and
12,000 from Benjamin.
People from Every Nation
9 After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands, 10as they shouted,
“Our God, who sits
upon the throne,
has the power
to save his people,
and so does the Lamb.”
11The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God 12and said,
“Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom,
thanks, honor, power,
and strength belong to our God
forever and ever! Amen!”
13One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
14 “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.”
Then he told me:
“These are the ones
who have gone through
the great suffering.
They have washed their robes
in the blood of the Lamb
and have made them white.
15And so they stand
before the throne of God
and worship him in his temple
day and night.
The one who sits on the throne
will spread his tent
over them.
16 They will never hunger
or thirst again,
and they won't be troubled
by the sun
or any scorching heat.
17 “The Lamb in the center
of the throne
will be their shepherd.
He will lead them to streams
of life-giving water,
and God will wipe all tears
from their eyes.”
translation_text:
Israel teeb labaar
1Li poorpo ki n la malakanba banna ki bi see tingbouŋ na loka ŋanna na ni, ki dia wouŋ nba nyi loka kur na ni, a li daa da tiŋ ni amii mɔkgbeŋŋa ni amii tisiaki. 2Li yoo ki n la malakaganɔ ki u nyii yendopo ki dia dɔk nba tee manfodaanɔ Yennu yar na; ki u yikin nan kunkɔsaakar ki yeen malakanba banna nba ki Yennu turib yaak a bin biir tiŋ nan mɔkgbeŋŋa na, 3a bi daa biir tingbouŋ na nan mɔkgbeŋŋa na nan tiinii na see ki bi tan dɔk Yennu toontunna bi yugbia paak sinsinn. 4-8Ki n gbat binba ki bi dɔkib nan Yennu dɔk na kann, ki bi tee niib tuskobik nan tusa piinna nan tusa ŋanna, binba nyii Israel nibooru kur ni.
Ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee nyii Juda booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Ruben booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Gad booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Aser booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Naftali booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Manase booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Simeon booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Liifai booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Isakar booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Sebulun booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Josef booru ni,
ki niib tusa piik nan ŋannlee mun nyii Benjamin booru ni.
Niburchiauŋ nba din lakin na labaar
9Li poorpoe ki n got ki la niburchiauŋ ki bi kaa kanni, ki nyii tingbouŋ na yent kur ni, nan nibooru booru kur ni, nan maaru kur ni, nan mabooru kur ni, ki lia liatfopeenii ki see naangbouŋ na nan Pebik na tɔɔnn po, ki dia dia biabfaat bi nii ni, ki yikin nan kunkɔsaakara ki yeen a, 10“Tinnu nyi ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu nba kar naangbouŋ na paake, ŋɔɔe nan Pebik na.” 11Ki malakanba na kur set ki lint naangbouŋ na, nan saakab na, nan bonfoa banna na, ki baa funfaba naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ki jiantir Yennu, 12ki yeen a, “Mɔmɔmm, paku nan dontir nan yan nan niipoouk turu nan nibaakir nan paŋ nan yikoo tee Yennu yare mɔkmɔk nan yoo nba kaa gbennu. Mɔmɔmm.”
13Ki saakab na yenɔ boin a “Ki binba lia liatfopeenii na tee lamme, bi nyii lee?” 14Ki n jiin ki yetɔ a “Chanbaa, fine mi, ŋaan min ki mi.”
Ki u yetin a, “Niib na nyii fargbeŋŋir na nie, ki wuur bi liatfɔkit na Pebik na sɔn ni ki li ji peen, 15a li paake ki bi see Yennu naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn ki tuun teenɔ nyiɔk nan yennu ni u jiantu diiuk na ni, a ki ŋɔɔ Yennu nba kar naangbouŋ na paak na sii be namm, ki u maasu sii dɔɔk bi paak; 16a ki kon nan nyunyukiru kan mi soorib, ki yentonu amii tongbeŋsiar kan mi ki toŋ bi gbanu, 17a ki Pebik nba be naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn na sii guub, ki chiamm saan namm nyunbuur nba teen manfoor na ni, a ki Yennu sa fiar bi ninnyuut kur.”

english_text:
The Seventh Seal Is Opened
1When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour. 2I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
3 Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne. 4Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
5 After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook.
The Trumpets
6 The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
7 When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
8When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood, 9a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
10 When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water. 11 The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
12 When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
13Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_text:
Yiit nba pukin ŋannlore na labaar
1Pebik na nba tabit yiit nba pukin ŋannlore na, li yooe ki Yendɔuŋ ni ŋmin surimm, ki li yann waan. 2Li poorpoe ki n la malakanba bannlore nba see Yennu tɔɔnn na; ki u turib kikai ŋannlore. 3Ki n bia la malakaganɔ ki u dia sarima seŋ ki saan saa set binbintir na tɔɔnn. Ki Yennu turɔ wubit bonchiann a wun pukin Yennu niib kur miaru na ni ki jii paan sarima binbintir nba see naangbouŋ na tɔɔnn po na, ki tur Yennu. 4Ki wubit na munyuut nan Yennu niib na miaru nyii malaka nba see Yennu tɔɔnn na nii ni ki do sanpaapo. 5Ki malaka na jii sarima seŋ na ki mɔɔr muu binbintir na paak ki gbeen seŋ na ki kpaar mɔɔn tingbouŋ na ni. Ki saak tian, ki fuut fu, ki sanyikintii nyikii, ki tiŋ damm.
Kikai ŋannlore labaar
6Ki malakanba bannlore nba dia kikai ŋannlore na teen siir nan bin peb kikai na.
7Sinsinn malaka na nba dɔŋ pebir u kikaak na, ki sakaana nan muu ki li ŋmat nan sɔn ki mɔ tingbouŋ na ni, ki tingbouŋ na bɔkitu taar munntaa na yenn dii muu, ki tiinii bɔkitu taar munntaa mun dii muu, ki mɔmaasa kur dii muu.
8Malaka nba pukin bannlee na nba pebir u kikaak na, ki bi lu siar ki li naan nan jɔgbeŋŋir na ki di muu, ki tan baa tingbouŋ na mɔkgbeŋŋa kur ni, ki mɔkgbeŋŋa na bɔkitu munntaa yenn kpant sɔn, 9ki linba be mɔkgbeŋŋa nyun na ni bɔkitu taar munntaa na yenn kpo kpo, ki ŋarint nba paa mɔkgbeŋŋa nyun na paak na, ki li bɔkitu kann taar munntaa yenn kur biir.
10Malaka nba pukin banntaa na nba pebir u kikaak na, ki ŋmaabir gbeŋŋir nba di ki naan nan muu na nyii sanpaapo ki tan baa mɔka nan nyunbua nba be tingbouŋ na ni li bɔkitu taar munntataa yenn ni. 11(Ŋmaabir na sann din yi “Ton.”) Ki nyun na bɔkitu taar munntaa yenn kpant ton; ki niib bonchiann nba nyuu nyun na kpo, kimaan nyun na nba ton na paak. 12Malaka nba pukin banna na nba pebir u kikaak na, ki yennu nan ŋmaarik nan ŋmaabira li chian yenni taar munntaa na ni yenn yenn bɔnn. Ki yennu nan ŋmaarik nan ŋmaabira yentu na li chian yenni taar munntaa bɔnn. Ki yentu nba tuu yeen yennu ni na, ki li chian yeŋ taar munntaa ni ji ki yeeni, ki ŋmaarik nan ŋmaabira mun ji ki yeeni, ki nyiɔk bɔnnu mun bɔkit li biaŋ ki mun bɔnn nna. 13Li yoo ki n got ki la kanbagbeŋŋir ki u yuuk sanpagbant ni, ki yikin nan kunkɔsaakar a “Parbiir be, parbiir be, a malakanba banntaa nba biar na-i taŋŋi pebir kikai na, ki li mɔnii na bui, parbiir tan sii be tingbouŋ na niib paak.”

english_text:
1When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit. 2 As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke. 3 Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
4 The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads. 5The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion. 6 In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
7 These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces. 8 Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion. 9 On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle. 10Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months. 11Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
12The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
13 Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God. 14The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.” 15The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
16By listening, I could tell there were more than 200,000,000 war horses. 17 In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths. 18One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur. 19The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
20 The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk. 21No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_text:
1Ki malaka nba pukin baŋŋmu na pebir u kikaak na, ki n la ki ŋmaabir nyii sanpagbant ni ki tan baa tingbouŋ na ni; ki bi turɔ bootuk nba sumii bonchiann ki kaa terik na li peenu. 2Ki ŋmaabir na loot bootuk na, ki munyuut nyii leŋ ki tee kaman nan musaakau munyuut na, ki li munyuut na dɔkin yennu, ki tingbouŋ na bɔnn. 3Ki tɔɔrii nyii munyuut na ni ki sik tingbouŋ na ni, ki Yennu turib tonu kaman nan nanii nba mɔk tonu tingbouŋ na ni biaŋinba na. 4Ki Yennu yetib a bi daa biir mɔɔt nan bonburkara nan tifaatii, a ŋaan mukis niib nba ki mɔk Yennu dɔkit bi yugbia paak na; 5ki u turib mɔb a bi daa kpi niib maŋ, a ŋaan te ki bin di fara ki saa dian ŋmaarii ŋaŋŋmu, ŋaan bi-i jun nirɔ li tonu sii tee kaman nan naŋ tonu nee. 6Li yoo maŋ, li nibooru sa lon kuun ŋaan kan la, bi bia tan sii loon bin kpo, ŋaan kuun sa chiar ŋaan nyikibe. 7Ki tɔɔrii na tee kaman nan taanii nba tuu teen siir a bin took tɔb nee; ki bi yir linba naan nan sarima fokirii na, ki bi numm po naan nan nifosaarik numm po na, 8ki bi yut naan nan poob yut na, ki bi nyana naan nan yambɔra nyana na. 9Ki bi lia naagbankɔŋŋa ki li naan nan kut na ki dɔkin bi para paak, ki bi kpinkpant fu fuut kaman nan tɔb taanii nba tuu teen siir ki daar torit ki tookit tɔb na. 10Ki bi mɔk joa nan lɔbii ki li naan nan nanii yar na, ki lɔbii maŋ mɔk paŋ ki sa mukis nifosaarii ki saa jaŋ ŋmaarii ŋaŋŋmu nae. 11Ki malaka nba guu bootsunsuŋ maŋ tee bi bat, ki Hiibru maan ni bi yiu Abadon, ki Griik maan ni bi mun yiu Apolion. (Sann maŋ paak tee “Biirtɔɔ.”)
12Sinsinn parbiir na gara, ŋaan parbiir munnlee biar ki waa poor.
Kikaak nba pukin ŋannloob ni labaar
